This functionality makes it very suitable for satellite communications, radar systems, 5 G wireless networks, and defense systems, where circular polarization enhances signal strength and reduces polarization mismatch, electromagnetic interference shielding (EMI).

title: A Thermally Switchable and Polarization Independent Metasurface for Controlled THz Transmission
authors: Ajeet Singh Verma, Somak Bhattacharyya
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a polarizationindependent, thermally switchable metasurface for controlled transmission in the terahertz region. The unit cell of the metasurface consists of a gold square ring integrated with a Jerusalem cross and four vanadium dioxide <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{VO}_{2})$</tex> patches, all deposited on a silicon dioxide substrate. The structure exhibits a dual stop-band response in the insulating state of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{VO}_{2}$</tex>, spanning 0.78 1.2 THz and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.1-2.84 \text{THz}$</tex>, with minima at 1.02 THz and 2.48 THz respectively. In the metallic state, the structure offers a single stop-band from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.83-1.3 \text{THz}$</tex>, centered at 1.1 THz. The metasurface features a compact design with a periodicity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim \lambda / 11$</tex> and thickness of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim \lambda / 64$</tex>, relative to the lowest cutoff transmission frequency. Owing to its switching and compactness features, the proposed structure is well-suited for applications in 6 G communications, biomedical imaging, and THz spectroscopy.

title: Research on Key Technologies for Source Load Cooperative Optimal Design of Distributed Photovoltaic Systems in Coal Mining Enterprise
authors: Xiaodong Li, Yanfang Ren, Shiqi Wang, Xinyue Liu, Yanhong Ren, Hongbo Gao
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Mechanical Engineering and Power Engineering (MEPE)
abstract: Against the backdrop of rapid global economic development, energy demand continues to rise, while the limited reserves of traditional fossil fuels and increasingly severe environmental pollution problems make the search for clean and sustainable energy urgent. In this context, photovoltaic (PV) power generation, with its green, environmentally friendly, and inexhaustible advantages, has become a crucial direction for energy transition. This paper focuses on the electricity demand and energy structure characteristics of coal mining enterprises, conducting in-depth research on the design and optimization of distributed PV systems. Using PVsyst simulation software, a PVsyst was modeled. Based on the 10 MW load capacity of a specific coal mine substation, a 5MW PV power generation system was planned and designed, aiming to achieve the goal of pure self-consumption. Through the appropriate selection of PV equipment and innovative design of the electrical components, this study delves into the key technologies for source-load cooperative optimization. This approach not only enables the stable power supply from the PV system to the coal mining enterprise, achieving self-consumption, but also provides technical references and practical examples for distributed PV projects in the coal mining industry and other industrial fields. It thereby contributes to the optimization of the energy structure and the realization of sustainable development goals.

title: V2X Visible Light Communication Using Rolling Shutter Image Sensor with Offset Unary Encoding
authors: Ryota Kondo, Tsukasa Shimizu, Shintaro Shiba, Norimasa Kobori, Shan Lu, Takaya Yamazato
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This paper presents a visible light V2X communication framework employing a linear LED bar and a rolling shutter image sensor to facilitate robust, high-speed data exchange between vehicles and infrastructure. By exploiting the row-wise exposure characteristic of rolling shutter sensors, rapidly blinking LED signals appear as stripe patterns within the captured images, enabling the efficient extraction of transmitted data. To further improve the transmission rate, we implement offset unary encoding, which utilizes code length rather than absolute brightness to enhance resistance to ambient noise. In addition, a cyclic shift technique is introduced to minimize average transmission time by cyclically rotating quaternary symbols and selecting the most compact arrangement. The system also incorporates a localized image analysis procedure that isolates the LED region, reducing the impact of background interference. Field experiments conducted in a controlled test environment demonstrated reliable communication at speeds of up to 60 km/h, with accurate data transmission under those specific conditions, suggesting the approach's potential for higher-speed operations. The use of standard automotive camera sensors and cost-effective LED devices highlights the practicality of this solution. Overall, the proposed system can serve as an efficient, low-cost, and easily deployable platform for future V2X applications, enhancing connectivity in intelligent transportation systems.

title: MSF-AE: An Autoencoder with Multi-Scale Fusion for Imbalanced Bearing Fault Diagnosis
authors: Ke Meng, Jun Wang, Chuancang Ding, Yifan Huangfu, Weiguo Huang, Zhongkui Zhu
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Rolling bearings are critical components in mechanical driving systems. Accurate bearing fault diagnosis is vital for stability and safety of the machinery. However, in practical industry, normal data often far exceeds faulty data, leading to class imbalance problem. This imbalance situation reduces the classification model's ability to recognize minority fault classes, thereby negatively impacting diagnostic performance. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a new Autoencoder with Multi-Scale Feature Fusion (MSF-AE), which can generate high-quality synthetic data for data augmentation of minority fault classes. The MSF-AE captures multi-scale fault features through multi-branch convolution, improving the model's capacity to represent different fault states. Furthermore, it dynamically fuses the features by a random combination strategy to enhance both robustness and adaptability of the synthetic data. Experimental results demonstrate that the synthetic data generated by MSF-AE contributes to good fault diagnosis performance, providing a robust solution for rolling bearing fault diagnosis, especially under noisy conditions.

title: An Enhanced Cuckoo Search Algorithm for Maximum Power Point Tracking of PV Systems under Partial Shading Conditions
authors: Hayatul Islam Navin, Mohammed Nazmul Islam Nahin, Mahdee Nafis
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: This work proposed an enhanced cuckoo search (ECS) algorithm that utilized a cuckoo chorus call (CCC) mechanism to improve convergence speed for maximum power point tracking (MPPT) under partial shading conditions. The CCC mechanism drew inspiration from cooperative calling behavior observed in cuckoo species and triggered collective convergence when a superior solution was identified, effectively mimicking social coordination in biological parasitism. Comprehensive simulation studies across two representative dynamic partial shading scenarios demonstrated that ECS reduced MPPT tracking time compared to particle swarm optimization (PSO), grey wolf optimization (GWO), and conventional CS. Furthermore, ECS achieved dynamic tracking efficiency improvements while maintaining exceptional GMPP accuracy exceeding 99.6% across all examined partial-shading and fast-irradiance profiles. The MATLAB/Simulink environment was used to verify the superiority of the proposed work.

title: Compact Four-Port MIMO Antenna Incorporating Parasitic Elements for Decoupling in 5G NR n79 Band Applications
authors: Prabhu Kumar Kothavari, Nuthakki Venkata Rajasekhar
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This study unveils a miniature, four-port MIMO antenna specifically adapted to New Radio (NR) applications of fifth-generation (5G) operating bound by N79 frequency band <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(4.4-5.0 \text{GHz})$</tex>. The developed antenna with a FR4 substrate incorporates a novel parasitic element-based decoupling structure, consisting of a hexagonal ring integrated with four stubs, avoiding mutual coupling between the closely spaced radiating elements. The radiating elements are positioned by an element-toelement spacing of 11 mm, ensuring a compact form factor while maintaining high isolation. The proposed MIMO antenna exhibits a reflection coefficient (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{11}$</tex>) lower than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-\mathbf{4 1 d B}$</tex> and maintains an inter-element isolation higher than -18 dB across the entire operational band. The design delivers 4.99 dBi of peak realized gain with a radiation efficiency of 95%. Furthermore, it demonstrates excellent diversity performance with a diversity gain (DG) exceeding 9.99 dB, envelope correlation coefficient (ECC) below 0.001, channel capacity loss (CCL) of 0.33 bits <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$/ \mathrm{s} / \text{Hz}$</tex> and a mean effective gain (MEG) imbalance maintained within -3dB. These results confirm that the proposed parasitic element-based decoupling approach effectively enhances isolation and diversity performance, making the antenna a promising candidate for compact 5G NR N79 band wireless terminal systems.

title: Modeling of Visually Observable Virtual Space for Flight Simulators
authors: Vladimir Roganov, Boris Dolgovesov, Michail Micheev, Aleksandr Savochkin, Ilgiz Sabanchin, Alexey Solodko
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: Modern personal computers with Geforce video cards have created conditions for developing simulators that synthesize visually observable 3D-models of reference objects during a flight on an aviation simulator, synthesized in a real-time cycle of no more than 120 ms. Their placement on the surface of a 3D-model of a flight area allows solving the problems of training pilots in piloting and the problems of training pilots in solving navigation problems to the maximum extent. Previously, the Customer required the developers of aviation simulators to teach pilots professional skills in piloting an aircraft in special flight conditions (training situations). To do this, it was enough to place several 3D-models of reference objects around the 3D-model of the runway. To train pilots in solving navigation problems, navigator simulators were used, allowing them to determine the location of the aircraft model above the earth’s surface model using the readings of the simulators of radio navigation devices. In this case, the flight on the navigator simulator was conducted in “solid cloud cover”. Currently, it has become possible to train pilots to solve navigation problems with orientation using visually observed 3D-models of reference objects visible in different ranges of electromagnetic radiation. This became possible with the development of simulators synthesizing 3D-models of reference objects visible in the visual range of electromagnetic waves, in the IR-range (monochrome coloring) and in the IR-range (multispectral coloring) at the same point in virtual space. The article discusses a solution to the problem that allows combining the locations of 3D-models of reference objects visible in different ranges of electromagnetic radiation.

title: Research on Application of TD-SVSWR Measurement Method for Anechoic Chamber Site Verification
authors: Difei Li, Tieying Feng, Pan Huang, Lifeng Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Information Communication and Software Engineering (ICICSE)
abstract: The time-domain site voltage standing wave ratio (TD-SVSWR) measurement method is a new method proposed by the standard ANSI C63.25 in recent years to verify the radiation emission performance of electromagnetic compatibility anechoic chambers. Different from the CISPR Spatial Sampling measurement method, the TD-SVSWR method has the advantages of dense measurement frequency points, straightforward measurement process, and sensitivity to site characteristics. However, the measurement of TD-SVSWR relies on a vector network analyzer (VNA) with a time-domain option, which undoubtedly increases the hardware cost of the measurement. This paper presents a method based on frequency-domain data post-processing. The time-domain measurement process of TDSVSWR can be completed using a VNA without time-domain option, which reduces the cost of the measurement hardware. Furthermore, an automated measurement software system has been developed to improve measurement efficiency. The paper analyzes the important factors that affect the measurement results, such as the time-gate width, window function setting, and the autocorrelation of data, and clarifies the parameter setting principles that are not specifically described in the standard. This provides a reference-worthy study for TD-SVSWR measurement application.

title: Sparsity-Aware Logarithmic Hyperbolic Cosine Adaptive Filter With Variable Center
authors: Mahima Chouksey, Sandesh Jain, Vimal Bhatia
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: Sparsity-aware adaptive filtering algorithms based on the hyperbolic cosine (HC) and logarithmic HC adaptive filter (AF) called LHCAF have emerged as robust approaches for sparse channel estimation under non-Gaussian noise environments. However, the conventional zero-attracting LHCAF (ZA-LHCAF) algorithm delivers suboptimal performance under non-zero-mean non-Gaussian noise, as it relies on error statistics centered around the origin. To address this issue, we propose a novel ZA-LHCAF with variable center (ZA-LHCAF-VC) algorithm that is robust against non-Gaussian distortions having non-zero mean by incorporating higher-order central moments of error. Furthermore, we propose different variants of sparsity-aware LHCAF-VC (SA-LHCAF-VC) by incorporating the re-weighted L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf>-norm and the arc-tangent based sparsifying norm into the LHCAF-VC cost function. To further enhance the convergence performance, a variable step size (VSS) mechanism is also integrated into SA-LHCAF-VC to yield a novel VSS-SA-LHCAF-VC algorithm. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm exhibits superior convergence performance compared to existing state-of-the-art methods.

title: A Hybrid Neural Approach to Translate Code-Mixed Banglish to Standard Bengali Script
authors: Farjana Rahman, Umme Habiba, Mohammad Ahnaf Tahmeed, Zaynab Begum, Nasif Istiak Remon
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: As information and communication become increasingly globalized, the necessity of a reliable and effective language translation system has become more important. One of these challenges is to transliterate Banglish, a mixed-language mix of Bengali and English that is often used in online communication in Bangladesh. Because of its particular syntactic and semantic characteristics, it poses great challenges to conventional machine translation systems. In this work, we present a new hybrid Neural Machine Translation (NMT) model for translating Banglish to correct Bengali sentences. The architecture of the proposed hybrid model combines CNN structures with Bi-LSTM and Bi-GRU networks to take advantage of their complementary strengths. The model is trained on a dataset consisting of around 20,000 user comments sampled from Facebook and YouTube. We compare the performance of our hybrid model with other NMT models: Bi-GRU, and Transformer. Experimental results show that the hybrid model achieves superior performance, obtaining a BLEU-4 score of 0.4179 and a test accuracy of 97.41%, outperforming the Transformer (94.87%) and Bi-GRU (93.13%) models. These results show the effectiveness of the hybrid approach for Banglish-to-Bengali transliteration. A novel dataset is also introduced for further research on hybrid language transliteration.

title: Miniaturized Circularly Polarized Patch Antenna for Wireless Networks
authors: Amit Kumar Pathak, Maneesha Dwivedi, Deepak Ram, Somak Bhattacharyya, Neelam Srivastava
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This article presents circularly polarized antenna based on defected ground structures. The antenna is designed with a circular radiating patch and a circular slot etched into the ground plane, constructed on a 1.6 mm thick FR4 substrate with a copper backing. The proposed antenna exhibits a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{- 1 0}-\mathbf{d B}$</tex> reflection coefficient of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{- 3 6. 2 7} \mathbf{~ d B}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{- 2 2. 5 1}$</tex> dB, along with 3-dB axial ratio (AR) Bandwidth and Impedance Bandwidth (IBW) of 39.66 % and 4.72 % operating at 2.53 GHz and 4.31 GHz respectively. At 2.53 GHz, the antenna exhibits maximum realized gain of 3.50 dBi. Demonstrating close alignment between measured and simulated results, the compact circularly polarized antenna <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.211 \lambda o \times 0.211 \lambda o \times 0.0135 \lambda o$</tex> at 2.53 GHz), is optimized for dual-band use in wireless networks such as Wi-Fi, IoT systems, and defense communication and etc.

title: Practical Implementation of the Miniaturized Matched Five-Port Network with Equal Power Division
authors: Aduitya Kaushal, Rakesh Sinha
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents the miniaturization of a fiveport network by replacing conventional transmission lines with equivalent symmetric T structures. In addition, a systematic optimization technique for the physical layout of microstrip-linebased networks is discussed. A conventional five-port network designed for 2 GHz occupies an area of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$13.68 ~\text{cm}^{2}$</tex>, whereas the T-structure-based five-port network designed for 2 GHz reduces the area to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7.60 ~\text{cm}^{2}$</tex>. This corresponds to an area ratio of 0.55, achieving a reduction of 44.45% in size. Full-wave simulations and optimizations were performed using MATLAB 2025b. Two prototypes were fabricated and measured using a Keysight N5222B four-port vector network analyzer.

title: Design of Multi-Tone Timed Array with Directed and Omni-Directed Beams Employing Evolutionary Algorithms
authors: D Suneel Varma, Gopi Ram, Guy A E Vandenbosch, Arun Kumar Gande
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper aims to utilize the multi-tone radiation from timed antenna array (TAA) to radiate simultaneous directed and omni-directed beams employing the evolutionary algorithms. The simultaneous radiation includes directed radiation in fundamental frequency and omni-directional radiation in first harmonic frequency. The optimization is employed to enhance the power in first harmonic omni-directional pattern to extend the use of TAA. Further, the optimization is conducted by utilizing Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and Teaching Learning Based Optimization (TLBO) for the identical simulation study. The simulation is conducted in three simulation scenarios to steer fundamental beam to 30, 45, and −30 degrees. The simulation study conveys that the TLBO out performs PSO in terms of enhancing the power in the first harmonic beam.

title: Hazard Identification and Risk Early-warning Method for Civil Aircraft Product
authors: Xu Meiqi, Zheng Youshi
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Civil aircraft safety is the cornerstone of the development of civil aviation industry. ICAO and CAAC have put forward higher requirements for the product safety of civil aircraft manufacturers. This paper begins with the civil aircraft product safety hazards, through the construction of a structured risk factor database and the extraction of product safety indicators, establishes a set of product safety hazard identification and dynamic monitoring and early-warning methods suitable for the civil aircraft industry, and finally elaborates the application of methods in detail with specific examples. Our research covers from identification to management of civil aircraft product hazards, which is conducive to improving the quality of identification and management of safety hazards in the whole life cycle of civil aircraft, shedding light on safety management of aviation industry.

title: A Comparative Evaluation of Image Pre-processing Techniques for Corn Leaf Disease Classification
authors: Sakib Imtiaz, Emrana Kabir Hashi, Md. Ashikuzzaman Ashik, Md. Efat Al Khalid Shahadout, Zahara Tasnim
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: The swift progression of deep learning has markedly advanced corn leaf disease detection, enabling highly efficient and accurate classification from corn leaf imagery. Yet, the influence of image pre-processing on model performance remains a critical and nuanced area of inquiry, particularly for complex, real-world agricultural datasets. This study offers a comparative assessment of prominent pre-processing techniques Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE) and Gaussian blur on the performance of several leading deep convolutional neural network architectures, including VGG16, ResNet50, InceptionV3, Xception, MobileNetV2, DenseNet121, and EfficientNetB0, for corn leaf disease classification. Utilizing a publicly available corn leaf disease dataset from Kaggle, we systematically applied and examined the impact of each pre-processing method, both individually and in combination, across all selected models. Notably, our findings indicate that the highest classification accuracy 98% was achieved using the ResNet50 architecture with Gaussian blur as the sole pre-processing step. This result surpasses the performance of the same model under other pre-processing conditions and exceeds that of all other model-pre-processing combinations evaluated. While CLAHE and the combined use of both pre-processing techniques yielded moderate gains for specific architectures, neither approach surpassed the ResNet50 with Gaussian blur. These outcomes suggest that judicious application of certain image enhancement methods can still deliver meaningful improvements, even as advanced networks demonstrate robust capability to compensate for raw image variability internally. This work highlights the necessity of critically benchmarking pre-processing strategies, rather than adopting them by default, and provides valuable guidance for researchers and practitioners in constructing resilient disease classification pipelines for precision agriculture.

title: Efficomm: Bandwidth Efficient Multi Agent Communication
authors: Melih Yazgan, Allen Xavier Arasan, J. Marius Zöllner
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Collaborative perception allows connected vehicles to exchange sensor information and overcome each vehicle's blind spots. Yet transmitting raw point clouds or full feature maps overwhelms Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) communications, causing latency and scalability problems. We introduce EffiComm, an end-to-end framework that transmits <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<40 \%$</tex> of the data required by prior art while maintaining state-of-the-art 3-D object-detection accuracy. EffiComm operates on Bird's-Eye-View (BEV) feature maps from any modality and applies a two-stage reduction pipeline: (1) Selective Transmission (ST) prunes low-utility regions with a confidence mask; (2) Adaptive Grid Reduction (AGR) uses a Graph Neural Network (GNN) to assign vehicle-specific keep ratios according to role and network load. The remaining features are fused with a soft-gated Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) attention layer, offering greater capacity and specialization for effective feature integration. On the OPV2V benchmark, EffiComm reaches <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 8 4}-\mathbf{m A P} {@} \mathbf{0. 7}$</tex> while sending only an average of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 1.5 \text{MB}$</tex> per frame, outperforming previous methods on the accuracy-per-bit curve. These results highlight the value of adaptive, learned communication for scalable Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) perception.

title: BanglaPoliText: A Balanced Corpus and Model Benchmark for Bengali Political Statement Detection
authors: Khondoker Muntahin Hassan Ankur, Md Nasit Jakoan, Md Shohanur Rahman Shohan, Saraouth Jahan Prapty, Mina Akter, Afrina Ferdaus Upama, Sadia Nasir
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: The intensive increase of political content on digital media underscores the necessity of the provision of high-quality automatic classification of political utterances, particularly in low-resource languages such as Bengali. Although the Bengali language is one of the most spoken languages in the world, the classification of political texts has not been explored much, and the majority of previous studies have been done on sentiment, hate speech, or toxicity. In order to bridge this gap we introduce BanglaPoliText, which is a manually collected dataset of 9,289 samples of Bengali with a specific interest in binary political and non-political text classification. We compare 20 models, such as the traditional ML, deep neural networks, hybrid and Transformers. The highest accuracy was recorded by the fine-tuned BanglaBERT model of 93.39% with the BanglaBERT + CNN + GRU + Attention hybrid model coming closely behind. Other measures like Cohen’s Kappa and MCC, also confirm the robustness of models. The contribution we make creates a good standard in the field of classifying political text in Bengali and further develops inclusive NLP research in underrepresented languages.

title: An Attention Guided Lightweight U-Net Framework for Colon Polyp Segmentation in Colonoscopy Images
authors: Abdullah Al Asif, Abduz Zami, Sheikh Fazle Rabbi, Tanvir Mahtab Zihan, Naheen Mohd. Kadir, Alifa Shanzidah Manarat
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: To reduce the risk of colorectal cancer, colon polyps must be precisely segmented. However, sensitivity to small or low contrast polyps, computational efficiency, and generalization are issues with many deep learning models. We propose a lightweight segmentation framework based on UNet that employs Squeeze and Excitation (SE) channel attention to address these issues. With only 1.9 million parameters, our architecture maintains a compact design while improving feature recalibration by embedding SE modules within each convolutional pair. As a result, the model can concentrate more on clinically significant structures without consuming additional processing power. Three popular datasets: CVC-ColonDB, CVC-ClinicDB, and Kvasir-SEG were used to train and evaluate the framework. We employed a consistent preprocessing and augmentation pipeline to ensure its dependability. The model’s Dice scores of 0.8667 on CVC ColonDB, 0.8783 on CVC ClinicDB, and 0.851 on Kvasir demonstrate its effectiveness under various imaging scenarios. The model performed well in maintaining the boundaries and steadily converged despite its smaller size. These results imply that the suggested SE enhanced UNet successfully strikes a balance between computational cost and precision, making it suitable for real time or resource constrained clinical settings. Additionally, the framework provides a solid foundation for upcoming additions that make use of transformer based designs and hybrid attention.

title: PhantomNet: A Lightweight Convolutional Network for Distraction Detection in Automotive Drivers
authors: Rahimul I. Mazumdar, Anirban Dasgupta, Parijat Bhowmick
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Driver distraction is a critical factor contributing to road accidents, underscoring the need for real-time, nonintrusive monitoring solutions. This paper presents PhantomNet, a lightweight convolutional neural network specifically designed for distraction classification using side-view images from the State Fram Distracted Driver Detection (SFDDD) dataset. The proposed PhantomNet introduces an efficient hybrid module that extracts expressive features with minimal computational overhead. The proposed model achieves a classification accuracy of 96.07 %, outperforming existing lightweight architectures such as GhostNet, while significantly reducing model size and inference time. These results highlight PhantomNet's potential for real-time deployment in embedded automotive systems to proactively enhance the driver safety.

title: Defected Ground Metasurface for RF Energy Harvesting
authors: Vineesh K Vinod, R. Aneesh Kumar, Chinmoy Saha
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel approach for RF energy harvesting at 2.45 GHz using a metasurface integrated with a selectively etched ground plane. Introducing a suitably engineered ground defect into the metasurface structure enables energy absorption into the load comparable to that of a full-ground configuration, with negligible additional loss. Moreover, such a defected ground structure can permit placement of rectifiers in the ground plane itself, without the need of an additional layer. This has a potential for easy integration of energy harvesting system in compact size. By investigating the surface current distribution in a simple, standard unit cell structure, a selective ground-plane etching strategy was adopted for creating the ground defect. This helps in designing a defected ground metasurface without disrupting the resonant mode. A study on etched ground was carried out to arrive at the final dimensions. The simulated results of proposed defected ground metasurface structure demonstrates an RF to AC conversion efficiency of around 90% which is close to that obtained for a conventional metasurface structure with full ground. The proposed single-layer, subwavelength metasurface can offer a compact and easy manufacturable solution for selfpowered wireless sensor systems.

title: Modeling IGBT Modules Degradation Process based on Fractional Weibull
authors: Yixin Zhang, Jiaqi Hua, Wanqing Song
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Insulated Gate Bipolar Transistor (IGBT) is widely used, so the prediction of its remaining life (Remaining Useful life; RUL) is of great practical significance. Through the analysis of IGBT faults and failures, this article proposes a complex health index (Complex Health Indicator; CHI) construction method based on multi-sensor fusion, and proposes a multi-mode fractional Weibull process (fWp) to modeling the degradation model of the IGBT module. In the proposed degradation model, the drift parameters and diffusion parameters have a direct proportional relationship.

title: Semantically-Aware Exploration in Zero-Shot Object Goal Navigation via Bayesian Memory
authors: Junyi Hou, Wenxin Zhu, Qinyuan Ren
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Navigating in unfamiliar environments to an open-vocabulary target object which has never been seen before poses a significant challenge. This work proposes an efficient exploration strategy for Zero-Shot Object Goal Navigation (ZS-OGN) tasks which exploits spatial object-target relationships derived from temporal observations to guide exploration. The exploration strategy includes a fine-grained scene understanding module that enables the agent to extract comprehensive semantic relationships from open-vocabulary objects, followed by a robust Bayesian spatial memory that integrates temporal scene understanding results to maintain robust environmental representation in unfamiliar environments, thus enabling semantically-aware navigation decision making. By comprehensively scene understanding and robust memory constructing, the agent can navigate to regions most likely to contain the target object based on object relationships, thereby enhancing exploration efficiency. Evaluated on the HM3D and MP3D datasets, this strategy significantly outperforms baselines in both success rate and exploration efficiency. Comprehensive ablation studies further validate that scene understanding and spatial memory synergistically improve exploration efficiency.

title: Experimental Validation of Miniaturized Sharp Rejection IBPF with Improved Spurious Response for Broadband RF Receivers
authors: Surendra Kumar Bitra, Vyshnavi Avusali, Venkata Satya Karthik N, Debaprasad Barad, Bhushan Deshpande, Balachary Molupoju
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: In this paper, the design and development of a compact interdigital band pass filter (IBPF) based on uniform transmission lines (UTLs). The filter is designed to operate over the frequency range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2-3 \text{GHz}$</tex> with a bandwidth of 1 GHz, with a FBW of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>40 \%$</tex>. The <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$11{ }^{\text{th }}$</tex> order IBPF provides a sharp rejection of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 69 \text{dB}$</tex> insertion loss at 3.5 GHz. The second harmonic is completely suppressed, and the spurious response at the second harmonic is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<-50 \text{dB}$</tex> of insertion loss over the required frequency range. The Rogers 5880 material with 20 mil-thick substrate for designing the IBPF, and the overall size of the filter is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$28 \text{mm} \times 28 \text{mm} \times 0.508 \text{mm}$</tex>. The proposed work is best suitable for defense applications for highly accurate systems. The filter performance with the RF receiver system is demonstrated in this paper.

title: Safe Reinforcement Learning Control for Maglev Train Levitation System with Robust Safety Assurance
authors: Xiaoning Zhao, Yougang Sun, Junqi Xu, Haiyan Qiang
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: High-speed maglev trains represent a novel intelligent transportation, where the performance of the levitation system directly determines the safety and stability of train operation. To address the issues of poor adaptability in traditional methods and insufficient safety in reinforcement learning approaches for high-speed maglev train levitation control systems, this paper designs a safe reinforcement learning (RL) method for the levitation system based on highorder control barrier functions (CBF). A hierarchical control architecture is constructed to integrate a CBF safety correction layer, and differentiable optimization methods are employed to embed CBF into the RL algorithm, enabling safety constraints and stable convergence of the levitation control system. Additionally, Gaussian processes are introduced to predict external disturbances, enhancing robustness and theoretically ensuring robust safety during both training and operation. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method maintains stable levitation under static levitation initiation and irregularity disturbance conditions, with the maximum error reduced by 47.3% compared to traditional methods in certain scenarios, verifying its superiority in safety and adaptability.

title: A Comparative Study of Multi-Vehicle Knowledge Fusion Methods
authors: Shanghang Zhou, Shuo Yang, Zhenyu Ma, Wei Du, Xincheng Li, Jiaming Xing, Yanjun Huang
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Self-learning techniques show great potential to improve the adaptability of autonomous vehicles in dynamic environments. However, existing self-learning approaches mainly rely on isolated learning paradigms, resulting in fragmented knowledge accumulation. To expand the scope of self-learning techniques and strengthen the generalization capabilities of autonomous driving agents, many studies have explored multivehicle knowledge fusion, leveraging the knowledge accumulated across multiple agents to build more robust and scalable models. This paper reviews current knowledge fusion techniques and presents a case study of two core approaches: policy distillation and model merging. We evaluate these methods in representative traffic scenarios and discuss their respective strengths, limitations, and unique characteristics. Experimental results demonstrate that policy distillation delivers high accuracy at the cost of substantial resources, whereas model merging is more resource-efficient but less precise. By integrating both methods, we drastically reduce computational costs while achieving 92% of expert model performance in diverse environments, effectively combining the complementary strengths of data-driven and model-based approaches.

title: Goal-Based Trajectory Prediction for Improved Cross-Dataset Generalization
authors: Daniel Grimm, Ahmed Abouelazm, J. Marius Zöllner
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: To achieve full autonomous driving, a good understanding of the surrounding environment is necessary. Especially predicting the future states of other traffic participants imposes a non-trivial challenge. Current SotA-models already show promising results when trained on real datasets (e.g. Argoverse2 [1], NuScenes [2]). Problems arise when these models are deployed to new/unseen areas. Typically, performance drops significantly, indicating that the models lack generalization. In this work, we introduce a new Graph Neural Network (GNN) that utilizes a heterogeneous graph consisting of traffic participants and vectorized road network. Latter, is used to classify goals, i.e. endpoints of the predicted trajectories, in a multi-staged approach, leading to a better generalization to unseen scenarios. We show the effectiveness of the goal selection process via crossdataset evaluation, i.e. training on Argoverse2 [1] and evaluating on NuScenes [2].

title: A Kalman Filter-Based Disturbance Observer for Steer-by-Wire Systems
authors: Nikolai Beving, Jonas Marxen, Steffen Müller, Johannes Betz
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Steer-by-Wire systems replace mechanical linkages, which provide benefits like weight reduction, design flexibility, and compatibility with autonomous driving. However, they are susceptible to high-frequency disturbances from unintentional driver torque - known as driver impedance which can degrade steering performance. Existing approaches either rely on direct torque sensors - which are costly and impractical - or lack the temporal resolution to capture rapid, high-frequency driver-induced disturbances. We address this limitation by designing a Kalman filter-based disturbance observer that estimates high-frequency driver torque using only motor state measurements. We model the driver's passive torque as an extended state using a PT1-lag approximation and integrate it into both linear and nonlinear Steer-by-Wire system models. In this paper, we present the design, implementation and simulation of this disturbance observer with an evaluation of different Kalman filter variants. Our findings indicate that the proposed disturbance observer accurately reconstructs driver-induced disturbances with only minimal delay (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 14 \text{ms}$</tex>). We show that a nonlinear extended Kalman Filter outperforms its linear counterpart in handling frictional nonlinearities, improving estimation during transitions from static to dynamic friction. Given the study's methodology, it was unavoidable to rely on simulation-based validation rather than real-world experimentation. Further studies are needed to investigate the robustness of the observers under real-world driving conditions.

title: Real-Time Traffic Incident Detection with Sparse Observations: A Masked Spatiotemporal Graph Learning Framework
authors: Xiaolei Zhu, Qiaowen Bai, Ghim Ping Ong, Biplab Sikdar
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Timely and accurate incident detection is essential for maintaining the efficiency and resilience of transportation systems, especially under sparse sensing conditions. This paper proposes a Masked Spatiotemporal Graph Learning (MSTGL) framework for robust incident detection using sparse traffic observations. The framework integrates a spatial graph attention encoder to model localized traffic interactions and a BERT-based temporal encoder to capture evolving temporal dynamics. A masked supervision strategy is introduced during training to enhance the predictive capability of the traffic state decoder, which forecasts future traffic conditions from spatiotemporal embeddings. On top of this, we propose a dualwindow anomaly detection module, which utilizes both shortterm and long-term prediction residuals to identify incidentinduced deviations in real time. The proposed method is evaluated on a campus-scale traffic network in Singapore with sparse fixed-point camera coverage. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework significantly improves detection performance, achieving a 17.76 % higher F1-score compared to state-of-the-art baselines.

title: Electromagnetic Modeling and Analysis of Radome Wall with Char Residue Using Inhomogeneous Planar Layers
authors: Rudra Narayan Barik, Hrishikesh S. Sonalikar
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper examines the effect of char residue on the electromagnetic performance of an Avcoat radome wall. Airborne radomes experience aerodynamic heating during flight, causing material ablation. This might lead to the formation of char residue on the outer surface of the radome. The presence of char alters the material's dielectric properties. This leads to the attenuation of electromagnetic signals, affecting critical performance metrics like power transmission and insertion phase delay. In this paper, different stages of a charred radome wall are modeled and analyzed using the inhomogeneous planar layer model. The performance metrics of the radome wall are computed using the equivalent transmission line method. On the onset of char formation, power transmission decreases to approximately 42% of the initial power. The maximum phase change observed is 22°. These results might be useful for designing the airborne radomes for high-speed applications.

title: Cross-Mission Health Index Construction and Predictive Maintenance: A Universal Post-Processing Framework for PHM Time Series Foundation Models
authors: Jiachen Jin, Yili Wang, Liming Xuan, Honghua Zhao, Wei Cheng, Yuanxiang Li
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: While time series foundation models excel at single-mission anomaly detection in prognostics and health management (PHM), they fundamentally lack the capability to capture cumulative degradation patterns across multiple operational cycles—a critical limitation for long-term health monitoring in complex systems. This paper introduces a universal post-processing framework that transforms foundation model outputs into interpretable health indices, enabling systematic cross-mission degradation trend analysis. Our approach integrates residual-based integration components with statistical measures, incorporating temporal decay mechanisms to construct mission-specific health indices that aggregate into comprehensive predictive maintenance indicators. The framework maintains compatibility with arbitrary PHM time series foundation models while requiring no architectural modifications. Extensive validation on a certain model of large civil aircraft bleed air system demonstrates superior performance: 4.6-mission advance warning capability with 71.4% precision, achieving 56.5% improvement in early warning lead time and 33% reduction in false alarms compared to conventional methods. This work bridges the critical gap between foundation model capabilities and industrial predictive maintenance requirements, enabling the transition from reactive single-mission detection to proactive multi-mission health management.

title: Reliable Lightweight SAR Small Target Detection Algorithm Based on Parallel Fusion of Multi-scale Convolution Kernels
authors: Changbing Zhai, Zhuoran Cai
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: In the domain of synthetic aperture radar (SAR) imagery, detecting small ships is challenging due to environments with resource constraints. The research presents a reliable technique derived from the YOLOv11n network, aimed at enhancing the identification of small ships in scenarios of low altitude and reducing computational costs. The approach is anchored in three pivotal areas. Firstly, the enhancement of small target ship feature fusion through the utilization of a weighted Bidirectional Feature Pyramid Network (BiFPN). Secondly, the substitution of conventional upsampling structures with a more efficient and lightweight dynamic sampling mechanism, termed DySample. And lastly, the introduction of a multi-scale convolutional kernel parallel module, MSC3k2, which facilitates multi-scale feature representation, hierarchical feature fusion, and the selection of heterogeneous convolutional kernels. Upon evaluation using LS-SSDD-v1.0 and HRSID datasets, the algorithm recorded mAP50 scores of 75.9% and 94.0%. Additionally, it decreased parameters by 22.1% and the computational burden by 10.1% relative to the standard YOLOv11n. The findings emphasize its enhanced precision and reliability in identifying minor targets within intricate SAR images.

title: An Improved Localization Method for Mudslide Monitoring, Based on 3D DV-Hop and Differential Evolution Algorithm
authors: Shiquan Fang, Tong Hao
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Information and Communication Networks (ICICN)
abstract: Monitoring and early warning systems for mudslide have always been critical in disaster prevention. Traditional mudslide monitoring methods (e.g., remote sensing or ranging-based positioning technologies) suffer from technical or cost limitations. In contrast, non-ranging node localization methods, such as the distance vector hop (DV-Hop) method in wireless sensor networks (WSNs), offer promising operability and safety in adapting to complex mudslide localization scenarios. This study constructs a 3D simulated mudslide environment for monitoring purposes and introduces a mudslide monitoring differential evolution DVHop (MMDE-DV-Hop) algorithm to achieve node localization with improved accuracy. By refining the initial range of the differential evolution (DE) algorithm and dynamically adjusting search factors, we enhance the DE algorithm’s optimization capability. By simulating node displacement after a mudslide event and comparing the results with the traditional DV-Hop, and its variant algorithms (e.g., DE-DV-Hop, and GA-DV-Hop), the proposed MMDE-DV-Hop algorithm improves the average localization accuracy by 64.67%, 23.58% and 14.89%, respectively.

title: A Metasurface-Based Wideband CP Patch Antenna with Partially Grounded and Improved Gain
authors: Deepak Ram, Amit Kumar Singh, Somak Bhattacharyya
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: A circularly polarized (CP) microstrip patch antenna featuring a partial ground plane and incorporating a metasurface (MS) structure is introduced in this study. The design is composed of a truncated square patch, which is etched onto a 1.6 mm thick FR-4 substrate and integrated with a ringshaped loaded with plus slot along with two hexagonal slots on the same patch. A metasurface superstrate, consisting of a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \times 4$</tex> array of ring elements, is placed above the patch and fabricated on a similar FR-4 substrate. An impedance bandwidth of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{- 1 0 ~ d B}$</tex> is achieved from 4.46 GHz to 6.81 GHz, and an axial ratio bandwidth between 5.0 GHz and 5.33 GHz is obtained. At 5.10 GHz, a peak realized gain of 6.88 dBic with left-hand circular polarization (LHCP) is observed. The prototype is fabricated, and its performance is measured, showing a high degree of agreement with the simulated results. With dimensions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 6 4 \lambda _ { 0 }} \times 0.64 \lambda_{0} \times 0.027 \lambda_{0}$</tex> at 5.12 GHz, the proposed antenna is considered well-suited for WLAN and satellite uplink communication applications.

title: Air-written First Six Bangla Vowel Character Recognition using Statistical Feature Extraction and Classical Shallow Learning Models
authors: Tahrima Sayem Sowa, Sweety Akter Sima, Meherun Fabiha, Adiba Mahjabin Nitu, Md. Abdulla Al Mamun
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Air-written character recognition technology has placed itself as a promising alternative to traditional text input methods in wearable devices and assistive systems, as they allow surface-free writing through motion tracking. However, recognizing complex scripts remains challenging due to high inter-character similarity, variations in writing styles and sensor noise. Prior studies in this area mainly focused on the English, Latin, and Arabic languages, and most of them used deep learning approaches. Furthermore, limited studies have been done on Bangla literature, with very few works employing shallow learning models and not enough available data on sensor-based datasets. To address this gap, this study explores IMU-based recognition of the first six Bangla vowel characters using four shallow machine learning models. A dataset comprising 3,006 samples, having 501 samples per character, was collected using a finger-mounted IMU sensor capturing six motion channels: three gyroscope and three accelerometer axes, preprocessed through labeling and resizing to fixed length for consistency, extracted 8 time-domain statistical features: minimum, maximum, mean, median, standard deviation, RMS value, skewness, and kurtosis, and employed SVM, Random Forest, KNN, and GBM on them. The SVM model achieves the highest accuracy of 81.68% among Random Forest (80.53%), KNN (77.23%) and GBM (74.92%). These findings demonstrate the feasibility of shallow learning methods for Bangla air-written vowel recognition and they highlight their potential for developing lightweight, real-time and language-inclusive input systems in wearable and assistive technologies.

title: SPM-Based Adaptive Resonant Frequency Band Extraction and Fault Diagnosis Method Using Restructured Feature Mode Decomposition
authors: Yongjia Peng, Kun Feng
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: To address the limitations of traditional Shock Pulse Method (SPM) in extracting resonance frequency bands and its poor adaptability to varying resonance frequencies, this paper proposes an adaptive fault diagnosis approach based on a restructured Feature Mode Decomposition (FMD) algorithm. The proposed method integrates FMD with SPM to enhance bearing fault detection by enabling adaptive resonance band extraction. The restructured FMD algorithm incorporates multi-indicator fusion, entropy-weighted mode selection, and mutual information-based redundancy suppression to improve the accuracy of modal selection. Additionally, Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) and the half-power bandwidth technique are applied to automatically identify resonance band boundaries. Experimental validation using a custom-built bearing fault simulation test rig demonstrates that the proposed framework significantly enhances the adaptability, diagnostic precision, and fault localization accuracy of conventional SPM, enabling robust and accurate bearing fault diagnosis under varying operating conditions.

title: MultiDrive: A Co-Simulation Framework Bridging 2D and 3D Driving Simulation for AV Software Validation
authors: Marc Kaufeld, Korbinian Moller, Alessio Gambi, Paolo Arcaini, Johannes Betz
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Scenario-based testing using simulations is a cornerstone of software validation for Autonomous Vehicles (AVs). So far, developers needed to choose between low-fidelity 2D simulators to explore the scenario space efficiently, and high-fidelity 3D simulators to study relevant scenarios in more detail, thus reducing testing costs while mitigating the sim-to-real gap. This paper presents a novel framework that leverages multi-agent co-simulation and procedural scenario generation to support scenario-based testing across low- and high-fidelity simulators for the development of motion planning algorithms. Our framework limits the effort required to transition scenarios between simulators and automates experiment execution, trajectory analysis, and visualization. Experiments with a reference motion planner demonstrate that our framework reveals discrepancies between the planner's intended and actual behavior, thus exposing weaknesses in planning assumptions under more realistic conditions. Our framework is available at: https://github.com/TUM-AVS/MultiDrive.

title: An X-Ku Band Direct Frequency Synthesizer Using Dual DDS Architecture
authors: Soumyadeep Biswas, Sonal G. Naik, Y. Hemalatha
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of an X-Ku band direct frequency synthesizer using a dual Direct Digital Synthesizer (DDS) architecture. The first DDS generates a VHF-band signal with fine resolution. This signal is upconverted to the L-band using a Radio Frequency (RF) mixer. The second DDS serves as a programmable Local Oscillator (LO) for the mixer. Tunable Bandpass Filters (BPFs) are placed at the mixer output to suppress undesired intermodulation products. Frequency multipliers are used after the mixer to further extend the output band. This architecture provides frequency agility along with low phase noise and spurious levels in the upconverted signal. It also avoids the filtering challenges typically encountered due to spectral foldback, phase truncation spurs, Digital-toAnalog Converter (DAC) nonlinearities, and quantization artifacts in a DDS. These problems become significant when a single DDS is operated across a wide frequency band close to its Nyquist frequency. The implemented synthesizer hardware achieves a frequency switching time of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq 500 \text{ns}$</tex> with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 1 \text{MHz}$</tex> accuracy. It also demonstrates spurious levels <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq-\mathbf{5 0 ~ d B c}$</tex> and phase noise <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq-105 \text{dBc} / \text{Hz}$</tex> at a 10 kHz offset. The proposed design is well suited for surveillance and monitoring in fastscanning microwave receiver systems.

title: Design of High-Throughput MMIC Parallel-Combined Power Amplifier for 5G Application
authors: Indranil Karmakar, Ashok Chauhan, Kuldip Singh, Shubhankar Majumdar, Pijus Kundu
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a compact three-stage MMIC power amplifier (PA) using UMS 150 nm GaN-on-SiC HEMT technology with a parallel-combined power amplifier (PCPA) topology to minimize phase deviation over a wide frequency range. The PA achieves a small signal gain over 35dB, saturated power <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{P}_{\text{sat }})$</tex> of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 7. 5 - 4 0. 3 d B m}$</tex>, and a peak PAE of 20 36.9% in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$24-28 \text{GHz}$</tex> range with CW excitation. Input and output return losses are below −10 dB, indicating a well-matched design. In multi-tone simulation, it demonstrates good linearity −10dBc IMD3 and over 42 dBm TOI at higher input levels, with nominal AM-AM and AM-PM variation, making it suitable for 5G mm-Wave communication.

title: Investigation of Transmission Line Alignment in SLCDR-Based Angular Displacement Sensors
authors: Premsai Regalla, Praveen Kumar A. V., Amir Ebrahimi, Kamran Ghorbani
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This study investigates the various alignments of transmission lines loaded microwave resonator based angular sensor configurations for displacement sensing applications. This opens the scope for analyzing the impact of transmission line alignment on the performance of microwave resonator-based angular displacement sensors. The sensor operates on the principle of electromagnetic coupling, where the rotational movement of the proposed resonator i.e., strip loaded cylindrical dielectric resonator (SLCDR) alters the coupling strength between the lines. This manifests as a change in the transmission coefficient (|S21|) at resonance, which forms the basis for angular displacement measurements. The sensor designs are simulated using ANSYS HFSS to evaluate the sensor key performance parameters. A comparative analysis is presented in tabular form, highlighting the benefits and limitations of each configuration across multiple sensing criteria.

title: Corrosion Level Assessment of Rebars in Reinforced Concrete Structures with Eddy Current Technique
authors: Islam M. Bogotov, Valery P. Lunin
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: This paper presents the study and development of a device for monitoring the corrosion condition of steel bars in reinforced concrete structures using the eddy current method. To carry out the study, a finite element model was constructed for a multi-turn coil integrated into a resonant RLC circuit. The analysis focuses primarily on two key parameters: the effective resistance $R_{\text {eff }}$ and the effective inductance $L_{\text {eff }}$ of the system, both of which show a stable dependence on the degree of corrosion damage. The results demonstrate the high sensitivity of the developed scheme to changes in the reinforcement condition, enabling not only the detection of corrosion but also the quantitative assessment of damage severity. These findings support the potential of the proposed method for efficient, nondestructive monitoring of reinforced concrete structures without compromising the integrity of the protective concrete cover.

title: Electromagnetic Modeling of RIS-Antenna Configuration Using MoM-PO Approximation
authors: Sourasami Nandi, Soumyajit Roy, Sandip Ghosal
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) are constituted using a periodic arrangement of unit cells whose full-wave electromagnetic characterization is computationally challenging due to their large electrical size. Although plane-wave approximation modeling with periodic Green's function can provide a rough estimate of its scattering characteristics, the mutual interaction of the unit cell elements starts to play a non-trivial role in the high-frequency band. In addition, when a finite transmitter is placed in the closer vicinity of the RIS, the plane-wave approximation becomes no longer valid. To address such use cases, this work proposes the idea of leveraging a hybrid MoM-PO technique for fast and accurate modeling of the coupled configuration of a large finite RIS and finite transmitter. Two different examples are reported to study the proposed modeling approach. The proposed technique can be further integrated to optimize different performance parameters of RIS-assisted MIMO communication.

title: Multi-Objective Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic Pricing and RideSharing Optimization
authors: Cheng-Fen Hsueh, Ta-Yin Hu
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This paper presents an integrated framework for autonomous mobility-on-demand (AMoD) systems, focusing on dynamic pricing, ride-sharing, and decentralized coordination. Built on a high-fidelity, city-scale environment calibrated with NYC taxi data, the framework dynamically generates passenger and shared autonomous vehicle (SAV) agents based on realworld spatiotemporal demand patterns. The system integrates a multi-objective multi-agent deep reinforcement learning (MOMADRL) framework with centralized training and decentralized execution (CTDE), allowing agents to optimize individual incentives and system-level social welfare jointly. Adaptive pricing strategies, flexible ride-matching mechanisms, and zone-based geographic abstractions are included to enhance computational efficiency while maintaining geographic realism. Experimental results demonstrate that our framework consistently improves key performance indicators like passenger waiting times, vehicle utilization, and pricing stability, outperforming purely centralized or decentralized methods. This research provides a robust platform for testing adaptive policies and advancing scalable, equitable AMoD system design.

title: Scalar Load-Pull Characterization: Calibration and Measurement Techniques
authors: Dhiman Sarkar
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This article presents a tutorial review of a radio frequency (RF) load-pull measurement method for a generalpurpose and cost-effective RF test bench designed for large-signal swept-power scalar characterization of both linear and nonlinear DUTs. The proposed setup is suitable for a range of measurement applications, including load-pull and source-pull characterization of transistors and antennas, as well as the extraction of compression parameters in transistors and power amplifiers. The method proposes a semi-in-situ approach to scalar calibration and measurement, with detailed discussions on the associated techniques. The measurement setup can also be utilized for smallsignal or large-signal swept-frequency measurements such as Sparameters for both linear and nonlinear DUTs with appropriate changes.

title: Design of a Shared Aperture Patch Antenna Array (X and Ka Band) for Cubesat Applications
authors: Arunya Paul, Chinmoy Saha, Priyadarshanam Hari, Sudhakar Sahu
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a compact, dual-band shared-aperture antenna tailored for CubeSat applications, integrating <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{X}$</tex> - and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{K a}$</tex>-band functionality within a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 2} \mathbf{~ c m} \boldsymbol{\times} \mathbf{1. 2}$</tex> cm footprint. The design employs an aperture-in-aperture architecture with strategically corner-cut concentric patches on a single-layer substrate, enabling enhanced impedance matching, bandwidth, and inter-band isolation without multilayer complexity. A dual-array configuration- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 2$</tex> for Xband and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \times 3$</tex> for Ka-band-ensures broadband, high-gain radiation with optimized inter-element spacing. The antenna achieves resonant operation at 10.4 GHz and 37.2 GHz, with peak array gains of 9.34 dBi and 16.93 dBi, respectively. A CubeSat-compatible integration strategy is proposed, enabling multi-panel, multi-band deployment within a standard <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 U}$</tex> platform, supporting future high-frequency satellite communication systems.

title: CARLACharge: A Versatile Simulator for Wireless Charging Roadways in Diverse Urban, Highway, and High-Traffic Environments
authors: Gabe Tonks, Taryn Jones, Tyson Buxton, Mario Harper
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Wireless in-road charging presents a scalable solution for extending the range of electric vehicles (EVs), reducing battery size, and supporting continuous operation in high-traffic corridors. To facilitate early-stage planning and reduce the cost of physical prototyping, we introduce CARLACharge, an open-source extension to the CARLA simulator designed for modeling electric vehicle energy consumption and in-road wireless charging interactions. CARLACharge integrates vehicle powertrain modeling, dynamic state-of-charge tracking, and experimentally grounded wireless charging profiles, allowing researchers to assess the effectiveness of infrastructure under diverse traffic and behavioral scenarios. The platform supports both autonomous and human-operated driving and offers tools for evaluating charger placement, energy delivery, and vehicle-level impacts. By simulating wireless charging infrastructure within realistic roadway environments, CARLACharge provides ITS researchers and planners with a practical tool for informed, simulation-driven electrification strategies. Source code is publicly available at: https://github.com/DIRECTLab/carla-energy-consumption.

title: External Driver Classification Using Reservoir Computing Enhancing Automated Vehicle Safety
authors: Zhonglin Hou, Brett Molesworth, Yonggang Zhang, Oleksandra Molloy, Jingjing Guo, Joel Li, Hong Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Ensuring the safety of Autonomous Vehicles (AVs) in mixed traffic environments is a considerable challenge due to the unpredictable behaviors and diverse driving styles of human drivers. This paper introduces a novel framework for driver classification of the surrounding vehicles from the external viewpoint of AVs, utilizing Reservoir Computing (RC) and Transfer Learning (TL) with observable data such as speed, acceleration, and speed limits, preserving driver privacy. Privacy feature augmentation based on TL generates comprehensive characteristics from both source and target domains, while time-series data augmentation increases classification accuracy within a small-time window. Augmented feature metrics are processed by an RC-based classifier to predict driver characteristics. Performance analysis shows the F1-score can reach up to 0.997, and comparison studies confirm the framework achieves state-of-the-art performance. The experiments demonstrate the ability of the framework to enhance the accuracy and reliability of driver classification, improving the real-time adaptability of AVs in complex traffic scenarios.

title: An fNIRS-Based Multi-Sensor Fusion Approach for Passenger Comfort Assessment
authors: Yang Zhao, Yewei Yu, Kecheng Shi, Ruoyu Deng, Hong Cheng
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This paper presents a multimodal framework for passenger comfort assessment in intelligent vehicles. By integrating functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS), vehicle dynamics data, and subjective ratings, we propose a Multi-Branch Joint Network (MBJNet) capable of extracting complementary features from heterogeneous sources. To address inter-subject variability, transfer learning and elastic weight consolidation are incorporated, enabling improved generalization across passengers with limited labeled data. Experimental evaluations on a real-vehicle platform demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms existing baselines in both within-subject and cross-subject classification tasks. Our approach achieves 91.73% accuracy in within-subject settings and 74.99% in cross-subject evaluations, highlighting its robustness and practical viability. This work provides a scalable and adaptive solution for comfort-aware intelligent cockpit systems.

title: Accuracy is Not Agreement: Expert-Aligned Evaluation of Crash Narrative Classification Models
authors: Sudesh Bhagat, Ibne Farabi Shihab, Jonathan Wood, Anuj Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)

title: S-Parameters and CMA-Aided Modal Investigation in Microstrip Patch Antennas: Analysis, Analogies and Visualizations
authors: Nitish Kumar, Kamla Prasan Ray, Prasobh Martin
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This study explores the modal dynamics of microstrip patch antennas with rectangular, circular, and triangular geometries across the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.5-6.5 \text{GHz}$</tex> range. Leveraging theoretical <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{T M}_{m n}$</tex> formulations, full-wave simulations, and Characteristic Mode Analysis (CMA), we analyze resonance hierarchies, degeneracy behavior, and symmetry-governed excitations. We introduce <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$T_{m n}$</tex> mode grid diagrams, a compact visual framework that maps mode indices to empirical <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{11}$</tex> responses, elucidating bandwidth tendencies and geometric modal preferences. CMA validation reinforces the identification of dominant modes and underscores structural influence on radiative traits. This unified visual-theoretical methodology offers a powerful tool for mode interpretation and lays the foundation for advanced antenna design and analogies with wave phenomena in other physical domains.

title: A Techno-Economic Study of Hydrogen-Enabled Hybrid Microgrids for Sustainable Residential Electrification in Bangladesh
authors: Md. Mahedi Hasan, Mst. Sadia Afroz, Md. Feroz Ali, Md. Esmay Azam, K. M. A. Salam, Md. Abdur Razzak
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: This paper presented a techno-economic assessment of a grid-connected hydrogen-enabled hybrid microgrid for residential electrification in Tangail, Bangladesh. A hybrid renewable energy microgrid was designed and investigated to supply electricity to 200 residential houses located in Tangail District, Dhaka Division, Bangladesh. The proposed configuration integrated solar photovoltaic (PV), wind turbine (WT), fuel cell (FC), electrolyzer, hydrogen (H₂) storage, and battery energy storage system (BESS) and was modeled using HOMER Pro (v3.14.2). Simulation results indicated an optimal cost of energy (COE) of $0.0278/kWh, with a net present cost (NPC) of $501,600, a capital investment of $419,464, and an annual operating cost of $6,156. Carbon dioxide emissions were reduced to 156,240 kg/yr, while hydrogen achieved a levelized cost of $3.55/kg, demonstrating that hydrogen-enabled hybrid microgrids provided a reliable, economical, and sustainable solution for residential electrification in Bangladesh.

title: Secrecy Performance in EMI-Resilient RIS-Assisted Hardware-Impaired Cell-Free Massive MIMO
authors: Sowrabh Banik, Malay Chakraborty, Kalpesh K. Patel, Ekant Sharma, Hien Quoc Ngo
abstract: This paper investigates the secrecy performance of a spatially correlated multi-reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS)-assisted cell-free massive multiple-input multiple-output system in the presence of imperfect channel state information, electromagnetic interference (EMI), and transceiver hardware impairments (T-HWI). Under these imperfections, we derive the linear minimum mean-square error channel estimate and a closed-form expression for the downlink secrecy sum rate (SSR). We employ artificial noise at each access point (AP) in the downlink data transmission phase to improve the achievable SSR. We deduce several analytical insights from the derived closed-form expressions to validate the system performance. We further formulate a joint optimization problem that maximizes the SSR by optimally allocating the power sharing factor and the RIS phase shift matrix. A closed-form solution for the optimal power sharing factor is obtained using a fixed-point equation method, while the RIS phase shifts are designed through a generative diffusion model. Numerical results validate the derived analytical expressions and reveal a substantial degradation in secrecy performance due to the presence of T-HWI and EMI. Notably, the legitimate user-side hardware impairments have a more detrimental effect on the system performance compared to the AP side. Furthermore, a trade-off is observed between the number of RISs and EMI power in achieving optimal SSR. Moreover, simulation results demonstrate that the proposed joint optimization scheme yields substantial gains in achievable SSR over the individual optimization schemes and the random baseline.

title: Efficient Context-Aware Traffic Trajectory Monitoring From Roadside LiDAR for Occlusion Handling
authors: Zhihui Chen, Hao Xu, Ziru Wang, Fei Guan, Muchen Tian
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Frequent occlusions in complex urban environments pose a major challenge for roadside LiDAR-based traffic monitoring, often leading to fragmented trajectories and kinematic errors. To address this, we propose a context-aware framework that shifts the sensing paradigm from individual object tracking to lane-level platoon modeling. By encoding raw LiDAR data into Lane Occupancy State, we employ an encoder–decoder LSTM architecture to capture collective traffic dynamics, enabling robust trajectory reconstruction even during prolonged occlusions. To ensure real-time scalability, a hardware-aware embedding scheme is designed to directly parse data packets from mechanical LiDAR, achieving an inference efficiency of 18.75 ms/frame. Evaluations on real-world datasets demonstrate a 95.3% occlusion reconstruction rate with low kinematic errors (0.0055 m/s speed deviation), while an ablation study confirms that integrating an attention mechanism further boosts the reconstruction rate to 97.9%. This work establishes a robust, traffic-context-centric foundation for high-fidelity digital twins and intelligent transportation systems.

title: Mechanisms of Chip-Scale Thermal Concentration in SiC MOSFETs During Avalanche Breakdown
authors: Yifan Wu, Chao Wang, Chi Li, Yuzhi Chen, Yalong Liu, Zedong Zheng
abstract: Avalanche failures in Silicon Carbide (SiC) MOSFETs often localize to small burned areas, revealing significant thermal concentration that limits device ruggedness. This work systematically investigates the underlying physical mechanisms, identifying three primary contributors: non-uniform heat generation and dissipation governed by chip layout and package design; cell-level current crowding driven by chip-scale temperature gradients; and impact ionization anisotropy inherent to the 4H-SiC material. Finite-element simulations, spanning from the chip to the cell scale, clarify these coupled electro-thermal processes. The mechanisms are experimentally validated using specially fabricated SiC MOSFETs through unclamped inductive switching (UIS) tests, decapsulation (DECAP) analysis, and statistical correlation of failure locations with single-pulse avalanche energy (EAS). The results conclusively confirm that mitigating thermal concentration via the proposed framework enhances avalanche ruggedness, providing device designers with a systematic approach to alleviate design trade-offs and a practical guideline for rapid failure analysis.

title: Fast-Converging Distributed Signal Estimation in Topology-Unconstrained Wireless Acoustic Sensor Networks
