abstract: In the development of automated driving and advanced driver assistance systems, it is essential that the underlying algorithms are thoroughly tested. Simulation is a valuable tool for evaluation of such algorithms, especially in safety-critical scenarios. Simulations can also provide the opportunity to generate and repeat rare, yet important, test cases and can be applied at various stages of development. For simulation to serve as a reliable substitute for real-world testing, it must offer high fidelity. Therefore, advanced models for both sensors, and the sensed environment are required. A thorough understanding of adverse weather conditions within the operational design domain is consequently crucial to maintain fidelity. This work develops statistical models of radar backscatter from rain for the perception by automotive radars which can be implemented in a simulated environment. To demonstrate the method's validity, both simulation and real-world data are used to assess the fidelity of rain radar signal predicted or generated using the model. The work presented here offers a state-of-the-art understanding of how rain (based on drop size distributions) affects the radar model and the background signal to be expected across the radar field of view, including the velocity dimension. With the use of such models in simulation it is possible to anticipate how the radar will react in the real-world under similar conditions. Future work includes integrating these findings into a radar-in-the-loop environment and developing additional adverse weather models for radar.

title: Analyzing and Modeling Different Patterns of Vehicle's Cut-In Behavior in Platoons
authors: Wei Ye, Yueru Xu, Yichen Li, Yichang Shao
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This paper proposes a Bayesian Stackelberg game model to analyze and predict vehicle insertion behaviors in platoons, emphasizing the dynamic decision-making process involved in merging vehicles. The model categorizes insertion strategies into four types: staying in the current lane, immediate insertion, waiting for a better opportunity, and adjusting position to find a suitable gap. It incorporates a leader-follower structure and accounts for uncertainty in the follower's response. The model is validated through controlled driving simulation experiments involving 56 drviers. Our proposed game-theoretic framwork achieves a prediction accuracy of 94% (under the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1: 2$</tex> ratio of insertion and non-insertion scenarios), significantly outperforming other game-theoretic baseline models. The results demonstrate that the proposed model provides a more accurate representation of real-world insertion behaviors compared to traditional methods, with improved accuracy and elimination utility heterogeneity across different insertion modes. This study offers valuable insights for enhancing cooperative driving systems, improving the efficiency and safety of platoon-based driving strategies, and advancing vehicle merging technology.

title: Multi-Scenario Cost Analysis of Microgrid Operation with Feed-in Tariffs and EMS Optimization
authors: Tasnim Sarker Joyeeta, Md. Sohel Rana, Md. Mahmudul Hasan, Md Raju Ahmmed
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: An effective strategy to meet the escalating energy demand is to integrate renewable energy resources (RERs). However, the fluctuating nature of these sources poses a significant challenge, requiring smart management strategies. This work focuses on the optimal coordination of hybrid (PV-wind) systems and energy storage solutions alongside the main power grid. The particle swarm optimization (PSO) is applied to manage varying household energy consumption over a 24-hour period, aiming to reduce electricity expenses. The method strategically schedules resource usage and determines when to rely on or disconnect from the grid based on solar and wind source availability and the energy storage system's state of charge (SOC). The proposed optimization technique considers feed-in tariff (FiT) scheme, i.e. customers will be provided with a credit amount due to surplus electricity injection into the utility grid. The effectiveness of the proposed energy management system (EMS) is evaluated through realistic case studies and compared against the existing linear programming (LP) based optimization technique. The model's performance is validated by comparing optimization outcomes with static tariff scenarios, demonstrating notable cost savings up to 316 cents/day retail and 82.34% reduction than the conventional approach.

title: A Comparison Between Behavioral Cloning and Inverse Reinforcement Learning Allocation of Tire Forces for an Optimal Yaw Moment Control
authors: Sarah Imene Khelil, Imane Taourarti, Bruno Monsuez, Adriana Tapus, Maud Geoffriault, Javier Ibanez-Guzman
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Modern over-actuated vehicle systems depend on precise force coordination to achieve optimal yaw moment control, critical for vehicle stability, safety, and handling. While traditional optimization-based control allocation (CA) methods are effective, they become computationally demanding as actuator complexity grows. This work explores imitation learning as a scalable alternative. We present a comparative study between Behavioral Cloning (BC) and Maximum Entropy Inverse Reinforcement Learning (MaxEnt IRL) for neural network-based CA in over-actuated automotive systems. Both approaches are trained using realworld data from a Renault Austral prototype to imitate an optimization-based tire force allocator for yaw control. LSTM architectures are used to capture temporal dependencies. The methods are evaluated across generalization, safety, and computational performance. BC demonstrates low inference latency and strong nominal performance, while IRL achieves similar outcomes, even with reduced training coverage. Under actuator failure, both methods exhibit comparable behavior, consistent with training data characteristics. These findings suggest that imitation learning could be explored as an alternative to traditional optimization-based control allocation in future high-actuation systems, particularly where computational efficiency and scalability become a concern. However, for the present case, optimization based allocation remains the most reliable and well-performing solution. This study serves as a foundational step toward imitating computationally demanding high-level Model Predictive Control (MPC) strategies using neural nets, enabling safe and efficient deployment in real-time automotive environments.

title: Bayes-Optimized EfficientViT: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for Multi-Modal Brain Stroke and Alzheimer’s Classification
authors: Meerab Rahim Khan, Khalid Bin Oalid, Md Jahirul Islam, Md Tanveerul Alam, Shohely Muntaha Drishty, Asm Mohaimenul Islam
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Alzheimer’s disease (AD) and brain stroke (BS) are two common neurological conditions that result in significant cognitive and physical deficits; prompt and accurate image-based diagnosis is crucial for early management. However, most automated approaches now in use focus on single-disease classification and often have limited applicability on heterogeneous imaging modalities, significant processing cost, and poor clinical interpretability. To address these limitations, this study proposes EffViTNet, a Bayesian-optimized hybrid deep learning framework that combines EfficientNet-B0 and Vision Transformer (ViT-B16) for dual-disease multi-class classification utilizing MRI (AD) and CT (BS) data. The ViT branch uses transformer encoding and patch-wise tokenization to capture long-range global contextual links, whereas the EfficientNet branch learns fine-grained local structural patterns. For the final prediction, the compact hybrid representation produced by concatenating the two feature streams is submitted to a fully connected Softmax classifier. To ensure efficient and stable convergence, key training hyperparameters (learning rate and weight decay) are adjusted using Bayesian Optimization by maximizing validation performance. Grad-CAM is also used to enhance clinical trust by highlighting disease-relevant areas and providing visual explanations. Experimental results indicate that the proposed model achieves superior performance compared with existing approaches, reaching an accuracy of 96.7%, 96.06% precision, 96.88% recall, and an F1-score of 0.9645. The suggested approach performs well for automated neurological disease diagnosis across different modalities in terms of accuracy, robustness, and explainability.

title: Performance of NOMA-Enabled Integrated Sensing and Communication System with Imperfect CSI
authors: Shravan Kumar Namdeo, Amit Baghel, Vimal Bhatia
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: This work explores an integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) system where the base station operates in full-duplex mode, and simultaneously serves both uplink and downlink devices for communications, and also detects the presence of a target. Improving both communication and sensing performance, as well as spectral usage, motivates the adoption of non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) techniques in the ISAC system. Here, NOMA-ISAC integration is leveraged to maximize spectral efficiency, and with ISAC, we get greater data rates in future 6 G wireless technologies. This paper investigates and compares the performance of NOMA-ISAC and orthogonal multiple access (OMA)-ISAC frameworks under both perfect and imperfect channel state information, focusing on outage probability and spectral efficiency for communication, as well as the probability of detection and false alarm for sensing the targets. Both the theoretical and simulated results verify that ISAC-NOMA surpasses ISAC-OMA in outage probability and the probability of detection across both perfect and imperfect channel state information.

title: A Two-stage Hierarchical Optimal Operation Considering Multiple Uncertainties
authors: Xin Rong, Jinyang Du, Mingjun Wang, Ruijin Qu, Yuan Tang, Bensong Zhang, Yunzhi Teng
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Mechanical Engineering and Power Engineering (MEPE)
abstract: To manage the challenges posed by the intermittency and randomness of renewable energy, this paper proposes a two-stage hierarchical optimal scheduling strategy to handle multiple uncertainties from photovoltaics, electric vehicles, and energy storage systems. The strategy first develops an initial scheduling plan through a day-ahead optimal scheduling model, based on forecasts of photovoltaic output, building loads, and the status of electric vehicles and energy storage, with the objective of minimizing market transaction and system operation costs. Subsequently, in the intraday rolling optimization stage, the model dynamically revises and updates the day-ahead strategy on a 15-minute time scale according to real-time information, thereby improving scheduling accuracy. Simulation results show that during scheduled power cuts this two-stage model can effectively reduce operating costs, and the intraday strategy has higher accuracy and lower costs than the day-ahead strategy.

title: Non-Invasive Blood Glucose Detection Using Highly Sensitive Metamaterial Sensor
authors: Subha Mandal, Amit Kumar Singh, Rahul Mondal, Akhilesh Kumar, Gobinda Sen
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a metamaterial-inspired resonator type sensor for blood glucose detection, incorporating an Interdigital Capacitor (IDC) and a Defected Ground Structure (DGS) to enhance sensitivity. The sensor is designed on a Neltec substrate (relative permittivity <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon_{r}=2.2$</tex>, thickness = <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 7 6 2 ~ m m}$</tex>) and exhibits fundamental resonance at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3. 2 ~ G H z}$</tex>, with an electrical footprint of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.211 \lambda_{0} \times 0.211 \lambda_{0}$</tex>. The proposed sensor achieves a maximum sensitivity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$25.6 \text{MHz} / \text{mg} \cdot \text{dL}^{-1}$</tex> and a high-quality factor (Q-factor) of 434.8. A PLA container (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon_{r}= 2.1$</tex>, loss tangent <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.003$</tex>) is used to hold 0.289 mL of bloodequivalent test samples. Full-wave electromagnetic simulations using CST Microwave Studio Suite software are performed by varying the dielectric properties to emulate different glucose concentrations. The resulting resonance frequency shifts validate the sensor's capability to detect permittivity changes associated with glucose variation. Regression models are formulated to map the relationship between resonance frequency, relative permittivity, and glucose concentration, facilitating accurate calibration for practical biosensing applications.

title: Design of Dual-Mode X-Band Reflectarray for Defense Communication
authors: Anjana Krishna, Swarnadipto Ghosh, Ashwini Kumar, Soumava Mukherjee, Chinmoy Saha
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents the design and analysis of a passive X-band reflectarray metasurface operating across the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8-12 \text{GHz}$</tex> frequency range, intended for adaptive and robust communication in defense, security, and disaster response scenarios. The surface consists of uniformly spaced rectangular patch unit cells, with reflection phase control achieved by varying the length of one side of the patch, enabling geometrical phase mapping without the need for active tuning elements. Designed for fixed <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-30^{\circ}$</tex> reflection, the metasurface demonstrates high-gain directional performance and angular stability from 9 GHz to 11 GHz, with peak gain observed near 10 GHz. At 8 GHz and 12 GHz, the surface exhibits vortexshaped radiation patterns, highlighting its dual-mode capability. Such vortex beams, characterized by orbital angular momentum (OAM), can be exploited for multiplexed communication channels or anti-jamming links in military networks. The proposed design offers key advantages for military communication systems, including low profile, wide bandwidth, and dynamic beam shaping-all without the need for active circuitry or mechanical reconfiguration. Full-wave simulations confirm the reflectarray's wideband behavior, making it a lightweight and cost-effective solution for rapidly deployable high-gain antenna platforms in mission-critical scenarios.

title: A Method for Managing and Optimizing Software Bill of Materials in Cloud-Native Environments
authors: Lei Zhang, Yue Qian, Lingbin Zeng, Xueyin Fang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Information Communication and Software Engineering (ICICSE)
abstract: The advantages of elastic scaling and rapid iteration inherent in cloud-native environments render them a favored architecture for enterprise development. However, the security challenges posed by the diverse open-source software integrated within these systems can not be overlooked, and the Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) is a promising solution. This paper analyzes the challenges encountered in SBOM Management within Cloud-Native Environments and proposes management measures, including constructing a minimum SBOM, automated generation and updating of SBOM and integration with cloud-native tool chains. In addition, we also provide the following optimization methods, including mining potential associations through big data, enhancement of safety monitoring for SBOM, and establishing relevant mechanisms. Through the aforementioned methods, we reinforce the security risk control system throughout the entire software life cycle. Which not only promotes the standardization of software development but also guarantees a baseline quality for deliverables, thereby offering substantial support for software development in cloud-native environments.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning-Based Optimization of Imperfect Maintenance for Wind Turbine Blades under Random Shocks
authors: Xiaohong Zhang, Junying Ding, Guannan Shi, Yunzheng Zhang, Jie Gan, Jinhe Wang
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: As the core energy-conversion component of wind turbines, blades are subjected to both internal performance degradation and random shocks during operation—two factors that collectively determine their health conditions, which in turn influence the reliability of wind turbines. Most existing studies assume that shock-induced damage to blade degradation is independent of the blade’s own degradation state, while mostly adopting static maintenance strategies and assuming perfect maintenance. This paper focuses on the optimization of imperfect maintenance for wind turbine blades under random shocks based on deep reinforcement learning. First, a coupling relationship model is established based on the modeling of blade degradation and shocks, which quantifies the shock-induced increment in blade degradation as a function of both shock amplitude and the blade’s degradation state. Second, an imperfect maintenance model is developed, in which the blade’s maintenance effect depends on the cumulative number of maintenance actions and the blade’s pre-maintenance state. Furthermore, a reinforcement-learning-based agent using a Double Deep Q-Network architecture is constructed. This agent offers two important advantages: it works without a predefined preventive maintenance threshold, and it can operate in a continuous degradation state space—thus solving the imperfect maintenance optimization problem in the continuous state space of blades under shock influence. Finally, experiments are conducted to investigate how variations in degradation, shock, and cost parameters influence the maintenance actions and costs determined by the agent. The proposed approach is demonstrated to be effective and it significantly reduces long-term maintenance costs as compared with other conventional maintenance strategies.

title: POS-Augmented N-Gram Model for Real-Word Error Detection and Correction in Bangla–English Code-Switched Text
authors: Sadia Afrin, K. M. Azharul Hasan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Code-switching, the process of switching between languages within a sentence or discourse, is increasingly common in Bangla–English communication, especially in informal online forums. Such language adaptation leads to spelling irregularities, contextual misuse of valid words, and syntactic irregularities that standard spell-checkers are not efficient in handling. Therefore, real word error detection and correction in code-switched Bangla text is an untapped yet crucial challenge for multilingual natural language processing. This paper introduces a parts of speech(POS) augmented n-gram model of language that can perform real-word error detection and correction in Bangla–English code-switched text. The proposed model utilizes lexical as well as syntactic knowledge by combining both word-level and POS-level n-gram probabilities. A sufficient augmented training corpus was created and POS-tagged using a hybrid rule-based and statistical model so that the model can acquire fine-grained con-textual dependencies. Correction of error is done by generating candidate substitutions and ranking them in context probability in the n-gram model trained, rather than simply surface similarity or edit distance. Experimental evaluation on a 26k trained sentences and 4,000-sentence error-injected test set indicate that the method achieves a substantial improvement in precision and recall in real-word error correction over baseline statistical methods. Performance indicates the power of supplementing POS-based syntactic context with probabilistic n-gram modeling. The work contributes a linguistically grounded, data-efficient framework to robust real-word error correction in code-switched Bangla, with scope for broader multilingual text normalization applications.

title: Millimeter-Wave Dielectric Huygens' Metasurface for Symmetric Beam Splitting
authors: Abhishek Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents an aggressively discretized millimeter-wave dielectric Huygens' metasurface consisting of two polarizable particles per period with the phase difference of 180°. Under normal incidence, the proposed design achieves symmetric beam splitting at an angle of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 50^{\circ}$</tex>, carrying more than 80 % of the total scattered power.

title: A Dual-Layer Wideband Metasurface Absorber for 5 Ghz Wi-Fi Applications
authors: Naveen Bhukya, Amartya Banerjee, Tapas Chakravarty, Hemant Kumar Rath
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a compact, dual-layer meta surface absorber specifically optimized for the 5.5 GHz Wi-Fi band. The absorber employs a symmetrical fan-blade-shaped circular resonator patterned on an FR4 substrate, designed to support multiple closely spaced resonant modes. This geometry ensures polarization insensitivity and angular stability due to its four-fold rotational symmetry. The proposed structure achieves greater than 95 % absorption over a 1 GHz bandwidth centered at 5.5 GHz, effectively covering the entire <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.0-6.0 \text{GHz}$</tex> range. Simulation results confirm stable performance under both TE and TM polarizations, with absorption exceeding 90 % up to 60° incidence. Compared to existing designs, the proposed absorber offers a rare combination of wide bandwidth, high angular tolerance, and structural simplicity-making it a promising candidate for electromagnetic interference suppression and device-level integration in next-generation wireless systems.

title: Intelligent modeling for creep deformation prediction and reliability analysis of hot end components
authors: Hai-Feng Gao, Bing Xu
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: To address insufficient accuracy and low computational efficiency in creep deformation prediction of high-temperature components, this paper proposes an IALGC model integrating improved particle swarm optimization-enhanced adaptive LASSO with convolutional neural networks. The three-stage framework employs adaptive LASSO for intelligent feature selection, GWPSO incorporating grey wolf mechanisms for CNN hyperparameter optimization, and CNN for extracting deep nonlinear stress-temperature-time features. Validated on cabin assembly structures, the IALGC model achieves MAPE of 1.87% and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.9936, reducing prediction error by 77.8% compared to benchmark models. This method provides an efficient solution for reliability optimization of complex mechanical assembly structures.

title: FIDS: Accelerating Network Intrusion Detection Through Strategic Feature Selection
authors: James Elmore, Ayan Roy
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: Network intrusion detection systems (IDS) typically analyze complete network flows to identify malicious traffic, requiring flows to conclude before classification. This approach creates detection delays for attacks like Slowloris that intentionally keep connections open for extended periods of time. This paper introduces a novel approach that classifies network traffic using only features available from the first few packets of a flow, enabling faster detection while maintaining high accuracy. We evaluate three random forest models on the CICIDS2017 dataset using expanding sets of features: the first-packet model trained on features available from the first backward packet, the few-packet model which includes features estimable from the first few packets of a flow, and a full-flow model using all features available in the dataset for reference. Our few-packet model achieves 99.64% accuracy, 99.80% precision, and 99.64% recall-comparable to state-of-the-art approaches using full-flow information-while enabling significantly faster detection. This approach is effective against slow-rate DoS attacks, achieving over 99% F1 scores for both Slowloris and slowhttptest traffic. At a low false positive rate of 0.01%, our model maintains a 99.17% true positive rate.

title: LLM-Cave: A benchmark and light environment for large language models reasoning and decision-making system
authors: Huanyu Li, Zongyuan Li, Wei Huang, Xian Guo
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Information and Communication Networks (ICICN)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) such as ChatGPT o1, ChatGPT o3, and DeepSeek R1 have shown great potential in solving difficult problems. However, current LLM evaluation benchmarks are limited to one-step interactions. Some of the existing sequence decision-making environments, such as TextStarCraftII and LLM-PySC2, are too complicated and require hours of interaction to complete a game. In this paper, we introduce LLM-Cave, a benchmark and light environment for LLM reasoning and decision-making systems. This environment is a classic instance in the era of Symbolism. Artificial intelligence enables the agent to explore the environment and avoid potential losses by reasoning about nearby dangers using partial observable state information. In the experiment, we evaluated the sequential reasoning ability, decision-making performance and computational efficiency of mainstream large language models (LLMs) such as GPT-4o-mini, o1-mini, and DeepSeek-R1. Experiments show that while Deepseek-R1 achieved the highest success rate on complex reasoning tasks, smaller models like 4o-mini significantly narrowed the performance gap on challenges by employing Chain of Speculation and Planner-Critic strategies, at the expense of reduced computational efficiency. This indicates that structured, multi-step reasoning combined with an LLM-based feedback mechanism can substantially enhance an LLM's decision-making capabilities, providing a promising direction for improving reasoning in weaker models and suggesting a new reasoning-centered benchmark for LLM assessment. Our code is open-sourced in https://github.com/puleya1277/CaveEnv.

title: Dual-Beam Leaky-Wave Antenna for Angle-of-Arrival Estimation at mmW Frequencies
authors: Tanguy Lopez, Julien Sarrazin
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: The frequency-scanning property of leaky-wave antennas (LWAs) is a promising feature for angle-of-arrival (AoA) estimation in mobile telecommunication systems. However, at millimeter-wave (mmW) frequencies, the relatively narrow available bandwidth combined with the limited dispersion of typical LWA structures makes it difficult to achieve adequate angular coverage. In this work, we propose a novel dual-beam LWA concept using two different sets of radiating slots on a single rectangular waveguide. This configuration effectively splits the field-of-view (FoV) into forward and backward regions, each scanned across by one of the two sets of radiating elements. A dispersion analysis is used to ensure continuous coverage of the mmW-compatible <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$90^{\circ} \text{FoV}$</tex>, which is validated by full-wave simulations over the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$24.4-28.5 \text{GHz}$</tex> band.

title: Network-Wide Traffic Prediction Via Bayesian State-Space Neural Networks and ARIMAX
authors: Mohammad Reza Mohebbi, Niloofar Davoudi Jaghargh, Mario Döller, Mohammad Javad Hassan Zada, Iuliia Yamnenko
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Accurate prediction of critical traffic variables, such as flow, speed, and occupancy, is crucial to maximize efficient urban traffic management and enable Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS). However, traffic data are often high-dimensional and subject to spatio-temporal dependencies, complicating accurate predictions. In this paper, a hybrid framework is presented in which a Bayesian State-Space Neural Network (BSSNN) is combined with ARIMAX to improve the forecasting of traffic variables. BSSNN effectively captures nonlinear interactions in the data, while ARIMAX refines the prediction by capturing the structured residual dependencies. The experiment proves that the designed hybrid framework outperforms traditional machine learning and deep learning models considerably by providing higher prediction accuracy. The performance of the model was assessed on the metrics of Mean Squared Error (MSE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE), and coefficient of determination <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(R^{2}\right)$</tex>, with the latter having a high value of 0.965. The high value <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> proves the stability and therefore the generalizability of the proposed model across varying prediction horizons. The results indicate that this proposed hybrid framework is highly compatible with actual ITS applications and can be extended to use external input data along with adaptive learning protocols.

title: An Adaptive Ant Colony Optimization Algorithm for Routing Selection in Mobile Ad Hoc Networks
authors: Xin Liu, Jihan Feng, Xin Liu
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Information and Communication Networks (ICICN)
abstract: This paper proposes an adaptive routing selection (ARACO) algorithm based on ant colony optimization (ACO) algorithm. By improving the state transition function and the pheromone update strategy, and introducing a weight update mechanism, the algorithm dynamically adjusts the weights of pheromones and heuristic factors, ensuring strong exploration in the early stages and rapid convergence in the later stages. The algorithm takes a comprehensive approach to pheromone updates by considering route costs, including key performance indicators such as delay, jitter and packet loss rate. This paper introduces a path utility function that jointly evaluates transmission rate and hop count, aiming to maximize its value while effectively considering quality of service (QoS) requirements. Additionally, considering the complex ground environment of mobile ad hoc networks (MANETs), this paper incorporates the Rayleigh fading model to characterize the channel. Simulation results demonstrate that the ARACO algorithm outperforms the traditional ACO algorithm and the genetic algorithm (GA) in key performance metrics.

title: Joint Impact of Nodes Mobility and Imperfect SIC on the Outage Analysis of Multi-Tag Ambient Backscatter Systems Over Rayleigh Fading
authors: Ashutosh Rastogi, Suneel Yadav, Radhika Gour, Devendra S. Gurjar, Juraj Gazda
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: In this paper, we investigate the combined effect of nodes mobility and imperfect successive interference cancellation (SIC) on the outage performance of a multi-tag ambient backscatter communication (AmBC) system over Rayleigh fading. The AmBC system consists of a mobile ambient RF source, K energy harvesting enabled mobile tags, and a mobile reader. Under the nodes mobility scenario, all the fading links are modeled using a first-order autoregressive process. We also employ a best tag selection scheme, while the reader utilizes both perfect SIC (pSIC) and imperfect SIC (ipSIC) techniques. Under this system setup, we derive the closed-form expressions for the outage probability (OP) under both pSIC and ipSIC scenarios. We also present the asymptotic OP analyses in the high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) regime to extract key insights into the system's diversity order. In addition, we explore several practical scenarios, including static nodes configuration and large time-varying errors, to characterize their effects on the system performance. Finally, simulation results are provided to validate the accuracy of the derived analytical expressions.

title: An ISM Band Flexible Printed Rectenna for Wearable Applications
authors: Shanmugarajan Balasubramanian, Apala Banerjee, Mohammad Vaseem, Atif Shamim
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: The work presents the design, simulation and experimental validation of a flexible compact printed rectenna for wearable applications optimized for wireless power transfer at 2.4 GHz. The rectenna system comprises of a miniaturized rectifier circuit fed by an Inverted F Antenna (IFA), both fabricated on a flexible Kapton substrate to support the purpose of wearable application. The chosen antenna has dimension of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$59 \text{mm} \times 49.5 \text{mm} \times 0.125 \text{mm}$</tex>, while the rectifier occupies an area of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$30 \text{mm} \times 23 \text{mm} \times 0.125 \text{mm}$</tex>. The rectifier system consists of a matching network, Schottky diode, and a uniquely designed DC pass filter that achieves RF to DC conversion of 68.2% at the designed input power of 10 dBm and frequency of 2.4 GHz for the use of wearable applications.

title: Multimodal Fault Diagnosis for Railway Switch Machine Based on Semantic Information Contrastive Learning
authors: Lu Ding, Wenhao Li, Bo Jin, Tingliang Zhou, Dong Han
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: The railway switch machine is a critical component of the railway signaling system, significantly influencing the efficiency and safety of train operations. However, most existing fault diagnosis technologies for switch machines struggle to differentiate samples with similar characteristics, leading to low diagnostic accuracy. To address this limitation, we propose a novel multimodal fault diagnosis method for railway switch machines. Via integrating current signals of switch machines (CSSM) with their corresponding semantic descriptions during training, this method, termed the Multi-modal CSSM-TEXT Fault Diagnosis (MCTFD) model, can notably improve the performance of fault classification only using numerical current signals during inference. MCTFD utilizes contrastive learning to achieve modal alignment between text and time series data. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work that employs semantic information for the fault diagnosis of switch machines. Experimental results using real-world and generated data of Shanghai Metro demonstrate that the proposed MCTFD model achieves superior diagnostic accuracy, compared to existing machine learning and deep learning methods that rely on single-modal data. By effectively incorporating semantic information, our method shows strong potential for enhancing fault diagnosis capabilities in rail transit systems.

title: BusScan: A Simulation Platform for Evaluating Bus-Based On-Street Parking Detection Strategies
authors: Junhan Fu, Yaxuan Li, Ruizhi Liao
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Urban on-street parking management faces critical challenges, such as rising car ownership, limited spaces for parking, and a lack of parking occupancy data. This results in drivers circling aimlessly for parking, exacerbating city traffic congestion. Most current smart parking solutions rely on fixed sensors on the spot, but the large-scale installation and maintenance incur prohibitive infrastructure costs. Mobile sensing through public transit vehicles (e.g., buses) offers a cost-effective alternative, yet no dynamic simulation platform exists to validate these systems' performance across different urban settings. This paper presents BusScan, an open-source and lightweight simulation framework that models real-time parking detection through bus-mounted sensors while accounting for traffic dynamics and driver behavior patterns. The simulation architecture integrates: 1) bus route simulation with configurable traffic models, 2) parking behavior prediction using the Kaplan-Meier estimator, and 3) sensor deployment and model generalization. The modular design of the platform enables municipalities to evaluate parking policies, sensor deployment, and routing strategies through interactive simulations. By bridging the simulation gap in mobile sensing research, BusScan provides urban planners with an evidence-based toolkit for scalable smart city implementations.

title: Identification of Drunk Driving using Deep Learning Networks on Virtual Reality Simulation
authors: Sida Jiang, Jiaming Wu, Bogdan Diaconu
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: The paper addresses safety concerns associated with electric micromobility vehicles, particularly e-scooters, which accounted for 1% of trips but nearly 4% of traffic accidents in Sweden, 2019. To mitigate potential risks from increased micromobility usage, we developed robust AI algorithms to detect drunk driving. Based upon our own developed Virtual Reality simulations, involving 30 participants, we utilized hand-tracking technology to enhance realism and reduce nausea. Results indicated that LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory network) model successfully identified 86% of drunk riders. A Transformer method was tested with further aggregated data where we have achieved 100% precise identification of drunk drivers. LSTM methods that we tested in the project have shown significant improvement for detecting drunk drivers, compared with machine learning methods e.g. Random Forest and Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs). This research contributes to Vision Zero, zero fatalities and severe injuries from road traffic in the Nordics by 2050, by providing robust sensing algorithm for identifying e-scooter misuse, with future work focusing on refining models, expanding data collection, and integrating advanced neural networks. Our findings offer valuable insights for stakeholders like shared e-scooter operators and urban transport administrations to enhance urban mobility safety.

title: Compact Wide-Beamwidth Polarization Converter Metasurface Integrated with 3D-Printed Horn Antenna for Millimeter-Wave Applications
authors: Akhilesh Kumar, Amit K. Singh, Rahul Mondal
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a low-loss, wide-beamwidth linear-to-circular polarization converter metasurface (PCM) integrated with a pyramidal horn antenna. The PCM unit cell consists of a pair of identical, modified circular-ring metallic patches separated by a single-layer dielectric substrate. A <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \times 3$</tex> array of unit cells is positioned at the horn antenna aperture. Axial rotation (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\phi$</tex>) enables polarization conversion: for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\phi=-45^{\circ}$</tex>, linear polarization (LP) is converted to left-hand circular polarization (LHCP), and for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\phi=45^{\circ}$</tex>, LP is converted to right-hand circular polarization (RHCP), while maintaining a wide beamwidth. The PCM-integrated horn antenna achieves 3-dB axial ratio (AR) bandwidth of 2.6 GHz(26.68-29.28 GHz) and a 3-dB AR beamwidth of 143°. A peak broadside gain of 11.77 dB with 38% aperture efficiency is attained, demonstrating that the integration preserves the standalone horn antenna's gain performance. The proposed design is suitable for millimeter-wave, 5 G, and satellite communication applications.

title: Explainable AI for NAFLD Prediction Using Clinical and Lifestyle Data
authors: Md. Ebrahim Hossain, Bimol Chandra Das, Shahriar Siddique Ayon, Jewel Mia, Md. Mehedi Hasan, Mursalin Khan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Non-Alcoholic Fatty Liver Disease is an emerging yet often it neglected health problem closely associated with lifestyle and metabolic conditions. In this paper, we present a machine learning framework for the early detection of NAFLD using simple, non-invasive clinical and lifestyle data from the NHANES 2017-2020 dataset. For feature selection, we employed a hybrid feature selection methodology, Principal Component Analysis combined with Information Gain (PCA-IG), for identifying key factors-smoking status, gender, triglyceride level, and liver stiffness-as relevant. We utilized KMeans-SMOTE to address class imbalance. This greatly improved the accuracy of the models. Various machine learning models were utilized, such as Random Forest, SVM, KNN, and Artificial Neural Networks. The performance was improved for our machine learning models by the use of hyperparameter tuning. To understand model predictions and identify the key risk and protective factors, Explainable AI techniques were employed, namely LIME. The best performance from our models was exerted by the Random Forest model with KMeans-SMOTE, with an accuracy of 98.35%, an F1 score of 98.64%, and an AUC of 1.0. This work not only identifies critical NAFLD risk factors but also offers a dependable and interpretable tool that will help in early diagnosis and guide public health interventions.

title: Abstractive Arabic Text summarization Using AraT5 Model with Active Learning
authors: Noor A. Rashak, Dhamyaa A. Nasrawi
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Active learning has emerged as a highly effective paradigm in the machine learning literature, aiming to minimize the cost of labeling datasets. Instead of relying on a fully annotated dataset, it discovers and retrieves the most informative samples for training, which enhances model performance with less labeled data. This aspect is particularly valuable since annotation for NLP tasks is expensive and time-consuming. Text summarization has a single output label, this problem outputs a sequence, specifically a shorter version of the input text. In the so-called abstractive summarization, the model tries to produce new phrases and sentences instead of selecting parts of the input that it expects to output. For this purpose, our method uses active learning strategies combined with a pretrained Arabic language model (AraT5) to both improve summarization quality and diminish the dataset needed. The aim of this paper is to show that active learning is an effective means for improving model efficiency by comparing uncertainty-based, diversity-based, and random selection strategies. Results demonstrated that uncertainty sampling reduced annotation costs and led to higher ROUGE scores; a ROUGE key value-based model performed significantly better on average than models trained on all data.

title: Robust Multi-Sensor Feature Matching for HD Map-Based Localization Using Graph Optimization
authors: Pedro Vieira Oliveira, Robin Smit, Alaa Alassi, Jochem Brouwer, Jorrit Goos
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This work presents a robust multi-sensor localization framework that integrates feature matching with highdefinition (HD) maps using graph-based optimization techniques. The proposed system leverages lane and pole detections extracted from LiDAR-camera and radar-camera (RACam) systems, alongside odometry derived from IMU and wheel encoders. A novel lane factor based on multivariate implicit line fitting is introduced, enabling accurate feature association even in complex road geometries. Furthermore, the robustness of the object detection stack is enhanced with PointPainting+, a refined feature projection algorithm that significantly reduces false positives caused by shadow points. The feature matcher is incorporated into a localization fusion module, closing the loop with a global EKF for improved accuracy and resilience to drift. Experimental results on Dutch highways demonstrate that our method reduces the lateral positioning error by more than 90% when compared to a localization fusion using GNSS. The proposed method achieves lane-level accuracy with lateral errors between <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx[0.06,0.1] m$</tex> with an accuracy of more than 94% within a 0.2 m bound, while maintaining real-time performance.

title: MRDIMM: The Next Generation Memory Interface for AI and Datacenter Workloads
authors: Koley Saheli, Boddula Srinu, J Shah Pinkesh
publish: 2025 IEEE 6th International Women in Technology Conference (WINTECHCON)
abstract: In recent years, the rapid advancement in artificial intelligence (AI) workloads has created unprecedented demands on memory technologies, driving the need for innovations that can deliver higher bandwidth, lower latency, and increased capacity while utilizing existing Double Data Rate 5 (DDR5) Registered Dual In-line Memory Module (RDIMM) socket platforms. To meet these challenges Multiplexed Rank Dual In-line Memory Module (MRDIMM) has emerged as a promising new memory solution. This paper explores the critical role of MRDIMM in AI and high performance computing (HPC) workloads, emphasizing its design enhancements and performance improvements over DDR5 to support high bandwidth and low latency. The paper outlines the key stages of an AI pipeline, their inherent hardware challenges, and elucidates how MRDIMMs integrated with Intel Xeon servers address prevalent DDR5 bottlenecks. It includes performance comparison between DDR5 RDIMM and MRDIMM across various AI and HPC workloads, discusses the challenges, and future directions for the memory technology.

title: Surface3D: A Surface-Aware Framework for Refining 3D Object Detection
authors: Mingyu Liu, Yutong Hou, Ekim Yurtsever, Xingcheng Zhou, Jiajie Zhang, Haolin Zhang, Alois Knoll
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: D object detection aims to identify objects and localize them by predicting bounding boxes from LiDAR point clouds. However, the inherent sparsity of LiDAR data, partial occlusions, and challenges posed by long-tail viewing angles often limit their performance. To address these limitations, we propose Surface3D, a surface-aware refinement approach that integrates learned surface features derived from part segmentation to enhance bounding box predictions. Existing datasets primarily focus on indoor scene segmentation and lack applicability to our task. To address this, we develop a semi-automated pipeline to generate training data for part segmentation. This module extracts object surface features from the initial detection outputs. A refinement module then leverages these features to improve the accuracy of bounding box sizes and orientations. Extensive experiments on the KITTI dataset using five widely adopted 3D object detectors demonstrate that incorporating surface features substantially improves detection performance. Specifically, it achieves up to 1.75% and 1.25% gains over PointPillars and VoxelRCNN, respectively, on the hard car class under 3D <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{AP}_{R11}$</tex> metrics. The codes and datasets are available at https://github.com/MingyuLiu1/Surface3D.git

title: Spatial Performance Analysis of NOMA-V2X with Cooperative Superposed Transmissions
authors: Tomoki Fuchikami, Takeshi Hirai, Naoki Wakamiya
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This paper analyzes the spatial distributions of the packet reception ratios (PRRs) in the vehicle-to-everything (V2X) with non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA), called NOMA-V2X, with cooperative superposed transmissions (CSTs). To this end, this paper proposes an analytical model with the CSTs with an arbitrary number of interfering vehicles. In the proposed model, such vehicles transmit packets at the same time-frequency resource, and receivers try to decode those signals in a NOMA manner. Each vehicle performing the CST can transmit its own packet signal superimposed with another packet signal received in the previous time slot in a power domain. Our proposed analytical model has two key ideas for expressing the PRR of NOMA-V2X with CST. The first idea is to reflect the dynamic decoding order in the successive interference cancellation (SIC) based on the locations and numbers of interfering vehicles in NOMA-V2X. The second idea is to appropriately model the channel gain experienced by the superposed packet signal transmitted by a vehicle performing the CST. Our analysis showed that the NOMA-V2X with CST achieved up to 2.3 times larger areas with PRR over 0.9 than the NOMA-V2X without CST.

title: Compact Dual-Ring Slot Microstrip Antenna with Modified Ground Structure for Sub-6 GHz Wearable Systems
authors: Anisha Biswal, Ranjith Kumar R, Parthasarathy Ramanujam, Ashwin Tom Reji, R Thrisha, Shabeeb Ahamed KP
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: The paper presents the design and simulation of a compact dual-band wearable microstrip antenna designed for sub- 6 GHz applications, including Wireless Body Area Networks (WBANs) and 5G NR n78/n79 communications. The antenna is installed on a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$32 \times 32 \times 1.6 \text{mm}^{3}$</tex> FR4 epoxy substrate and features a dual ring slot-loaded microstrip patch that incorporates a central triangular notch and lateral rectangular slots to allow resonance tuning and surface current control. A vertical rectangular Defected Ground Structure (DGS) is integrated into the ground plane to enhance impedance matching and bandwidth without increasing the physical profile. The antenna supports dual resonance operation at 3.05 GHz and 4.72 GHz, achieving simulated return losses of −30.03 dB and −22.74 dB, peak gains of 5.54 dBi and 4.12 dBi, and radiation efficiencies of 89% and 77%, respectively. The key novelty of the design is its single layer configuration, which combines slot-based resonance optimization with ground-plane modification to achieve compact and efficient dual-band performance, making the proposed antenna suitable for integration into 5G-enabled wearable and biomedical communication systems.

title: System-Level Study of Non-Coherent Uplink Transmission Gains for 6G Evolution
authors: Kaustuv Saha, Biswa P. S. Sahoo, Sheshachalam BS, Dinesh Prasad
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: In 3GPP Release 15, UEs with non-coherent UL transmission capability can only utilize antenna-specific PMI and must reduce the PUSCH power by a predefined factor for Rank 1 transmissions. As a result, these UEs cannot transmit at maximum power according to their UE power class (PC). From Release 16 onward, 3GPP removed the power scaling factor for non-coherent UEs and introduced a new PMI that allows transmission using multiple antennas based on the UE Mode. This enhancement enables such UEs to transmit at the maximum power as per their PC. This paper reports and analyzes system-level simulation results to assess the effects of 3GPP Release 16 updates on cell edge performance, quantify the gains, and evaluates implications for 6G.

title: Day-of-the-Week Awareness in Time of Day Breakpoints for Traffic Light Plans
authors: Ori Rottenstreich, Eliav Buchnik, Shai Ferster, Tom Kalvari, Ron Tsibulsky, Danny Veikherman, Avishai Zagoury, Jack Haddad, Dotan Emanuel, Avinatan Hassidim
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Time-of-day breakpoints (TODs) refer to the times over the day in which the plan of a traffic light is changed. Traditionally, TODs are selected jointly for all weekdays (MondayFriday), typically with additional TODs dedicated to weekends. In this paper, we present an alternative approach motivated by traffic characteristics that can differ among the weekdays Monday-Friday and consider TODs which are day-of-the-week aware. The traffic-aware approach studies similarities among days and computes TODs that can be shared among days with similar characteristics but can also have other forms for weekdays with unique characteristics. Based on traffic properties derived from anonymized trajectories, we apply the new methodology to compute time-of-day breakpoints that are day-of-the-week aware in the city of Rio de Janeiro, Brazil and estimate the impact of the new methodology.

title: Formalization of Computational Process Using Informative Coloring of User Resource Requests in a Local Area Network Node
authors: Oleg Demidenko, Vladimir N. Kulinchenko, Aliaksandr I. Kucharau, Yuri Nikityuk, Denis S. Sych, Anton G. Uymin
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: This article describes formalization of the computational process in a local area network node by using complex structure transaction, in the “body” which there are information-colored structure of the workload. The article shows examples of formalization of the computational process in a local area network node by using complex structure transactions, the “body” of which contains the informationcolored structure of the workload. Formalization of the computational process of the LAN node by using complex structure transactions, the “body” of which contains the information-colored structure of the workload on the LAN node, is suggested.

title: Every Scene All at Once: Exhaustive Multi-Agent Interaction Generation with Controlled Diffusion Model
authors: Xiyan Jiang, Xiaocong Zhao, Yiru Liu, Peng Hang, Jian Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Autonomous vehicles (AVs) must safely navigate a vast range of complex multi-agent interactions for widespread deployment. However, their decision-making systems are typically trained on observational data that capture only a single realized outcome for any given event, leaving them blind to the full spectrum of plausible, yet unobserved, alternatives. This “mode collapse” severely limits their ability to anticipate and handle rare but safety-critical scenarios. Here we introduce a generative framework that overcomes this limitation by systematically enumerating and synthesizing the complete set of all dynamically feasible multi-agent interaction modes for a given scene. Our method first decomposes complex interactions into negotiations at conflict points and enumerates all possible passageorder permutations. We then use a graph-based spatiotemporal optimization to compute kinematically feasible anchor points for each permutation. These anchors subsequently guide a controllable diffusion model to generate realistic, full-scene trajectories representing every possible interaction mode. Using naturalistic driving data, we demonstrate that our framework generates comprehensive sets of interaction modes, including low-probability counterfactuals that are physically and socially plausible. For instance, in a complex three-vehicle interaction, our model generated all three dynamically possible outcomes while correctly identifying a fourth, seemingly plausible mode as infeasible due to subtle environmental constraints. By creating datasets that encompass all possible futures, our approach provides a critical tool for robust testing and development of AV decision-making systems, enhancing their safety in real-world environments.

title: A Compact Frequency-Reconfigurable Conformal Antenna for Sub-6 GHz Body-Centric Health Monitoring Applications
authors: Poonam Tiwari, Vishant Gahlaut, Saptarshi Ghosh
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This work presents a compact, lightweight, and conformal frequency reconfigurable (FR) microstrip antenna designed for Sub-6 GHz body-centric wireless health monitoring systems. The antenna is designed on a Jeans fabric substrate with the dimensions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.367 \lambda_{0} \times 0.275 \lambda_{0} \times 0.0128 \lambda_{0}$</tex> (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\lambda_{0}$</tex> being the free-space wavelength at the lowest frequency) to ensure conformability and mechanical flexibility. Two PIN diodes are integrated at different locations in the geometry, and by varying their biasing conditions, the antenna can be reconfigured to operate at multiple resonant frequencies including <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.2 \text{GHz}, 3.4 \text{GHz}, 4.6 \text{GHz}, 4.7 \text{GHz}, 4.83 \text{GHz}, 6.7$</tex>GHz, and 7.0 GHz, thereby supporting for WLAN, LTE, WiMAX, ISM, and 5G communication bands. The critical parameters of the structure, such as reflection coefficient, gain, radiation efficiency, surface current distribution, and radiation patterns are evaluated under different diode switching states. Additionally, mechanical robustness is examined by varying the bending radius, while the electromagnetic safety is validated through specific absorption rate (SAR) analysis conducted using a human hand phantom. The SAR values of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.106 ~\mathrm{W} / \text{kg}$</tex> at 3.2 GHz and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.097 ~\mathrm{W} / \text{kg}$</tex> at 4.25 GHz are well below the threshold of biological safety, confirming the suitability of the proposed design for wearable healthcare and multifunctional communication applications.

title: Metamaterial Inspired Coaxial Fed Two Element Antenna for Brain Tumor Detection and Localization
authors: Saheefa Farooq, Javaid A. Sheikh, Umhara Rasool Khan
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This study introduces, a metamaterial coaxialfed two-element antenna with a unidirectional radiation pattern resonating at 3.88 GHz in open space. The proposed design has a minimal footprint area of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.336 \lambda \times 0.2845 \lambda \times 0.010 \lambda$</tex> and showcases the remarkable results. The prospective design is made on a substrate (Rogers TMM) with a relative permittivity of 4.4 and an interdigitated rectangular parasitic patch positioned at the top of the substrate. The humanoid head phantom comprising 6 layers (brain, CSF, dura, skull, fat & skin) and the tumor, which possesses various physical and electromagnetic characteristics is designed within the HFSS. The detection of tumors in the brain is done by analyzing the difference in scattering parameters for healthy and diseased tissue. However, the localization and sizing of the cancer are done by extracting the phase and magnitude of the electric field rather than utilizing complex imaging algorithms. Furthermore, the antenna's Specific Absorption Rate (SAR) for a regular head phantom is 0.16779 W/Kg, and a phantom with a tumor is 0.202 W/kg, less than the average SAR of 2 W/kg

title: Analysis of GNSS Asynchronous Spoofing Pull-In Rate for Security Testing of Static Receiver
authors: Yili Song, Xin Yang, Jingyue Luo, Wenxiang Liu, Wei Xiao, Ming Ma, Guangfu Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: With the widespread application of Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS), the security testing of critical infrastructure against GNSS spoofing attacks has become a crucial research topic. Among these attacks, asynchronous spoofing can seamlessly infiltrate receivers and take control of their loops. However, existing studies lack comprehensive analysis on the pullin rate threshold of such attacks. In this study, by analyzing asynchronous spoofing scenarios and their impact on loop mechanisms, we establish an analytical model that summarizes the pull-in rate threshold of spoofing in typical loop structures. Through software receiver simulations, the effect of spoofing interference on typical code loop structures is demonstrated, leading to the determination of the pull-in rate threshold for asynchronous spoofing signals. We systematically analyze the influence of code loop order, loop bandwidth, and damping factor on the spoofing speed limit. The results show that increasing loop bandwidth and power gain significantly enhance the spoofing speed limit, while the damping factor and loop order regulate the speed limit by affecting system stability and dynamic tracking performance. This study offers critical insights and tools for enhancing the resilience of critical infrastructures against future navigation spoofing attacks.

title: An Improved Theory for Reflection-Electrode-Grounded Single-Phase Unidirectional Transducers (SPUDTs) With Better Unidirectionality
authors: Zhen-Hui Qin, Sheng-Nan Liang, Shu-Mao Wu, Hao Yan, Chen-Bei Hao, Si-Yuan Yu, Yan-Feng Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article presents an improved theory and design methodology for single-phase unidirectional transducers (SPUDTs), developed through systematic analysis of electrical reflections from multiple electrodes to significantly enhance unidirectionality. It is compatible with all reflection-electrode-grounded SPUDTs and supports diverse piezoelectric materials and acoustic modes. Leveraging the Lamb wave S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</sub> mode in LiNbO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> thin films, the improved DART (IDART) demonstrates over five times the unidirectionality of conventional designs for 10-period transducers. Transmission line experiments with IDARTs achieve a 7.9% 3-dB bandwidth at 3 GHz, with insertion losses of 5.9 dB at room temperature and 4.9 dB at 50 K, reducing losses by up to 1.2 dB compared to conventional DART. Also, the improved EWC-SPUDT (IEWC-SPUDT) offers advantages over conventional designs. Both simulations and experiments confirm the superiority of our SPUDTs in terms of unidirectionality, bandwidth, and in-band ripple. This new discovery and innovation, stemming from a reexamination of the classical SPUDT theory, are expected to systematically enhance the performance of microwave acoustic devices that rely on SPUDTs, including filters, delay lines, and sensors.

title: Bringing Clarity to Composite Fading: A λ-κ-μ Shadowed Distribution for Complex Wireless Channels
authors: Huan Wang, Guanjun Xu, Junliang Liu, Cheng Zhang, Dusit Niyato
abstract: Composite fading distributions provide tractable modeling of shadowing and multipath effects, enabling accurate channel characterization and improving communication reliability. Motivated by this, we propose λ-κ-μ shadowed distribution to precisely describe the signal fading behavior caused by the joint impact of shadowing effects and multipath clusters in non-homogeneous channels. Specifically, deterministic expressions for the probability density function, cumulative distribution function, and high-order raw moments are derived, immediately followed by key performance metrics, such as average bit error rate, ergodic capacity, and effective rate. Subsequently, asymptotic analyses of these metrics are conducted in the high signal-to-noise ratio regime to derive simplified expressions that facilitate system evaluation. Furthermore, we systematically analyze the relationship between these expressions and their parameters. The results demonstrate that our proposed λ-κ-μ shadowed distribution possesses good mathematical tractability, with its accuracy validated by Monte Carlo simulations. Additionally, we conduct the truncation error analysis to examine the convergence of the proposed model, and its practical applicability was confirmed through field measurements. More importantly, our proposed λ-κ-μ shadowed distribution subsumes multiple classical distributions as special cases, exhibiting strong flexibility and applicability.

title: Multi-Scenario Channel Generation for mmWave UAV Communications Using Conditional Generative Adversarial Networks with Dual-Attention Mechanisms
authors: Haozheng Lv, Ji Bian, Jie Tian, Yu Liu, Cheng-Xiang Wang, Xiangwei Zheng
abstract: In sixth-generation systems, unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) communications have become indispensable due to their enhanced coverage, flexible deployment, and line-of-sight (LoS) propagation advantages. To facilitate system testing and optimization for future UAV communications, this paper proposes a conditional generative adversarial network (CGAN)-based channel generation model for UAV communication systems. The model learns the bidirectional wideband propagation of multipath components (MPCs), capturing key parameters such as delay, power, and three-dimensional departure and arrival angles for multiple scenarios. Using the synthesized parameters, the channel impulse response (CIR) can be reconstructed in accordance with 3GPP evaluation standards. To improve feature learning, we design dual-attention mechanisms that operate in both spatial and parameter domains. The spatial attention module models dependencies among MPCs, while the parameter attention module captures correlations among multipath parameters. These modules are employed in parallel within transformer blocks, with inter-block aggregation further enhancing the channel feature representation. Comprehensive evaluations using metrics such as Kullback-Leibler (KL) divergence, Jensen-Shannon (JS) divergence, Earth Mover's Distance (EMD), and correlation matrix distance (CMD) demonstrate that the proposed model achieves improved agreement with reference channel on most channel statistics, providing better performance compared with representative generative baselines.

title: Quadrotor UAV Actuators Fine-grained Fault Diagnosis with Cross-domain Incremental Learning
authors: Jinju Zhou, Zhiqiu Huang, Weiwei Li, Hang Zhou, Peng Han
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: The actuator is the key module of the unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) for operation control. With the growing application of UAV in complex tasks, accurate fault diagnosis (FD) of actuators has become essential. However, most existing researches focus on coarse-grained fault diagnosis (CFD) which only detects the faulty actuator, but fail to address fine-grained fault diagnosis (FFD) within the actuator. Moreover, the current FD model trained on software simulation data has not shown applicability in actual flight due to the distribution discrepancy between the two domains. To address these issues, a novel fine-grained fault diagnosis framework is proposed to detect the faults within actuators. In this framework, a feature-extraction broad learning system with incremental learning (FE-BLS-IL) model is proposed. The model incorporates a feature extraction layer and a cross-domain incremental learning approach, significantly improving the FD accuracy in actual UAV flight tasks. Comprehensive experiments conducted on the software simulation and actual datasets demonstrate that the proposed framework effectively and accurately diagnoses the fine-grained actuator faults, highlighting its robustness and reliability.

title: Comprehensive Electromagnetic Transient Modeling of Switched Reluctance Motor Drive System
authors: Seyedarmin Mirnikjoo, Mohammed Naidjate, Jean Mahseredjian, Nicolas Bracikowski, Paul Akiki
publish: IEEE Open Access Journal of Power and Energy
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive electromagnetic transient model for a switched reluctance motor drive system using a circuit-based representation of the machine, power converter, and control system. The proposed model seamlessly integrates a nonlinear permeance network with the electrical circuit and the equivalent circuit of the machine's mechanical dynamics. The machine’s electromagnetic model is automatically constructed using a mesh-based approach allowing for local studies within the machine while the complete drive system is considered. The airgap is modeled with variable permeances, enabling transient studies without remeshing or reconnecting nodes during runtime. Comparison with the finite element-based model confirms the accuracy of the proposed model and demonstrates its lower computational time. The proposed model is used to simulate transient scenarios of the entire switched reluctance drive system under faulty conditions.

title: Intrusion Detection and Prevention for Intra- and Inter-vehicle Networks: A Comprehensive Survey
authors: Aya El-Fatyany, Jabar Mahmood, Michael Abebe Berwo, Xiaohang Wang, Li Lu, Qiang Xue, Kui Ren
abstract: The security of intra- and inter-vehicle networks relies heavily on the implementation of intrusion detection systems (IDS) and intrusion prevention systems (IPS). These crucial technologies serve as the primary defence mechanisms against cyber threats, continuously monitoring and mitigating malicious activities within vehicles and across interconnected vehicular networks to ensure secure and reliable communication. This survey examines research works of IDS and IPS for intra- and inter-vehicle networks from 2020 to 2025, introducing an innovative taxonomy. This study analyzes various intrusion detection and prevention strategies, as well as attack surfaces, scenarios, and benchmark datasets for intra- and inter-vehicle networks. Furthermore, this paper emphasizes the necessity of collaborative IDS/IPS for both intra- and inter-vehicle networks and discuses directions for future research in this field.

title:
A One-Port Floating-Target Capacitance-to-Digital Converter with Consecutive Double-Sided Conversion and Extended Counting ADC Achieving 157.5 aF
                    <sub>rms</sub>
                    at 316-pF Input Capacitance
authors: Sanghwa Han, Hyeongseon Eom, Kwonbeen Du, Hyunjoong Lee, Nam Soo Kim, Jooyeol Rhee
abstract: This paper presents a one‑port capacitance‑to‑digital converter (CDC) for floating‑target displacement sensing. A charge‑amplifier front end employing consecutive double‑sided conversion is combined with a first‑order incremental ΔΣ extended‑counting analog-to-digital converter that provides uniform input sample weighting and a successive-approximation-register residue conversion stage. Together, these blocks intrinsically reject low‑frequency interference on the floating target while enabling short conversion time and high linearity. A detailed analysis of the charge-amplifier-based CDC clarifies the trade-offs between settling time and noise under large sensing capacitance. Fabricated in a 0.13‑μm CMOS, the prototype occupies 0.54 mm² and achieves 157.5 aF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">rms</sub> input‑referred noise at 316-pF total input capacitance with a 1.407-ms conversion time, consuming 1.907 mW from 3.3‑V (analog) and 1.5‑V (digital) supplies. The modified Schreier figure-of-merit is 172.7 dB.

title: Front Cover
authors: N/A

title: MotionPrior: Exploring Efficient Learning of Motion Concepts for Few-shot Video Generation
authors: Yaosi Hu, Chang Wen Chen
abstract: The diffusion-based text-to-image generation has achieved remarkable progress and realistic content generation performance, greatly promoting the development in text-to-video generation. Although equipped with powerful image diffusion models, video generation modeling still requires massive labeled data and a high training resource cost. Recent, work has been focused on cost-effective video generation in a one-shot or few-shot manner based on the image diffusion model with minimum demand for video data and computing resources. However, these video generation models only support the generation of one single motion pattern/concept. This raises an important question: Can we improve generation freedom with a light training burden? In this paper, we explore a cost-effective video generation scheme for adaptive motion concepts by learning motion priors from a small set of video data. Specifically, we construct a learnable bank for motion concepts and propose the Dual-Semantic-guided Motion Attention module to locate the corresponding motion elements from the bank with the guidance of textual semantic and visual semantic. The extracted motion elements are inserted into video latents via lightweight motion injection layer, which is capable of integrating motion semantic effectively with much fewer parameters compared to the conventional temporal attention layer. In addition, we introduce a temporal-aware noise prior and an inter-frame consistency constraint to strengthen the learning of temporal dependency and improve video smoothness. Extensive experiments validate that the proposed method can learn motion priors adaptively from a small set of training videos to generate smooth videos that involve either single or multiple motion concepts. The results demonstrate that the proposed scheme achieves superior performance compared to existing few-shot video generation methods and even some large-scale video generation models. More information and results are available at https://youncy-hu.github.io/motionprior/.

title: One Step Diffusion-based Super-Resolution with Time-Aware Distillation
authors: Xiao He, Haoao Tang, Zhijun Tu, Junchao Zhang, Kun Cheng, Hanting Chen, Yong Guo, Mingrui Zhu, Jie Hu, Nannan Wang, Xinbo Gao
abstract: iffusion-based image super-resolution (SR) has shown strong potential in recovering high-fidelity details from low-resolution inputs. However, the need for tens or hundreds of sampling steps leads to substantial inference latency. Recent works attempt to accelerate this process via knowledge distillation, but often rely solely on pixel-level loss or overlook the fact that diffusion models capture different information across time steps. To address this, we propose TAD-SR, a time-aware diffusion distillation framework. Specifically, we introduce a novel score distillation strategy to align the score functions between the outputs of the student and teacher models after minor noise perturbation. This distillation strategy eliminates the inherent bias in score distillation sampling (SDS) and enables the student models to focus more on high-frequency image details by sampling at smaller time steps. We further introduce a time-aware discriminator that exploits the teacher’s knowledge to differentiate real and synthetic samples across different noise scales, using explicit temporal conditioning. Extensive experiments on SR tasks demonstrate that TAD-SR outperforms existing singl-estep diffusion methods and achieves performance on par with multi-step state-of-the-art models.iffusion-based image super-resolution (SR) has shown strong potential in recovering highfidelity details from low-resolution inputs. However, the need for tens or hundreds of sampling steps leads to substantial inference latency. Recent works attempt to accelerate this process via knowledge distillation, but often rely solely on pixel-level loss or overlook the fact that diffusion models capture different information across time steps. To address this, we propose TADSR, a time-aware diffusion distillation framework. Specifically, we introduce a novel score distillation strategy to align the score functions between the outputs of the student and teacher models after minor noise perturbation. This distillation strategy eliminates the inherent bias in score distillation sampling (SDS) and enables the student models to focus more on highf-requency image details by sampling at smaller time steps. We further introduce a time-aware discriminator that exploits the teacher’s knowledge to differentiate real and synthetic samples across different noise scales, using explicit temporal conditioning. Extensive experiments on SR tasks demonstrate that TAD-SR outperforms existing single-step diffusion methods and achieves performance on par with multi-step state-of-the-art models D.

title: Discrete-Time Improved Equivalent-Input-Disturbance Control with Dynamic Decoupled Adaptive Damping Regulator for PMSM Current Enhancement
authors: Jiaxing Ye, Mingyi Wang, Shun Cai, Chaoyu Zhang, Junchi Li, Sihang Cui, Jinrui Xie, Zihan Hu, Chengming Zhang, Liyi Li
abstract: Conventional motor drives are subjected to extensive disturbances and uncertainties across entire frequency spectrum, which significantly deteriorate current control performance within electric propulsion system. To address this issue, this paper proposes a discrete-time composite control strategy that integrates an improved equivalent-input-disturbance (IEID) approach with a dynamic decoupled adaptive damping (DDAD) regulator, aiming to suppress external disturbances and improve the transient current response. Specifically, a proportional-integral augmented observer (PIAO) is introduced into the IEID framework, where the integral of the measured current is treated as an augmented variable.
