This modification enhances both the estimation accuracy of internal states and the system’s resilience to the current measurement noise. Furthermore, a modified causal filter, constructed building upon an integral-resonant internal model structure, is incorporated into the IEID estimator to achieve simultaneous and effective attenuation of both disturbances and harmonics. Finally, an improved adaptive damping function that is adjustably updated with load current as well as motor speed and the associated dynamic decoupled control law are systematically designed using poles placement techniques, enabling the superior transient dynamics and the flexible damping characteristics. Comparative experimental results validate the practical applicability of the proposed control method.

title: A Holistic Process-Chain Optimization Framework for High-Reliability High-I/O Silicon-to-Silicon Flip-Chip Packaging
authors: Zhiqiang Chen, Guoliang Zhao, Wei Quan, Zhongbin Xie, Yun Zhang, Yunqi Cao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: High-density flip-chip (FC) packaging enables thousands of inputs/outputs (I/Os) for advanced microsystems but remains susceptible to reliability degradation arising from solder coplanarity variation, interposer warpage, surface contamination, and residual flux after reflow. Rather than addressing global coefficient of thermal expansion (CTE) mismatch, this work establishes a qualification-driven, defect-suppression-oriented process-chain optimization framework for high-reliability silicon-based high-I/O FC packaging. A reliability-oriented “presoldering– soldering–post-soldering” optimization strategy is proposed and experimentally validated, integrating rigorous solder quality assurance, interposer warpage control, vacuum plasma surface activation, void-suppressed vacuum reflow, and post-reflow flux removal. Three types of silicon dies and through-silicon-via (TSV) interposers were employed to assess process robustness and consistency. The optimized approach achieved solder coplanarity below 15 μm, solder-level shear strength exceeding 177.62MPa, and chip-level shear force above the measurable limit of 122.5 N. Vacuum reflow reduced the average void ratio from approximately 10% (conventional hot-air reflow) to undetectable levels, while plasma activation improved solder wettability and suppressed non-wet defects. Daisy-chain resistance variation remained below 1% after 100 thermal cycles between −65 °C and 150 °C, and cross-sectional inspection revealed no crack initiation, delamination, or void propagation after up to 1000 cycles. Although silicon-to-silicon integration inherently minimizes global CTE mismatch, localized defects such as solder voids and residual flux remain critical reliability risks in dense high-I/O packaging. The results demonstrate that systematic defect suppression across the entire process chain is essential for achieving qualification-grade reliability. The proposed methodology is particularly suited for high-reliability silicon-based microsystem platforms and provides a scalable framework for extension to more aggressive packaging architectures with appropriate validation.

title: CIRCUS: A Causal Intervention-Based Framework for Enhancing Counterfactual Fairness in Trained Classifiers
authors: Qifen Yang, Yuhui Deng, Jiande Huang, Lijuan Lu, Peng Zhou, Geyong Min
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: Ensuring model fairness for preventing potential biases based on any sensitive attribute is crucial for the societal acceptance of artificial intelligence in critical applications. Among various fairness concepts, counterfactual fairness has gained prominence as it is grounded in causal inference. This concept requires that an individual’s prediction in the original world remains consistent with that in the counterfactual world where the sensitive feature value is modified. In this article, we aim to mitigate counterfactual biases of the model through causal intervention. Specifically, we first achieve effective causal intervention and counterfactual generation by proposing the causal inference tabular generative adversarial network (CITGAN) architecture. Unlike prior approaches based on variational autoencoders (VAEs) that inherently lack structural causal model (SCM) fidelity due to simultaneous generation, CITGAN strictly enforces causal consistency via an end-to-end topological generation process. By integrating exogenous variable inference with sequential generation, CITGAN ensures that functional dependencies are structurally preserved. Building on CITGAN, we propose the CIRCUS framework, a causal intervention-based framework designed to intuitively enhance the counterfactual fairness in trained classifiers. CIRCUS generates counterfactually discriminatory samples (CDSs) via causal intervention, guided by gradients and feature contributions, and subsequently applies bias correction preprocessing to their labels for classifier retraining. Experimental results demonstrate that CIRCUS effectively enhances counterfactual fairness while maintaining robust classification performance. Specifically, for the deep neural network (DNN) model, the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text {MMD}_{\text {L}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text {MMD}_{\text {K}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> values are reduced by averages of 39.7% and 40.4%, respectively, compared with the second-best result. For the residual network (ResNet) model, these reductions amount to 56.7% and 54.5%, respectively.

title: 6Global: Dynamic IPv6 Active Address Scanning Assisted by Global Perspective
authors: Junqing Wang, Lejun Zhang, Zhihong Tian, Kejia Zhang, Shen Su, Jing Qiu, Yanbin Sun, Ran Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: Network scanning is crucial for both network management and cybersecurity. However, due to the vast address space of IPv6, brute-force scanning is infeasible. Seed-based target generation algorithms have recently attracted considerable research attention. However, existing target generation algorithms lack a deeper exploration of patterns, leading to poor capture of dense regions and consequently low hitrate. To address this issue, we propose 6Global, a dynamic IPv6 active address scanning method assisted by global perspective. 6Global first performs rapid clustering of seed addresses based on their descriptive attributes. Then, for each cluster, patterns are generated in a bottom-up manner based on entropy, using subranges to represent patterns and resulting in denser patterns. Finally, dynamic scanning is conducted using these patterns. During scanning, the reward of each pattern is dynamically adjusted based on its active density and global statistics, which enhances the capability in capturing dense regions. Experimental results on six seed datasets show that 6Global overall outperforms seven baseline methods and demonstrates significant advantages across multiple datasets.

title: ChatTracker: Enhancing Visual Tracking via LLM-Driven Iterative Description Refinement
authors: Yu Zhang, Yiming Sun, Mi Zhang, Fan Yu, Shaoxiang Chen, Yang Li, Changbo Wang, Jianke Zhu, Steven C.H. Hoi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Visual object tracking focuses on locating a target object within a video sequence based on an initial bounding box. Recently, Vision-Language (VL) trackers have been proposed to utilize additional natural language descriptions to enhance versatility in various applications. Despite this potential, VL trackers still underperform the State-of-the-Art (SoTA) visual trackers in terms of tracking accuracy. We find that this inferiority is primarily due to their heavy reliance on manual textual annotations, which include the frequent provision of ambiguous language descriptions. In this paper, we identify, for the first time, that over 10% of textual annotations in existing VL tracking datasets suffer from inaccuracies through manual evaluation. To address this problem, we propose ChatTracker to leverage the wealth of world knowledge in the Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) to generate high-quality language descriptions and enhance tracking performance. To this end, we propose a novel Reflection-based Language Description Refinement Module to iteratively refine the ambiguous and inaccurate descriptions of the target with tracking feedback. To further utilize semantic information produced by MLLM, a simple yet effective VL tracking framework is proposed, which can be easily integrated as a plugand- play module to boost the performance of both VL and visual trackers. Experimental results show that ChatTracker achieves comparable performance to existing SoTA tracking methods. In addition, language descriptions generated by ChatTracker enhance the performance of various VL trackers and exhibit better text-to-image alignment than annotations in the original dataset. Moreover, our proposed framework can improve the performance of various visual tasks, including Referring Expression Comprehension (REC), Referring Expression Segmentation (RES), and Referring Video Object Segmentation (R-VOS) tasks by providing more accurate language descriptions, which demonstrates the universality of ChatTracker. We release the manual evaluation results and the generated textual descriptions, aiming to drive advancements in VL tracking.

title: A highly sensitive osteocalcin immunosensor based on MWCNT - COOH/PVA/PEDOT:PSS modified electrode_supp1-3671748.docx
authors: Shaohui Zhang
abstract: Osteocalcin, a key biomarker of bone metabolism, is of significant clinical value for the diagnosis and management of osteoporosis through dynamic monitoring. The development of highly sensitive detection methods is therefore of great importance. This study developed a highly sensitive electrochemical immunosensor for osteocalcin detection based on a nanocomposite of carboxylated multi-walled carbon nanotubes (MWCNT-COOH), polyvinyl alcohol (PVA), and poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene):polystyrene sulfonate (PEDOT:PSS). A uniform and stable nanocomposite film was prepared via a simple and rapid method involving physical blending and vortex dispersion. Osteocalcin (OC) antibodies were covalently immobilized onto the surface of a functionalized screen-printed electrode using an EDC/NHS coupling system. The detection principle relies on the specific antigen-antibody binding event hindering interfacial electron transfer, enabling the quantitative detection of OC via differential pulse voltammetry (DPV). Under optimal conditions, the sensor exhibited an excellent linear response (R² = 0.996) over a concentration range from 10 pg/mL to 100 μg/mL, with a detection limit of 1.2 pg/mL. Furthermore, the sensor demonstrated outstanding selectivity, reproducibility, and stability, showcasing its potential as a promising tool for the quantitative analysis of bone metabolism biomarkers in biological fluids.

title: Winners of 2025 John Howard Memorial EMC Education Grant Announced
authors: Daryl Beetner
abstract: Educating the next generation of EMC engineers is one of the core missions of the EMC Society. The John Howard Memorial EMC Education grant was created with that mission in mind. This grant provides up to $10,000 over two years for activities and investments which improve educational opportunities in the area of EMC.

title: 2026 Young Professional Event Updates
authors: Patrick DeRoy
abstract: Greetings, YP readers! I hope your 2026 is off to a great start. Our EMC Society YP volunteers have been busy finalizing the details for our major events this year. In fact, 2026 will be the first year we hold YP events at all three major conferences sponsored by our Society: APEMC, the EMC+SIPI Symposium, and EMC Europe. If you're a YP and plan to attend one (or more) of these events, don't forget to sign up and seize the opportunity to meet other YPs. Relationships formed within the EMC Society can lead to future collaborations and provide valuable contacts when you need a friend to bounce ideas off.

title: GeoCTP: Structure-aware Prediction of Multifunctional Cancer Therapy Peptides via Graph Transformer and Contrastive Learning
authors: Jiahui Guan, Lantian Yao, Peilin Xie, Zhihao Zhao, Dian Meng, Zimeng Chen, Tzong-Yi Lee, Ying-Chih Chiang, Junwen Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Cancer therapy peptides (CTPs), as multifunctional peptides, possess the ability to target cancer cells or related proteins, exhibiting significant therapeutic potential. However, traditional experimental screening methods are time-consuming and labor-intensive, limiting the pace of discovery. To address this challenge, we propose GeoCTP, a geometric deep learning framework that integrates both sequence and structural information for accurate CTP prediction. Specifically, GeoCTP employs ESMfold to generate peptide 3D structures and utilizes a Graph Transformer to extract structure-aware representations. For semantic feature extraction from sequences, the ESM-2 language model is adopted. Additionally, a two-level contrastive learning strategy is employed to enhance feature alignment across modalities and improve inter-class discriminability. Experimental results indicate that GeoCTP outperforms state-of-the-art peptide function prediction methods. As the first predictive tool specifically designed for multifunctional CTPs, GeoCTP not only achieves strong classification performance but also identifies high-attention regions indicative of potential functional sites. Overall, this study highlights the potential of CTP functional prediction and its implications for bioinformatics in precision medicine.

title: AccelFaaS : Accelerating FaaS via Pre-warmed Memory and Control Channel Offloading
authors: Seong-Joong Kim, Seungwon Shin, Myoungsung You
abstract: Serverless computing offers a cost-efficient and scalable architecture for cloud applications by processing client requests through a set of functions. However, the existing server less data plane suffers from significant cold-start latency and degraded inter-function data transfer performance due to its inherent complexity. Previous studies using function pre-loading and shared-memory channels partially mitigate these issues but remain limited by their incompatibility with scale-from-zero and substantial memory inefficiencies. We propose AccelFaaS, a fast and memory-efficient serverless data plane. AccelFaaS identifies that control channel setup is the dominant bottleneck in cold-starts and decouples this procedure from the critical path, significantly reducing startup latency without relying on pre loading. AccelFaaS improves inter-function communication by introducing a cached shared memory channel that reduces page faults and fragmentation, while dynamically resizing its capacity according to workload demands to minimize memory usage. Evaluations show that AccelFaaS reduces cold-start latency by 11–27×, increases chaining throughput by 4×, and lowers memory consumption by 2–16× compared to existing systems.

title: Cinergy: Deterministic Power Monitoring for Carbon Accounting in the Cloud
authors: Pierre Jacquet, Camille Coti, Marcos dias De Assunção, Romain Rouvoy
abstract: Cloud providers now commonly offer tools to monitor the environmental impact of hosted services, aiming to inform customers about the carbon footprint of their infrastructures. A significant share of this impact arises from the power consumption of servers. To allocate server-related emissions, these methodologies typically rely on resource-quantity-based attribution and do not account for the effective usage of virtual resources. We demonstrate that resource usage can drastically affect carbon estimations. This article proposes a framework, called Cinergy, to standardize how cloud providers integrate the power consumption of virtual resources into their carbon calculators. It defines a baseline to deterministically assess the power consumption of a provisioned Virtual Machine (VM)—i.e., the same usage should yield the same power estimate—while still accounting for consolidation gains enabled by virtualization. Our approach, constructed and evaluated empirically, achieves high precision, with a mean absolute error of 6.6% across various hardware. Using metrics collected from cloud providers, we reveal the impact of instance type on energy efficiency, with differences reaching 85% for the same VM size. We also show that quantity-based carbon accounting methodologies can drastically underestimate actual CO2 emissions-by up to a factor of three.

title: 3SP-DUNet: An Interpretable Deep Unfolded Network with Spatial-Spectral Sparse Priors for Hyperspectral Pansharpening
authors: Yong Yang, Xuan Liu, Shuying Huang, Xiaozheng Wang, Jinlin Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Most existing deep learning-based pansharpening methods adopt typical black-box models to achieve the fusion of panchromatic (PAN) and low-resolution hyperspectral (LRHS) images. This type of architecture has limited interpretability, making it difficult to ensure the fidelity of spatial and spectral features in the fusion results. To address this issue, we propose an interpretable deep unfolding network with spectral-spatial sparsity priors (3SP-DUNet), which is designed based on the unfolding optimization algorithm for solving the HS pansharpening problem. Firstly, the objective function for solving HRHS images in the HS pansharpening task is optimized to obtain the optimized solution formula. Then, based on the solution terms in the formula, spectral degradation/reconstruction blocks (SpeD/SpeR) and spatial degradation/reconstruction blocks (SpaD/SpaR) are constructed to simulate the degradation and reconstruction process of spectral and spatial features. Regarding the prior regularization terms in the formula, spectral/spatial sparse prior blocks are designed to constrain the solution space, ensuring the fidelity of spectral and spatial features. In addition, to reduce feature loss caused by increasing network depth, a cross-stage memory fusion unit (CMFU) is constructed to supplement features by fusing reconstructed images from adjacent stages. To guide the model to reconstruct accurate spectral features, a spectral similarity loss is defined by calculating the cosine values of the corresponding spectral vectors between the fused image and corresponding ground-truth (GT) image. Extensive experiments demonstrate that 3SP-DUNet surpasses some state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods in both quantitative metrics and visual quality, while offering superior interpretability. The source code of this work will be released on GitHub.

title: High-Fidelity GPR Diffraction Extraction via PSO-Guided Reflection Reconstruction in Wavelet-Dip Domains
authors: Hao Zhang, Qianwei Dai, Bin Zhang, Yi Lei, Run He, Le Lyu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Ground penetrating radar (GPR) diffractions typically manifest weak responses resulting from electromagnetic wave interactions with subwavelength heterogeneities (e.g., cracks, voids) or geometric discontinuities (e.g., scattering edges, corners). These subtle yet informative wavefield components are critical for enabling enhanced illumination and dedicated imaging of subsurface micro-features in infrastructure assessment and geological hazard mitigation. Current diffraction extraction methods in GPR face significant practical limitations: kinematic local-slope techniques are computationally intensive and may lead to loss of vertex events, whereas coherence analysis requires meticulous calibration of scaling factors and time-moveout corrections. Such constraints can compromise the resolution of imaging modalities such as migration, tomography, and full-waveform inversion (FWI). To address these challenges, this study introduces an adaptive diffraction extraction workflow that integrates three core modules in wavelet and dip domains: a wavelet-based reflection model for structural preservation, dip-adaptive smoothing operators for wavefield decomposition, and particle swarm optimization (PSO) with multi-objective criteria (Laplacian sharpness, wavelet entropy, and focusing metrics) for parameters optimization. The workflow iteratively extracts residual diffractions by optimizing wavelet decomposition levels, smoothing scales, and residual thresholds using PSO-driven fitness functions. Validated through heterogeneous random media model, the Sigsbee2a benchmark, and field experiments, the PSO-guided workflow demonstrates quantifiable improvements in edge preservation, coherence artifacts reduction, spatial resolution, and kinematic consistency. By prioritizing wavefield integrity and structural fineness, the proposed method provides refined inputs for subsequent imaging, highlighting its potential to advance the resolution and reliability of near-surface GPR sensing applications.

title: Output Feedback Displacement Control for Piezoelectric Deformable Mirrors with Unknown Hysteresis and Dynamics Coupling
authors: Mohammad Al Saaideh, Lihong Zhang, Mohammad Al Janaideh
abstract: Deformable mirrors (DMs) are key components of adaptive optics (AO) systems, enabling precise wavefront control in a wide range of applications. When actuated by piezoelectric actuators (PAs), the resulting PA-DM can exhibit nonlinear behaviors, including hysteresis and dynamic coupling between actuators, which degrade performance. This paper presents a displacement-control strategy for PA-DMs that explicitly accounts for these unknown nonlinearities and inter-actuator couplings. The proposed approach combines state feedback control for accurate displacement tracking with an extended high-gain observer (EHGO) to estimate the unknown nonlinear effects. A 41-actuator PA-DM is used as a case study to evaluate the controller's performance. Simulation results show that the proposed controller significantly improves performance, achieving the desired Zernike-mode aberration reconstruction with a peak residual of 0.01 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu$</tex-math></inline-formula>m, compared to 1.2 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu$</tex-math></inline-formula>m obtained using a conventional control approach. The controller is further tested under realistic atmospheric conditions: a fast-changing wavefront with a Greenwood frequency of 35 Hz representing mid-level turbulence, and a slow-changing wavefront with a Greenwood frequency of 3.8 Hz representing low-level turbulence. The results demonstrate consistent performance across both scenarios, with an average RMSE of 0.23 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu$</tex-math></inline-formula>m for the fast-changing wavefront and 0.021 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu$</tex-math></inline-formula>m for the low-changing wavefront.

title: A Novel Frequency-Reconfigurable Antenna Employing Liquid Dielectric Variation
authors: Shrinjoy Chatterjee, Anamika A Kamath, Nrusingha Charan Pradhan, Chinmoy Saha
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a frequency reconfigurable microstrip patch antenna with a novel methodology of passive mechanical tuning of its resonant frequency through the use of layered dielectric substrate containing distilled water as a liquid dielectric. This work delves into the mechanism of the proposed antenna showcasing the reconfigurability through the tuning of layered dielectrics. By altering the ratio of the distilled water to air inside the dielectric, the effective dielectric constant, effective length and the resonant frequency achieves reconfigurability. A formula for computing the resonant frequency using the height of distilled water inside the dielectric layers is derived. The results of the simulated design are provided alongside a brief comparative study of the tuning ranges of this work, with existing forms of reconfigurable antenna. Finally, an application of this design and its accompanying algorithm in a Cognitive Radio (CR) system is enumerated.

title: Sub-Terahertz Far-Field Beam Steering Using a Two-Bit Graphene-Based Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface for 6G Applications
authors: Abhishek Chauhan, Amit K. Singh, Rahul Mondal, Akhilesh Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents the design and analysis of a sub-terahertz (sub-THz) reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) for dynamic far-field beam steering. A 2-bit square-shaped reflective unit cell is designed to operate efficiently at 0.27 THz, with dimensions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.4 \times 0.4\ \text{mm}^{2}(0.34 \lambda \times 0.34 \lambda)$</tex>, where <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\lambda$</tex> is the free-space wavelength. The unit cell consists of a metal-graphene patch on a polyimide substrate. Discrete phase control is achieved by tuning the graphene chemical potential (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\mu}_{\boldsymbol{c}}$</tex>) across four distinct bias states: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${0\ \text{eV}}, {0. 1 8}\ \text{eV}, 0.25\ \text{eV}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 e V$</tex>, resulting in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$90^{\circ} \pm 5^{\circ}$</tex> phase shifts between the adjacent states. A compact RIS panel of size <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16 \times 16 m m^{2}(13.6 \lambda \times 13.6 \lambda)$</tex> is constructed using these unit cells. The proposed RIS enables beam steering over elevation angles from 0° to 40°, with a maximum scan loss of 3.3 dB. Compared to 1-bit RIS designs, the 2-bit configuration achieves 12.3 dB side lobe suppression, enhancing main-lobe directivity and beam quality for sub-THz 6G applications.

title: Evaluating Rail Yard Capacity with an Optimization Model
authors: Rexhina Hoxha, Simona Sacone, Geert Van de Weyer, Roxan De Ridder, Stefano Cangelosi, Enrico Zero
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Rail yards are a critical component of port terminal operations, enabling the efficient movement of large volumes of containers by rail. This paper presents a mathematical model of the rail yard at PSA Genova Pra' (PSA GP), developed to estimate the terminal's maximum train-handling capacity, defined as the maximum number of trains the terminal can accommodate and serve within a given time period, while considering physical layout, operational constraints, and available resources. The model accounts for key elements such as track availability and rail-mounted gantry (RMG) cranes. Through scenario-based analysis, we explore how variations in these parameters affect the overall capacity. Results show that the number of available RMG cranes has the most significant impact on the number of trains the yard can handle, providing valuable insights for optimizing resource allocation and improving terminal efficiency.

title: A Dual Frequency sub-6 GHz Leaky Dynamic Metasurface Antenna
authors: Praneet Jain, Saptarshi Ghosh, Simon Cotton, Okan Yurduseven
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This work presents a dual frequency dynamic metasurface antenna (DMA). The proposed DMA features an array of hexagonal-shaped and square-shaped complementary electric inductive capacitive (cELC) resonators. The cELC unit cells are embedded in the top layer of a substrate-integrated waveguide (SIW). The hexagonal-shaped cELC unit cells are arranged in a honeycomb formation and the square-shaped cELC unit cells are arranged in a straight line. P-i-n diodes are introduced to control the reactance of the unit cells to the wave propagating in the waveguide. The DMA can radiate at two different frequencies, i.e., 4.78 GHz and 5.7 GHz consequently. The overall beam coverage for the antenna is 160°. The antenna has an average gain of 3.23 dBi for 4.78 GHz operation and 5.3 dBi for 5.7 GHz operation. The antenna's performance has been validated through full-wave simulations. With its wide beam coverage and frequency agility, the antenna can be used as a sensing component and can be integrated with reflective intelligent surfaces (RIS).

title: Epilepsy Risk Prediction through Enhanced Machine Learning and Diverse Feature Selection Approaches
authors: Mir Hossain Ahmed, Badar Hossain, Tahsina Hasnat, S.M. Minhaj Chowdhury, Md. Jalal Uddin Chowdhury, Md. Saef Ullah Miah
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Epilepsy is a prevalent neurological disorder affecting approximately 56 million people worldwide, yet early diagnosis remains challenging. Current diagnostic approaches are largely reactive, requiring patients to experience multiple seizures before confirmation, thereby limiting opportunities for preventive intervention. In this study, we developed a machine learning framework to predict epilepsy risk using clinical and demographic indicators. Using the Epilepsy Onset Prediction Dataset from Kaggle comprising 7,000 patient records with 20 features including demographic information, medical history, and clinical indicators, we evaluated nine machine learning algorithms. To address class imbalance inherent in medical datasets, we compared two sampling strategies: Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) and random undersampling. All models underwent systematic hyperparameter optimization using GridSearchCV with 5-fold cross-validation. Our results demonstrate that oversampling substantially outperforms undersampling. Random Forest achieved an F1-score of 0.957 and ROC-AUC of 0.998 on the oversampled dataset. Furthermore, our stacking ensemble method achieved an F1-score of 0.984 and ROC-AUC of 0.999, representing approximately 6% improvement over existing state-of-the-art methods. The consistency between test and cross-validation metrics indicates robust generalization without overfitting. This study demonstrates that machine learning can effectively identify high-risk individuals for epilepsy, potentially enabling early intervention strategies that could benefit millions of patients worldwide.

title: Design and Analysis of a High-Gain Slot-Loaded Microstrip Patch Antenna for Sub-6GHz Applications
authors: Shreyas Singh, Shakthi Priya M, Nalini Pareek, Dhairya Singh Arya
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: Rapid development of 5 G networks in the sub6 GHz spectrum has made the licensed band of 3.3 to 3.8 GHz critical for 5 G communication systems. The 3.5 GHz frequency, in particular, offers an optimal balance between coverage and capacity, being extremely crucial in the deployment of wireless technologies such as WiMAX, making it a strategic choice for broadband communication. However, designing antennas within this spectrum to provide both wide bandwidth and high gain remains a challenge. In this paper, a microstrip patch antenna (MPA) featuring a defected ground structure (DGS) and strategically etched out slots from the patch, along with two copper reflector patches, is patterned on the substrate. These modifications are designed to enhance directivity, impedance bandwidth, and return loss. Simulation results reveal that the designed MPA achieves a bandwidth of 240 MHz centered around 3.50 GHz, with a peak directivity gain of 7.43 dB, return loss of - 27.09 dB and VSWR of 1.09.

title: doScenes: An Autonomous Driving Dataset with Natural Language Instruction for Human Interaction and Vision-Language Navigation
authors: Parthib Roy, Srinivasa Perisetla, Shashank Shriram, Harsha Krishnaswamy, Aryan Keskar, Ross Greer
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Human-interactive robotic systems, particularly autonomous vehicles (AVs), must effectively integrate human instructions into their motion planning. This paper introduces doScenes, a novel dataset designed to facilitate research on human-vehicle instruction interactions, focusing on short-term directives that directly influence vehicle motion. By annotating multimodal sensor data with natural language instructions and referentiality tags, doScenes bridges the gap between instruction and driving response, enabling context-aware and adaptive planning. Unlike existing datasets that focus on ranking or scenelevel reasoning, doScenes emphasizes actionable directives tied to static and dynamic scene objects. This framework addresses limitations in prior research, such as reliance on simulated data or predefined action sets, by supporting nuanced and flexible responses in real-world scenarios. This work lays the foundation for developing learning strategies that seamlessly integrate human instructions into autonomous systems, advancing safe and effective human-vehicle collaboration. We make our data publicly available at https://www.github.com/rossgreer/doScenes

title: Towards AI-Guided Fetal Ultrasound Education: Simulation and Immersive Visualization in AR/VR
authors: Apurva Srivastava, Veena Iyer, Deepak Mishra
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: Ultrasound imaging is a widely adopted diagnostic tool, particularly in obstetrics, due to its affordability, portability, and safety. However, mastering fetal ultrasound-especially during the third trimester-requires significant hands-on practice, real-time spatial reasoning, and interpretation of noisy images, often under expert supervision. To address this challenge, we propose an AI-augmented simulation framework integrated into an immersive Augmented Reality (AR)/Virtual Reality (VR) environment for training radiology and obstetrics residents. The system generates realistic B-mode ultrasound sequences using physics-based modelling SIMUS (MATLAB function) combined with optical flow for dynamic fetal motion. These sequences are embedded in Unity and rendered in real-time via Oculus and HoloLens platforms, allowing interactive probe manipulation. The proposed framework is designed for future integration with deep learning models trained on synthetic data to provide real time guidance, anatomical plane prediction, and feedback on probe orientation. This approach offers a scalable, data-efficient solution for skill development in ultrasound diagnostics where access to clinical training is limited

title: A Case for Agentic Transceivers in Physical AI-RF Circuits and Systems
authors: Arjuna Madanayake, Kasun Karunanayake, Robert Hacker, Ranga Rodrigo, Chamira Edussooriya, Shubhankar Majumdar, Leonid Belostotski
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This concept paper proposes an architecture that integrates agentic AI models with digital twin representations for each subsystem component in an RF transceiver. The digital twins, constructed using machine learning techniques such as deep learning and reinforcement learning, are paired with specialized, autonomous AI agent(s) capable of independent reasoning, planning, and adaptive action. Agentic AI models go beyond traditional rule-based/symbolic AI to autonomously select optimal strategies, synthesize data from diverse sources, and interact with both virtual and real-world environments. Ideas for simulations and experimental validations are proposed for future work.

title: Chain-of-Thought-Based Knowledge Extraction from Heterogeneous Infrastructure Database for Integrated Transportation Asset Management
authors: Mengtian Yin, Tao Huang, Sin Ming Wong, Pieter Pauwels, George Economides, Ioannis Brilakis
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: The fragmentation of infrastructure information systems has long been an obstacle to integrated transportation asset management (TAM). This paper presents a novel method for automatic knowledge extraction and ontology modelling from heterogeneous TAM databases using large language models (llMs). The method adopts a Chain-of-Thought framework to decompose the complex ontology modelling process into atomic tasks, harnessing the semantic understanding and reasoning capabilities of LLMs. As a result, class entities, class hierarchies, and relations are generated to construct an ontology model that supports semantic interoperability across diverse TAM systems. The method's performance was evaluated using four sets of TAM database schemas from UK road agencies. The results show that the overall recall rate for entity generation reaches 89.5% compared to the standard ontology. Furthermore, the accuracy rates for entity classification and relation classification are 82.1% and 75.6%, respectively, demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed LLM-based approach in addressing data fragmentation issues in transportation information systems.

title: LLMS as Virtual Traffic Police: Incident-Aware Traffic Signal Control Augmented by Large Language Models
authors: Qiqing Wang, Shiqi Wei, Kaidi Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Adaptive traffic signal control (TSC) has demonstrated strong effectiveness in managing dynamic traffic flows. However, conventional methods often struggle when unforeseen traffic incidents occur (e.g., accidents and road maintenance), which typically require labor-intensive and inefficient manual interventions by traffic police officers. Large Language Models (LLMs) appear to be a promising solution thanks to their remarkable reasoning and generalization capabilities. Nevertheless, existing works often propose to replace existing TSC systems with LLM-based systems, which can be (i) unreliable due to the inherent hallucinations of LLMs and (ii) costly due to the need for system replacement. To address the issues of existing works, we propose a hierarchical framework that augments existing TSC systems with LLMs. This framework leverages LLMs at the upper level to dynamically fine-tune selected parameters of signal controllers at the lower level in response to real-time traffic incidents. To enhance domain-specific reliability, we employ Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to draw knowledge from a tailored traffic language database that encompasses traffic conditions and controller operation principles. Our results show that LLMs can serve as trustworthy virtual traffic police officers that can adapt conventional TSC methods to traffic incidents with significantly improved operational efficiency and reliability.

title: A Complete Framework for Automatic Condition Evaluation of Trains' External Surface
authors: Claire Nicodeme, Borachhun You, Sovanleng LY
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Railway is a key pillar of both sustainability and transport decarbonization. Just as other industries, the railway sector is undergoing digital transformation. These new tools and innovative solutions are applied to reduce the ecological impact of rail transport even more and improve both its efficiency and effectiveness. Maintaining such complex systems requires a lot of resources, and intensive and cautious manual work. Computer Vision and AI may bring additional insight to improve and make the maintenance processes more robust. This paper presents a video analytics framework, to detect and identify a train, while applying condition monitoring algorithms to various elements. First, the method proposes to generate the train's panoramic image from event-based acquisition, giving a global view of the vehicle. Then logos, texts and liveries are analyzed to ensure regulatory conformity, at both European and national levels. The designed pipeline aims at faster and more reliable maintenance, while also improving defects detection. Results on real-world data demonstrate the effectiveness of visual elements recognition and defects detection.

title: An Ensemble Learning Approach for Multi-Class Intrusion Detection with Comparative Performance and Explainability Analysis
authors: Md. Shafiqul Islam, Md. Alamgir Hossain, Md. Samiul Islam
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Intrusion detection preserves network security by identifying attempts to breach or damage systems; however, legacy signature-based systems often miss novel, sophisticated attacks. Recent studies indicate that machine learning has the capability to identify threats that were previously undetected. In the conducted research, on the basis of ensemble methods we refine an intrusion detection system and test it using a publicly accessible dataset. We assess popular ensemble techniques, including Random Forest, Extra Trees, XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost, AdaBoost, and Gradient Boosting to determine which one performs best. To ensure interpretability for both practitioners and newcomers, we employ LIME to explain model predictions, revealing that combinations of protocol features, transport-layer statistics, and HTTP/DNS header fields drive the decisions. Across all tests, XGBoost consistently achieves accuracy rates exceeding 99%, coupled with low false positive rates and impressive outcomes in Precision, Recall, F1-score, Balanced Accuracy, and Cohen’s kappa. We present a clear, accurate, and explainable approach that enhances network defenses against evolving cyber threats.

title: Dual-Attention Enhanced Diffusion Model for Bearing Signals Denoising
authors: Weiwei Qian, Yuna Wang, Yuting Yang
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: In modern industrial systems, mechanical equipment operating under complex conditions generates vibration signals highly susceptible to noise interference. This significantly impedes fault feature extraction and poses substantial challenges for accurate fault diagnosis. This paper proposes a diffusion model incorporating a dual-attention module which integrating gated and Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) for bearing vibration signal denoising. Specifically, diffusion model with a dual-attention module employs two main branches to extract features from noisy input observations and latent variables respectively. Each branch comprises six convolutional blocks with LeakyReLU activation. A dual-attention module further processes features from the first branch before fusing them with the second branch. Additionally, a multi-stage reconstruction strategy enhances denoising performance. Experiments on the Case Western Reserve University (CWRU) bearing dataset demonstrate that Diffusion Model with a Dual-Attention module (DMDA) outperforms existing denoising methods. In summary, DMDA integrates dual self-attention into diffusion models to enhance temporal feature extraction for bearing vibration signal denoising.

title: Optimization Analysis of Interference Fit for Internal and external Gear Sleeves in Variable Gauge Wheelsets
authors: Chen Yand, Chen Yonghong
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Mechanical Engineering and Power Engineering (MEPE)
abstract: To overcome the constraints imposed by railway gauge differences in countries along the Belt and Road on international through transport, this study addresses the severe wear of the internal and external gear sleeve pair-a core component of the variable-gauge wheelset-resulting from frequent lateral sliding during gauge change operations. Research on interference fit optimization and dynamic mechanical characteristics is conducted. Based on GB/T 5371-2004, the minimum required and maximum allowable interference fits for the shaft-external sleeve and wheel-internal sleeve are calculated. The stress distribution under interference fit is analyzed via finite element simulation, and the interference values are optimized. Subsequently, a dynamic simulation model of the gauge change process is established to analyze the stress distribution of each component during gauge transition. The results indicate that drum modification of the external sleeve and chamfer modification of the internal sleeve significantly reduce root stress. After optimization of the interference fit for the internal and external sleeves, the maximum stresses are reduced to 621 MPa (at the middle of the tooth slot) and 811 MPa (at the wheel-end tooth slot), respectively. The smoothness of the gauge change process is not solely dependent on the fit of the internal and external sleeves but is also significantly influenced by the interference of the sliding bearing. A sliding bearing interference of 0.2 mm ensures a stable gauge transition. The research findings can provide references for the structural design of variable-gauge wheelsets.

title: Structural Shifts in Machinery: Analysis of Fast-Growing Organizations
authors: Oleg Dranko, Aleksander Rezchikov
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: Structural shifts in the economy in the context of macroeconomic instability determine the promising profile of technologies and products. This paper considers a model for identifying and forecasting fast-growing organizations using the mechanical engineering industry as an example. The threshold of average annual revenue growth of 50% in current prices is used as a criterion for identifying fast-growing organizations. Big data analysis methods are used to identify fast-growing organizations within the segment under consideration. 1.8 thousand fast-growing organizations in the Russian industry with revenues exceeding $\mathbf{1 0 0}$ million rubles have been identified. An assessment of their growth using a sigmoid (logistic curve) shows a significant growth potential of $\mathbf{1 5 0 \%}$. The parameters of the logistic curve are identified using the least squares method. The data source is open data from the electronic Government of Russia, primarily from the Russian Federal Tax Service.

title: Graphical Method for Designing Distributed Matching Network for Broadband GaN PAs
authors: Siddharth Thakur, Harshvardhan Singh, Shubham Sharma, Nagaditya Poluri, Avinash Lahgere, Yogesh Singh Chauhan, Sandip Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a graphical approach to design a distributed series matching network (DSMN). The proposed approach extends the conventional Smith Chart-based matching techniques that target a single spot frequency. DSMN iteratively transforms frequency-dependent complex impedances across the entire target band through reactance cancellation at band edges and impedance rotation, visually guided by the Smith Chart. This approach is validated by the design of a 6 W broadband power amplifier (PA) using CGH40006P GaN HEMT operating across 2.1-2.7 GHz (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5 \%}$</tex> fractional bandwidth). The PA achieves more than 60% power-added efficiency (PAE), 15.3-18.9 dB gain, 38.5 dBm output power, and input/output return losses better than 5 dB across the 600 MHz bandwidth. The design methodology establishes a new paradigm for broadband matching, well suited for modern wireless communication systems demanding consistent performance over wide frequency ranges.

title: A Hybrid Machine Learning Framework for Early Detection of Mental Health Conditions of University Students
authors: Nupur Alam, Washifa Akter, Fatema-Tuz-Zahura Anannya, Md. Nazmul Abdal
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: The urgent need in scalable and describable mental health diagnostic solutions has been stimulated by increasing stress levels, anxiety and depression among university students. This learning basically presents a hybrid machine learning model that can be used in early detection, monitoring and diagnosis of mental health issues among students based on ground truth self reporting questionnaires. Preprocessing a dataset Containing 2028 university students was done using k-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) to fill the missing values. SMOTE-ENN was applied for balancing class imbalances. For dimensionality reduction Principal component analysis (PCA) was used. Classifiers that were used to train and evaluate the system included Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Multilayer Perceptron (MLP), the ensemble methods (Stacking and Voting) got the highest results. The stacking model provide the high values of accuracy in classification as 100, 99.69 and 99.18 percent in anxiety, depression and stress, respectively, phenomenal current standards. This is an explainable and scale-up model, which clear the way for early intervention in mental health and provide solid guidance to instructions and mental health professionals.

title: Compact, Planar and Conformal Antenna Array Solution for Electronic Warfare Applications
authors: Urvi Joshi, Sanket Kalamkar, Rajesh K. Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This work presents the design and development of a compact, wideband, and conformal antenna and its array tailored for EW application in next-generation missile and unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) platforms. A tapered slot (Vivaldi-based) antenna architecture is employed to achieve wideband performance across the frequency range of 3-18 GHz, supporting high-resolution angle-of-arrival (AOA) detection and precise direction finding. To accommodate modern aerodynamic constraints, the array is designed on thin, flexible substrates such as Rogers laminates, allowing seamless integration onto curved surfaces, including hemispherical domes and UAV bodies. The conformal array configuration ensures mechanical adaptability across diverse platforms while preserving electromagnetic performance.

title: On Optimizing Maritime Routes in Particularly Sensitive Sea Areas
authors: Angela Di Febbraro, Davide Giglio, Stefano Monfroni, Roberto Ronco
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: The problems related to ship routing have attracted the interest of many researchers and practitioners for decades. The interest has always been renewed by both changes in regulations and laws, and technological advances. In the last years, several efforts have been devoted towards the preservation of marine species, which, combined with existing protocols on emission reductions, are prospected to provide effective support towards the defense of marine ecosystems and ocean integrity. In this paper, we investigate the problem of determining maritime routes for a fleet of ships through Particularly Sensitive Sea Areas (PSSAs), which are designated by the International Maritime Organization (IMO) in alignment with UN Sustainable Development Goal 14, aiming at protecting selected sea regions. A PSSA-constrained maritime routing problem is here proposed, and a Mixed-Integer Linear Programming formulation to solve it is provided. The application to a case study relevant to the Mediterranean Sea, which encompasses two of the 18 PSSA currently established in the world, is described and discussed.

title: MetaLLMetro: LLM-Centric Virtual Reality Navigation Platform for Metro Stations
authors: Yongjie Fu, Bowen Fang, Mengxuan Liu, Xuan Di
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This paper introduces MetaLLMetro, a Virtual Reality (VR) subway station system that integrates Large Language Models (LLMs) to enhance user navigation and realtime traffic information acquisition. Navigating underground subway stations remains challenging, particularly when the stations are complex and difficult to locate the correct directions or platforms. To overcome these limitations, we propose a VR platform where a fine-tuned LLM interprets users' natural language requests, accurately infers their intended destinations, and dynamically generates navigation paths within a virtual 3D subway environment. By fine-tuning the LLM on transit-specific tasks and queries, MetaLLMetro delivers a more accurate and intuitive navigation experience, effectively bridging the gap between ambiguous user input and precise, actionable spatial directions. Furthermore, our system leverages LLMdriven queries to interface with the Google Maps API, offering users a routing assistant that provides real-time geographic context and step-by-step navigation guidance.

title: Graphene-Based Conformal Antenna for Secure and Sustainable Military Wearable Communications in the X-Band
authors: Manya K J, Yashita Varsha K, Manthan B T, Hruthik T, Suganthi J
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: Wearable military communication systems demand antennas that are lightweight, low-profile, flexible, and resilient under dynamic operating conditions. This work presents the design and comprehensive evaluation of a graphene–PDMS (poly-dimethylsiloxane) conformal microstrip patch antenna optimized for X-band (8–12 GHz) operation. The antenna employs an E-slot-loaded patch to enhance bandwidth and impedance matching while maintaining mechanical flexibility. To validate conformal performance, the antenna was subjected to progressive bending angles (30°, 45°, 60°, and 90°), emulating realistic on-body curvatures. Across all deformation states, the antenna demonstrates excellent impedance stability (S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf> < −15 dB, VSWR ≈ 1.1) and consistent radiation performance (gain ≈ 9–9.9 dBi, beamwidth ≈ 60°). These results confirm minimal frequency detuning and negligible performance degradation under bending. Owing to its lightweight, corrosion-resistant, and sustainable graphene–PDMS composition, the antenna offers a secure and environmentally responsible solution for next-generation wearable military and 6G tactical communication platforms.

title: Bandwidth Redistribution Algorithm Between Traditional and Multi-ONUs Customers for Improving the QoSs of PON System
authors: Mehraba Manarat, Monir Hossen
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Passive Optical Networks (PONs) can serve a broad range of users, from residential and business customers to mobile network operators. Each customer may be allocated one or multiple ONUs, with ONUs often developing unbalanced traffic loads, leading to the underutilization of some ONUs while others are congested. The Interleaved Polling with Adaptive Cycle Time with Multi-ONU Service Level Agreements (SLAs) support (MOS-IPACT) algorithm allows unused bandwidth to be redistributed among all the ONUs that belong to the same customer, thereby enhancing overall resource utilization. This paper enhances the performance of MOS-IPACT by proposing an analytical model for estimating the idle bandwidth duration and evaluates its performance in a PON system with both traditional and multi-ONU customers. Simulation results show that the proposed approach significantly improves bandwidth efficiency and fairness, resulting in better resource utilization while providing consistent service quality for all users.

title: Wireless Power Transfer for Polarization-Insensitive Battery-Less IoT Beacon
authors: Vikas Kumar Malav, Viren Sharma, Ashwani Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a microwave-based Wireless Power Transfer (WPT) technique to power polarizationinsensitive and ultra-low-power battery-less Internet of Things Beacon (IoTB). The IoTB includes a compact square patch rectenna with an isosceles triangular-shaped ground, a Power Management Unit (PMU), a Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) sensor node and a BLE-bridge gateway. The square patch rectenna integrates four Schottky Diodes (SDs) in a conjugately matched manner to achieve maximum power transfer and a two-parallel DC combining network, which is placed perpendicular to each other for achieving full-wave rectification of the incoming Radio Frequency (RF) signals at any orientation. Therefore, the IoTB achieves polarization-insensitivity at any orientation of the incoming wave. The harvested power is delivered to the PMU via rectenna, where a dedicated RF energy transmitter operating in the range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5-5.2 \text{GHz}$</tex> with the transmit RF power of 25 dBm. The IoTB is a well-suited design for deployments of IoT applications that require batteryless and polarization-insensitive features.

title: Camera-LiDAR Traffic Light Relevance Estimation as Graph-Matching for Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Ottavia Belotti, Alessandro De Luca, Riccardo Pieroni, Matteo Corno, Sergio M. Savaresi
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of identifying which traffic light in an intersection regulates the motion of the ego vehicle. We propose a modular pipeline that combines standard traffic light recognition with a novel relevance estimation module based on human-like logic. The core contribution is a graph-based reasoning method that encodes semantic cues from camera images and geometric information from LiDAR into a structured scene graph. This graph is matched against an offline topological map using inexact graph matching to determine the most relevant traffic light. We evaluate our approach on a proprietary dataset collected on Italian roads, demonstrating promising performance in identifying relevant signals and ensuring appropriate behavior at intersections. Our results highlight the potential of the proposed method as a reliable and efficient component for traffic light understanding in autonomous driving systems.

title: Multi-Objective Deep Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic Algorithm Selection in Open RAN
authors: Saish Urumkar, Byrav Ramamurthy, Sachin Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: Open Radio Access Networks (Open RAN) provide flexible, modular multi-vendor interoperability. Growing mobile data demand requires balancing network performance with power efficiency. Mobile operators need intelligent resource management to achieve Key Performance Indicator (KPI) targets while maintaining operational efficiency. This paper proposes a solution using a multi-objective deep reinforcement learning (MODRL) model deployed on the Open RAN Intelligent Controller (RIC). Three customizable operator profiles (Power Saving, Balanced, and Performance) are used which define specific priority ratios between performance and power saving objectives. To evaluate, individual algorithms (CPU scheduling and UE connection state switching) are implemented in Open RAN, achieving 5–20%CPU power savings with bounded throughput degradation observed during high-traffic scenarios when multiple User Equipments (UEs) are connected. The MODRL model is tested for successful selection between two algorithms across three operator profiles for specific test scenarios. Performance validation of throughput and power savings using MODRL for algorithm selection in real-time network conditions with additional test scenarios remain part of future work.

title: SEPose: A Synthetic Event-Based Human Pose Estimation Dataset for Pedestrian Monitoring
authors: Kaustav Chanda, Aayush Atul Verma, Arpitsinh Vaghela, Yezhou Yang, Bharatesh Chakravarthi
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Event-based sensors have emerged as a promising solution for addressing challenging conditions in pedestrian and traffic monitoring systems. Their low-latency and high dynamic range allow for improved response time in safetycritical situations caused by distracted walking or other unusual movements. However, the availability of data covering such scenarios remains limited. To address this gap, we present SEPose - a comprehensive synthetic event-based human pose estimation dataset for fixed pedestrian perception generated using dynamic vision sensors in the CARLA simulator. With nearly 350 K annotated pedestrians with body pose keypoints from the perspective of fixed traffic cameras, SEPose is a comprehensive synthetic multi-person pose estimation dataset that spans busy and light crowds and traffic across diverse lighting and weather conditions in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4}$</tex>-way intersections in urban, suburban, and rural environments. We train existing state-of-the-art models such as RVT and YOLOv8 on our dataset and evaluate them on real event-based data to demonstrate the sim-to-real generalization capabilities of the proposed dataset.

title: ForeCAST-GAT: Context-Aware Spatio-Temporal Graph Attention Transformer for Event-Driven Traffic Forecasting
authors: Sifatul Mostafi, Khalid Elgazzar, Tamer Mohamed Abdellatif
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Accurate traffic forecasting is critical for intelligent transportation systems to effectively manage disruptions on highways caused by accidents, lane closures, and severe weather events. While traditional traffic forecasting models primarily rely on historical traffic data, they often fail to account for the sudden impacts of such events. To address this gap, we introduce ForeCAST-GAT, a Context-Aware Spatio-Temporal Graph Attention Transformer for event-driven traffic forecasting. By embedding both spatio-temporal dependencies and event-specific contextual features into each highway segment's node representation, ForeCAST-GAT dynamically adapts to changing traffic patterns during events. The model leverages Graph Attention Networks (GATs) to capture spatial relationships among highway segments and utilizes transformerbased mechanisms to model temporal dynamics, enabling it to account for both inherent traffic speed patterns and external disruptions. This integration improves the model's forecasting accuracy during event-driven scenarios. Evaluations conducted on real-world traffic and event data collected from Highway 401 reveal that ForeCAST-GAT achieves up to a 35.9 % reduction in MAE and a 20.2% reduction in RMSE compared to existing models that do not consider event-specific features.

title: Coordinated Trajectory Planning for High-Speed Train Group Operation Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Zixu Zhao, Jidong Lv, Zhengwei Luo, Jiahui Lyu, Ming Chai, Hongjie Liu, Junqiang Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: The high-speed train group (HST-G) is one of the emerging modes to improve the transportation efficiency of high-speed railways. Coordinated trajectory planning is of a great challenge for HST-G considering multi-objective optimization. This paper proposes a multi-objective coordinated trajectory planning method based on deep reinforcement learning algorithm. Firstly, the dynamics model of HST-G is established, in which multiple optimization objectives including operational synchronization and headway are considered. Secondly, by capturing the state of group trains as the state variables, the coordinated trajectory planning problem is modeled as a MDP. And then the PPO algorithm for HST-G trajectory planning is delivered featuring the continuity of control action space. Finally, the global offline and online adjustment collaborative planning simulation of trains is carried out based on real line data. The experimental results show that the PPO-based coordinated trajectory planning improves line capacity utilization compared to traditional mode. The PPO algorithm outperforms the GA algorithm in planning efficiency and group trains punctuality, while also achieving a better balance among running synchronization, ride quality, and punctuality.

title: Terahertz Explorer for Atmosphere of Mars (TEAM) Payload for Martian Atmospheric Sounding
authors: Prantik Chakraborty, Mahendra P.S. Bhadoria, Ch. V. Narasimha Rao, Nilesh M. Desai
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents the system design aspects of Terahertz Explorer for Atmosphere of Mars (TEAM) payload for the martian atmospheric sounding. TEAM payload is configured as a rover-based upward looking, compact and self-calibrating instrument using hyperspectral sounding technique. It uses absorption lines of Carbon Monoxide (CO), Water Vapor <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)$</tex> and Oxygen <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathrm{O}_{2}\right)$</tex> at 461.04GHz, 474.69GHz and 487.25GHz to generate vertical profile of temperature, water vapor and oxygen for martian atmosphere respectively. TEAM payload can provide the localized simultaneous measurements in presence of dust storms, which deteriorates the performance of sensors (on rovers, landers, satellites, earth based telescopes, etc.) previously deployed for martian study, especially at lower altitudes <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(<5 ~\text{km})$</tex>. It is designed to cater to all three sounding bands using single IF Backend channel and tunable frequency synthesizer, providing hyperspectral observations, with 1MHz spectral resolution and 0.1K radiometric sensitivity over these bands. TEAM payload is shortlisted for final consideration of payloads for Mars Lander Mission (MLM) - Rover Mission.

title: Integrated UAV Performance Across Antenna Placement and Virtual Flight Test Analysis
authors: Anudeep Bellary, R Akhil Karthik, Srivani Kothidigi
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This study investigates the optimal placement of S-band and X-band antennas on a UAV platform to ensure effective radar performance and coverage. This research involves a comprehensive analysis of antenna radiation patterns, platform-induced distortions, and inter-band interference study using Altair Feko. A virtual flight test was conducted using Altair WRAP to evaluate the real-time coverage performance across various flight maneuvers. The results demonstrate that strategic antenna placement significantly enhances both coverage and radar performance, especially at higher frequencies where beam directivity is more critical. This study provides practical insights into integrating multi-band radar systems on aircraft platforms, offering a framework for further experimental validations and development of adaptive antenna configuration strategies for advanced airborne sensing missions.

title: The Early Fault Intelligent Diagnosis Method of EHA Based on Hypergraph Attention Network
authors: Yibo Song, Shuanglu Li, Chun Zhao, Zhan Zhang, Xiansong He, Xiaoli Zhao, Jianyong Yao, Chuanjie Lu
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: To address the limitations of current deep learning-based fault diagnosis methods in effectively identifying early-stage faults in hydraulic systems, this paper proposes an early fault diagnosis method for Electro-Hydrostatic Actuators (EHA) based on a Hypergraph Attention Network. The core of the proposed AEHGAT algorithm consists of a novel hypergraph attention convolution layer, termed AEHGATLayer. First, experimental data generated from an EHA fault simulation platform are used to construct a hypergraph structure. AEHGATLayer is then employed to extract global relational features from the hypergraph. These global features are subsequently fed into a multi-head self-attention classification layer to extract local features, ultimately yielding the diagnostic results. Comparative experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm and diagnostic framework, achieving an average diagnostic accuracy exceeding 90%.

title: MS-VLMDet: Multi-Scale Feature Enhanced Vision-Language Model for Pedestrian Detection
authors: Zekai Dai, Xingyuan Dai, Yisheng Lv, Xin Pei, Xu Wang, Xiaoyan Gong, Yu-Liang Liu, Wuling Huang
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Pedestrian detection, as a critical component of Intelligent Transportation Systems, encounters numerous challenges. Pedestrians are often situated against complex backgrounds, appear as small targets in images, and adopt diverse poses, all of which make detection challenging. This research proposes MS-VLMDet (Multi-Scale Feature-Enhanced Vision-Language Model for Pedestrian Detection), which fuses multi-scale features by integrating multi-level visual representations from a Feature Pyramid Network into large vision-language models to overcome the models' limitations in precisely localizing small objects. MS-VLMDet contains three key modules: a multi-scale feature extraction module that captures pedestrian features at different resolutions; a feature fusion module that integrates the extracted features with the original image and text prompts; and a feature enhanced vision-language model inference module that uses the fused features to guide the model's attention toward pedestrian regions. Experimental results demonstrate that MS-VLMDet outperforms existing deep learning and vision-language models in small-target pedestrian detection across various complex traffic scenarios, achieving an F1 score improvement of 4.6 times over the original Qwen2.5-VL model on the CityPersons dataset.

title: CropNet-XAI: An Explainable 1D-CNN Framework for Transparent Crop Yield Prediction
authors: Abdullah Al Mahmud Joy, Md. Faizer Islam, Md. Sunzid Islam, Shakil Ahmed, Mirza Niaz Morshed, Humayra Ahmed
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: One predictor of food security and sustainable agriculture is crop yields. Nevertheless, despite their accuracy, traditional machine learning techniques frequently lack transparency, which restricts their uptake by non-expert stakeholders. This paper presents XAI-Yield, an interpretable deep learning framework that combines a one-dimensional convolutional neural network (1D-CNN, also known as CropNet) with Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) processes (SHAP, LIME). For performance comparison, several models were benchmarked, including deep CNN architectures (VGG16 and VGG19), RF, DT, XGBoost, and SVM. CropNet outperformed conventional machine learning techniques by up to 18.2%, achieving the highest test accuracy of 93.46%. The strongest positive yield predictors were Fertilizer_Used_True (0.2074) and Rainfall_mm (0.1946), while Irrigation_Used_True (-0.1649) had the opposite effect.XAI Yield connects data-driven modeling with real-world agricultural decision-making by combining high predictive accuracy with transparent interpretability to provide farmers and policymakers with actionable insights.

title: Autonomous Vehicle Intersection Management through Speed Optimization and Finite State Machine-Based Decision Making
authors: Chiara Merolla, Simone Specchia, Andrea Galimberti, Matteo Corno, Giulio Panzani, Sergio Savaresi
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: In an autonomous driving system, intersection management consists in determining the sequence of actions allowing the vehicle to cross an intersection safely. This paper presents an intersection management strategy for urban environments, integrating decision-making and speed planning. The proposed algorithm features a decision-making module that determines the vehicle behavior based on traffic rules while ensuring collision avoidance. A comfort-oriented speed optimization module generates a speed profile that aligns with the high-level decision. The approach is validated through real-world testing on a fully autonomous vehicle operating on public roads open to traffic. Experimental results indicate that the proposed method achieves a driving style comparable to that of human drivers in terms of comfort. Moreover, the system exhibits a more cautious and safety-oriented behavior when navigating intersections, enhancing overall reliability in complex traffic scenarios.

title: Remaining Useful Life Prediction for Linear Actuator Systems Based on the Robust Student's t-Based Kalman Filter
authors: Zihan Zhang, Song Xue, Peiyuan Lian, Yu Cu, Qian Xu, Congsi Wang
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Actuators serve as critical driving components in various mechanical equipment, and their failure may compromise the normal operation of entire machinery. Consequently, state monitoring and fault diagnosis of actuators have become a research focus across multiple domains. Addressing the limitations of traditional single degradation indicators, such as insufficient information content and the difficulty in predicting Remaining Useful Life (RUL) under limited data samples, this paper proposes an RUL prediction method based on Principal Component Analysis (PCA) feature fusion technology and the Robust Student's t-Based Kalman Filter (RSKF). Monotonicity analysis was employed to screen 14 original features. For the selected features, PCA-based feature fusion was utilized to derive a comprehensive degradation indicator for the actuator. The RSKF was then applied to update the parameters of a Random Effects Exponential Degradation Model (REEDM) for RUL prediction. Experiments were conducted using both a public dataset and a dataset collected from a custom-built actuator drive system test platform. The results demonstrate that compared to the standard Kalman Filter, the RSKF-based approach reduced the average relative error in RUL prediction from 10.1% to 2.1%. These findings validate the effectiveness of the proposed method for predicting the RUL of actuator drive systems under conditions of limited data samples.

title: TuberFusion: An Ensemble Deep Learning Model for Automated Tuberculosis Detection from Chest X-Ray Images
authors: Arafat Zaman, Ahmed Abrar Jawad, Tahmidul Haque, Md Sakib, Nesayem Siddiquee
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Chest diseases such as tuberculosis are health risks to human beings worldwide and lead to avoidable morbidity and mortality. Apart from reducing the quality of life, it also poses a gigantic burden on the healthcare system. Early and accurate diagnosis is the key to successful management, but it is dependent on the presence of trained radiologists and high-technology diagnostic machines, which are not universally available and certainly not in low-resource settings. Chest X-rays (CXR) remain a frequent, minimal invasive, and inexpensive diagnostic test for the diagnosis of thoracic disease. They are labor-intensive and human-error-sensitive to read, however. Artificial intelligence (AI) technologies through deep learning (DL) have demonstrated spectacular potential to enhance diagnostic quality and rate. Deep Learning algorithms are able to filter large CXR volumes of data in an attempt to enable radiologists to provide quick and correct diagnoses. Furthermore, some models have the capability to forecast long-term health risk, expanding their use in healthcare. This research strives to develop and implement deep learning algorithms automatically to identify tuberculosis from CXR images to alleviate global healthcare issues, in which specialized medical professionals may be out of reach. This research involves dataset acquisition from hospitals and training a number of deep learning models to identify tuberculosis. By comparing the performances of the models, the research will select the most accurate and effective approach to improving diagnostic performance. Integration of AI tools in medical imaging has the potential to transform detection and disease management worldwide and result in improved public health as well as quality access to care. In this study, we will recommend TuberFusion, which is an ensemble deep learning model that is autonomous in identifying tuberculosis from chest X-ray images. We start by comparing four renowned deep learning models such as DenseNet, InceptionV3, Xception, and VGG19, to a curated TB dataset. InceptionV3 and Xception were the best models which were fine-tuned to improve feature extraction on the radiographic features of TB. This was followed by the implementation of a soft probability averaging ensemble, which aimed to combine the strengths of the two models, taking advantage of the complementary feature representation. Experimental evidence shows that the ensemble model has a higher classification of 99.7%, which is higher than the classification results of the individual fine-tuned models. These results emphasize how ensemble deep learning systems can be used to assist in the efficient screening of TB and minimize diagnostic load in clinical care, especially in conditions with low radiological knowledge.

title: Results with a Hybrid FE-BI Solver for Coaxial Cable Coupling Analysis
authors: William R. Dommisse, Matthys M. Botha, Thomas Rylander, Jan Carlsson
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: Undesirable electromagnetic (EM) field coupling into shielded cables is an important consideration when assessing electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) of high-frequency EM engineering systems. To mitigate such effects, the capability to accurately model cable coupling is vital. This paper considers applying the full-wave hybrid finite element-boundary integral (FE-BI) method to coaxial cable coupling analysis.
