This paper collects and analyzes the full-lifecycle data of all optical modules in a real intelligent computing network environment, and uses LSTM and Transformer to construct prediction models for forecasting the remaining useful life of optical modules. This provides a technical solution for the intelligent operation and maintenance of optical modules in intelligent computing networks.

title: MIMO Isolation Enhancement in Dome-Shaped Patch Antenna Using U-Shaped Metamaterial Absorber and Partial Ground Technique
authors: Showrajit Saha, Md. Motahar Hossain, Debprosad Das, Md. Azad Hossain
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: This study proposes a dome-shaped patch antenna incorporating a U-shaped meta-material absorber (MMA) to enhance isolation in MIMO antenna systems. The antenna is designed on a lossy FR4 substrate (ε<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">r</inf> = 4.3) with overall dimensions of 2.21λ × 2.21λ × 0.085λ (≈ λ at 5.8 GHz). The excitation of the antenna is achieved through a waveguide port, while the integrated U-shaped metamaterial absorber (MMA) to improve isolation between the elements. Excellent performance is observed in the simulation results, with a reflection coefficient of –45.99 dB at 5.80 GHz and a minimum isolation of –28.08 dB, demonstrating improved decoupling efficiency over conventional antenna designs. The antenna also delivers a peak gain of 4.44 dBi and a total active reflection coefficient (TARC) of –38 dB at 180° excitation, confirming better port mutual coupling. Moreover, it exhibits outstanding diversity performance, with a diversity gain (DG) of 10 dB, an envelope correlation coefficient (ECC) below 0.000045, and a channel capacity loss (CCL) as low as 0.003 bits/s/Hz. Owing to its compact size, stable radiation behavior, and high isolation, the proposed design is regarded as a promising candidate for next-generation 5G sub-6 GHz wireless communication systems.

title: Design of a Slot-Loaded Microstrip Antenna for Telemetry in Hypersonic Boost Systems
authors: Chethanrama L, V. Srinivasa Rao, Harsh Sanjay Kanoje
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: A high-performance, compact and robust S-band microstrip patch antenna designed for high-speed airborne telemetry in S-band, fabricated on PTFE based laminate with dielectric constant of 2.33. The antenna introduces a rectangular slot offset above the coaxial probe feed to enhance impedance bandwidth by more than 100MHz and giving maximum gain of 6.6 dBi, achieving operational requirement. Unlike conventional substrate-only designs, the antenna includes full packaging with housing and a mounted radome structure, ensuring thermal, and environmental resilience under hypersonic boost flight conditions.

title: High-Contrast Subsurface Nearfield Microwave Imaging for Anomaly Detection Using Metasurface-Loaded Patch Antenna
authors: Soumya Chakravarty, Anwesha Khasnobish, M. Jaleel Akhtar
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: A novel near-field microwave imaging technique for high-contrast detection of subsurface biomedical anomalies using the single-port reflection measurements is presented. The proposed imaging scheme utilizes a metasurface integrated 3.5 GHz patch antenna in the near field of the target to measure the reflection coefficient. It is demonstrated that optimizing the standoff distance between the antenna and target in the near field significantly improves the image clarity, enabling effective discrimination between water inclusion and the surrounding sand. Experimental validation using a raster scan over an area of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$200 \text{mm} \times 200 \text{mm}$</tex>, where an anomaly in the form of a waterfilled container representing a biological tissue is embedded inside sand, confirms that the optimized standoff distance paired with the metasurface loading of the patch antenna substantially boosts the detection performance. Most importantly, optimizing the standoff distance between the antenna structure and the target provides greater contrast for imaging without significant pre-processing of data such as denoising. Future work will explore electronically-reconfigurable metasurface arrays to capture richer subsurface detail and further refine imaging resolution.

title: Low Mutual Coupling SIW Cavity-Backed MIMO Antenna for Ka-Band 5G Applications
authors: Nidhi Misra, Abhay Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper proposes a cavity-backed SIW antenna element that is placed orthogonally in the Ka band and can be used in multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) applications. An antenna element is fed by coaxial feed and is giving a very good isolation of more than 24 dB among the adjacent antenna elements and around 30.74 dB among the diagonal elements. The gain of the antenna is 7.08 dBi with the single element and 8.64 dBi with the MIMO structure. ECC achieved is giving very good isolation of 0 around the operating bandwidth. The results are simulated in HFSS v2023.

title: Extremely Low-Profile Multi-Band Monopole Antenna for IoT Applications
authors: Anvesh Pandey, Yogesh Mishra, Kunwar Rananjay Singh, Rupam Bharati, Arun Kumar Saurabh, Manoj Kumar Meshram
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a compact half-monopole antenna designed for Internet of Things (IoT) and nextgeneration wireless communication systems. The antenna is exceptionally low-profile, with a compact footprint of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.22 \lambda_{\mathrm{g}} \times 0.27 \lambda_{\mathrm{g}}$</tex>, where <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\lambda_{\mathrm{g}}$</tex> is the guided wavelength at the lowest operating frequency of 3.3 GHz. Its design integrates a semi-circular radiator with precisely tuned arc-shaped parasitic stubs to excite multiple resonances, enabling extensive multi-band performance. The impedance bandwidth is further enhanced by a rectangular ground plane featuring a central coupling stub. Full-wave simulations validate a reflection coefficient <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\left\vert\mathbf{S}_{11}\right\vert\right)$</tex> below −10 dB across five key bands: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.3-3.49 \text{GHz}$</tex> (sub- 6 GHz <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 G}$</tex>), <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7.14-9.15 \text{GHz}$</tex> (X-band), <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10-14.25 \text{GHz}$</tex> (Ku-band), 16.7218.95 GHz, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$22.55-30 \text{GHz}$</tex> (Ka-band). The antenna exhibits stable omnidirectional radiation characteristics and achieves a total radiation efficiency of up to 95% across its operating frequency range. Its compact size, wideband operation, and high efficiency make it a suitable candidate for applications in IoT, 5 G NR, and forthcoming wireless systems.

title: A Deep Reinforcement Learning Method with Generative Adversarial Network for Imbalanced Bearing Fault Diagnosis
authors: Yuxuan Wang, Tianci Guo, Penghui Shang, Yicheng Xiao, Jianxiao Zou
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Bearing fault diagnosis plays a crucial role in ensuring production safety. However, existing methods often encounter significant challenges when dealing with class imbalance problems. At present, one promising strategy to address this issue is to combine data augmentation techniques with reinforcement learning algorithms. In this paper, a fault diagnosis framework named CWGAN-GP-D3QN is proposed. While introducing generative adversarial network with Wasserstein distance and gradient penalty (CWGAN-GP) to enhance the data, Double Dueling Deep Q-Network(D3QN) is applied to improve the problem of overestimating Q-value, so that the value of actions can be estimated accurately, which guarantees the precise determination of fault classes. Experiments conducted on the CWRU Bearing Dataset and the Xi'an Jiaotong University SQ Bearing Dataset demonstrate that CWGAN-GP-D3QN achieves superior performance in terms of effectiveness, robustness, and generalization ability, outperforming current common methods.

title: Aircraft Structural Strength Verification Based on Cyber-Physical Fusion
authors: Simeng Ding, Yangming Guo, Yan Kang, Xiaodong Wang, Qiang Min
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: With the continuous development of aviation technology, the complexity of aircraft structures continues to increase, and higher requirements are placed on their strength test verification. Traditional test verification methods can no longer meet the requirements of new aircraft structures for high precision, real-time performance, and data fusion. This paper studies the application of cyber-physical fusion technology in aircraft structural strength testing, focusing on key technologies such as PINN-based virtual model calibration and improvement, and multi-source heterogeneous data fusion. Through these methods, stress field reconstruction, sensor data fusion noise reduction, and dynamic response display under limited measurement point conditions are achieved, thereby significantly improving the accuracy and efficiency of structural strength verification. The research results provide new technical support for structural reliability assessment, early fault diagnosis, and health management, and contribute to proactive maintenance and life cycle management.

title: Multi Physics Thermal Simulation and Passage Optimization of a Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor
authors: Yongzhe Liu, Cheng Liu, Xiaoyu Xie
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Mechanical Engineering and Power Engineering (MEPE)
abstract: A new method for multi physics field coupling analysis based on genetic algorithm and applicable to the structural optimization of permanent magnet synchronous motors is proposed in order to solve the problem of existing motor design methods only focusing on a single physical field and low simulation accuracy. Established an electromagnetic field model for a certain permanent magnet synchronous motor operating at the speed of 3000 r/min and the peak driving current of 800 A, and used the simulated electromagnetic losses as boundary conditions for the model. Conducted parameterized 3D model for the permanent magnet synchronous motor, generated simulation input data based on the optimal space filling design, and simulated the fluid field and temperature field. After obtaining the simulation results, response surfaces for three optimization indicators were established, and MISQP algorithm was used to optimize the passage size, obtaining the optimal channel width 38.08 mm and channel height 11.99 mm. The results indicate that the principle of this method is correct and can select the optimal passage size design within the specified range.

title: Single-Cell Multiomics Data Reconstruction and Natural Grouping: An Unsupervised Approach
authors: Md Shaharia Hossen, Md. Motaharul Islam
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: This research demonstrates single-cell multi-omics data’s noise reduction and its prediction of natural grouping. Instead of bulk cell analysis, the single-cell multi-omics data is usable for complex biological predictions. The real data may have limitations, which are the actual labels of the dataset. The noisy biological data hindered the actual prediction of biological insights. To address these issues, this research proposed an unsupervised tailored model for noise cancellation of omics data and its natural grouping, on BRCA, TCGA, and ROSMAP data. The omics and concatenated multi-omics data have been trained using a denoising autoencoder to reconstruct those, and K-means clustering integrated with SMOTE has been used to cluster those data. For the selection of k, the elbow mechanism has been chosen. Initially, a k-means clustering has been used to get the pseudo-labels and then resample those using SMOTE for balanced cluster formation, finally re-cluster those data using K-means. The proposed method will help to reconstruct real noisy data without a label, with minimal losses, approximately 0.05%, and for predicting well-separated cluster formations. This research utilizes deep learning and machine learning models for unlabeled multi-omics data, which is crucial when the data lacks labels.

title: High-Sensitivity Terahertz Metamaterial Biosensor Using Connected Concentric Circles for Early Stage Cancer Detection
authors: Shrinjoy Chatterjee, Reshma Ramesh, Anamika A Kamath, Gobinda Sen, Chinmoy Saha
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: A novel and highly efficient terahertz metamaterial sensor has been engineered for early-stage cancer detection, leveraging a configuration made from connected concentric circles fabricated from graphene. This resonant structure is integrated onto a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SiO}_{2}$</tex> dielectric substrate layered above a gold ground plane. Electromagnetic simulation results reveal a distinct resonance dip at 0.976 THz for blood samples with a refractive index of 1.36. In the presence of analytes with higher refractive indices such as leukemia cells <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(n=1.39)$</tex>, the resonant frequency shifts. The sensor demonstrates exceptional refractive index sensitivity, measured between 800 and 1200 GHz/RIU, based on the full-width at half-maximum (FWHM) of the resonance response. With its compact footprint, label-free sensing capability, and high sensitivity, the proposed design holds strong promise for seamless integration into next generation biomedical diagnostic platforms.

title: Vision-based Wear Detection Method for Elevator Traction Steel Belts
authors: Yimin Wei, Dong Lei, Xiaoli Leng, Shaokang Li, Shujian Chen
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Elevator traction steel belts are vulnerable to surface wear, which is difficult to assess and poses risks to passenger safety. Current detection relies on periodic maintenance and lacks real-time, quantitative methods. This paper presents a vision-based approach for diagnosing wear severity. A finite element model of the belt–sheave contact is developed in Abaqus, revealing stress concentrations at internal steel-wire strand locations. These high-stress zones align with the direction of belt motion and match the observed bright stripes in worn surface images. To evaluate wear, we introduce a non-contact method based on grayscale image analysis. Surface images are converted to gray-level matrices, and the average gray values along belt width are used to form a grayscale curve. Two indicators are defined: overall mean grayscale for global wear and peak–valley difference (ΔGray) for local wear. Asymmetric baselines in the grayscale curve indicate uneven wear. Experiments show a 92% accuracy in detecting such faults. This study reveals a stress–wear–image relationship and offers a dual-indicator evaluation framework suitable for online monitoring.

title: AI Recommendation Systems for Lane-Changing Using Adherence-Aware Reinforcement Learning
authors: Weihao Sun, Heeseung Bang, Andreas A. Malikopoulos
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: In this paper, we present an adherence-aware reinforcement learning (RL) approach aimed at seeking optimal lane-changing recommendations within a semi-autonomous driving environment to enhance a single vehicle's travel efficiency. The problem is framed within a Markov decision process setting and is addressed through an adherence-aware deep <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Q$</tex> network, which takes into account the partial compliance of human drivers with the recommended actions. This approach is evaluated within CARLA's driving environment under realistic scenarios.

title: Design and Realization of 91.65 GHz LO Module with Integrated Frequency Multipliers for Spaceborne Humidity Sounder
authors: Ravi Khatri, Piyush Sinha, Mukeshkumar K. Patel, Ch. V. N. Rao
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents the design, integration, and characterization of a compact 91.65 GHz Local Oscillator (LO) module developed for the humidity sounding unit onboard the EOS-07 satellites. The LO is used to drive a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${183} {\pm} {15.75}$</tex> GHz sub-harmonic mixer for passive atmospheric water vapor sounding. Starting from a 15.275 GHz synthesizer signal, the module employs a cascaded X2 and X3 frequency multiplication scheme using four MMICs to generate the desired LO output. The design includes customized packaging with WR-10 waveguide output, bondwire compensation networks at all interconnects, and a microstrip-to-waveguide transition using an E-plane probe on a 5 mil alumina substrate. Eigen-mode analysis and full 3D EM simulations using Ansys HFSS ensured optimal matching and mode suppression. The LO module demonstrates a spurious-free output exceeding 50 dBc and measured output power of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>5 \text{dBm}$</tex>, surpassing the specification. The LO module was qualified as space hardware and successfully flown with the 183 GHz humidity sounding receiver on Earth observation satellite (EOS) - 07 mission.

title: Electronically Tunable Ka-Band Metasurface with Integrated Varactors for Wide-Angle Beam Steering at 27 Ghz
authors: Poojitha Maddineni, Julien Sarrazin, Chinmoy Saha, Massimiliano Casaletti
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This work presents the design and characterization of a reconfigurable metasurface for beam steering at 27 GHz, based on a compact H-shaped unit cell integrated with a varactor diode. A 240° reflection phase tuning range is achieved through full-wave simulations, supported by a via-based DC biasing network with minimal RF interference. The metasurface, composed of a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$40 \times 40$</tex> array, demonstrates wide-angle electronic beam steering under both ideal plane wave and realistic horn excitation. A hybrid phase synthesis approach-combining fixed geometric phase shifts along one axis with varactor-based tuning on the other-enables narrow pencil beams with reduced biasing complexity. Results show high gain and low sidelobe levels, confirming the metasurface's effectiveness for compact and adaptive millimeter-wave antenna systems.

title: Deep Learning for accelerated debugging in Design Verification and Post Silicon Validation of Scandump design
authors: Shreya Jayateerth Joshi, Poonam M Shettar, Sanjoy Saha, Shrinidhi S. Rao, Alok Kumar, Garima Srivastava
publish: 2025 IEEE 6th International Women in Technology Conference (WINTECHCON)
abstract: As modern System-on-chip (SoC) designs grow in complexity, traditional debug mechanisms involving manual waveform and log analysis struggle to keep up with the volume and intricacies of failures. Complex SoCs integrate multiple application-specific processors posing a challenge in the debugging phase of Design Verification (DV) and Post-Silicon Validation (PSV). We propose Artificial Intelligence (AI) augmented verification strategies to enhance debugging and improve verification efficiency. The proposed work uses Deep learning models alongside SystemVerilog (SV) based assertions in test bench (TB) and Python routines. By leveraging the capability of Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN) to remember sequential data, we attempt to simplify the bus protocol error debug in DV. We observe that the Long short-term memory (LSTM) algorithm consistently outperforms the RNN algorithm in terms of accuracy in bus error detection and classification. LSTM Autoencoder is used for anomaly detection in waveform mining, which is then used to identify required TB or design updates through Python routine. An integrated dashboard improves the efficacy of using this AI-Machine Learning (ML) based solution for debugging. Overall, with this approach, we observed that verification effort became 0.54x.

title: A Protection Method for Virtually Coupled Trains Based on Emergency Braking Acceleration Coordinated Configuration
authors: Wenxiao Yan, Jiayu Wang, Tao Tang, Hongjie Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Virtual Coupling (VC) has recently emerged as a prominent topic in the railway sector. It aims to offer more frequent services by allowing trains to operate at closer distances. However, current safety protection methods fail to meet the requirements of VC in terms of train separation. Implementing VC requires an advanced safety protection method that enables trains to run closer together without compromising safety. Therefore, to ensure the safety of small interval tracking of VC, a space-time interval protection method is proposed. At the same time, to reduce the safety distance and enhance train operation efficiency, this paper introduces the concept of coordination in emergency braking acceleration configuration. By actively controlling the braking performance of each train unit within the VC, ensure that the braking performance of the following train is superior to that of the leading train, thus reducing the safety distance and meeting the requirements of VC. Finally, experiments validate the effectiveness of the proposed method. Results show that the safe distance can be shortened by 45.6% when the VC Train units (VCTs) drive at 80 km/h.

title: FIRA-ED: A Novel Face Inversion Attack Framework on Edge Devices
authors: Yujie Ying, Fei Ding, Yan Li, Guidong Wang, Shiwan Han, Huankang Cui
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Information and Communication Networks (ICICN)
abstract: With the rapid development of edge computing, lightweight face recognition models (such as VGG16 and FaceNet64) are widely deployed on edge devices due to their efficiency and low resource consumption. However, while these models enhance convenience, they also raise significant concerns regarding potential privacy leakage. In this paper, we propose a novel face image reconstruction attack framework, FIRA-ED (Face Inversion Reconstruction Attack for Edge Devices), targeting black-box attack scenarios. By integrating a high-confidence data augmentation strategy with a two-stage Conditional Generative Adversarial Network (CGAN), FIRA-ED achieves efficient and high-fidelity reconstruction of target users' face images. Specifically, the method first employs the target model to perform confidence inference on a public dataset, selecting high-quality samples that closely align with the model's decision logic. Then, through a two-stage CGAN design with a refined loss function, it generates reconstructed images that are both highly realistic and capable of bypassing the target model's detection. Extensive experiments on public datasets such as CelebA and FFHQ demonstrate that FIRA-ED achieves an Attack Success Rate (ASR) of over 95% and a Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) score as low as 18.1, significantly outperforming traditional inversion attack methods in both image quality and attack effectiveness. This research not only exposes potential security vulnerabilities in face recognition systems on edge devices but also provides new insights and challenges for the development of privacy protection technologies.

title: Improving Vulnerable Road-Users Detection Through Hybrid Collaborative Perception and Detection Refinement
authors: Selma Oubouabdellah, Minh-Quan Dao, Ezio Malis, Elwan Héry, Julien Moreau, Vincent Frémont
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Ensuring the safety of autonomous vehicles in complex urban environments critically depends on accurate 3D object detection. While LiDAR sensors provide reliable depth information, their effectiveness is limited by sparsity at long distances and occlusions, particularly in intersection scenarios. Collaborative perception addresses these challenges by enabling information sharing among vehicles and infrastructure sensors, with intermediate fusion offering a balance between communication efficiency and detection accuracy. However, existing collaborative perception frameworks exhibit a notable performance gap between detecting vehicles and vulnerable road users such as cyclists and pedestrians. In this work, we propose a novel hybrid collaboration framework designed to reduce this gap. Our method leverages late-stage information from communicating agents to augment the ego agent's point cloud, then applies a standard intermediate fusion strategy, followed by a refinement stage that further improves the detection accuracy of various objects. Experiments on the Mixed Signals dataset demonstrate that our approach sets a new state-of-the-art in the detection of vulnerable road users in urban V2X scenarios.

title: String-Stable CACC of Connected Automated Vehicles with Heterogeneous Braking Capabilities
authors: Xiaohua Ge, Dengfeng Pan, Derui Ding, Qing Wu, Qing-Long Han, Xian-Ming Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: With the advancement of connected automated vehicle (CAV) technologies, cooperative adaptive cruise control (CACC) has emerged as a key enabler for enhancing the safety, efficiency, and stability of vehicle platoons. However, many existing CACC designs either overlook the influence of heterogeneous braking capabilities across vehicles or lack rigorous string stability analysis. This study develops a CACC approach that ensures both individual vehicle stability and string stability for CAV platoons with heterogeneous braking capabilities. Towards this aim, a novel speed-dependent gap reference is firstly proposed, explicitly accounting for the heterogeneity of service braking and emergency braking capabilities, enhancing transient safety and adaptability. Furthermore, string stability is systematically analyzed from both frequency- and timedomain perspectives, leading to the derivation of practical gain selection criteria for controller design. Finally, numerical simulations are carried out to validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach and demonstrate its superiority over the conventional CACC approach.

title: Highly Flexible Lightweight Silver Nanowire-Based Sandwiched Thin Film for EMI Shielding Applications
authors: Anshuman Choudhary, M. Jaleel Akhtar
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: A lightweight, flexible, multilayer nanonetworked sandwiched (FMNS) film is developed for electromagnetic interference (EMI) shielding applications. Initially, silver nanowires (AgNWs) are synthesized using the polyol method and subsequently deposited onto a flexible PTFE sheet via vacuum filtration. The high aspect ratio of AgNWs promotes the formation of conductive nanonetworks, thereby enhancing the effective EMI shielding. Further, A PDMS layer is deposited on the top of AgNWs layer to form a three-layer sandwiched film and thus preventing any shedding of AgNWs and promoting overall mechanical stability. The resulting film is characterized using a vector network analyzer to assess its dielectric properties. The film exhibits a high dielectric loss tangent ranging from 1 to 1.42, indicating its lossy behaviour. This film is found to have quite good EMI shielding property with shielding effectiveness (SET) between 13.9 and 11.6 dB across the entire X-band. Additionally, the structure is also simulated using CST Microwave Studio, and the measured SEt values closely follow the simulated results. The proposed FMNS film is lightweight and highly flexible and thus making it ideal for EMI shielding in defence and aerospace applications.

title: Dynamic Gesture-Guided Pedestrian Intention Prediction via Dual-Stream Information Fusion
authors: Yaoning Li, Zhang Zhang, Ming-Zhe Li, Da Li, Liang Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: In the field of autonomous driving, Pedestrian Intention Prediction (PIP) aims to accurately determine whether pedestrians intend to cross the street or not. Previous studies have explored various information, such as the speed of the ego vehicle, the pedestrian boundary boxes, and the human pose to enhance PIP performance. However, most current advanced models, which primarily rely on spatio-temporal relationships between pedestrians and ego-vehicles, often neglect the dynamic gestures of pedestrians. This omission of fine-grained motion information can lead to underestimating crossing intentions, resulting in low recall rates in PIP, which is impractical for safety-critical autonomous driving applications. To address this issue, we propose a Dynamic Gesture-Guided (DGG) PIP model, where a dual-stream information fusion network is proposed to integrate both global spatio-temporal features and local gesture motions at two levels. At the feature level, a crossattention module is adopted to combine local motion features with the spatio-temporal features. At the score level, a logistic regression-based gating module is employed to adaptively select the most reliable prediction from the dual-stream outputs. Extensive experimental results on the PIE and JAAD datasets demonstrate that the proposed DGG model significantly improves recall while maintaining high accuracy in pedestrian intention prediction.

title: Panoptic-CUDAL: Rural Australia Point Cloud Dataset in Rainy Conditions
authors: Tzu-Yun Tseng, Alexey Nekrasov, Malcolm Burdorf, Bastian Leibe, Julie Stephany Berrio, Mao Shan, Zhenxing Ming, Stewart Worrall
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Existing autonomous driving datasets are predominantly oriented towards well-structured urban settings and favourable weather conditions, leaving the complexities of rural environments and adverse weather conditions largely unaddressed. Although some datasets encompass variations in weather and lighting, bad weather scenarios do not appear often. Rainfall can significantly impair sensor functionality, introducing noise and reflections in LiDAR and camera data and reducing the system's capabilities for reliable environmental perception and safe navigation. This paper introduces the Panoptic-CUDAL dataset, a novel dataset purpose-built for panoptic segmentation in rural areas subject to rain. By recording high-resolution LiDAR, camera, and pose data, Panoptic-CUDAL offers a diverse, information-rich dataset in a challenging scenario. We present the analysis of the recorded data and provide baseline results for panoptic, semantic segmentation, and 3D occupancy prediction methods on LiDAR point clouds. The dataset can be found here: https://vision.rwth-aachen.de/panoptic-cudal

title: Spatio-Temporal Analysis of Cyclist-Cyclist Multidirectional Interactive Behaviours
authors: Qiuyun Luan, Cheng Zhang, Bo Du, Jun Shen
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Understanding interactions between cyclists in various spatial contexts is critical for improving safety performance of cycling infrastructure. This study investigates the behavioural characteristics of cyclists under multiple interactions at different types of road conditions. Using a series of controlled experiments, key behavioural parameters such as interaction speed, interaction duration, yaw rate, speed standard deviation, and the nearest neighbour distance during interactions are analysed. The results reveal that multidirectional and corner interactions present longer interaction durations, higher yaw rate, and increased speed variability, indicating elevated manoeuvring complexity and interaction intensity. In contrast, bidirectional scenarios, especially without obstacles, exhibit more stable and consistent cycling behaviours. These findings offer quantitative insights into cyclist dynamics under various interaction scenarios and provide valuable references for urban cycling infrastructure planning and flow management.

title: Federated Learning-Based Fault-Tolerant RIS Reconfiguration for Multi-User IoT Networks
authors: Vikash Kumar Bhardwaj, Abhishek Singh, Mahendra K. Shukla, Om Jee Pandey
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (RIS) is an emerging and highly promising technology for addressing the challenges of future wireless communication systems, especially in 6G networks. As future network environments will be more complex and operate at higher frequencies, the transmitted signals will face severe attenuation. In such scenarios, RIS plays a vital role by introducing a controlled phase shift to the incoming signal, directing it toward the desired location, such as the Base Station (BS). Traditionally, RIS optimizes its phase configuration for a specific transmitter, i.e., User Equipment (UE). However, in a multi-user scenario, this approach becomes impractical, as deploying separate RIS units for each user is not feasible. This problem becomes even more critical when some RIS elements malfunction and fail to reflect the signal toward the BS, either due to hardware faults or deliberate attacks targeting the RIS surface. Motivated by this challenge, we propose a Federated Learning (FL)-based solution for RIS reconfiguration and phase optimization in a multi-user environment. In our method, each UE locally optimizes the RIS configuration and phase based on its current channel conditions and sends the updated parameters to a central server. The server, for each user cluster, applies the Federated Averaging (FedAvg) algorithm to compute the optimal phase shift for the corresponding UEs. We conducted 20 independent experiments and plotted the average results. The proposed method was evaluated against both the brute-force method and the baseline method. Results show that the average SINR and sum rate achieved by the proposed method are close to those of the brute-force method, while its computational complexity is significantly lower. At the same time, it consistently outperforms the baseline method, demonstrating its robustness and effectiveness in multi-user scenarios.

title: Development of a Microfluidic Synchronization Device Based on a 4-Bit Counter in Integrated Implementation
authors: Lilia Yu. Duda
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: This paper presents the results of developing a synchronization device, including the schematic diagrams of a 4-bit counter based on a counting trigger, as well as an experimental testbench for studying the transfer characteristics of the synchronization device. Computer models of the relay and 4-bit counter, which are part of the microfluidic synchronization device, have been created.

title: Intelligent Management of IT Projects Using a Neuro-fuzzy Model
authors: Oleg M. Protalinsky, Ruslan S. Gavrilov, Anna A. Khanova
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: The article considers methods to IT-projects management on the basis of formalization of the optimization model talking into account key constraints: costs, terms and quality of performance. Quantitative models of productivity and execution control are described, which allow to evaluate the team performance and adjust the resource utilization. A target cost function is introduced, the minimization of which ensures the selection of the most efficient resource configuration under given constraints. Special attention is paid to the quality parameter included in the model as a hard constraint, violation of which may lead to project failure. To take into account the high uncertainty and complexity of interrelationships between the parameters, the use of intelligent methods, in particular neuro-fuzzy inference systems is proposed. The presented method is oriented to increase the stability of management decisions and the efficiency of realization of complex and dynamic IT projects.

title: Efficient Relay Selection and Transmission Scheduling in Non-Terrestrial Assisted Vehicular Networks
authors: Rohit Prajapati, Atharva Navarkar, Aditya Guhagarkar, Vimal Bhatia
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: Millimeter-wave communication provides high capacity channels suitable for vehicular networks, yet it suffers from susceptibility to dynamic environmental blockages caused by urban infrastructure, terrain, and mobility. To mitigate these limitations, we introduce an innovative two-way relay architecture supported by unmanned aerial vehicles, aimed at improving connectivity and expanding coverage for moving vehicles. Additionally, in scenarios where direct vehicle-to-vehicle links or unmanned aerial vehicle-assisted relaying are unavailable due to severe obstructions or coverage gaps, the system seamlessly falls back on satellite-to-vehicle communication to maintain reliable data delivery. The overall scheduling process is modeled as a joint optimization problem combining relay selection and transmission scheduling, with the goal of minimizing end-to-end communication latency while satisfying throughput demands. To solve this problem, we explore a range of techniques including constraint programming, deep Q-learning, and proximal policy optimization. Simulation results show that the proposed PPO based approach achieves near-optimal performance, with up to 7.5% higher throughput and 6.27% fewer time slots than JRDS across various deployment scenarios.

title: A Parameter Optimization Method for Multi-Carrier Communication Based on Genetic Algorithm
authors: Peiyu Chen, Jiahao Zhang, Jiaheng Du, Xuanhe Yang, Xiaqing Miao
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Information and Communication Networks (ICICN)
abstract: By adjusting the transmission subcarriers frequency in multicarrier modulation, it is possible to effectively mitigate the impact of co-channel jamming. However, in covert communication scenarios, failure to achieve optimal configuration of subcarrier frequency, bandwidth, and power parameters may lead to system performance degradation and even result in the communication signal being detected by eavesdroppers. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a parameter optimization method for multicarrier covert communication under jamming scenarios. In order to maximize the system Signal to Jamming plus Noise Ratio (SJNR) under complex constraints while guaranteeing covertness requirements, an improved genetic algorithm-based power-band optimization approach is introduced, which utilizes SJNR information to optimize parameter estimation.

title: Strip-Slot Incorporated Reconfigurable Tri-Polarized Planar Array Antenna with Diagonally Extended Patch
authors: Debprosad Das, Md. Motahar Hossain, Md. Azad Hossain
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: This work describes a reconfigurable polarization antenna array that can be modified and used for tri-polarization. Two parallel microstrip lines feed the array via an inverted T-shaped slot line with a width of 0.2 mm in the ground plane. The slot line in the center of the construction forms a hybrid strip-slot divider, allowing the antenna to function so that individual excitation does not impact other ports. The relative power level difference in both planes (xz and xy) is above 30 dB, which ensures significant circular polarization (CP) of both senses. The simulation results show that the impedance matching, isolation, and radiation pattern are all satisfactory. The antenna attains an axial ratio bandwidth of 1.97% and a 10 dB impedance bandwidth of 8.08%. The antenna has an efficiency of more than 70% at its designed frequency and a gain of more than 7 dBic for CP and 10 dBi for linear polarization.

title: Additively Manufactured Wideband Transmissive Metasurface for Dual-Beam, Dual-Mode OAM Beams Generation
authors: Akhilesh Kumar, Amit K. Singh, Rahul Mondal
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents an additively manufactured wideband transmissive metasurface for the generation of dualmode, dual-beam orbital angular momentum (OAM) waves in the Ka-band. The metasurface consists of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$80 \times 80$</tex> unit cells with cross-shaped air cavities embedded in polylactic acid (PLA), where variation in cavity height enables full 360° transmission phase control while maintaining a transmission coefficient greater than 0.85 across the entire band. Symmetric unit-cell geometry maintains polarization-invariant response to incident waves. The proposed metasurface simultaneously generates OAM mode <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\ell=-1$</tex> at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\theta=35^{\circ}, \phi=0^{\circ}$</tex>, and mode <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\ell=+2$</tex> at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\theta=25^{\circ}$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\phi=180^{\circ}$</tex>, with mode purities exceeding 85 %. The wideband OAM bandwidth is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 0 \%} \boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{2 6 - 3 8 ~ G H z} \boldsymbol{)}$</tex>, with peak gains of 22.2 dBi for both modes at 30 GHz. These results confirm the metasurface's potential for high-capacity, multiplexed millimeter-wave communication systems.

title: An Antenna Testing System for Antenna Array Applications
authors: Neeraja Peddinti, Solon J. Spiegel
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a novel harware and software antenna array testing solution using quad-core beamforming ASICs designed for multi-port antenna array measurements. By accurately determining complex weighting functions, the proposed approach minimizes the need for extensive calibration and significantly reduces uncertainties in the evaluation of Active Electronically Scanned Array (AESA) systems, ensuring measurement precision. The amplitude and phase errors of the weight coefficients in the antenna array testing solution are maintained within 0.5 dB and 1 degree, respectively.

title: Study of Error Resilience Efficiency of ViterbiConvolutional Coding Combined with STBC in MIMO Systems
authors: Ngiem Van Dong
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: This study evaluates the error resilience performance of a MIMO system combining Alamouti space time block coding (STBC) with convolutional encoding and Viterbi decoding. The proposed architecture integrates spatial diversity with temporal redundancy, providing robustness against both multipath fading and additive noise. Simulation results demonstrate the high potential of this approach for fifth generation communication systems and Wi-Fi 6/7, where high reliability and moderate computational complexity are simultaneously required.

title: Development of a Tunable Microwave Photonic Filter Based on Stimulated Brillouin Scattering
authors: Anju Thomas, Koushik Basak, Pranav Kumar Pandey, Chandra Prakash Sharma, M. Senthilkumar
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: Radio Frequency (RF) filters are essential for selecting or rejecting signals within specific frequency bands of the radio spectrum. These filters are integral within the system and signal interfaces to ensure the fidelity of the final output. Microwave Photonic Filters (MPFs) are photonic subsystems that perform similar functions to conventional microwave filters while offering additional advantages inherent to photonics, such as low loss, wide bandwidth, immunity to electromagnetic interference (EMI), tunability and reconfigurability. Various techniques have been explored for MPF implementation, including specially designed Fiber Bragg Gratings (FBGs), Fabry-Pérot etalons, and Stimulated Brillouin Scattering (SBS). Among these, SBS stands out as a highly promising approach due to its superior performance characteristics. This paper presents the design and hardware implementation of an SBS-based MPF with tunable center frequency and passband. The experimental results are validated against simulations conducted using OptiSystem software. The implemented MPF demonstrates center frequency tunability in the range of 1 GHz to 20 GHz and a 3 dB bandwidth tunability from 20 MHz to 85 MHz.

title: Hybrid Compression for Accurate End of Life Prediction on Edge Battery Management System
authors: Taeyi Kim, Younggeon Seo, Stephane Barde
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Accurate real-time prognostics for lithium-ion batteries are critical for electric vehicle safety and longevity, but deep learning models often exceed the computational limits of automotive battery management system (BMS) hardware. To address this, we introduce a unified compression pipeline that integrates knowledge distillation, structured pruning, and post-training quantization to transform a high-capacity teacher LSTM into a compact student network. When evaluated for battery End-of-Life prediction on NASA and CALCE benchmark datasets, our compressed model demonstrates superior or comparable accuracy to the original teacher. The student model achieved an absolute error of 11.25 cycles on NASA data (versus 12.50 for the teacher) and 9.50 cycles on CALCE data (versus 16.25 for the teacher). Crucially, this performance was achieved with a final model size of only 5 KB, a 99% reduction from the teacher's initial 471 KB average size on the NASA dataset. By matching or exceeding the teacher's accuracy while dramatically reducing the model size, our approach demonstrates practical feasibility for deployment on resource-constrained BMS platforms.

title: Co-prime Sampling-Based PUMA for Time-delay Estimation Using Ground Penetrating Radar
authors: Huimin Pan, Jingjing Pan, Xiaofei Zhang, Xudong Dong, Yide Wanga, Fulvio Gini
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Information and Communication Networks (ICICN)
abstract: Time-delay estimation (TDE) in ultra-wideband ground penetrating radar (GPR) faces challenges due to the hardware limitations of a uniform sampling strategy, which requires dense and extensive frequency samplers. In this paper, we introduce a co-prime sampling strategy, consisting of two sub-sampling sequences, to reduce the sampling rate and computational complexity of the TDE method. The initial estimation for each sub-sampling sequence is obtained using principal utilization for modal analysis (PUMA). Since the sampling intervals of the sub-sampling sequences are sparse, the initial estimates exhibit periodic ambiguities within the feasible region. These ambiguities are resolved, and the final time-delay estimate is derived based on the co-prime relationship. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed co-prime PUMA method effectively reduces complexity while improving resolution and accuracy.

title: Photonic Reservoir Computing Based on Six-Valued Mask with Improved Performance of Channel Impairments Compensation
authors: Shunan Xu, Hailong Zhu, Zeyu Xu, Le Liu, Xianfeng Tang, Xiaoguang Zhang
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Information and Communication Networks (ICICN)
abstract: In this paper, photonic reservoir computing (RC) based on six-valued mask is proposed for channel impairments compensation in optical communication. To enhance the compensation performance of photonic RC, the six-valued mask increases the dimensionality of the feature space by introducing more state values into the reservoir compared to the conventional binary mask. To investigate the impact of input mask selection on the performance of channel impairments compensation, a 50 Gbit/s optical transmission system was established with the signal distorted after 50 km transmission at a laser power of 10 mW. Results show that the normalized mean-square error (NMSE) of signal processed by the proposed photonic RC with the six-valued mask is lower than that of the binary mask, which reaches 4.20e-5. Additionally, the six-valued mask enhances the channel impairments compensation performance of the photonic RC, surpassing the binary mask and reducing the bit error rate (BER) from 5.4e-3 to 1.2e-3.

title: Application and Evaluation of Large Language Models for Forecasting the Impact of Traffic Incidents
authors: George Jagadeesh, Srikrishna Iyer, Michal Polanowski, Kai Xin Thia
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This study examines the feasibility of applying large language models (LLMs) for forecasting the impact of traffic incidents on the traffic flow. The use of LLMs for this task has several advantages over existing machine learning-based solutions such as not requiring a large training dataset and the ability to utilize free-text incident logs. We propose a fully LLM-based solution that predicts the incident impact using a combination of traffic features and LLM-extracted incident features. A key ingredient of this solution is an effective method of selecting examples for the LLM's in-context learning. We evaluate the performance of three advanced LLMs and two state-of-the-art machine learning models on a real traffic incident dataset. The results show that the best-performing LLM matches the accuracy of the most accurate machine learning model, despite the former not having been trained on this prediction task. The findings indicate that LLMs are a practically viable option for traffic incident impact prediction.

title: A Dual-Polarized Triple-Band RDRA by Metal Strip Inclusion
authors: Sahin Islam, Susanta Kumar Parui, Goffar Ali Sarkar
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents the design of a dual-polarized (DP) triple-band rectangular dielectric resonator antenna (RDRA). Two metallic strips have been inserted diagonally into the RDRA, enabling two additional bands. The RDRA has been fed by a microstrip line. The simulated result shows triple-bands at 5.0, 5.9, and 8.0 GHz with corresponding impedance bandwidths of 4.77%, 8.27%, and 5.12%, respectively. The peak gains of 5.9, 6.3, and 4.9 dB have also been obtained for the 5.0, 5.9, and 8.0 GHz, respectively, in the simulated result. It is observed that the proposed RDRA radiates in a broadside direction at 5.0 and 5.9 GHz bands, whereas it radiates in a slant direction for the 8.0 GHz band, which may exhibit a greater opportunity for real-time simultaneous communication with two geographically dispersed locations. Besides the horizontal linear polarisation at 5.0 GHz, the antenna notably exhibits slant polarisation at 5.9 and 8 GHz, enabling it as a DP antenna. Therefore, in contemporary wireless communication applications, the suggested antenna shows great promise for dual-polarized triple-band operation.

title: Simulation of a Contactless Human Vital Signs Monitoring System and Analysis of the Results Using Neural Networks in the Mathematical Environment of Engee
authors: Gorbunov Alexandr, Balakin Dmitry, Akmalov Marat, Semichastnov Alexey, Tsymbal Mikhail
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: An end-to-end model for contactless vital sign monitoring based on an FMCW radar has been developed using model-based design in the Russian mathematical environment Engee. The system integrates physical modeling of the chest cavity, radar signal propagation, and neural network-based data processing.

title: WAP-Net: A Wavelet Attention Prototypical Network for Rolling Bearing Fault Diagnosis Under Few-Shot and Multi-Operating Conditions
authors: Zhinan Xia, Yanna Zhang, Yifeng Li
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Recently, deep learning (DL) techniques have been widely applied in rotating machinery fault diagnosis to guarantee the safe and stable operation of industrial equipment. However, existing DL-based fault diagnosis methods are highly dependent on large-scale sample data, exhibiting limited generalization capability under small-sample scenarios and significant degradation in diagnostic accuracy under multi-operating conditions. To address this bottleneck, this paper embeds the prior knowledge of signal processing into the metric learning framework and proposes a wavelet attention prototypical network (WAP-Net). First, morlet wavelet convolution is utilized to directly map raw vibration signals into a time-frequency feature space with physical interpretability, providing high discriminative power inputs for subsequent metric learning. Subsequently, an efficient channel attention (ECA) mechanism is introduced to dynamically enhance feature channels associated with fault signatures, thereby shaping more robust and compact class prototypes. Finally, within the prototypical network framework, fault diagnosis is accomplished by computing the Euclidean distances between query samples and various class prototypes. Experimental results on the Case Western Reserve University (CWRU) bearing datasets demonstrate that WAP-Net exhibits exceptional few-shot diagnostic capabilities. Even under the condition of only 100 samples, its diagnostic accuracy remains stably above 90%, which is significantly superior to other comparative models.

title: Stability Assessment for Paralleled Grid-Forming Converters Based on Virtual Impedance with Wide Range of Connection Impedance
authors: Kai Sun, Pande Jiao, Chunyan Wu, Ran Li, Kai Yuan, Dayong Zhou
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Mechanical Engineering and Power Engineering (MEPE)
abstract: With the capability of reshaping frequency and providing damping, grid-forming (GFM) converters have great potential to be applied in converter-interfaced generators. Unfortunately, previous studies illustrated that the GFM converter has many limitations, such as power coupling, transient overcurrent, etc. To address these challenge, virtual impedance is served as a promising solution. In this paper, the analysis demonstrates that the virtual impedance may introduce additional instability risk when the connection impedance varies over a wide range, where two destabilizing oscillation modes located at 10-20 Hz and several hundred Hz are found. Based on this, the feasible range of the virtual impedance is obtained when the connection impedance changes in the case of adopting steady-state virtual impedance (SSVI) and complete dynamic virtual impedance (CDVI), respectively. Moreover, the influence of the feedforward control and the virtual excitation control on the small-signal stability is investigated. Finally, simulations are conducted to validate the theoretical analysis.

title: Digital Twin-Enhanced Remaining Useful Life Prediction for Bearings using Multi-Scale CNN-Transformer Hybrid Model
authors: Jie Zhou, Zhensheng Lin, Gang Niu
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Bearings are critical and highly susceptible components in mechanical equipment, with their operational status and lifespan being vital to the stable operation of the entire system. Addressing issues such as memory degradation, low training efficiency, and limited prediction accuracy when handling long-sequence data in Remaining Useful Life (RUL) prediction, this study explores a digital twin-based MSCNN-Transformer model. Initially, due to random fluctuations in the collected vibration signals influenced by various environments, wavelet transform is employed to denoise the signals. Subsequently, for the vibration signals during the bearing degradation phase, a multi-domain feature set is established. By constructing a comprehensive evaluation metric that considers feature monotonicity and trendability, the final degradation feature set is selected. The selected degradation features are then linearly weighted to construct a comprehensive health indicator. Finally, an MSCNN-Transformer model is developed. The MSCNN enhances the model's ability to extract features at different scales. By fitting the trend of the bearing's health indicator, the model predicts the bearing's remaining useful life. On this basis, a digital twin model that accurately reflects the physical characteristics and behaviors of the bearing is constructed and updated in real-time based on experimental data. The MSCNN-Transformer model algorithm is embedded into a visualization system, and a visualization platform developed using Virtual Reality technology integrates multidimensional data to optimize maintenance and repair processes in practical engineering applications.

title: ReDiNet: Residual Network for Bengali Dialect Classification
authors: Mahinur Rashid, Safwan Ahmad, Ramisa Anjum, Faiza Binte Arif, Sanjida Arefin Rimu, Farig Yousuf Sadeque
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: In a country with a diverse language dialects like Bangladesh, addressing regional differences is crucial for developing voice based technologies, among them are speech recognition, localization and cultural preservation. Due to the increasing prevalence of AI-powered voice applications within the education, media and communication system, the need to identify the correct dialect is ever increasing. This paper presents ReDiNet, a deep learning network that can be used to categorize Bengali regional dialects using short five second audio samples. The proposed model achieves an accuracy of 94%; it is able to identify the minor spectral and prosodic differences between the 10 Bengali dialects. The results of this paper indicate that deep learning models have a huge potential in regional language processing; enhancing speech recognition, linguistic analysis and creating digital tools to work with low resource languages such as Bengali.

title: Active Local Navigation System for Telecommunication High-Altitude Platforms
authors: K. A. Vytovtov, M. G. Ivanov, A. L. Samofalov, S. A. Khakhomov
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: The paper solves the problem of automatic stabilization of the position of telecommunication tethered highaltitude platforms in the airspace under the influence of external factors such as wind, birds, turbulence, etc. An autonomous active local navigation system for tethered unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), performing the functions of telecommunication modules and repeaters of base station signals in a wide frequency range, is proposed. The distinctive feature of proposed system is operation in active mode, which significantly increases the accuracy of determining coordinates and noise immunity of the system.

title: SLFedUNet: A Super-Lightweight Federated 3D U-Net for Volumetric Brain Tumor Segmentation
authors: Tarik Rahman, Rafiqul Islam
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Federated learning (FL) makes it possible to perform collaborative training of central model across medical organizations without compromising patient privacy, yet is also confronted with the conventional 3D segmentation networks that suffer from heavy computation and communication costs. In order to solve this, Many models based on sequential learning have been widely used while their computational cost of training is prohibitive for the large dataset. To address this challenge, we propose a super-lightweight 3D U-Net network for federated brain tumor segmentation, so that the computation cost is dramatically decreased while keeping its segmentation performance competitive. The model architecture uses depthwise separable convolutions, instance normalization and compact encoder–decoder blocks to significantly reduce the number of parameters required while maintaining the capacity for volumetric feature learning. Experiments based on the BraTS dataset under the federated setting show that the proposed architecture yields a mean Dice value of 0.86, while keeping in pace with full scale 3D U-Net models. Even though the model is over 99% smaller, it converges faster, and with much less communication per round, demonstrating its suitability for deployment in resource-constrained or distributed clinical settings.

title: Vehicle Occupant Age and Gender Estimation Using Convolutional Neural Networks on RGB and NIR Images
authors: Patrick Laufer, Clemens Gühmann
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: To enhance safety within the vehicle interior, the New Car Assessment Protocol (NCAP) emphasizes the importance of estimating occupant features, such as age and gender, utilizing integrated sensors like vehicle interior cameras. This work presents a data augmentation strategy designed to enhance the performance of a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) in estimating the facial age and gender of both the driver and co-driver from vehicle interior images captured in nearinfrared (NIR) and visual (RGB) modalities. The approach was initially tested on publicly available datasets and later evaluated using an in-house dataset derived from a study involving 50 unique test subjects, who acted as drivers and co-drivers within a car. In the in-house dataset, facial images were generated using both coarse and tight cropping strategies to identify the optimal approach. Results indicate that the proposed data augmentation strategy, combined with tighter cropping, significantly enhances gender and age estimation performance in both RGB and NIR modalities while outperforming state of the art methods.

title: Dual Circularly Polarized High Gain Impedance Modulated Metasurface Based Leaky-Wave Antenna for Dual Azimuthal Plane Radiation
authors: Ashwani Kumar, Anjana Krishna, Swarnadipto Ghosh, Soumava Mukherjee, Chinmoy Saha
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This article presents the design and comprehensive characterization of a dual-beam, dual-planar, dual-polarized scalar holographic metasurface leaky-wave antenna (HMSLWA) using surface impedance (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{Z}_{\text{surf }}$</tex>) mapping techniques. The proposed HMSLWA supports orthogonal circular polarizations-left-hand circular polarization (LHCP) and right-hand circular polarization (RHCP)-radiated in two spatially separated planes. A novel phase correction factor (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varphi_{\text{ext }}$</tex>) is introduced to mitigate polarization-dependent nulls and enhance beam symmetry. The metasurface (MTS) consists of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3600(60 \times 60)$</tex> scalar unit cells occupying an aperture of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$14.5 \lambda_{0} \times 14.5 \lambda_{0}$</tex>, optimized for 15.5 GHz operation. The first beam, radiating at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\theta_{1}, \varphi_{1}\right)=\left(-25^{\circ}, 0^{\circ}\right)$</tex>, exhibits high-purity LHCP, while the second beam at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\theta_{2}, \varphi_{2}\right)=\left(25^{\circ}, 45^{\circ}\right)$</tex> achieves clean RHCP with an excellent axial ratio performance. Both beams demonstrate strong polarization isolation and high crosspolar isolation. The simulated peak gain reaches <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5. 2 1}$</tex> dBic, with good agreement to MTS aperture, confirming the effectiveness of the dual-planar, dual-polarized HMSLWA architecture for advanced beamforming and polarization diversity applications.

title: Modeling the Process of Information Transfer to Clusters of Biological Objects
authors: Dmitry Zheleznyakov, Julia Golovko, Anatoli Likhter
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: The article is devoted to modeling the process of information transfer to a cluster of control objects in multimodal systems. An optimality criterion for designing these systems is introduced, based on maximizing the capacity function of the channels for transmitting information to the cluster. An example is shown of the influence of the delay time of one of the control signals, leading to a change in the initial volume of the cluster during its movement and, as a consequence, to a change in the amount of information transmitted to it. A practically important case of analyzing the process of transmitting a bimodal signal (light and sound) to hydrobionts is considered.

title: A Downscaling Approach for Electric Vehicle Dynamics: An Application of Similitude Laws
authors: Ahmed Wassim El Gamah, Ouafae El Ganaoui-Mourlan, Dominique Gruyer, Antonio Sciarretta, Remi Sainct
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This article explores the application of similitude theory to analyze electric vehicle dynamics and the challenges of downscaling full-size vehicles for experimental testing. It discusses the complexities of maintaining dynamic similarity between full-scale and reduced-scale models, focusing on the influence of mass scaling and inertial effects on acceleration and braking. The study presents the fundamental equations governing electric vehicle motion and emphasizes the crucial role of scaling factors in relating parameters between different scales. A key aspect is the analysis of power delivered to the wheels, with derived equations establishing relationships between power output in full-scale and reduced-scale models. The article also proposes several approaches to achieve power similitude, each with specific assumptions and conditions, and highlights the derivation of a theoretical relationship enabling power output estimation between reduced-scale and full-scale electric vehicles.

title: Reputation-Based Cooperative Spectrum Sensing Against Byzantine Attacks
authors: Hong Du, Xiangqian Liu, Qian Tang, Suting Xu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Information Communication and Software Engineering (ICICSE)
abstract: The open nature of wireless channels exposes cognitive radio networks (CRNs) to malicious users (MUs), posing severe threats to cooperative spectrum sensing (CSS) from Byzantine attacks. This paper presents a robust CSS scheme that integrates four synergistic mechanisms: confidence-gated reputation updates with asymmetric learning, soft weight mapping with state-dependent attenuation, weight-ordered sequential fusion with early stopping, and a hysteresis state machine for stable user classification. These components form a closed loop that effectively discriminates between occasional sensing errors and persistent malicious behaviors. Extensive simulations demonstrate that our algorithm significantly outperforms conventional methods by simultaneously reducing the global error probability and the average number of sensing samples required, substantially boosting the robustness and availability of CSS.

title: Error Performance Analysis of MIMO-FSO Systems under MRC and EGC Merging Mode
authors: Xu Jianwu, Liu Xiguo, Mao Zhongyang, Zhao Zhiyong
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Information Communication and Software Engineering (ICICSE)
abstract: The bit error performance of MIMO-FSO communication systems under different combining schemes and channel conditions is a critical factor in system design. This study analyzes the bit error performance of the system under varying turbulence intensities when the receiver adopts either maximum ratio combining (MRC) or equal gain combining (EGC) for multiple signals, providing theoretical guidance for subsequent MIMO-FSO system design. A MIMO-FSO system model was established under three channel conditions: weak turbulence, strong turbulence, and moderate turbulence. The bit error performance of MIMO-FSO systems with MRC and EGC at different numbers of transceiver antennas and turbulence intensities was compared and analyzed. The bit error calculation expression was derived, and detailed simulation analysis was conducted. Simulation results show that EGC's performance improvement under weak turbulence remains limited with increasing antenna numbers, while under strong turbulence, EGC's bit error performance improves steadily with antenna number growth. For MRC, significant performance improvements were observed across all three turbulence intensities as transceiver antenna numbers increased. MRC demonstrated notable performance advantages over EGC under weak turbulence, with a 1 -order difference in bit error rates at an average SNR of 40 dB.

title: A Prior-knowledge-embedded Causal-aware Adaptive Regulation Network for Open-set Domain Generalization in Fault Diagnosis
authors: Ning Jia, Lei Gu, Weiguo Huang
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: In complex industrial systems, the high uncertainty of operating conditions may lead to the presence of atypical fault samples in the testing data that are absent during training, thereby posing significant challenges for open-set domain generalization diagnosis. To address this issue, this paper proposes a prior-knowledge-embedded causal-aware adaptive regulation network, aiming to enhance fault identification capability and generalization performance under open-set conditions. Specifically, a causal feature extraction module based on a causal intervention mechanism is constructed, and a causal-aware loss function is introduced to strengthen the model's ability to capture domain-invariant causal features associated with faults. To improve interpretability and decision stability, prior features that represent the dynamic response characteristics of bearings are included to jointly drive model decisions. To enhance the model's sensitivity to unknown-class samples, a soft real-label strategy and a dynamic probability regulation mechanism are designed to effectively suppress the misclassification of unknown classes as known ones. During the target domain decision-making phase, a dual-branch alignment strategy and a discriminative quantification mechanism are proposed to improve open-set diagnostic accuracy and provide class-level guidance for fault maintenance. Extensive experiments conducted on two bearing datasets demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed method in open-set domain generalization diagnostic tasks.

title: A Hybrid Attention-Enhanced Spatio-Temporal Graph Network for Continuous-Time Multi-Task Urban Human Mobility Forecasting
authors: Mingyue Li, Xinghua Li, Yuntao Guo
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Understanding urban human mobility is essential for intelligent transportation systems (ITS) and applications such as traffic management and public transit planning. Although high-resolution spatiotemporal data from GPS devices, smart cards, and mobile services have enhanced mobility modeling, effectively capturing the continuous, multiscale, and context-dependent nature of human movement remains challenging. Existing Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Networks often rely on discrete-time assumptions, treat key tasks independently, and neglect external factors like weather and public events. To address these limitations, we propose HA-SCTNet (Hybrid Attention-Enhanced Spatio-Temporal Graph Network), a unified encoder-decoder framework for multi-task urban mobility forecasting. HA-SCTNet integrates continuoustime representation learning, hierarchical spatial reasoning, and residual-based task-aware optimization to jointly predict origin-destination demand, inflow, and outflow. It incorporates Spatio-Temporal Cross Attention Blocks to model multi-scale spatial-temporal dependencies and employs a memory-augmented message-passing mechanism for long-term information retention and asynchronous temporal state updates. Additionally, external contextual factors such as weather, holidays, and special events are embedded into the model to improve robustness. Experimental results demonstrate that HA-SCTNet outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in capturing the complex dynamics of urban mobility. This work contributes to the development of more accurate and context-aware forecasting models for ITS.

title: Frequency Selective Surface Unit Cell Consisting of Arbitrary Loop and Parametric Loop
authors: Ramesh Chandra Gupta, Vijay Kumar Singh, Milind B. Mahajan
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This article reports design, analysis and optimization of a new unit cell element for FSS (Frequency Selective surface). This FSS Unit Cell (UC) is a combination of two layers, consisting of Arbitrary Shaped Loop and Complex Geometry Loop. It is analyzed using Periodic method of moments (pMoM) technique in Ticra Tool 24.1 software. Multi-level coordinate search (MCS) and minimax optimization techniques of Ticra Tool have been used for optimization of RF Performance of this FSS-UC. This configuration provides extra degree of freedom for designing and analyzing the FSS unit cell. This UC exhibits good RF Performance at Ka-Band (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$22.65-28 \text{GHz}$</tex>) and S-Band (2.00-2.23 GHz). This type of unit cell would find application in dichroic-sub-reflector based Ka and S-Band transmit and receive single Cassegrain antenna.

title: Container-State-Aware Energy Consumption Minimization for Serverless Functions
authors: Jinquan Zhang, Han Cao, Long Chen, Siyuan Li, Xiaoping Li, Bing Ai
abstract: In this paper, we investigate the problem of minimizing the total energy consumption for serverless functions (SFs) while ensuring that the cold start probability of each SF remains below a specified threshold. In serverless computing, containers are reused to reduce the occurrence of cold starts, which increases the static energy consumption and decreases the dynamic energy consumption of physical machines (PMs). The trade-off between cold start reduction and energy consumption introduces significant challenges, especially considering that container states (idle, cold start, and running) have distinct energy characteristics. To address these challenges, we propose a container-state-aware energy consumption model that accurately captures the dynamic energy characteristics of PMs. Furthermore, we develop an enhanced Bayesian Optimization-Based Energy Consumption Minimization (BOECM) algorithm, which determines the optimal reuse times for different serverless functions (SFs) by decomposing the high-dimensional search space into a series of lower-dimensional sub-problems. In addition, several tailored heuristic strategies are proposed for request allocation and container deployment. The proposed BOECM algorithm is compared with several existing heuristic and meta-heuristic algorithms for similar problems. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly reduces total energy consumption, maintains cold start probabilities within acceptable limits, and outperforms the baselines.

title: Table of Contents April 2026

title: A Deep Dynamic Graph Generative Framework for Blockchain Phishing Detection
authors: Siyi Xiao, Lejun Zhang, Xinwei Zhang, Sen Zhang, Shen Su, Jing Qiu, Ran Guo, Haibo Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Blockchain phishing scams cause billions in annual losses, yet extreme data imbalance severely hampers existing detection algorithms. Current dynamic graph generation methods fragment structures and generate erroneous connections, failing to capture local dynamic patterns vital for node classification. This raises critical questions: Can models minimize isolated subgraph generation? How can they learn and replicate structured, recurring interaction patterns? To answer these questions, we introduce GraphFlowGen, an end-to-end deep generative framework. To minimize isolated subgraph generation, GraphFlowGen employs a novel preprocessing module that jointly extracts structural and temporal contexts from transaction data, preventing fragmentation and information loss. To learn and replicate structured interaction patterns, it incorporates a Transformer encoder with Graph Attention Networks (GAT) to capture node connection dynamics and temporal evolution. To ensure high fidelity while reducing erroneous links, a reinforcement learning (RL) mechanism iteratively refines generated graph structures. Empirical validation on three real-world datasets demonstrates the effectiveness of our algorithm in local dynamic graph generation and its utility for downstream phishing detection tasks.
