title: Stacked Intelligent Metasurfaces for 6G Wireless Networks: Principles, Applications, and Research Directions
authors: Enyu Shi, Jiayi Zhang, Zhilong Liu, Ziheng Liu, Arumugam Nallanathan, Mérouane Debbah, Shi Jin, Bo Ai
abstract: The sixth-generation (6G) wireless networks are expected to deliver ubiquitous connectivity, resilient coverage, and intelligence-driven services in highly dynamic environments. To achieve these goals, distributed wireless architectures such as cell-free massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) have attracted significant attention due to their scalability and fairness. Recently, stacked intelligent metasurfaces (SIMs) have emerged as a promising evolution of reconfigurable intelligent surfaces, offering multi-layer electromagnetic domain processing with enhanced controllability and spatial degrees of freedom. By integrating SIMs into distributed wireless networks, advanced wave-domain operations can be realized, enabling efficient interference management, improved energy and spectral efficiency, as well as robust physical-layer security. This article provides a comprehensive overview of SIM-aided distributed wireless networks, including their application scenarios, classification, and system architectures. Key signal processing challenges, such as hierarchical frameworks, user association, and joint precoding, are discussed, followed by case studies demonstrating significant performance gains. Finally, future research directions in hardware design, energy consumption modeling, algorithm development, and artificial intelligence integration are highlighted, aiming to pave the way for scalable and intelligent 6G distributed wireless networks.

title: A Surrogate-Assisted Optimized Calibration System Using a Magnetic Source Array for OPM-Based Magnetocardiography
authors: Changxu Zhu, Xu Zhang, Xiaokang Geng, Mengyang Yuan, Mingxiao Shao, Kangqi Tian, Jianzhi Yang, Min Xiang, Jianli Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Optically pumped magnetometers (OPMs) are pivotal for ultra-weak biomagnetic field detection in magnetocardiography (MCG). In biological ultra-weak magnetic measurements, the orientation, position, and gain of magnetic sensors are key factors that affect the accuracy of subsequent source-level analysis. However, optical methods are confined to calibrating the sensor geometric pose, leading to an incomplete parameter set, while techniques using standard magnetic sources are limited by the OPM near-zero field environment and further constrained by its limited dynamic range. To overcome these limitations, a novel, surrogate-assisted optimized calibration system centered on a reconfigurable magnetic source array is introduced in this work. First, a multi-objective optimization model for a saddle-shaped coil array is established, which explicitly accounts for the critical factor of ferromagnetic boundary coupling. Second, an efficient design framework is developed by integrating Latin Hypercube Sampling (LHS) with Gaussian Process Regression (GPR) to construct a high-fidelity surrogate model, thereby enabling rapid Pareto-front exploration for the coil geometry. Finally, a full-parameter calibration platform is implemented, capable of simultaneously determining the position, orientation, and gain for uniaxial OPM arrays. Simulations confirm that the optimized coil design significantly enhances calibration robustness. Experimental validation within an MCG device demonstrates a sensor coordinate uncertainty of 0.079 mm and an average reduction in dipole localization error of 97.65%. These results validate the system’s effectiveness and establish a new foundation for high-accuracy source reconstruction in OPM-based biomagnetic imaging.

title: From Channel Bias to Feature Redundancy: Uncovering the “Less Is More” Principle in Few-Shot Learning
authors: Ji Zhang, Xu Luo, Lianli Gao, Difan Zou, Heng Tao Shen, Jingkuan Song
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Deep neural networks often fail to adapt representations to novel tasks under distribution shifts, especially when only a few examples are available. This paper identifies a core obstacle behind this failure: Channel Bias, where networks develop a rigid emphasis on feature dimensions that were discriminative for the source task, but this emphasis is misaligned and fails to adapt to the distinct needs of a novel task. This bias leads to a striking and detrimental consequence: Feature Redundancy. We demonstrate that for few-shot tasks, classification accuracy is significantly improved by using as few as 1-5% of the most discriminative feature dimensions, revealing that the vast majority are actively harmful. Our theoretical analysis confirms that this redundancy originates from confounding feature dimensions—those with high intra-class variance but low inter-classseparability—which are especially problematic in low-data regimes. This “Less is More” phenomenon is a defining characteristic of the few-shot setting, diminishing as more samples become available. To address this, we propose a simple yet effective soft-masking method, Augmented Feature Importance Adjustment (AFIA), which estimates feature importance from augmented data to mitigate the issue. By establishing the cohesive link from channel bias to its consequence of extreme feature redundancy, this work provides a foundational principle for few-shot representation transfer and a practical method for developing more robust few-shot learning algorithms.

title: Spatio-Temporal Graph-Based Grid Integration of Large-Scale Electric Vehicle With Heterogeneous Charging Flexibility Aggregation
authors: Shuying Lai, Zhao Yang Dong, Yuechuan Tao, Jing Qiu, Tianjing Wang, Zhijun Zhang, Xianzhuo Sun, Junhua Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: While Electric Vehicles (EVs) have the potential to contribute to low-carbon transportation, the burgeoning adoption of EVs and uncoordinated charging can place a significant burden on the power grid. This paper addresses the challenges of the integration of massive EVs into smart grids by introducing a comprehensive framework with heterogeneous flexibility aggregation. First, a Spatio-Temporal Heterogeneous Graph Neural Network (STH-GNN) is proposed to accurately predict EV charging demands by analyzing complex spatio-temporal relationships. Furthermore, a bi-level coordinated charging scheduling framework optimizes grid operations and individual EV charging schedules, relieving grid burden and enhancing efficient energy use based on the STH-GNN prediction. It solves the gaps between prediction errors and real-time actual charging demand. Additionally, the EV flexibility set is modelled to effectively aggregate heterogeneous EV resources, allowing for optimized deployment of large-scale EV assets by constructing the inner approximation of the Minkowski sum of the individual flexibility sets. The proposed method is verified in simulation, utilizing data from a realistic urban setting with diverse EV charging behaviours and grid conditions. Simulations demonstrate the effectiveness of the STH-GNN in accurately forecasting EV charging demands across different times and locations. The bi-level charging scheduling framework successfully manages grid burden while showcasing significant improvements in operational efficiency and cost reduction. Results validate the proposed model’s robustness and scalability, proving its potential applicability in real-world smart grid environments.

title: Book Review: “Advanced Signal Integrity for High-Speed Digital Designs” by Stephen Hall and Howard Heck
authors: Benoit Derat
abstract: This book is an excellent advanced reference for engineers working on highspeed digital design and signal integrity. It combines rigorous theory with a strong practical focus and uses clear visual explanations to connect electromagnetic concepts to the real challenges of multigigabit systems.

title: PADReg: Physics-Aware Deformable Registration Guided by Contact Force for Ultrasound Sequences
authors: Yimeng Geng, Mingyang Zhao, Fan Xu, Guanglin Cao, Gaofeng Meng, Hongbin Liu
abstract: Ultrasound deformable registration estimates spatial transformations between pairs of deformed ultrasound images, which is crucial for capturing biomechanical properties and enhancing diagnostic accuracy in diseases such as thyroid nodules and breast cancer. However, ultrasound deformable registration remains highly challenging, especially under large deformation. The inherently low contrast, heavy noise and ambiguous tissue boundaries in ultrasound images severely hinder reliable feature extraction and matching. Existing methods often suffer from poor anatomical alignment and lack physical interpretability. To address the problem, we propose PADReg, a physics-aware deformable registration framework guided by contact force. PADReg leverages synchronized contact force measured by robotic ultrasound systems as a physical prior to constrain the registration. Specifically, instead of directly predicting deformation fields, we first construct a pixel-wise stiffness map utilizing the multi-modal information from contact force and ultrasound images. The stiffness map is then combined with force data to estimate a dense deformation field, through a lightweight physics-informed module inspired by Hooke's law. This design enables PADReg to achieve physically plausible registration with better anatomical alignment than previous methods relying solely on image similarity. Experiments on in-vivo datasets demonstrate that it attains a HD95 of 12.90, which is 21.34% better than state-of-the-art methods. The source code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/evelynskip/PADReg</uri>.

title: Fault Diagnosis for Power Inverter Based on Feature Transformation with Imbalanced Samples
authors: Quan Sun, Jiahao Dong, Zhijie Zhang
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: The lack of fault characteristics and sample quantity information will seriously lead to the fault diagnosis effect of motor drive system. In order to mine deep fault information and improve fault detection accuracy, a fault diagnosis strategy based on WGAN-GP data enhancement and WVD image feature extraction is proposed in this paper. Firstly, the frequency spectrum characteristics of the three-phase current signal are obtained by FFT algorithm. Meanwhile, the wasserstein generative adversarial network with gradient penalty (WGAN-GP) is used to generate sufficient data of fault samples. Subsequently, the samples under each fault mode are subjected to Wigner-Ville distribution (WVD) which can obtain two-dimensional image feature. Finally, the WVD fault features are classified by CNN model to identify the fault modes. The experimental results show that, compared with other feature transformation methods such as continuous wavelet transform (CWT) and markov transition fields (MTF), the identification results can reach to 99.77% by the proposed method, which verifies the feasibility and superiority under unbalanced sample conditions.

title: Asymptotic Analysis of the Resource Queueing System with Rare Changes in the States of a Random Environment
authors: Svetlana Moiseeva, Ekaterina Pankratova, Irina Turenova
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: This article examines a heterogeneous queuing system featuring three random-volume request streams for two types of resources. One of these streams generates requests for both resource types. The intensities of incoming streams are influenced by the state of a Markov random environment, and the duration a request occupies a resource is a random positive variable characterized by an arbitrary distribution function. Additionally, the time required for resource requests is also a positive random variable with specified probability distribution functions. The investigation employs the method of asymptotic analysis, assuming extremely rare state changes in the random environment. As a result, a bivariate characteristic function for the probability distribution of total occupied resources volumes is derived.

title: Assessing Large Language Models on Zero-Shot Poetry Summarization Using ROUGE, BERT, and LLM-as-a-Judge Metrics
authors: Md. Ismiel Hossen Abir, Nayeema Ferdous, Afsara Tasnim, Nabiha Mustaqeem
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Poetry summarization presents unique challenges for Large Language Models due to its dense use of metaphor, imagery, rhythm, and stylistic elements. This study analyzes the zero-shot techniques for poem summarization using the five state-of-the-art Large Language Models (LLMs), such as Gemini 2.5 Flash, Groq Mini, DistilBART CNN-12-6, LLaMA 3.3 70B Versatile, and Moonshot AI Kimi K2 Instruct. This study also evaluated the summaries using ROUGE-1, ROUGE-2, ROUGEL, and BERTScore to measure both lexical overlap and semantic similarity. After that, use a novel LLM-as-a-Judge approach where evaluate summaries on content similarity, quality, completeness, accuracy, and overall match, and average score for each of the LLMs. Experimental results show that DistilBART CNN12-6 achieves the highest ROUGE and BERTScore performance, while Gemini 2.5 Flash achieves the best performance in LLM-as-a-judge evaluation. This study provides a clear and scalable way to evaluate poem summarization. Overall, this study shows that zero-shot techniques in LLMs can effectively generate high-quality poem summaries.

title: Delay-Bounded Adaptive MAC for IEEE 802.15.4e DSME Networks: Enhancing Resilience under Bursty and Dynamic IoT Traffic
authors: Sonali Anand, Alekhya Gorrela, Raziur Rahman, Nikumani Choudhury, Anakhi Hazarika, Dipamani Choudhury, Tamoghna Ojha
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: IEEE 802.15.4e’s Deterministic and Synchronous Multi-channel Extension (DSME) provides time-slotted and channel-hopping MAC enhancements for Internet of Things (IoT) applications. A key feature of DSME is its structured superframe comprising Contention Access Periods (CAPs) and Contention-Free Periods (CFPs). CAPs support dynamic slot negotiation and event-driven traffic using CSMA/CA, while CFPs handle scheduled transmissions. To optimize throughput and energy efficiency, DSME introduces static CAP Reduction (CR), which minimizes CAPs within a multi-superframe. However, under dynamic and bursty traffic, this static approach results in significant queue buildup and delay. This paper proposes a novel Selective CAP Preservation mechanism, that is, an adaptive MAC mechanism that preserves CAPs in selected superframes based on delay-aware feedback and enhances the resilience of DSME networks. The approach monitors CAP-related queue lengths and activates additional CAPs when the delay exceeds a pre-defined threshold. Simulation results demonstrates that the proposed scheme contributes to the resilient MAC-layer design and significantly reduces packet delay, channel usage and energy consumption compared to static CAP Reduction.

title: Reconfigurable Microwave Hybrid Systems Via Magnetization-Tuned Photon-Magnon Coupling
authors: Sachin Verma, Garima Yadav, Abhishek Maurya, Rajeev Singh, Biswanath Bhoi
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: Hybrid photon-magnon systems are emerging as key enablers for reconfigurable quantum technologies, including quantum information processing and sensing. In this study, we investigate the influence of saturation magnetization (Ms) on the strength of photon-magnon coupling (PMC) in a planar structure that integrates a Yttrium Iron Garnet (YIG) thin film. The system features an inverted octagonal-ring resonator (IORR) on the Printed Circuit Board (PCB) ground plane, with the YIG film positioned on a microstrip line fabricated on the top layer. By tuning Ms from 175 mT to 90 mT, we achieve controlled modulation of PMC at room temperature. To explain the observed behavior, we present a semiclassical electromagnetic model that highlights the central role of spin density in governing photon-magnon interactions. These findings establish Ms as a crucial parameter for engineering tunable hybrid systems for next-generation quantum devices.

title: DynaTAS: Dynamic Time Aware Shaper Configurations in 5G-TSN Converged Network
authors: Prabharoop C C, Subhasri Duttagupta, Deepak Nadig
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: With emerging Industry 5.0 applications, such as smart healthcare and industrial automation, the need for reliable, low-latency communications is increasingly crucial. IEEE Time-Sensitive Networking (TSN) standards address deterministic communications by using mechanisms such as Time-Aware Shaper (TAS) but are currently available mainly on Ethernet. Integration of TSN with 5G wireles networks is capable of providing determinism in communications, but introduces complications in the implementation of rigorous delay constraints. These networks are dynamic and require frequent TAS reconfigurations to adjust to changing topologies and traffic flows, which is an NP-hard problem. This paper proposes DynaTAS, a Reinforcement Learning-based approach, that determines TAS configurations ensuring bounded latency and stable jitter, to support next-generation smart healthcare networks in a 5G-TSN converged environment. DynaTAS computes efficient time-aware schedules that dynamically adjust to network conditions. A detailed performance evaluation in OMNeT++ simulations demonstrates that the proposed solution is able to reduce latency and jitter by up to 95.7% and 98.5%, respectively, compared to a static RL baseline approach.

title: Optimal Fleet Sizing for On-Demand Urban Air Mobility Services Using Queueing-Theoretical Approach
authors: Byeong Tak Park
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Urban Air Mobility (UAM) offers a solution to alleviate traffic congestion by providing on-demand mobility services in urban airspace. Due to the inherently stochastic nature of on-demand requests, maintaining high service avail-ability-particularly vehicle availability at vertiports-poses a significant challenge in planning on-demand UAM services. In this study, we develop a closed Jackson network model with passenger loss to model the dynamics of on-demand UAM systems. We formulate a profit-maximization problem to optimize fleet size while ensuring minimum service availability. The proposed mathematical formulation explicitly incorporates demand uncertainty and evaluates the trade-off between proactive fleet provisioning and reactive passenger compensation strategies. We present an iterative exact search solution method to compute optimal fleet size and demonstrate its effectiveness in a Seoul City case study, where a fleet of 40 vehicles maximizes profit in the baseline. We further show that incorporating reactive passenger compensation strategies increases optimal fleet sizes (to 48 and 55 vehicles for moderate and high compensation levels, respectively) to maintain the target service availability, at the cost of significantly reduced profitability. These findings provide quantitative insights for UAM fleet operators and policymakers planning on-demand mobility services.

title: A Pragmatic Approach to Modular Design of Frequency Generator Electronics for Radar Applications
authors: Shruti Sinha, Srishti Srivastava, Nilesh Dineshbhai Makwana, Venkata Reddy Gaddam, Mukeshkumar K Patel, Ch V Narasimha Rao
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a pragmatic and modular design approach to frequency generation tailored for airborne SAR systems. The design leverages a multi module architecture comprising of phase locked loops (PLLs), frequency translation modules comprising of multipliers and mixer to ensure wide tunability, high spectral purity with ease of integration. The modules are realized on low-cost hybrid and FR4 substrate and utilizes commercial off the shelf components. Developed compact modular prototype exploits basic <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 M H z}$</tex> as reference source and caters to the requirement of 750 MHz clock signal, Xband local oscillator signal, transmit chirp modulated signal at X-band with 675 MHz bandwidth. Phase locked loops usage adds reconfigurability in modular design approach. The results validate the performance, demonstrating the low phase noise, frequency agility and modularity under operational environment.

title: 3D Self-Supervised Pre-Training and 3D-to-2D Transfer Learning for Brain Tumor MRI Classification
authors: Kalyan Kanti Halder, Iffat Ara Babli
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: The labeled MRI datasets for brain tumors are generally very small, while there are a lot of unlabeled 3D scans. We introduce an in-domain, cross-dimensional self-supervised pipeline: we first train a 3D masked autoencoder (MAE) on multimodal BraTS2020 volumes and then deterministically transfer the trained 3D encoder to 2D slice classification through weight deflation (depth-averaging patch-embedding filters while directly transforming Transformer weights). We then fine-tune the resulting 2D ViT on two slice-level benchmarks (Kaggle four-class; Figshare/Cheng three-class).On the Kaggle dataset, the deflated initialization gives the most advantage during the low-label regime: at 10% labels it boosts test macro-F1 by 0.125 over DINO and 0.120 over random initialization, and it gets macro-F1 ≥0.80 with 25% labels (while DINO and random reach ≥0.80 at 50%). In the course of 3D pretraining, the optimal masking ratio is governed by the scale of unlabeled data: in the case of 50 BraTS instances, r =0.75 results in the lowest validation loss (0.0318), whereas in the case of 100– 200 instances the optimum shifts towards r =0.50 (best 0.0207 at 200), coming close to the full-run best (0.0201). On Cheng, 5-fold cross-validation demonstrates steady improvements over random initialization (macro-F1 0.8171±0.0128 vs. 0.7857± 0.0117). All in all, slightly performed 3D MAE pretraining on BraTS produces a domain-aligned, label-efficient initializer for 2D brain tumor classification, cutting down on the dependency on ImageNet weights.

title: Regulation Capability Assessment and Enhancement for Coordinated Renewable Energy and Storage Systems
authors: Zhang Jiangfeng, Lu Min, Wang Tianyu, Lei Lingyao, Qi Xiao
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Mechanical Engineering and Power Engineering (MEPE)
abstract: As carbon neutrality targets accelerate renewable penetration, the pronounced stochasticity and volatility of wind and solar generation erode their reliability as regulation resources. This paper proposes a storage-coordinated framework to assess and enhance the regulation capability of renewables. A probabilistic forecasting scheme yields the plant-level distribution of up-regulation capacity; co-optimized storage control actively reshapes this distribution, markedly narrowing the confidence interval under a fixed confidence level. A joint probabilistic model linking dispatch set-points and realizable regulation is further constructed to assess tracking accuracy for secondary frequency regulation (AGC). Comparative analysis of pre- and postcoordination confidence intervals quantifies the accuracy gains attributable to storage, thereby supporting risk-aware scheduling and resource allocation. Simulation results demonstrate that, relative to conventional approaches, the proposed method delivers superior performance in both available regulation capacity and regulation accuracy.

title: Decoding Cyber Threats: Advancement in AI-Powered Vulnerability Detection
authors: Shahriar Arefin Zummon, Fahim Ahmed
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Web application vulnerabilities are omnipresent and a source of critical threat in our digital data driven world, demanding more advanced defense mechanisms. This paper presents a groundbreaking comparative study that puts the power of advanced transformer-based deep learning to the test against six major vulnerability types: SQL Injection, XSS, CSRF, SSRF, Clickjacking, and Broken Authentication. Through rigorous evaluation of six state-of-the-art models from general-purpose giants such as BERT to specialized, code-aware architectures-we demonstrate that GraphCodeBERT emerges as the best option, achieving a near-perfect 99.57% accuracy by leveraging graph-based representations to understand the code structure; all the while significantly outperforming other models including its cousin CodeBERT (95.21%) and further proving that how a model "sees" the code is of paramount significance. Our analysis reveals that while certain threats like CSRF and Clickjacking are detected with 100% precision across the board, we also identify DistilBERT as offering an impressive 98.06% accuracy with remarkable efficiency for resource-conscious environments. By integrating CVSS severity scores and LIME explain ability, we provided not just detection but interpretable and actionable insights, achieving 100% recall on critical vulnerabilities. This work positively shows that code specific, structure aware Al is the future of automated security, providing a blueprint for selecting optimal models that balance cutting-edge accuracy with practical efficiency to strengthen the next generation of production security systems.

title: A Comparative Machine Learning Framework for Multi-Class Crop Recommendation using Soil and Weather Data
authors: Sayedur Rahman, Touhid Alam, Shusmita Anjum Aziz, Mohammad Sakib Mahmood, Md Kishor Morol, Abir Ahmed, Abdullah Al Noman
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Accurate crop recommendation is pivotal for enhancing agricultural productivity amid climate change. This study develops a comparative machine learning framework for real-time crop recommendation based on environmental and soil parameters. Using a dataset of 2200 instances, we evaluated several models, including Support Vector Machine (SVM), Logistic Regression (LR), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Neural Network (NN), and AdaBoost. After systematic hyperparameter tuning with GridSearchCV, the KNN model emerged as the top performer, achieving an exceptional testing accuracy of 99.77%, with corresponding precision, recall, and F1 scores of 99.78%, 99.77%, and 99.77% respectively. Notably, the KNN model demonstrated remarkable computational efficiency, requiring only 0.0037 seconds for execution, making it highly suitable for real-world, on-demand applications. Feature importance analysis identified rainfall, nitrogen, and humidity as the most critical factors for accurate predictions. Our results significantly outperform previous studies and underscore the potential of a well-tuned KNN model to provide precise, low-cost, and rapid crop recommendations, advancing the goals of precision agriculture.

title: A Behavioral Trust-Based Framework for Multi-Attack Detection in Social Internet of Things
authors: Priya Mukherjee, Surajit Kumar Roy, Chandan Giri
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: The Social Internet of Things introduces a model in which smart objects build social-like connections to enhance the efficiency of service interaction and discovery. While this interconnectivity offers numerous advantages, it also introduces significant security challenges particularly trust-oriented attacks that threaten network reliability. This paper proposes a streamlined Trust Management (TM) solution designed to detect a broad range of trust-based threats, including Ballot Stuffing, On-Off behavior, Self-Promotion by analyzing patterns within a signed trust graph. Our method leverages topological and behavioral indicators which serve as input features for a machine learning model tasked with distinguishing malicious interactions from legitimate ones. The proposed framework demonstrates strong effectiveness in accurately identifying trust anomalies, thereby contributing to the enhancement of security and reliability in SIoT systems.

title: Compact Wideband Circularly Polarized Antenna Using an All-Dielectric Partially Reflecting Surface
authors: Khushboo Singh, Dushmantha N. Thalakotuna, Karu P. Esselle
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a simplified approach to design compact, broadband circularly polarized low-profile resonantcavity antennas with partially reflecting surfaces made out of only a single dielectric material. A corner-truncated circularly polarized microstrip patch antenna is used to excite the cavity of the resonant-cavity antenna. Gain enhancement of over 8 dB across all frequencies between 16 GHz to 25 GHz is achieved by using a high-permittivity dielectric partially reflecting surfaces, which is flat at the bottom and has a stair-case profile on the top. A notable performance improvement is achieved in the proposed resonant-cavity antenna with a peak gain of 17 dBi, peak aperture efficiency of 77.2 % at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 7. 8 ~ G H z}$</tex>, and a significantly lower sidelobe level in the operating bandwidth. A simulated peak gain of 17.0 dBi with a 3 dB gain bandwidth of 33.33 % and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3} \text{dB}$</tex> axial ratio bandwidth of over 18 % is obtained. The aperture diameter of the proposed resonant-cavity antenna is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.5 \lambda_{0}$</tex> and the overall height of the antenna is only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.95 \lambda_{0}$</tex> at the design frequency of 20 GHz.

title: Origami-Inspired Reconfigurable Pyramidal Horn Antenna for Dynamic Beam Shaping in Radar Applications
authors: Nallamothu Sree Manasa, Shubhanshi Jain, Prithvisha Gupta, Manas Sarkar, Chinmoy Saha
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a novel reconfigurable pyramidal horn antenna inspired by origami principles, designed to operate at 1.3 GHz within the L-band. The antenna integrates a conventional pyramidal horn with a mechanically adaptive fan-fold origami structure along both the E and H planes, enabling real-time beam reconfiguration without the need for active circuitry. By varying the flare angles through structural transformation, the antenna achieves tunable gain and beam directionality, offering measured gains up to 15 dBi in the E-plane and 15 dBi in the H-plane, with beam steering capability of up to 30°. Both single-sided and double-sided flaring configurations demonstrate effective control over radiation characteristics. The design offers high directivity, mechanical flexibility, reducing hardware cost and compact deployability, making it highly suitable for radar and communication systems.

title: Downlink WBAN Actuation via RSMA: Exact Error Analysis for Nakagami-m Fading
authors: Gunjit Arora, Satya Kumar Vankayala, Manoj Thomas
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: The growing prevalence of chronic diseases in an aging global population demands continuous monitoring of patients’ health through wearable sensors. In closed-loop healthcare systems, wireless body area networks (WBANs) facilitate this by enabling a body control unit to collect data from sensors and, after processing, transmit control signals to on-body actuators in response to abnormal health conditions. However, ensuring the reliable reception of these commands is challenging due to dynamic human postures, strong intra- and inter-WBAN interference, and the computational burden of interference-cancellation operations in multiple-access schemes. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a downlink WBAN framework based on rate-splitting multiple access (RSMA), wherein the control message consists of a single composite common stream (CCS) and multiple private streams (PSs). This structure enables partial interference decoding and partial noise treatment while requiring only a single successive interference cancellation operation for CCS removal before PS detection, thereby reducing receiver complexity without compromising reliability. For a comprehensive evaluation, exact symbol error rate (SER) expressions are derived using a constellation-based approach for arbitrary quadrature amplitude modulation orders in both the CCS and each PS. Furthermore, closed-form average SER (ASER) expressions are derived over ordered Nakagami-m fading channels, and the analytical results are validated through extensive Monte Carlo simulations. Numerical results demonstrate that, for 12bit control signals, allocating 6 bits to the lowest-power PS and distributing the remaining 6 bits such that the higher-power PS receives the least and the CCS receives the rest achieves an ASER = 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−3</sup> with a maximum SNR gain of 12 dB compared to other combinations.

title: ERPL: Energy-Aware Multi-Metric RPL for Distributed 6G-IoT Networks
authors: Chandranath Kapat, Jayasree Sengupta, Sipra DasBit
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: The emerging 6G landscape envisions large-scale distributed Internet of Things (IoT) systems that integrate sensing, communication, and computation across resource-constrained devices. In such environments, achieving energy-efficient and low-latency connectivity is critical to sustain scalable and autonomous operations. The Routing Protocol for Low Power and Lossy Networks (RPL) has become a standard for IoT routing; however, its conventional Objective Functions (OF0 and MRHOF) face limitations in maintaining energy balance and minimizing delay, particularly in dense or dynamic deployments. To address these challenges, this paper proposes two enhanced versions of RPL, termed as Energy-saving RPL (ERPL1 and ERPL2). ERPL1 combines Expected Transmission Count (ETX), energy consumption, and hop count, while ERPL2 incorporates ETX, energy consumption, and latency as key metrics in the parent node selection process. Simulations conducted using the Cooja simulator under both static and mobile network conditions show that the proposed ERPL schemes outperform OF0, MRHOF, and the state-of-the-art ETXRE. Specifically, ERPL1 and ERPL2 demonstrate up to 41% lower average energy consumption, 17% higher Packet Delivery Ratio (PDR), and 23% reduced end-to-end delay across various network topologies. These improvements are achieved through better load balancing and optimized routing paths, making the proposed ERPL protocols highly suitable for energy-constrained and distributed IoT environments. The study highlights the promise of multi-metric, energy-aware routing as a foundation for sustainable and efficient connectivity in future 6G-enabled IoT networks.

title: Impact of Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface Failure on Network Security – Towards Identifying Potential Vulnerabilities in Future Telecom Networks
authors: Aakash Bansal
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) are composed of a two-dimensional array of active element loaded unit cells (UC) that can electronically change the phase response of an incoming wave. The spatial phase change of the incoming wave using RIS allows us to electronically manipulate and change the electromagnetic (EM) beam properties at will and hence, introduce a new degree of freedom. RIS has become an extremely popular subject of interest with significant investment towards its use in future 6 G networks. However, the presence of a large number of electronic elements makes them susceptible to failure that can introduce new security concerns within the mobile telecommunication network. This paper presents initial results on how the performance of a RIS is affected as the electronic components on board start failing which can lead to new network security concerns.

title: Domain Generalization Fault Diagnosis of Rotating Machinery Based on Multimodal Ensemble Learning
authors: Hongpeng Xiao, Zhe Cheng, Zhitao Xing, Niaoqing Hu, Guoji Shen, Yi Yang, Ruizhi Li, Duofu Wang
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Rotating machinery is one of the most common and critical types of machinery in engineering. This type of machinery often operates under harsh conditions for extended periods, which makes it prone to failure during use. Therefore, condition monitoring and fault diagnosis can help address issues promptly and reduce the risk of accidents in rotating machinery. Currently, vibration signals are the predominant single modality used for mechanical fault diagnosis. However, unimodal approaches often lack robustness and struggle to provide reliable diagnostics in practical industrial environments. Complementary data sources can provide unique insights into different aspects of physical degradation. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a multimodal domain generalization diagnostic method based on vibration and acoustic signals. First, the method extracts and fuses features using a feature extraction and fusion model. Then, it employs ensemble learning to achieve transfer diagnosis across different operating conditions. This approach effectively resolves issues of insufficient robustness in single-modal diagnosis and diagnosis under varying operating conditions.

title: Multimodal HD Mapping for Intersections by Intelligent Roadside Units
authors: Zhongzhang Chen, Miao Fan, Shengtong Xu, Mengmeng Yang, Kun Jiang, Xiangzeng Liu, Haoyi Xiong
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: High-definition (HD) semantic mapping of complex intersections poses significant challenges for traditional vehicle-based approaches due to occlusions and limited perspectives. This paper introduces a novel camera-LiDAR fusion framework that leverages elevated intelligent roadside units (IRUs). Additionally, we present RS-seq, a comprehensive dataset developed through the systematic enhancement and annotation of the V2X-Seq dataset. RS-seq includes precisely labelled camera imagery and LiDAR point clouds collected from roadside installations, along with vectorized maps for seven intersections annotated with detailed features such as lane dividers, pedestrian crossings, and stop lines. This dataset facilitates the systematic investigation of cross-modal complementarity for HD map generation using IRU data. The proposed fusion framework employs a two-stage process that integrates modality-specific feature extraction and cross-modal semantic integration, capitalizing on camera high-resolution texture and precise geometric data from LiDAR. Quantitative evaluations using the RS-seq dataset demonstrate that our multimodal approach consistently surpasses unimodal methods. Specifically, compared to unimodal baselines evaluated on the RS-seq dataset, the multimodal approach improves the mean Intersection-over-Union (mIoU) for semantic segmentation by 4% over the image-only results and 18% over the point cloud-only results. This study establishes a baseline methodology for IRU-based HD semantic mapping and provides a valuable dataset for future research in infrastructure-assisted autonomous driving systems.

title: Effects of Signal Remaining Time Information on Driver Reaction and Start-Up Behavior at Signalized Intersections
authors: Eunjin Choi, Hyangmi Han
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This study investigated the effects of providing red signal remaining time information via an in-vehicle information system (IVIS) on driver behavior at fixed-time signalized intersections. Two key behavioral metrics were examined: brake reaction time and start-up lost time. Field driving experiments were conducted along a predefined route comprising 13 intersections, with 4 fixed-time signalized intersections selected for detailed analysis. Each participant completed two test runs under different conditions: baseline driving without signal information and driving with a red signal countdown displayed on an in-vehicle screen. Brake reaction time was defined as the time difference between the signal phase change and the driver's brake release, whereas start-up lost time referred to the time from the onset of the green phase until the vehicle exceeded a threshold speed of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \text{km} / \mathrm{h}$</tex>. The results showed that providing signal information significantly reduced the brake reaction time from 0.41 s to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.036 \mathrm{s}(\mathrm{p}<0.05)$</tex>, demonstrating enhanced driver anticipation. However, the corresponding reduction in start-up lost time, from 1.8191 s to 1.6770 s, was not statistically significant <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{p}=0.134)$</tex>. However, the increase in anticipatory responses did not lead to premature vehicle movements, which indicates that the signal information improved readiness without inducing risky driving behavior. These findings support the potential of red signal countdown information to enhance driver responsiveness in intelligent transportation systems without compromising safety.

title: Using Artificial Intelligence as an Assistant in Information Systems Security Testing
authors: Vasili Vaskevich, Maksim Vinokurov, Tatsiana Melnikova, Daniil Shumilo
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: This paper provides an overview of the main functions of popular generative artificial intelligence chatbots: ChatGPT, Grok, DeepSeek, Gemini, and Copilot. Examples of the use of artificial intelligence in modern cybersecurity software solutions are considered. The results of using artificial intelligence as an assistant in vulnerability testing and analysis of information security logs are presented. The paper determines how these chatbots can assist novice information security specialists in identifying necessary actions for exploiting vulnerabilities and searching for suspicious entries in the saved logs of attacked servers. The main advantages and disadvantages of using AI-powered chatbots in the cybersecurity field are described.

title: Unveiling Cybersecurity Research Topics: A Hybrid Topic Modeling Framework
authors: Jothirmoy Sarker Shuvo, Abdus Salam, K. M. Imtiaz-Ud-Din
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Over the past two decades, a number of advancements have happened in the field of Cybersecurity, and several research papers have been published on this topic. However, this large collection of documents makes it difficult for researchers to extract valuable insights or identify the flow of advancements in this field. This study proposes an innovative approach to tackle this challenge by applying conventional topic models such as K-Means, Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA), and an advanced topic model BERTopic. These topic models are widely used unsupervised models capable of learning topics from large amounts of text documents. To improve the performance of conventional topic models a hybrid method was used, combining the LDA and K-Means algorithm. The dataset was collected from the arXiv repository using automated web-scraping, and it contains the abstracts from the papers published between 2005 and 2025. Experimental results show that after applying all models, each model excels on specific metrics but BERTopic provides the most balanced overall performance. Although the highest topic coherence is achieved by K-Means and LDA achieved the highest silhouette score, BERTopic shows the highest topic diversity along with competitive coherence and BERTopic successfully extracted the most distinct topics alongside semantically meaningful topics. In addition, this method can help researchers better understand and identify the flow of advancements in the field of cybersecurity.

title: A Dual-Recurrent Attention Architecture for High Recall Sentiment Analysis in Noisy Twitter Texts
authors: Arpita Saha Sukanna, Shafin Mahmood, Sayma Akter Trina, Provakar Sagor, Amirul Islam
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: The eponential growth of informal, sentiment-rich text across social-media platforms has intensified the demand for robust models capable of interpreting emotional polarity within short, context-fragmented expressions. This paper presents Deep Recurrent Attention Fusion (DRAF), a hybrid sequential architecture that combines an extra LSTM refinement layer with a Self-Attention Bidirectional LSTM (SA-BiLSTM) to improve contextual depth and recall sensitivity for sentiment detection in noisy tweet data. By using hierarchical attention weighting, the suggested model is able to capture both forward-backward temporal dependencies and subtle emotional transitions. A comparison with four other attention-based hybrids SA-BiLSTM + FNN, + RNN, + CNN, and + GRU, shows that the SA-BiLSTM + LSTM configuration achieves a better recall (0.83) and F1-score (0.7865), confirming its ability to detect subtle polarity shifts without overfitting. Its generalization and semantic consistency are further supported by statistical measures like R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> (0.0991) and MCC (0.553). DRAF is positioned as a lightweight yet potent substitute for transformer-heavy methods since it achieves high interpretability and efficiency with a notably smaller number of parameters. The experimental results provide a new paradigm for sentiment modeling in resource-constrained environments by demonstrating that dual recurrent depth under an attention modulation mechanism improves emotional context retention and recall performance. The study comes to the conclusion that DRAF bridges the gap between conventional recurrent networks and contemporary attention-based architectures by providing a human-aligned, recall-optimized framework for contextual sentiment interpretation.

title: Cross-Shaped Dual-Band Polarization-Insensitive Metamaterial Absorber for 5G mm-Wave Applications
authors: Jaiverdhan, Sumit Rautela, Ashish Kumar, M. M. Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a dual-band polarization-insensitive metamaterial absorber (MMA) designed for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5 G}$</tex> millimeter-wave applications at 25.2 GHz and 37.98 GHz, corresponding to key n258 and n260/n262 bands. The proposed absorber utilizes a hollow cross-shaped resonator patterned on an RO-3003 substrate with a metallic ground plane, achieving near-unity absorption of 99.6 % and 99.4 % at the two operating bands. Simulations confirm reflection coefficients of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{- 2 9. 5 7 ~ d B}$</tex> and -24.48 dB, indicating effective impedance matching at resonance. The design maintains high absorptivity across incident angles up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{4 5}^{\circ}$</tex> for both TE and TM polarizations. With a compact unit cell and substrate thickness of 0.8 mm, this absorber is suitable for EMC enhancement, mutual coupling reduction, and packaging of 5 G front-end modules operating in the mm-wave spectrum.

title: Denoising and Reconstruction of Vehicle Trajectories Using Millimeter Wave Radar Groups
authors: Chengmin Li, Junhua Wang, Tao Wang, Ting Fu, Qiangqiang Shangguan, Jiangtian Xue, Yuhang Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Reconstructing vehicle trajectories becomes significantly more challenging with the involvement of roadside millimeter-wave radars (MMRs), as the combination of noise, outliers, missing data, and inconsistencies in multi-dimensional observations (e.g., speed and position) complicates traditional methods. This paper proposes an optimization-based reconstruction method tailored for wide-area trajectories from MMR groups, handling complex anomalies while maintaining internal consistency across dimensions. The method incorporates a prior-guided mechanism comprising two key components: a masking strategy to address data missing caused by MMR blind zones, and a scaling factor to ensure adaptability and transferability across diverse operational scenarios. Extensive field experiments confirm the method's robustness and transferability. Ablation studies further highlight the critical roles of the masking strategy and scaling factor in managing anomalies and ensuring generalization. This work paves the way for further research in large-scale trajectory reconstruction and multi-sensor integration. The code for our method can be found at https://github.com/tuqing123/MMRsTraRec.

title: Enabling Multi-fabric Net Tracing: A Step Towards Holistic EDA System Integration
authors: Shweta Kumari, Rohit Kumar, Vidhi Bansal, Tapan Kumar Singh, Amit Kumar, Rudy Couget
publish: 2025 IEEE 6th International Women in Technology Conference (WINTECHCON)
abstract: As modern electronic systems grow in complexity, driven by advancements in heterogeneous integration, they present significant challenges in ensuring seamless connectivity across multiple design fabrics, encompassing silicon IC, package substrates, printed circuit boards (PCBs), and multiple inter-connected ICs or sub-fabrics. The Electronic Design Automation (EDA) industry is driven to develop unified, system-aware tools. This paper discusses the necessity of multi-fabric net tracing (tracing a single net across multiple IC sub-fabrics, package, and board) and how such capabilities address critical system-level design bottlenecks. We highlight current EDA limitations, propose a solution for integrated tracing support, and argue its significance for faster verification, better co-design, and improved manufacturability.

title: TreeCSP: An Efficient Way to Generate Household Relationships for Population Synthesis
authors: Duc Minh La, Hai L. Vu
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Population synthesis plays a crucial role in AgentBased Models (ABMs) and transportation planning by creating realistic representations of individuals and households. However, existing methods often lack flexibility in modeling complex household relationships and integrating domain knowledge without relying on rigid assumptions. Thus, we introduce Tree-based Chained Sampling Pools (TreeCSP), a novel framework that organizes household and person generation through a hierarchical relationship tree and multi-step sampling. TreeCSP enables both fully data-driven learning and the seamless integration of domain knowledge, improving scalability, flexibility, and explainability. Using the Victorian Integrated Survey of Travel and Activity (VISTA) dataset as ground truth, we benchmarked two variants of TreeCSP: CSP-Seed, which uses the VISTA data directly, and CSP-BN, which incorporates Bayesian Networks (BNs), against two widely used methods: Iterative Proportional Updating (IPU) and Conditional Tabular Generative Adversarial Network (CTGAN). Results show that TreeCSP consistently outperforms the baselines, with CSP-BN achieving the lowest Jensen-Shannon Distance (JSD) across both household and relationship attributes. Moreover, TreeCSP effectively captures rare and complex household structures while maintaining model transparency and avoiding infeasible combinations.

title: Digital Twin Simulation and Test Platform for VCTS: A Fundamental Study of Train Multi-Body Dynamics Modeling
authors: Shirun Tian, Fei Yan, Arnab Majumdar, Xiaohua Huang
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: The Virtual Coupling Train Set (VCTS) is transitioning from conceptual frameworks to on-site applications, demanding rigorous test verification. Digital twin technology, through virtual models highly map physical entities, simulates VCTS's realistic operational scenarios, reducing on-site testing costs and risks while overcoming the inadequacies of traditional VCTS test platforms in terms of authenticity and accuracy. However, the key challenge in this process lies in developing VCTS train dynamics models with sufficient fidelity for testing. This paper presents a structure-dependent multi-body dynamics (MBD) modeling methodology implemented through dedicated dynamics analysis software. First, longitudinal-transverse mechanical interactions are analyzed to clarify force propagation mechanisms in VCTS. Secondly, physics-based modeling of critical components is implemented using Beijing Metro Line 11's design parameters within specialized dynamics software. Thirdly, metro-line-specific operational simulations validate the model's accuracy. The developed train dynamics model achieves precise characterization of microscale dynamic parameters during VCTS operations, providing a dynamic modeling basis for the subsequent VCTS digital twin simulation test platform.

title: View-Aware High-Precision Vehicle Tracking Using Roadside RGB-D Camera Network
authors: Jingda Chen, Hanyang Zhuang, Chunxiang Wang, Ming Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: The infrastructure-based perception system utilizing a distributed RGB-D camera network effectively addresses challenges related to occlusion and limited fields of view. This innovative approach has been validated to offer high-precision vehicle tracking using model-registration-based methods. However, as the vehicle moves across different sensor units, fragmented and incomplete point clouds are generated, negatively impacting tracking accuracy. To overcome this issue, this paper introduces a view-aware, high-precision vehicle tracking framework for infrastructure-based RGB-D camera network. The primary contribution is the development of an adaptive model crop algorithm that takes into account the perspectives of roadside RGB-D cameras to eliminate misalignment between scan and model. Subsequently, the cropped model and scan data undergo point-to-model registration, followed by an Extended Kalman Filter employing a constant turn rate and velocity motion model. Our method significantly enhances model-observation overlap, reduces registration failures in highly fragmented point clouds, and provides more robust pose estimates. Furthermore, we conducted experiments in practical scenarios to verify the efficacy of our approach.

title: Structural Digital Twin for the Health Monitoring of Aircraft Wings
authors: Chenyufan Hu, Teng Wang, Yongbo Li
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Intelligent, autonomous, and reliable structural health management for aircraft has been a critical focus in the aerospace industry for a long time. This study proposes a digital twin-based structural health management (SHM) framework for aircraft wing structures, integrating physical modeling and data-driven approaches to enable real-time structural monitoring and fatigue damage prediction. A fatigue crack growth model based on linear elastic fracture mechanics (LEFM) is developed, and stress intensity factors are computed using finite element analysis.with Gaussian process regression employed to construct surrogate models for damage evaluation. Subsequently, a state-space model is introduced for model updating, and short-term load forecasting is realized using an ARIMA-based approach to support fatigue life prediction. The effectiveness of the proposed framework is validated based on a set of numerical studies, showing its potentials for practical engineering.

title: Lightweight Multi-Agent-Based Service Migration Strategy for Cross-Network Scenarios
authors: Lingqiu Zeng, Hao Yang, Qingwen Han, Xinyue Song, Lei Ye
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: In the cross-network collaboration scenario, vehicles can request services through different communication methods. However, the fast movement of vehicles and the complexity of the network environment pose significant challenges. During the cross-network service migration process, effective migration strategies must be developed to ensure service quality and avoid service interruptions. This paper formulates cross-network service migration as an optimization problem and proposes a candidate node selection method that integrates graph search and k-nearest neighbors (k-NN) within the Markov Decision Process (MDP) framework. Building upon this, a lightweight model based on multi-agent, called LMA-D3QN (Lightweight Multi-Agent Double Deep Q-Network), is constructed. Simulation results show that the proposed method effectively improves the overall efficiency of service migration while reducing computational overhead.

title: Safety-Critical Traffic Simulation with Guided Latent Diffusion Model
authors: Mingxing Peng, Ruoyu Yao, Xusen Guo, Yuting Xie, Xianda Chen, Jun Ma
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Safety-critical traffic simulation plays a crucial role in evaluating autonomous driving systems under rare and challenging scenarios. However, existing approaches often generate unrealistic scenarios due to insufficient consideration of physical plausibility and suffer from low generation efficiency. To address these limitations, we propose a guided latent diffusion model (LDM) capable of generating physically realistic and adversarial safety-critical traffic scenarios. Specifically, our model employs a graph-based variational autoencoder (VAE) to learn a compact latent space that captures complex multi-agent interactions while improving computational efficiency. Within this latent space, the diffusion model performs the denoising process to produce realistic trajectories. To enable controllable and adversarial scenario generation, we introduce novel guidance objectives that drive the diffusion process toward producing adversarial and behaviorally realistic driving behaviors. Furthermore, we develop a sample selection module based on physical feasibility checks to further enhance the physical plausibility of the generated scenarios. Extensive experiments on the nuScenes dataset demonstrate that our method achieves superior adversarial effectiveness and generation efficiency compared to existing baselines while maintaining a high level of realism. Our work provides an effective tool for realistic safety-critical scenario simulation, paving the way for more robust evaluation of autonomous driving systems.

title: Enhancing CBG based HARQ Operations through Network Coding in RAN Systems
authors: Srihari Das Sunkada Gopinath, Satya Kumar Vankayala, Jeevan Prakash Ballem, Seungil Yoon
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: Our research focuses on improving network performance as we move towards 6G. In the context of physical layer Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request (HARQ) transmissions, the current practice involves retransmitting the entire Code Block Group (CBG) even if a single Code Block (CB) experiences error, thereby consuming significant downlink resources. Furthermore, it is imperative to focus on the delays introduced by HARQ retransmissions, particularly in delay sensitive applications. To address these challenges, we propose a Random Linear Network Coding (RLNC) based approach for CBG based HARQ operations, which effectively reduces downlink data resources through Network Coding and modified HARQ feedback approaches. Additionally, we introduce a tailored approach for delay-sensitive application traffic, aiming to reduce HARQ transmission delays in lossy environments. These innovative approaches facilitate a seamless transition from uncoded systems to coded systems, resulting in cost savings in terms of radio resources. The theoretical insights presented in this paper are validated and further explored through simulations of the network under different packet loss scenarios. The proposed conservative scheme achieves a 6% to 33% reduction in downlink data resources in reception loss scenarios of 1% to 10%, whereas the aggressive scheme effectively reduces average HARQ data delivery delay by 13% to 37% under similar conditions when compared to the 5G New Radio (NR).

title: Implicit Calibration of Point-of-Gaze on Scene Image Using Smooth-Pursuit Eye Movements for Driver Monitoring
authors: Riku Nakazawa, Satoru Takenaka, Yuya Homma, Ariki Sato, Junichiro Kuwahara, Kentaro Takemura
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Advanced driver assistance systems that estimate driver attention have been actively studied to reduce traffic accidents and improve driving comfort. Although significant progress has been made in the field of object detection, it is difficult to estimate driver attention effectively because there are several issues in calculating the point-of-gaze on scene images. Generally, user calibration is performed in advance; however, this is not a practical solution. Therefore, we propose implicit calibration using smooth-pursuit eye movements, in which the point-of-gaze is corrected using an optimal correction vector and regression. Experiments were conducted on a test course and public roads to demonstrate the effectiveness and feasibility of the proposed method while also highlighting its current limitations.

title: A Modification of the Integrated Risk Rating Mechanism for Justifying the Aggregation of Component Pairs
authors: Anastasiya Rey
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: It is imperative to acknowledge that any organisational system is inherently associated with a multitude of risks and elements that are intrinsic to its characterisation. However, the vast majority of studies devoted to risk management, in particular risk assessment, in complex organisational systems suggest dealing with risks in isolation, without taking into account their mutual influence and the interrelationship of their components, and resort to summarising local risk assessments. Conversely, disregarding the structural composition of system components during risk assessment can result in an inaccurate evaluation of the risk entity, consequently leading to the implementation of inappropriate management strategies. In this regard, a comprehensive approach to assessing the risk of an organisational system based on its structure is required. In the context of risk interdependencies, the most promising approach for this task is the integrated rating mechanism. The rules for constructing this mechanism allow the tree structure to be manipulated, thereby taking into account the influence of system elements on each other and on the integral risk. However, it should be noted that risk structures rarely represent binary trees. It is important to note that difficulties may arise when using this mechanism in order to justify the choice of a pair of components for aggregation. The present paper puts forward a generalised modification of the classical integrated rating mechanism. The objective of the modification is to formalise the procedure for transitioning from a risk decomposition structure to a binary tree. The proposed algorithm is considered using the example of the risk of an aviation accident.

title: Delay-Optimized Communication Strategies for Safe Braking in Vehicle Platoons
authors: Sree Nithi K, Sopika R, Janani R, Bhuvana K, Ananthi G
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: Vehicle platooning, where multiple vehicles travel closely in a coordinated formation, relies heavily on Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) and Vehicle-to-Infrastructure (V2I) communications to ensure safety and stability. Emergency braking in vehicle platoons demands rapid and reliable communication to avoid collisions and maintain platoon integrity. However, in large platoons, the limited range of Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) communication can lead to delayed message reception for distant vehicles. This paper proposes a hybrid communication framework combining V2V and Vehicle-to-Infrastructure (V2I) mechanisms to overcome these limitations. A comprehensive mathematical model for communication delay analysis is derived, considering message propagation from vehicles to Roadside Units (RSUs), between RSUs, and from RSUs to vehicles. Theoretical formulations are validated through SUMO-based simulations, highlighting the improvement in message dissemination speed and braking synchronization across a ten-vehicle platoon. The proposed approach demonstrates that hybrid V2V + V2I communication significantly enhances platoon safety and responsiveness during emergency scenarios.

title: Gearbox Fault Diagnosis Method Based on EEMD-MDS-1DCNN
authors: Xiaomei Dong
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Gearboxes are widely used in electromechanical equipment such as wind turbines, hydroelectric generators, and coal mining machinery. Their failures may compromise both operational safety and economic efficiency. Fault diagnosis of gearboxes serves as an effective means to prevent catastrophic failures, enhance reliability, and reduce downtime and maintenance costs. To this end, this paper proposes a novel fault diagnosis method based on Ensemble Empirical Mode Decomposition (EEMD), Multidimensional Scaling (MDS), and a 1-Dimensional Convolutional Neural Network (1DCNN). First, the collected fault signals are decomposed via EEMD to extract multi-scale fault information. Subsequently, MDS is applied to reduce the dimensionality of the multi-modal components, retaining the most discriminative features. During this process, the multi-scale information is compressed into a lower-dimensional representation. Finally, the low-dimensional features are fed into the 1DCNN for fault diagnosis. The proposed method was experimentally validated on two standard gearbox datasets from Southeast University and Huazhong University of Science and Technology. The results demonstrate a diagnostic accuracy exceeding 99.5%, confirming the method’s effectiveness in gearbox fault diagnosis.

title: Path-Following Model Predictive Control for Autonomous E-Scooters
authors: David Meister, Robin Strässer, Felix Brändle, Marc Seidel, Benno Bassler, Nathan Gerber, Jan Kautz, Elena Rommel, Frank Allgöwer
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: In order to mitigate economical, ecological, and societal challenges in electric scooter (e-scooter) sharing systems, we develop an autonomous e-scooter prototype. Our vision is to design a fully autonomous prototype that can find its way to the next parking spot, high-demand area, or charging station. In this work, we propose a path-following model predictive control solution to enable localization and navigation in an urban environment with a provided path to follow. We design a closedloop architecture that solves the localization and path following problem while allowing the e-scooter to maintain its balance with a previously developed reaction wheel mechanism. Our model predictive control approach facilitates state and input constraints, e.g., adhering to the path width, while remaining executable on a Raspberry Pi 5. We demonstrate the efficacy of our approach in a real-world experiment on our prototype.

title: Safety at Autonomous Intersections Under Connection Loss: Connected and Onboard Local Planning
authors: Yingchen Hao, Hoam Chung, Hai L. Vu
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: With the emergence of connected autonomous vehicle (CAV) technologies, autonomous intersection management (AIM) has become a preferred approach to manage traffic flows at intersections. In our earlier work, we identified potential execution discrepancies between CAV local planners and global AIM systems. We have also developed a Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) based local planner to enhance safety under perturbation conditions. Building on these findings, this study further assesses safety in scenarios with connection losses by employing onboard motion predictors (OMPs) to compensate for information gaps caused by intermittent connectivity. Results show that our scheme maintains safety under modest connection loss using simple extrapolation prediction replacement while incorporating advanced OMP helps near-miss scenarios under severe connection loss.

title: Functionality Assessment Framework for Autonomous Driving Systems Using Subjective Networks
authors: Stefan Orf, Sven Ochs, Valentin Marotta, Oliver Conder, Marc René Zofka, J. Marius Zöllner
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: In complex autonomous driving (AD) software systems, the functioning of each system part is crucial for safe operation. By measuring the current functionality or operability of individual components an isolated glimpse into the system is given. Literature provides several of these detached assessments, often in the form of safety or performance measures. But dependencies, redundancies, error propagation and conflicting functionality statements do not allow for easy combination of these measures into a big picture of the functioning of the entire AD stack. Data is processed and exchanged between different components, each of which can fail, making an overall statement challenging. The lack of functionality assessment frameworks that tackle these problems underlines this complexity. This article presents a novel framework for inferring an overall functionality statement for complex component based systems by considering their dependencies, redundancies, error propagation paths and the assessments of individual components. Our framework first incorporates a comprehensive conversion to an assessment representation of the system. The representation is based on Subjective Networks (SNs) that allow for easy identification of faulty system parts. Second, the framework offers a flexible method for computing the system's functionality while dealing with contradicting assessments about the same component, dependencies, and redundancies. We discuss the framework's capabilities on real-life data of our AD stack with assessments of various components.

title: An fNIRS-Based Multi-Sensor Fusion Approach for Passenger Comfort Assessment
authors: Yang Zhao, Yewei Yu, Kecheng Shi, Ruoyu Deng, Hong Cheng
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This paper presents a multimodal framework for passenger comfort assessment in intelligent vehicles. By integrating functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS), vehicle dynamics data, and subjective ratings, we propose a Multi-Branch Joint Network (MBJNet) capable of extracting complementary features from heterogeneous sources. To address inter-subject variability, transfer learning and elastic weight consolidation are incorporated, enabling improved generalization across passengers with limited labeled data. Experimental evaluations on a real-vehicle platform demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms existing baselines in both within-subject and cross-subject classification tasks. Our approach achieves 91.73% accuracy in within-subject settings and 74.99% in crosssubject evaluations, highlighting its robustness and practical viability. This work provides a scalable and adaptive solution for comfort-aware intelligent cockpit systems.

title: Regulation-Aware Game-Theoretic Motion Planning for Autonomous Racing
authors: Francesco Prignoli, Francesco Borrelli, Paolo Falcone, Mark Pustilnik
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This paper presents a regulation-aware motion planning framework for autonomous racing scenarios. Each agent solves a Regulation-Compliant Model Predictive Control problem, where racing rules—such as right-of-way and collision avoidance responsibilities—are encoded using Mixed Logical Dynamical constraints. We formalize the interaction between vehicles as a Generalized Nash Equilibrium Problem (GNEP) and approximate its solution using an Iterative Best Response scheme. Building on this, we introduce the Regulation-Aware Game-Theoretic Planner (RA-GTP), in which the attacker reasons over the defender's regulation-constrained behavior. This game-theoretic layer enables the generation of overtaking strategies that are both safe and non-conservative. Simulation results demonstrate that the RA-GTP outperforms baseline methods that assume non-interacting or rule-agnostic opponent models, leading to more effective maneuvers while consistently maintaining compliance with racing regulations.

title: A Multi-Scale Vehicle-Based Traffic Control Architecture via Deep Reinforcement Learning and CAVs Platooning
authors: Giacomo Basile, Cecilia Pasquale, Alberto Petrillo, Simona Sacone, Stefania Santini, Silvia Siri
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Connected and Automated Vehicles (CAVs) represent the key enabling technology for reshaping the mobility paradigm and contributing to more efficient, safe, and comfortable ground transportation systems. In this direction, CAVs can be leveraged to develop the vehicle-based control strategies for traffic dynamics regulation and congestion mitigation purposes. To this aim, by leveraging an enriched version of the Cell Transmission Model (CTM), this work proposes a Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) multi-scale control architecture, where: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$i)$</tex> a Deep Q-Network (DQN) controller, based on the effective traffic conditions, suggests the optimal speed profile and the desired spacing policy to be imposed on CAVs for moving in platoon formation; ii) a distributed PID controller, acting at the microscopic level, aims at properly driving the platoon motion according to the DQN-based traffic control policy. Numerical validations, performed on the real stretch of the A20 freeway in the Netherlands, demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed multi-scale control architecture in significant reducing the Total Travel Time with improvement of about 18% w.r.t. the uncontrolled traffic flow case.

title: A Conformal Textile Antenna for Frequency-Diverse Spectral Regularisation Based Tomographic Microwave Breast Imaging
authors: Athul O Asok, Anjitha V, Sukomal Dey
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: Tomographic microwave imaging (MWI) using the inverse scattering method (ISM) is gaining attention as a costeffective technique for early-stage breast tumour detection. Yet, ISM suffers from ill-posedness, high noise sensitivity and wavering stability. Bringing frequency diversity in the collected scan data, followed by spectral regularisation in the ISM algorithm, helps to overcome these shortfalls. However, frequency diversity requires a wideband antenna with acceptable gain over the operational range. In addition, imaging for breast tumour detection prefers a wearable scan mechanism for patient comfort, and hence, there is a need for antennas to be conformal. This paper contributes to this multi-constraint research objective by proposing a conformal wide-band textile antenna operating in a range from 1.7 GHz up to 11.3 GHz with a peak gain of 5.8 dBi and favourable gain throughout the intended band of operation. Suitability of the antenna for imaging application is confirmed through comprehensive analyses, thus establishing it as an effective front-end for regularisation-based ISM with spectral filtering on frequency-hopped data.

title: Design and Analysis of a 2×2 Circularly Polarized Microstrip Antenna Array Using Different Substrates for IoMT Applications
authors: Sasmita Kumari Nayak, Varigala Sai Charan Gupta, Sanjeev Kumar Mishra
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents the design and analysis of a 2×2 circularly polarized (CP) microstrip patch array antenna. The development begins with a linearly polarized patch, progresses to a CP-enabled single patch, and then to a four-element CP array using an innovative asymmetric corner truncation technique. The antenna's performance is evaluated on various substrates-felt, foam, FR4, and Rogers 5880-through simulation to compare axial ratio bandwidth (ARBW), gain, and radiation efficiency. A prototype is fabricated on an FR4 substrate with dimensions <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.76 \lambda_{0} \times 1.0 \lambda_{0} \times 0.0128 \lambda_{0}$</tex> at 2.4 GHz. Measured results show an impedance bandwidth of 3.76%, while simulated results indicate an overlapping ARBW of 1.25% and a gain of 5.5 dBi. With the compact size and optimized CP characteristics, the antenna can be used for Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) wireless applications.

title: LLM-Assisted Contrastive Learning for Robust Fault Diagnosis in Nuclear Power Plants
authors: Zongyu Xu, Hang Wang
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
