abstract: This study proposes a Cross-Modal Contrastive Learning (CMCL) framework for robust fault diagnosis in Nuclear Power Plants (NPPs), aiming to identify faults under data-scarce and previously unseen conditions. CMCL adopts a dual-encoder architecture to align time series (TS) data with natural language descriptions, consisting of an LSTM-based TS encoder and a frozen text encoder derived from the large language model (LLM) DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B. By optimizing a bidirectional contrastive loss, the model learns a semantically aligned embedding space in which data from different modalities but similar meanings are mapped closely together. This enables cross-modal recognition and reasoning without requiring explicit labels or fault samples. The proposed framework is evaluated in simulated operational scenarios of a pressurized water reactor (PWR), encompassing six fault types and one normal condition. Experimental results demonstrate that CMCL consistently outperforms traditional supervised LSTM models in both clean and noisy environments, maintaining high accuracy even under strong Gauss, Uniform and Salt-and-Pepper (SP) noise. In addition, CMCL provides interpretable similarity trajectories and quantifies uncertainty in fault identification, thereby enhancing diagnostic transparency and reliability. Overall, CMCL exhibits strong potential for accurate and interpretable fault diagnosis in complex industrial environments, offering an effective solution with enhanced robustness against noise and unseen conditions.

title: Wavelet-based Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Model for Rotating Machinery Fault Feature Enhancement
authors: Chao Teng, Zuogang Shang, Ruqiang Yan
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Rotating machinery fault feature detection under strong noise interference remains a significant challenge, especially for early-stage fault feature extraction. This paper proposes a novel feature enhancement framework based on a wavelet-based denoising diffusion probabilistic model (DDPM) to address this issue. Different from traditional denoising methods that rely on spectrum orthogonality assumptions, the proposed method reformulates the noise suppression problem as the attenuation of normal components while amplifying fault components. By constructing a non-orthogonal synthetic dataset by combining simulated fault impulses with real full life-cycle normal vibration signals, enabling effective DDPM training without requiring pure fault data. Experimental results on bearing full life-cycle data demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior fault enhancement performance compared to conventional techniques, particularly in few sample scenarios. The enhanced signals exhibit near-zero baselines during health states and significant fault-related harmonic amplification during degradation, validating the framework’s effectiveness for early fault detection.

title: Taxi Repositioning via LLM-Driven Multi-Agent Coordination and Experience Accumulation
authors: Hao Yu, Chonghao Zhang, Xiao Luo, Wenyu Yin, Zhe Liu, Jinyi Huang, Xuanyu Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Efficient taxi repositioning is essential to address the spatiotemporal mismatch between urban travel demand and vehicle supply. Traditional approaches often rely on heuristic rules or reinforcement learning, which suffer from limited generalization and poor interpretability. In this paper, we propose an LLM-driven framework for intelligent taxi repositioning that integrates three key components: (1) LLM-based decision reasoning, (2) spatiotemporal clustering for demand pattern abstraction, and (3) experience-driven responsibility assignment for adaptive policy optimization. A city-scale simulator is developed to evaluate the effectiveness of our approach. Experimental results show that our method achieves the highest response rate of 70.59%, the lowest average pickup time of 153.3 seconds, and the highest vehicle utilization rate of 48.94%, outperforming four baselines including clustering and optimization-based methods. Our simulator and code are publicly available at:https://github.com/cloverSaber/TrafficSimulator.

title: Load-Aware Adaptive Sampling for Efficient System Monitoring Across Time, Events, and Similarity
authors: Shay Horovitz, Guy Bar On, Yoav Rabinovitch
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Information Communication and Software Engineering (ICICSE)
abstract: Fixed-interval polling remains the de-facto approach for system monitoring, yet it wastes bandwidth during idle periods and still overlooks short-lived spikes. We present an integrated adaptive monitoring framework that unifies three complementary ideas—time-of-day adaptive sampling, cross-resource anomaly triggers, and cluster-based group-wise sampling—under a single, budget-aware scheduler. Implemented entirely with standard Performance Counters, the framework profiles historical traces to learn variability patterns, builds peak- and variance-aware clusters of processes, and reallocates samples on-the-fly when one resource foreshadows volatility in another. On a four-week dataset of 216 process trajectories, we cut reconstruction error by up to 22%, capture 56% more memory peaks, and achieve the same accuracy with 35% fewer samples compared with fixed-interval and single-technique baselines. These results demonstrate that intelligently coordinating where, when, and why to sample delivers a step change in monitoring efficiency without sacrificing diagnostic fidelity.

title: Multi-Layered Planar Antenna in LTCC Technology for V-Band Communication Applications
authors: Subhendu Chakraborty, S. Chakraborty, D.K. Kharbanda, Nikhil Suri, Debasish Pal, Ayan Kumar Bandyopadhyay
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: In this article, a millimeter-wave, multi-layered vertical interconnect fed microstrip antenna based on LTCC (Low Temperature Co-fired Ceramic) substrate technology has been investigated. Reported design utilizes DuPont-951 green tape as substrate material. The EM simulation observations of the proposed antenna shows a wide band impedance bandwidth (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<-10 \text{dB}$</tex>), around 60 GHz and a nearly constant peak radiating antenna gain between 4.5 dBi and 5.6 dBi in the broad-side direction. Measured reflectioncoefficient shows good agreement with the full-wave electromagnetic simulation results.

title: Coupled EM-Circuit Domain Analysis of Radiatively Excited Non-Linear Circuit
authors: Athul Shaji, K. J. Vinoy
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper investigates the analysis of propagation of harmonic components in nonlinear circuits. To enable accurate modelling of such systems, we propose a co-simulation methodology that couples the finite-difference time-domain (FDTD) method with SPICE-based circuit solvers. Unlike traditional split-domain approaches that treat electromagnetic and circuit simulations separately, our method enables direct interaction between the antenna and its nonlinear load, capturing the power-dependent input impedance variations of the rectifier. The effectiveness of the proposed method in validating a rectenna is done with commercially available circuit simulation software, Keysight ADS.

title: A Robust Polarization Mode Dispersion Measurement Method Based on Backpropagation Neural Network and Principal Component Analysis
authors: Yexin Zhang, Zaiyou Wang, Shirui Zhang, Zeyu Xu, Xianfeng Tang
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Information and Communication Networks (ICICN)
abstract: A new PMD measurement scheme is proposed based on the fixed analyzer (FA) method combined with backpropagation neural networks and principal component analysis(PCA). The dimension of the input data is greatly reduced using PCA and bayesian optimization(BO) is employed for automatic tuning of the network parameters. At a signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) of 5 dB, the proposed scheme demonstrates a mean relative error (MRE) equivalent to 2.4% of traditional FA-FFT method. These results indicate enhanced robustness in low-SNR environments, achieving higher measurement accuracy compared to existing methods. This scheme establishes a novel paradigm for PMD measurement, with strong practical value and application prospects.

title: Algorithm for automatic markup of theta-rhythms with using the Morlet wavelet transform
authors: A. A. Grishchenko, A. S. Jafarova, A.Y. Dolinina, E. M. Suleymanova, I. V. Sysoev
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: Various pathologies can be detected during sleep, so it is important to know what the norm looks like. In this paper, we consider theta rhythms in the brains of healthy rats during sleep. An automated algorithm for theta rhythm detection based on the Morlaix wavelet transform has been developed. It allows you to identify significant rhythmic activity and eliminate random fluctuations or noises. The use of the Morlaix wavelet transform provides good localization in time and frequency, which is important for analyzing the dynamics of brain rhythms and their role in cognitive processes. The results obtained contribute to a deeper understanding of the mechanisms of theta rhythm generation and its functional significance in the animal brain, and can also be used to diagnose disorders or study the mechanisms of interaction between various brain structures during sleep and wakefulness.

title: Sphereabout: A Spherical Intersection Design for Networked Urban Air Mobility
authors: Farzan Moosavi, Bilal Farooq
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Urban aerial mobility is rapidly expanding, specifically on–demand Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) delivery services in urban environments. This necessitates management of the low–altitude airspace network to ensure smooth and safe traffic throughput. This study introduces a novel three–dimensional aerial network design inspired by a terrestrial transportation graph network for UAV networked mobility. Utilizing two–way tube corridors for node–to–node delivery and a spherical roundabout model, as “Sphereabout”, to optimize the traffic flow through the spherical intersection. Through this architecture, three–dimensional conflict–free air mobility management can be achieved via numerical experiments and utilizing the geometrical features of this intersection.

title: Microwave Sensor for Real-Time Quality Assessment of Thermally Degraded Cooking Oils
authors: Umair Bin Farooq, Javaid Ahmad Rather, Kushmanda Saurav
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: A compact planar microwave sensor based on a complementary split-ring resonator (CSRR) with a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0} \Omega$</tex> surfacemounted resistor is proposed for real-time monitoring of degraded cooking oils. The overall sensor size is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.258 \lambda_{0} \times 0.155 \lambda_{0}$</tex>, where <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\lambda_{0}$</tex> is the free-space wavelength at the operating frequency of 1.55 GHz. Two configurations-with and without the resistor-were validated using 1 mL samples of fresh and thermally degraded sunflower oil. Notably, the resistorloaded configuration exhibited a significantly higher fractional sensitivity of 0.769% compared to 0.358% for the non-resistor case, enabling effective differentiation between used and reused oil samples. This enhanced dielectric sensitivity highlights the sensor's capability to capture subtle changes in oil quality. To further support the simulated behavior, an equivalent circuit model (ECM) was developed under unloaded conditions, showing strong agreement with full-wave results. The sensor's passive, low-cost structure, and enhanced selectivity make it highly suitable for portable food safety and oil degradation monitoring applications.

title: Non-Antipodal Signaling based Semi-Blind Detector in Uplink Cell-Free mMIMO Networks
authors: N V Divya Lakshmi, Adarsh Patel
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: This work presents a novel data detection algorithm for uplink Cell-Free massive multiple-input multiple-output (CF-mMIMO) networks that operates without pilot transmission overhead. In the proposed framework, user equipments (UEs) transmit non-antipodal signals to a central processing unit (CPU) via access points (APs), where the composite received signals are jointly processed to recover user information. An alternating least squares (ALS)-based Non-Antipodal Signaling Semi-Blind (NASS) detection algorithm is developed to enable pilot-free data detection in uplink CF-mMIMO systems. The proposed algorithm exploits the inherent tensor structure of the received signal to extend the conventional ALS framework, effectively resolving permutation and scaling ambiguities for accurate semiblind detection. Simulation results demonstrate that the NASS detector achieves significantly improved bit error rate (BER) performance compared to existing iterative and pilot-assisted algorithms, confirming its robustness, scalability, and practical suitability for next-generation deployments.

title: Anticipating the Invisible: A Knowledge-Based Agent for Occluded Pedestrian Collision Avoidance in Virtual Scenarios
authors: Angie Nataly Melo, Carlota Salinas, Miguel Angel Sotelo
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Poor pedestrian visibility is recognized as one of the common risk factors contributing to pedestrian crashes. Despite advances in perception algorithms and collision avoidance systems, there remains a significant gap in handling scenarios involving fully occluded pedestrians. In this work, we present a custom behavior agent that leverages a knowledge-based occluded pedestrian predictor to influence the behavior of an Autonomous vehicles (AV). We designed and implemented 72 scenarios in Virtual Reality (VR) to evaluate the performance of both the agent and the predictor, using metrics such as collision avoidance rate, Time To Collision (TTC), and Pedestrian Detection Anticipation Time (PDAT). We compared the results against traditional agents that rely on standard detection algorithms. Preliminary findings demonstrate the effectiveness of incorporating contextual information to predict fully occluded pedestrians: Our agent achieved an average pedestrian detection anticipation time of 4.27 seconds and a collision avoidance rate of 87.5% across all experiments.

title: A Dual-Band Low-Noise Amplifier for 20 GHz 6G Communication and 77 GHz Radar Sensing
authors: Sateesh Kourav, Pritesh Kumar Yadav, Manish Goswami, Prasanna Kumar Misra
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents the design of a dual-band low-noise amplifier (LNA) for joint communication in the 6 G band (20 GHz) and automotive radar applications (77 GHz) using 130 nm BiCMOS technology. The performance of LNA is evaluated in terms of power gain, S11, noise figure (NF), and power consumption. From the simulation study it has been observed that the LNA1 (20 GHz) can deliver a gain of 33.05 dB, an input return loss (S11) of −1 3. 7 3 dB, and a noise figure (NF) of 1.21 dB while consuming a power of 13.88 mW with a 2.2 V supply. Similarly, LNA2 (77 GHz) can deliver a gain of 19.29 dB, S11 of −44.55 dB, and NF of 3.73 dB, while consuming a power of 13.93 mW with the same supply voltage. With the help of a combiner, the performance of dual-band LNA is discussed for the joint 6 G communication and radar sensing application.

title: Detection and Correction of Driver Mode Confusion Using LLM-Based Semantic Feedback in SAE Level 2 Automation
authors: Ye Yang, Donghe Li, Li Sun, Sen Liu, Zhuobin Cao, Shitao Chen, Qingyu Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This research presents a novel real-time framework for detecting and correcting Driver Mode Confusion (DMC) in SAE Level 2 automated driving systems. Existing systems rely solely on static indicators (e.g., dashboard lights) to communicate system states, failing to assess or rectify drivers' internal cognitive states, resulting in delayed takeovers and safety-critical incidents. Our framework integrates three interconnected modules: System Mode Monitoring, Driver Mode Confusion Detection, and Controlled Semantic Prompt Generation. The methodology fuses vehicle telemetry, visual behavior (gaze direction, hand position, head pose), and verbal semantic features through a lightweight classifier to identify cognitive misalignment, subsequently employing large language models to generate adaptive semantic feedback. Experimental results demonstrate 86% accuracy and an F1-score of 0.711 on the test set, validating the critical importance of multimodal integration, particularly linguistic features. This research addresses a fundamental gap in human-automation interaction by transitioning from passive status indication to active cognitive calibration, thereby enhancing the safety and reliability of partially automated driving systems where responsibility is distributed between human and machine.

title: Development of an Integrated Thermoanemometer Model
authors: A.A. Sukhorukov
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: Computer models of the sensitive elements of integrated thermoanemometers for the conversion of information signals in micro-jet technology have been developed: 1) from a thin-film metal resistive material formed inside the channel; 2) from a magnetoresistive material with an inductive element. A comparative analysis of the The sensitive element materials was carried out. Schemes of thermoanemometric measurements with active and passive heating of the heating element are proposed.

title: High-Gain Compact CPW-Fed Antenna for UHF FOPEN Radar Systems
authors: Ashwitha K. Shetty, Madhusudhan Rangula, Princy Maria Paul, Krishnamoorthy Kandasamy, Pathipati Srihari, C. S. Cho
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a compact, high-gain coplanar waveguide (CPW)-fed planar antenna optimized for ultrawideband (UWB) operation in UHF foliage-penetrating (FOPEN) applications. Through five sequential slot and patch modifications, the proposed design significantly enhances the impedance bandwidth and gain while maintaining a low-profile form factor. The final design achieves a wide bandwidth of 865 MHz (668.1533 MHz), offering a fractional bandwidth of 76.4% and a peak gain of 3.53 dBi (41.77% improvement over the basic design). The proposed antenna achieves a 20% size reduction compared to a conventional CPW-fed patch antenna, without compromising bandwidth or gain performance. The antenna has physical dimensions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.223 \lambda_{l} \times 0.334 \lambda_{l} \times 0.0037 \lambda_{l}$</tex>, where <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\lambda_{l}$</tex> is the wavelength corresponding to the lowest operating frequency. Its low-profile, lightweight, and fabrication-friendly structure renders it highly suitable for integration into airborne FOPEN radar systems.

title: Optimal off-grid Hybrid Energy System Design and Energy Generation Performance Analysis Under Three Solar Tracking Systems
authors: Kazi Nazir, A.T.M Saiful Islam
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Energy generation in Bangladesh has been facing substantial economic, technical, and sustainable challenges for limited fuel resources as well as the inability to utilize its available renewable sources. The predominant energy production resources in Bangladesh come from gas and furnace oil, which have a significant impact on both the economy and the environment. To reduce the dependence on burning fuel, in this study we aim to develop a sustainable, optimized off-grid hybrid energy system and also to analyze the energy production performance incorporating three distinct solar tracker devices using Hoper Pro software. Analyzing the best three models designed by Homer Pro—Solar PV/Diesel Generator/Battery, Solar PV/Battery, and Diesel Generator/Battery—found that the Solar PV/Diesel Generator/Battery configuration exhibits the lowest value of Cost of Energy ($0.181/kWh) and Net Present Cost ($397,388.60), alongside a payback period of 3.01 years, a Return on Investment of 32.90%, and an Internal Rate of Return of 35.8%. Moreover, we perform a sensitivity analysis on the proposed energy generation system with three distinct solar trackers: horizontal, vertical, and two-axis, to assess the annual energy generation efficiency of the trackers for the project site.

title: Generalization as a Method to Eliminate Problems, Incompatibilities and Contradictions in Models and Solvers of Optimal Planning, Scheduling and Control in the Oil and Gas Industry
authors: Petr Shlepakov
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: The article examines business-critical systems for optimal planning, scheduling, management and control of objects, processes and projects from global IT leaders such as Bonner&Moor, Honeywell, AspenTech, SAP, Microsoft, Oracle, IBM, etc. The analysis is based on the author’s experience, who has implemented, supported, developed and integrated such systems in the oil and gas industry for 25 years and/or has completed vendor-certified training. There are structured the problems, incompatibilities and contradictions in the models and solvers of these systems, their practical consequences and causes – historical, mathematical, organizational, financial, economic, social, educational, technical. In essence, the main problem is a “zoo” of not entirely correct and not compatible models and systems. To overcome these problems, a new, not simple, but proven method is proposed: generalization. The “zoo” will disappear if all tasks are modeled and solved as special cases of a general correct mathematical model. The author has been developing, evolving and implementing such a model and a single mixed discrete-continuous solver Aleph-cube since 1977. The model combines and generalizes constraints and criteria of a wide range of production and financial tasks for enterprises (ERP, APO), productions (APS, MES, EAM), supply chains (SCM, SCP), processes (CM, DPU), logistics (VSR, WMS), projects (PM, HRM), business processes (BPM), workflow (WFM), etc. The article provides a link to typical solutions for these classes of tasks using the unified Aleph-cube service. The article is of interest to specialists in the mathematical modeling and can be useful for managers, customers, users and developers of application solutions.

title: Free-Piston Linear Electrical Generator: Analysis of the Optimum Air-Gap Length
authors: Mahamudul Hasan Uzzal, Md. Kawsar Hossain, Omar Farrok
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: This research optimized the effects of air-gap length and load resistance on the electromagnetic performance of the free-piston linear generator (FPLG). ANSYS Maxwell was used to build a finite element model, and dynamic optimization was performed by changing the air gap and load resistance while keeping the translator’s velocity constant. To improve magnetic coupling and reduce losses, the proposed design included M530-50A magnetic cores and a neodymium-iron-boron N48H grade permanent magnet. At an optimized air gap of 1.5 mm and a load resistance of 60 Ω, the proposed generator reduced force ripple by 32.9% compared to the initial generator, as depicted in the simulation result. Magnetic flux linkage, RMS output power, and peak output voltage of the proposed optimized generator increased by 6%, 2.9%, and 11.5%, respectively. The findings showed that determining the optimal air-gap significantly improved the FPLG’s overall performance and conversion efficiency.

title: Highly Sensitive N-FK51A/Cu/ZnSe/BP-Based SPR Biosensor for Dengue Virus Detection: A TMM and FEM Approach
authors: Adib Hassan, Al Hadith Moon, Tanvir Ahmed
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: This work demonstrates the design and optimization of a multilayer prism-based Surface Plasmon Resonance (SPR) biosensor intended for the early and accurate detection of blood cells infected with the dengue virus. Our proposed structure utilizes an N-FK51A prism that is coated with copper (Cu), zinc selenide (ZnSe), and a monolayer of black phosphorus (BP). This configuration creates an optimized plasmonic interface that improves light-matter interaction. To determine the optimal thickness of each layer, we applied Finite Element Method (FEM) simulation in COMSOL Multiphysics and Transfer Matrix Method (TMM) analysis in MATLAB. The biosensor shows a peak angular sensitivity of 327.71°/RIU, full width at half maximum (FWHM) of 1.89 deg., 0.7874 deg<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup> detection accuracy (DA), and a quality factor (QF) of 156.68 RIU<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup> for dengue-affected hemoglobin cells. Close agreement of results obtained from FEM and TMM confirms the computational approaches accuracy and the reliability of the proposed model. Overall, the proposed N-FK51A/Cu/ZnSe/BP biosensor offers a cost-effective and promising platform for rapid detection dengue virus.

title: Urban Traffic Carbon Emission Modelling via Random Forest: Feature Importance and Effects of Sensors
authors: Hongxu Chen, Qishen Zhou, Bugao Zhang, Hongjun Liu, Zhengyi Cai, Zheng Zhu, Simon Hu
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: With the rapid increase in vehicle ownership, urban traffic has become a major source of carbon emissions, highlighting the urgent need for effective emission control strategies. Accurately estimating traffic-related emissions at high spatial and temporal resolution is essential for effective mitigation strategies, yet remains challenging due to data constraints. In this study, we propose a mesoscopic carbon emission model utilizing the well-established open dataset of naturalistic trajectories of half a million vehicles from the pNEUMA, achieving an ideal estimation error of 13.8%. To enhance interpretability, we apply SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) to assess the relative importance of key features, including vehicle density, road segment length, average speed, average acceleration, and acceleration variance. We also explore the functional relationships between emission levels and these dynamic variables. Additionally, we also simulate data from real-world sensor environments and demonstrate that integrating Connected Vehicle (CV) data with Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR) data significantly improves estimation accuracy. Finally, we assess the generalizability of the proposed model across different scenarios.

title: Supplemental Taxonomy for SAE L3-L4: A Tri-Layer Environmental Grading Framework
authors: Chengxi Hu, Sikai Chen, Samuel Labi, Hongliang Ding, Hyungchul Chung, Tiantian Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: The SAE taxonomy classifies autonomous driving levels based on how driving responsibilities are allocated between human and automated systems. However, it fails to specify critical environmental influences, which result in safety concerns, ambiguous performance expectations, and barriers to commercialization. The purpose of this paper is to fill this gap by introducing a Tri-layer Environmental Grading Framework (TEGF), which is a structured system that evaluates autonomous vehicle adaptability across built, natural, and traffic environments. The TEGF maps environmental favorability against the perception capabilities that are required for safe autonomous operation by quantifying environmental favorability through expert assessments. Our framework focuses specifically on SAE Levels 3 and 4, which we categorize into five tiers of adaptability (A-E). The supplementary classification clarifies operational boundaries and system expectations, thereby guiding technological development, regulatory frameworks, and public understanding of autonomous driving. By incorporating critical environmental dimensions into the SAE taxonomy, TEGF enhances the precision of the SAE taxonomy and provides a solid foundation for developing, testing, and deploying safe and reliable autonomous vehicles.

title: Advancing Robustness in Deep Reinforcement Learning with an Ensemble Defense Approach
authors: Adithya Mohan, Dominik Rößle, Daniel Cremers, Torsten Schön
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Recent advancements in Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) have demonstrated its applicability across various domains, including robotics, healthcare, energy optimization, and autonomous driving. However, a critical question remains: How robust are DRL models when exposed to adversarial attacks? While existing defense mechanisms such as adversarial training and distillation enhance the resilience of DRL models, there remains a significant research gap regarding the integration of multiple defenses in autonomous driving scenarios specifically. This paper addresses this gap by proposing a novel ensemble-based defense architecture to mitigate adversarial attacks in autonomous driving. Our evaluation demonstrates that the proposed architecture significantly enhances the robustness of DRL models. Compared to the baseline under FGSM attacks, our ensemble method improves the mean reward from 5.87 to 18.38 (over 213% increase) and reduces the mean collision rate from 0.50 to 0.09 (an 82% decrease) in the highway scenario and merge scenario, outperforming all standalone defense strategies.

title: A Systematic Comparison of Computational Models for Detecting Adolescent Vulnerability in Bengali Text
authors: Mohana Banik Propa, Mst. Rifah Alvy, Sumyia Benta Azad, Md. Sazzadur Rahman
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: The internet has replaced the life of teenagers and the software installed to protect the young users on the internet do not understand any other language other than English. This becomes a huge population of teenagers who are native speakers of the Bengali language and who lack the appropriate protection against the risks of cyberbullying and harassment. This digital safety gap is explicitly filled in the study as it forms and critically evaluates computational models that detect signs of vulnerabilities in Bengali text. The current paper compares three levels of techno-based classification using a custom-built dataset of 5,209 manually annotated social media comments of traditional machine learning classifiers, deep neural networks (including CNNs and LSTMs) and state-of-the-art Transformer-based models. We observe a clear hierarchical improvement in performance across model categories. The deep learning models offer a meaningful addition to the more traditional methods, though the latter serves as a decent baseline. Transformer architectures, however, proved to be in a league of their own. The most efficient was the XLM-RoBERTa with fine-tuning and the remarkable F1-score of 98.63%. Such level of accuracy demonstrates that it is not only possible but quite possible to create highly consistent, automated systems of monitoring and flagging dangerous material in Bengali. This research establishes a significant precedent and gives a clear path of how to transform the Internet into a safer zone to the millions of young people who use it.

title: A Data-Driven Approach to Combating Social Media Addiction: Insights from Explainable AI
authors: Tashida Yeasmin, Md. Motaharul Islam
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Social media has transformed the way people communicate and connect, but it has also raised worries about psychological dependence and excessive use of technology. This paper proposes an interpretable machine learning framework for predicting social media addiction (SMA) using combined behavioral and psychological data collected from 10,780 respondents. To address class imbalance, ten classifiers were assessed using stratified cross-validation, LASSO-based feature selection, and SMOTE. Experimental results demonstrate that Logistic Regression (83.33%), Gradient Boosting (82.70%), SVM (82.60%), and AdaBoost (82.60%) produced the best dependable outcomes in terms of accuracy and balance. PCA visualization showed clear behavioral patterns, and SHAP and LIME were used for both global and individual-level explanations to increase transparency. Additionally, the causal influence of daily social media use on addiction risk was evaluated using Conditional Average Treatment Effects (CATEs), DoWhy, backdoor and frontdoor modifications, and Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs). In contrast to earlier XAI-based research, the suggested approach combines causal, local and global interpretability to convert black-box predictions into useful behavioral insights.

title: Development of a Deep Learning-Based Hybrid Calibration Method for Large-Scale Activity-Based Travel Simulator Using Passive Aggregate Population Data
authors: Shuntaro Tanaka, Daisuke Fukuda
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Activity-based models (ABMs) have been applied to analyzing large-scale and detailed human mobility patterns. However, previous studies have highlighted that the ABM simulator faces challenges in accurately reproducing real-world conditions. In this study, we integrated a deep learning model, a variational autoencoder, with an ABM simulator to perform large-scale parameter calibration in a single step, without modifying the aggregate population data. This integration enabled the development of a calibration framework that updates specific model parameters. To allow learning using the backpropagation method in deep learning, we employed a differentiable sampling method from a multinomial distribution using the Straight-Through Gumbel Softmax trick. We implemented a selection behavior process based on the utility functions of each option within the simulator. We conducted a case study using a submodel of an existing ABM simulator for the Tokyo Metropolitan Area and real-world aggregated population statistics, and showed that it can be applied to calibrate large-scale ABM simulators.

title: Web-Based Air Quality Monitoring and Mapping System using Fuzzy Logic Algorithm

abstract: None

title: Quality-aware robotic knee ultrasound images acquisition using multiscale feature extraction model
authors: Ziwen Wang, Baoliang Zhao, Liang Yao, Long Lei, Faqin Lv, Bing Li, Ying Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Ultrasound (US) examination is critical for diagnosing knee joint disorders, but its quality and reproducibility are heavily reliant on sonographers' experience, which causes fatigue and imaging inconsistency. This paper presents an autonomous knee US scanning robot system integrated with a multi-scale feature extraction model to address these issues. The robotic platform integrates a 7-DoF manipulator, dual RGB-D cameras, and a force sensor to ensure safe human-robot interaction and accurate knee joint localization. Meanwhile, a novel multi-scale image quality assessment network is proposed, which combines global features, target-specific regions, and local details to evaluate the visibility and clarity of knee cartilage. Gaussian Process Regression (GPR) is employed to model the relationship between probe angles and image quality. This enables the robot to autonomously search for the optimal probe contact angle. Experimental validation involving 20 trials on 5 volunteers demonstrated a 95% procedure completion rate and an angular error of 2.45° relative to expert adjustments. The system enables standardized and autonomous acquisition of diagnostic-grade knee US images.

title: Hardware-Efficient QC-LDPC Decoding Architecture with Reduced Bit-Width and Saturation Management
authors: Hyeongseok Moon, In-Cheol Park
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems II: Express Briefs
abstract: In quantized layered low-density parity-check (LDPC) decoding, the saturated a posteriori probability (APP) value prevents extrinsic information from being correctly accumulated and overemphasizes certain error paths, making it necessary to use a larger APP representation. The proposed method compensates for the information loss caused by such saturation and eliminates the need for a larger APP bitwidth, reducing the APP bitwidth to the level of the check-to-variable message while preserving the decoding performance. This compensation is achieved by disabling APP updates when saturation occurs, starting from the iteration identified by the shortest cycles in the parity-check matrix. The proposed control preserves correct message passing and prevents the accumulation of error-path weights in the APP. In the prototype LDPC decoder, the proposed method requires only a lightweight circuit to detect saturation, incurring a negligible area overhead and almost no degradation of processing delay. As a result, the proposed architecture reduces area and power by 15% and 25%, respectively, and achieves a throughput-to-area ratio of 25.08 Gbps/itr/mm², while maintaining the FER performance of the 7-bit APP representation.

title: Accurate EM Modeling of Multiport Circuits via a Gated Rational Complex Neural Network
authors: Ao Shen, Min Tang, Wenxing Zhu, Jianhua Zhang, Liang Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: Rational function approximation (RFA) techniques, including vector fitting (VF), are widely employed for modeling the frequency-dependent behavior of S-parameters. However, when applied to multi-port devices and wideband data, the performance of VF is often limited by reduced accuracy resulting from the processing of large S-parameter datasets. To address this issue, this paper proposes a gated rational complex neural network (GRCNN) for modeling multi-port S-parameters in passive microwave systems. Based on a rational complex neural network (RCNN) framework derived from rational function approximation, the GRCNN introduces a gating mechanism to enhance modeling adaptability. This mechanism adaptively selects the effective number of complex-conjugate and real pole–residue pairs. Model parameters are optimized end-to-end via backpropagation within a GPU-accelerated training framework. By incorporating causality constraints and a soft passivity penalty into the loss function, the GRCNN promotes causal behavior and reduces passivity violations on a sampled frequency grid, although strict global passivity over the continuous frequency domain is not guaranteed. Moreover, the models generated by the GRCNN can be directly converted into equivalent lumped circuits for macromodeling purposes. Comprehensive comparisons are conducted among VF, RCNN, weighted RCNN (WRCNN), GRCNN, and a gated complex-valued neural network (GCVNN). Experimental results on four test cases, ranging from 2 to 138 ports with wideband responses extending into the sub-THz range, demonstrate that the GRCNN achieves lower fitting error while maintaining causality and passivity.

title: Manifold Factor Graph Optimization-Based GNSS/IMU Integrated Navigation Testing Platform for Urban Vehicles
authors: Ning Liu, Bin Hu, Junfang Fan, Zhong Su, Kai Shen, Huamin Jie, Wensong Wang, Zhenyu Zhao
abstract: Multisource sensor fusion has become a research focus in navigation and positioning. However, the Kalman filter algorithm faces issues with real-time performance and positioning accuracy when handling asynchronous data fusion. The development of factor graph algorithms has significantly improved these issues. Meanwhile, research on vehicle-based manifold factor graph optimization algorithms in urban environments is relatively scarce. Therefore, this paper proposes a GNSS/IMU integrated navigation testing platform based on manifold factor graph optimization. The platform is designed to analyze the real-time performance and accuracy of the optimization algorithm under different path ranges. It optimizes IMU pre-integration using Lie group algorithms and constructs a manifold pre-integration factor model with keyframes. The testing algorithm integrates IMU pre-integration factors, GNSS factors, and state prior factors to achieve synchronous multisource fusion. By optimizing a sliding window, it performs nonlinear optimization of variables within the window and outputs the final navigation state. Simulation results show the proposed testing algorithm improves positioning accuracy by 96.67%, 95.36%, and 89.55% compared to three other algorithms. It maintains execution times below 10 seconds, demonstrating high real-time performance and efficiency.

title: Free-space FIFO Device for Few-mode Multi-core fibers based on 3D-Printed Microlens Array
authors: Yanan Zhong, Ting Lei, Luping Du, Ran Gao, Ying li, Xiaocong Yuan
abstract: Space division multiplexing using few-mode multi-core fibers (FM-MCFs) is a promising solution for ultra-high-capacity optical communication systems. A key component of such systems is the Fan-in/Fan-out (FIFO) device, which facilitates the multiplexing and demultiplexing of light across non-standard fiber cores. In this work, we present a compact free-space FIFO device based on a 3D-printed microlens array. The microlenses are fabricated using two-photon polymerization (TPP) 3D printing technology, which provides high shape fidelity and precise structural control. The device enables stable transmission of LP<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">01</sub>, LP<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11a/b</sub>, LP<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">21a/b</sub>, and LP<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">02</sub> modes over the C+L bands. At 1550 nm, the average insertion losses are 1.14, 1.63, 1.63, 3.20, 3.15, and 3.75 dB for LP modes. To verify the applicability of the FIFO device in coherent optical communication systems, we performed transmission experiments of 16-Gbaud DP-QPSK signals over a 1 km FM-MCF. By employing a single microlens array to replace multiple optical components, such as collimators and prisms, the design significantly reduces system complexity and physical footprint. The design is scalable and adaptable to various types of multi-core fibers, making it a promising candidate for widespread deployment in space-division multiplexed communication networks.

title: Federated Learning in Offline and Online EMG Decoding: A Privacy and Performance Perspective
authors: Kai Malcolm, Cèsar A. Uribe, Momona Yamagami
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: Neural interfaces offer a pathway to intuitive, high-bandwidth interaction, but the sensitive nature of neural data creates significant privacy hurdles for large-scale model training. Federated learning (FL) has emerged as a promising privacy-preserving solution, yet its efficacy in real-time, online neural interfaces remains unexplored. In this study, we 1) propose a conceptual framework for applying FL to the distinct constraints of neural interface applications and 2) provide a systematic evaluation of FL-based neural decoding using high-dimensional surface electromyography across both an offline simulation and a real-time, online user study. While offline results suggest that FL can simultaneously enhance performance and privacy, our online experiments reveal a more complex landscape. We found that standard FL assumptions struggle to translate to real-time, sequential interactions with user-decoder co-adaptation. Our results show that while FL retains privacy advantages, it introduces performance tensions not predicted by offline simulations. These findings identify a critical gap in current FL methodologies and highlight the need for specialized algorithms designed to navigate the unique co-adaptive dynamics of online neural decoding.

title: A New Fluctuation Enhanced Sensing-based Feature for Gases Identification Using MOX Gas Sensors
authors: Sami Gomri, Thierry Contaret, Tomas Fiorido, Khalifa Aguir, Mohamed Masmoudi
abstract: In this paper, we present a new gases identification feature based on noise spectroscopy. Using our previous model developed in our previous work, in which we have expressed the power spectral density (PSD) of the gas sensor noise in metal oxide (MOX) gas sensors, we calculate the exact theoretical expression of the integral of the PSD of the gas sensor noise. The obtained expression shows that there is a correlation between the integral of the PSD of the gas sensor noise and the nature of the detected gas. After comparing the obtained theoretical results with some experimental results performed on MOX gas sensors exposed to various gases, we conclude that the integral of the PSD of the gas sensor noise could be used as gases identifying parameter. The new feature holds prospects for finding powerful method for gases identification.

title: Design and performance research of a spindle-integrated rotational piezoelectric testing system for upset force measurement during welding processes_supp1-3671729.mp4
authors: Mingyu Yuan
abstract: Accurate monitoring of high-frequency upset force during friction stir welding (FSW) is a critical step for optimizing welding process parameters and ensuring joint quality. However, traditional platform-type force gauges cannot measure the upset force in the bobbin tool friction stir welding (BT-FSW) process, which poses a technical challenge. To resolve this issue, a high-frequency spindle-integrated rotational piezoelectric testing system (SIR-PTS) mounted on the equipment spindle was designed. This system uses piezoelectric sensors as measuring elements and comprises a force gauge, a built-in signal conditioning circuit, and monitoring software. First, a theoretical mechanical model under rotational conditions was established to determine the dimensional parameters of the piezoelectric quartz wafers. Subsequently, ANSYS finite element simulation was utilized to analyze the static structural characteristics and dynamic response of the testing system. Meanwhile, a modular hardware circuit integrating charge conversion, filtering, and wireless transmission functions was developed. A series of verification experiments were conducted to evaluate the system performance. The experimental results demonstrate that the system exhibits a static linearity error of 0.64% and a repeatability error of 0.34%. Its main-direction natural frequency is 2082.52 Hz, which meets the requirements of high-frequency dynamic testing. These results confirm that the system can realize high-precision, real-time dynamic monitoring of upset force in BT-FSW processes.

title: Leaderless Consensus of Multiple Euler-Lagrange Systems Under Dynamically Switching Topologies and DoS Attacks
authors: Yanping Peng, Jun Cheng, Mei Yan, Bin Zhang, Xinkai Chen
abstract: This study addresses the leaderless consensus problem for networked multiple Euler-Lagrange systems in Internet-of-Things (IoT) enabled cyber-physical environments under dynamically switching communication topologies and denial-of-service (DoS) attacks. To address these challenges, a distributed adaptive sampled-data control framework is developed, in which communication and control are co-designed with network security awareness. A threat-driven probabilistic adaptive sampling mechanism is introduced to dynamically regulate communication intervals according to real-time DoS risk perception, thereby reducing network load and enhancing resilience of IoT communications. In addition, an adaptive distributed control law incorporating a filtering mechanism and parameter update compensation is constructed to counteract the destabilizing effects induced by both topology switching and attacktriggered sampling mismatches. Rigorous stability analysis establishes leaderless consensus under joint cyber and network uncertainties. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves faster consensus convergence, reduced control oscillations, and improved communication efficiency compared with existing methods, highlighting its effectiveness for secure and resource-efficient IoT-enabled multi-agent systems.

title: Multi-Modal Sensing-Assisted Robust and Cost-Efficient 3D Underwater Acoustic Communication System for Underwater IoT
authors: Hyeonggeol Kim, Wongeol Ko, Yongtaek Woo, Yujae Song
abstract: In this study, we propose a robust and cost-efficient underwater acoustic communication system designed to support adaptive acoustic beam alignment between a surface vessel and an autonomous underwater vehicle (AUV) operating over a wide range of three-dimensional (3D) underwater environments. We first introduce a multi-modal sensing-assisted particle filter (PF) algorithm for AUV localization estimation performed at the surface vessel. This algorithm fuses low-cost inertial measurement unit sensor data with multi-modal acoustic modem observations, including signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), one-way travel time, and Doppler shift. A key innovation of our approach is the use of the real-time SNR to compute an adaptive process noise covariance, which intelligently guides the PF’s prediction phase to account for the fluctuating quality of the input data. This mechanism enables the PF to robustly handle non-linear uncertainties, achieving both high accuracy and the computational efficiency required for low-cost embedded systems. Subsequently, using the estimated AUV position, an adaptive acoustic beam alignment mechanism is implemented with a servo motor-driven pan–tilt unit to dynamically adjust the surface modem’s orientation toward the AUV, ensuring robust acoustic link quality despite the AUV’s 3D wide-ranging mobility. Both simulation and empirical water-tank experiments demonstrate that the proposed system significantly enhances localization accuracy and communication performance, while reducing system complexity and hardware costs relative to conventional approaches. These results underscore the potential of the proposed framework to enable scalable, reliable, and cost-effective underwater drone communications in future 3D underwater Internet of Things networks.

title: Multi-Layer Multi-View Input and Feature Tight Fusion-Driven Point Cloud Semantic Segmentation Network for Intelligent Vehicles
authors: Zhongzheng Li, Hairong Dong, Liye Zhang, Xiaoyu Sun, Dong Kong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: LiDAR-based semantic segmentation provides point-level semantic information crucial for tasks such as environment sensing, path planning, and decision-making in autonomous driving. However, segmentation methods based on point cloud projection often suffer from information loss, which not only reduces segmentation accuracy but also limits the applicability of autonomous driving in complex environments. To address this challenge, this paper proposes an effective point cloud segmentation network driven by multi-layer range view (RV) representation, bird’s-eye view (BEV) dual-feature encoding and tight fusion of multi-view features. Specifically, first, the proposed network utilizes hierarchical projections to generate RV images at different distances and adaptively assigns weights through a multi-attention mechanism to mitigate challenges such as projection-induced occlusions. Secondly, the BEV preliminary features and geometric structure features are extracted in parallel using Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) and Normal Distribution Transform (NDT), aiming to minimize the loss of point cloud height and geometric structure information due to projection. Finally, a multi-view feature tight fusion module is designed with the aim of exploring higher-order interactions between different view features. Extensive experiments on SemanticKITTI and nuScenes demonstrate that the proposed network achieves 68.1% mIoU and 78.5% mIoU respectively, validating not only its competitive accuracy but also its practical potential for real-time, safety-critical perception in autonomous driving.

title: Sampling-Based Motion Planning with Scene Graphs Under Perception Constraints_supp1-3674013.mp4
authors: Qingxi Meng
abstract: It will be increasingly common for robots to operate in cluttered human-centered environments such as homes, workplaces, and hospitals, where the robot is often tasked to maintain perception constraints, such as monitoring people or multiple objects, for safety and reliability while executing its task. However, existing perception-aware approaches typically focus on low-degree-of-freedom (DOF) systems or only consider a single object in the context of high-DOF robots. This motivates us to consider the problem of perception-aware motion planning for high-DOF robots that accounts for multi-object monitoring constraints. We employ a scene graph representation of the environment, offering a great potential for incorporating longhorizon task and motion planning thanks to its rich semantic and spatial information. However, it does not capture perceptionconstrained information, such as the viewpoints the user prefers. To address these challenges, we propose MOPS-PRM, a roadmapbased motion planner, that integrates the perception cost of observing multiple objects or humans directly into motion planning for high-DOF robots. The perception cost is embedded to each object as part of a scene graph, and used to selectively sample configurations for roadmap construction, implicitly enforcing the perception constraints. Our method is extensively validated in both simulated and real-world experiments, achieving more than <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim 36\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> improvement in the average number of detected objects and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim 17\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> better track rate against other perception-constrained baselines, with comparable planning times and path lengths.

title: Average BER Performance Analysis for XL-MIMO Detection with Imperfect VR Information
authors: Jiacheng Lu, Jun Zhang, Xiaoting Lu, Yu Han, Shi Jin, Xiao Li
abstract: With the deployment of the extremely large aperture arrays (ELAA), spatial non-stationarities start to emerge, implying that user signals can only be available to a portion of the base station (BS) antenna array, termed the visibility regions (VR). The existence of VR poses significant challenges to the design of transmission schemes in extremely large-scale multiple-input multiple-output (XL-MIMO) systems. In this paper, we investigate the uplink transmission of XL-MIMO system to evaluate the impact of imperfect VR information on the reliability of XL-MIMO detection. Firstly, an energy detector is introduced to estimate the VR during the pilot training phase, and then channel estimation and signal detection are performed on the estimated VR. Subsequently, based on the estimated VR channel, we derive the distribution of signal-to-interference-noise ratio (SINR) and the average bit error rate (BER) with a linear receiver under different modulation schemes. Furthermore, the average BER is minimized by optimizing the VR detection threshold. Simulations show that VR estimation not only significantly reduces the complexity of channel estimation and signal detection but also improves the average BER performance.

title: Partial multi-label learning based on sample-label subspace structure consistency
authors: Qingwei Jia, Yan Wang, Changzhong Wang, Tingquan Deng
abstract: Partial multi-label learning (PML) works on identifying and eliminating noisy labels in observed labels. Existing PML methods focus on separating ground-truth labels and noisy labels from observed labels, which ignore the intrinsic connection between features and ground-truth labels. To overcome the deficiency, a novel PML model based on sample-label subspace structure consistency (PMLSSC) is presented. Specifically, for samples in the same feature subspace, the corresponding labels should have a similar subspace structure. To achieve this goal, the topological structures of feature space and label space are jointly learned. Then, the ground-truth labels maintaining consistent subspace structures with the feature space can be extracted from the observed labels. To obtain a more compact subspace structure, low-rank and sparse constraints are applied to ground-truth labels and noisy labels, respectively. In addition, the projection relationship between features and ground-truth labels is constructed, and l2,1-norm is introduced to constrain the projection matrix, which realizes the effective utilization of feature information. For 12 different datasets, by comparing with 10 state-of-the-art methods, the experimental results show that the proposed PMLSSC is more effective and robust than the traditional methods, which can better accomplish the task of PML.

title: Multi-Agent LLM for Reducing Unsheltered Homelessness
authors: Shravya Kothapalli, Samhita Juloori, Vishvaas Vasudeva Rao, Joshna Medisetty, Ahmed Hambaba
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 7th Asia Conference on Machine Learning and Computing
abstract: None

title: SD4R: Sparse-to-Dense Learning for 3D Object Detection with 4D Radar
authors: Xiaokai Bai, Jiahao Cheng, Songkai Wang, Yixuan Luo, Lianqing Zheng, Xiaohan Zhang, Si-Yuan Cao, Hui-Liang Shen
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: D radar measurements offer an affordable and weather-robust solution for 3D perception. However, the inherent sparsity and noise of radar point clouds present significant challenges for accurate 3D object detection, underscoring the need for effective and robust point clouds densification. Despite recent progress, existing densification methods often fail to address the extreme sparsity of 4D radar point clouds and exhibit limited robustness when processing scenes with a small number of points. In this paper, we propose SD4R, a novel framework that transforms sparse radar point clouds into dense representations. SD4R begins by utilizing a foreground point generator (FPG) to mitigate noise propagation and produce densified point clouds. Subsequently, a logit-query encoder (LQE) enhances conventional pillarization, resulting in robust feature representations. Through these innovations, our SD4R demonstrates strong capability in both noise reduction and foreground point densification. Extensive experiments conducted on the publicly available View-of-Delft dataset demonstrate that SD4R achieves state-of-the-art performance. Source code is available at https://github.com/lancelot0805/SD4R.

title: Analysis of Average AoI in an Optimal Energy Efficient OFDMA Based Wireless System
authors: Siddharth Deshmukh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)

title: An IoT-Integrated System for Robust Microwave Dielectric Characterization Using Hybrid K-Means Ensemble Learning
authors: Ahmad Khusro, Zubair Akhter, Abhishek K. Jha, Atif Shamim, Mohammad Hashmi
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: Accurate characterization of microwave dielectric materials is essential for the effective design and optimization of high-frequency electronic systems, since material properties critically influence system performance. This research presents a novel Internet of Things (IoT)-integrated sensing architecture that synergistically combines unsupervised <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{k}$</tex>-means clustering with ensemble learning (EL) algorithms for robust extraction of complex permittivity from scattering parameter measurements. Unlike conventional cavity perturbation techniques limited by linear assumptions, the proposed hybrid framework effectively captures non-linear perturbation regimes through data-driven modeling. The system employs a cylindrical cavity resonator integrated with an IoT layer for automated data acquisition and remote analytics. The hybrid ML workflow enhances model robustness by restructuring the feature space via clustering prior to EL-based regression, improving predictive accuracy for both simulated and experimental datasets. Evaluation results confirm high accuracy and generalization capability even under extrapolated conditions, demonstrating the scalability of this smart sensing framework for industrial and scientific deployment.

title: Electrical Design and Optimization of an Integrated Solar, Storage, and EV Charging Station for Campus Applications
authors: Xiaodong Li, Yanfang Ren, Xinyue Liu, Shiqi Wang, Xin Yuan, Hongbo Gao
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Mechanical Engineering and Power Engineering (MEPE)
abstract: To achieve the large-scale application of clean energy in the civilian sector, this paper takes a school in Inner Mongolia as a case study, proposing and designing a new energy grid-interactive solar-storage-charging station system. This system integrates photovoltaic power generation, energy storage, and intelligent charging, establishing a high-tech green charging mode that uses solar power to sustain the charging infrastructure. Firstly, based on local solar resource availability and grid conditions, this study completes the overall planning of the photovoltaic system on the teaching building rooftop. The series-parallel configuration of the PV array is determined through theoretical calculation, and the system’s power generation capacity is estimated. For the main electrical connection design, a primary side configuration for the grid connection system with a backup circuit is proposed. This scheme ensures operational reliability and flexibility while also providing economic benefits for future electricity consumption. Furthermore, this paper elaborates on the power station’s electrical system design, including the main electrical connection design, selection and verification of key electrical equipment, and the design of the reactive power compensation scheme. This research provides a valuable engineering example and design methodology for promoting clean energy technology in similar regions.

title: KDLight: A Traffic Signal Control Algorithm for Scenarios with Partially Observable Vehicle States
authors: Zhiyuan Hu, Jianming Hu
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)

title: A Comprehensive Performance Analysis of ISFET by Varying The Sensing Film
authors: Md. Israil Hossain, Niloy Kundu, Md. Imran Hasan Refat
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: A simulated model of Ion-Sensitive Field Effect Transistors (ISFETs) which is used for measuring pH or sensing pH of a solution is obtained with Silicon (Si) as a substrate. At first, the sensing membrane of this model is Silicon di-oxide (SiO2) layer. Then, this model precisely encapsulates the various performance characteristics of ISFET based pH sensor. The main purpose of this paper is to observe changes when different types of sensing film (SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>, Si<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>N<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</inf>, and HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) is used instead of fixed sensing layer and also for different thickness. The performance of the sensors is observed under pH range 3-11 with varying membrane thickness 2nm to 30nm. This is achieved by multiphysics modelling with Finite Element Methods (FEM) using COMSOL Multiphysics software. In this work, a lower threshold voltage, (VT) with much higher transconductance, current sensitivity and voltage sensitivity can be recorded for all pH level (range 3-11). Further development of ISFET pH sensor using different sensing membrane with lowering oxide thickness contributes in creating an improved and more accurate pH sensor for medical and industrial application. In this work, Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> layer achieves highest voltage sensitivity which is 58.97 mV/pH and current sensitivity of HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> is high (0.9245 uA/pH) compared to other sensing membrane. Increased pH sensitivity like voltage and current sensitivity enables more precise and accurate detection of pH fluctuations, resulting in greater control and understanding of chemical processes and biological responses.

title: Reconfigurable Dual-Notch Filter and Displacement Sensor Using Graphene Based Flexible Superstrates
authors: Puppala Purna Phani Chaitanya, Srujana Kagita
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a graphene-Kapton-integrated microstrip circuit featuring dynamic dual-notch filtering and electromagnetic sensing. The design consists of a microstrip line loaded with square-shaped split ring resonators (SSRRs) on an FR4 substrate, producing prominent notches near 3.75 GHz and 8 GHz. By introducing uncoated and graphene-coated Kapton sheets as a superstrate, substantial enhancement in notch depths exceeding 60 dB is achieved. Furthermore, the use of patterned graphene lines and localized pads enables positionsensitive tuning of the resonant characteristics, offering strong electromagnetic interaction and sensing capability. The proposed structure serves as a compact, tunable platform for adaptive filtering and intelligent RF applications.

title: Cross and Four Quadrant Planar-Based Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface for Multiband
authors: Ashok Kumar Sahu, Ravi Kumar Gangwar
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation of a novel unit cell for a Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (RIS), capable of stable operation under varying angles of incidence. The structure supports triple-band functionality at 3.32 GHz, 5.437 GHz, and 14.601 GHz, with corresponding bandwidths of approximately 37MHz, 986 MHz, and 1.044 GHz, respectively. Designed as a 1-bit binary RIS, the unit cell ensures reliable performance across all bands. The simulation results demonstrate angular stability for dual-polarized waves up to an incidence angle of 40°, ensuring a robust phase response under varying conditions. A consistent reflection phase difference of 180° ± 30° is achieved between the ON and OFF states of the PIN diode within each operating band, enabling precise wavefront manipulation. The unit cell supports dual polarization, with independent control via PIN diodes, allowing effective switching in both polarizations. It reflects more than 80% of the incident electromagnetic energy, ensuring high reflectivity and reduced power loss. This high efficiency, combined with tight phase error control, enhances beam steering accuracy-particularly vital for low-complexity 1-bit RIS architectures. Additionally, the design is validated within a five-layer PCB stack-up, confirming its feasibility for practical, multilayer RIS implementations.

title: Non-Invasive Spiral Antenna Applicator for Hyperthermia Application of Skin/Neck Cancer
authors: Navneet Kaur, Aarti Bansal, Geetanjali Singla, Hitender Gupta, Rajesh Khanna
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: In the present study, a compact double-spiral antenna has been developed specifically for hyperthermia treatment targeting skin and neck cancers. The antenna is fabricated on a flexible polyethylene terephthalate (PET) substrate, chosen for its mechanical flexibility and biocompatibility with human tissue. The overall dimensions of the antenna are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$31.5 * 31.5 * 0.27 \text{mm}^{3}$</tex>, and it operates at the ISM frequency of 915 MHz with impedance an impedance bandwidth of 9.98 MHz ranging from 912.56 MHz to 922.54 MHz.To evaluate its performance, detailed simulation studies were conducted under three scenarios as in free space, in proximity to a tissue-mimicking phantom, and using the anatomical Gustav human model. The results demonstrate stable resonance behavior across these environments. The effectiveness of the proposed applicator was further validated through experimental measurements of S-parameters, and its specific absorption rate (SAR) distribution was thoroughly analyzed. In addition, thermal simulations were carried out to assess the heating potential of the antenna for controlled therapeutic use in superficial tumors.

title: Novel NIR-Polarimeter/Radiometer For Stratospheric Aerosol Study Onboard CubeSat Satellite
authors: Gennadi Milinevsky, Ivan Syniavskyi, Yevgen Oberemok, Mikhail Sosonkin, Vassyl Danylevsky, Yuriy Ivanov
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: The energy balance of the Earth's atmosphere depends significantly on the amount of stratospheric aerosol. Significant aerosol concentrations in the stratosphere, especially after volcanic eruptions, lead to substantial long-term changes in the amount of energy in the atmosphere. Therefore, research and monitoring aerosol content in the stratosphere are essential for forecasting and modeling the planet's climate. For these investigations, we propose a method of determining the aerosol optical depth and microphysical parameters of particles in the stratosphere from a satellite using polarimetric measurements of scattered radiation in the near-infrared (NIR) waveband 1378 nm. The waveband corresponds to strong radiation absorption by water vapor. Observation in that spectral band allows for the study of aerosol in the stratosphere separately from tropospheric aerosols due to strong absorption of radiation by water vapor, primarily concentrated in the troposphere. The instrument consists of a panoramic NIR-imaging polarimeter that uses the scheme with the division of aperture, and a radiometer. The planned measurements will allow monitoring of both aerosol parameters and water vapor content in the atmosphere. The instrument includes a radiometric channel with near-IR wavelengths 865, 910, 1020, and 1240 nm for this second purpose. The idea of the space experiment, the method of measurements, the optical layout of the polarimeter and radiometer, and the instrument's design are presented. The polarimeter is reliable for stratosphere aerosol investigation from satellites of different configurations. We consider the proposed NIR-imaging polarimeter as a payload for the 6U CubeSat format.

title: A Novel Data-driven Method for Performance Degradation Prediction of Composite Laminates
authors: Xin He, Datong Liu, Huizhen Hua, Hua Zhang, Yafei Xu
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Composite laminates is widely used in the aerospace industry due to its high strength and high specific stiffness. However, the degradation of its dynamic performance is still a challenge in structural health monitoring (SHM), which significantly restricts its application. The data-driven method for predicting the performance of composite laminates can effectively address the difficulty in establishing degradation models that arises in physics-driven methods, but is also limited by the quantity and quality of the data. In this paper, a novel deep learning framework is specially proposed to improve the prediction performance, which is applicable to various composite material structures. Firstly, a nonlinear correlation feature selection approach removes redundant high-dimensional degradation features. Subsequently, an end-to-end framework that combines Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM) and Long Short-term Memory Networks (LSTM) is proposed for learning and predicting nonlinear degradation representations. Genetic algorithm (GA) optimizes its hyperparameters to enhance performance and efficiency. The framework's effectiveness is validated on the degradation datasets. The results indicate that the data-driven method can provide a more reliable approach for performance degradation prediction of composite laminates.

title: Compensation of Beam Pointing Errors in Digital Beamforming Based Focal Array Fed Reflector Antenna Systems
authors: Suvrajit Ghosh, Akula Ramu, Atrish Mukherjee, Sravan Kumar Sagi, Milind B Mahajan
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: In this paper, digital beamforming (DBF) has been used to compensate beam pointing errors (BPE) in a focal array fed reflector (FAFR) antenna system. BPEs are responsible for the inadvertent deviation of the satellite's beam from its intended direction. In order to mitigate such errors, monopulse tracking technique has been demonstrated using DBF in an FAFR antenna system having a septet feed cluster. In order to verify the developed tracking technique, separate perturbations have been introduced in the reflector which correspond to the beam being shifted in the azimuth and elevation planes. The resultant angular error was ascertained, and the shifted beam has been shown to be traced back to its original direction by appropriately modifying the excitation coefficients of the feeds using DBF. This paper underlines the effectiveness of the DBF in BPE compensation applications, and can be integrated in future HTS and SDR based payloads.

title: Thermal Performance Testing of the GE 9FB Gas Turbine at the Hadriet Ennous Power Plant
authors: Alexander V. Andryushin, Edik K. Arakelyan, Julia Y. Yagupova, Alexey V. Neklyudov, Ilyas H. Berredam
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: This paper presents the results of thermal testing of the MS9001FB gas turbine unit conducted at the Hadjret Ennous combined-cycle power plant in Algeria. Based on the experimental data, the actual performance characteristics of the CCGT were determined and their dependencies on operating modes were constructed. Given the operation under hot climate conditions, special attention was paid to the influence of ambient air temperature on turbine efficiency, including the combustion chamber’s thermal power and turbine efficiency. A comparative analysis was carried out using data from similar gas turbine units operating in various regions of the Russian Federation, which made it possible to identify the impact of climatic factors on performance indicators. Since the ThermoFlow software does not contain a ready-made model for the MS9001FB turbine used at the station, all parameters and coefficients were manually adjusted to match the real operating conditions as closely as possible. This approach allowed for the development of a reliable digital model of the CCGT unit, accurately reflecting the specific operational environment in Algeria.

title: A Hybrid Traffic Crash Risk Analysis Modeling of Crash Data-Driven Analysis and Microscopic Simulations
authors: Chiwoo Roh, Sungeun Cho, Haneul Park, Jaehyun Jason So
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This study proposes a hybrid framework integrating macroscopic crash frequency modeling with microscopic surrogate safety validation to identify high-risk traffic zones. Machine learning models, including Random Forest, XGBoost, and Deep Neural Networks (DNN), were trained separately for expressways and urban expressways using roadway and traffic attributes. High-risk segments were selected based on SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) values, ensuring interpretable and data-driven selection. Microscopic simulations were performed using the Simulation of Urban Mobility (SUMO) platform, and surrogate safety measures-Time to Collision (TTC) and Deceleration Rate to Avoid Crash (DRAC)-validated the elevated risk levels. Results confirmed that SHAP-identified high-risk segments exhibited significantly higher critical event rates than normal segments. Driving behavior analysis further revealed distinct risk patterns: aggressive longitudinal control on expressways and frequent lane changes on urban expressways. The proposed framework offers a robust and interpretable method for proactive traffic safety management, and future studies will focus on integrating environmental and driver-related factors to enhance generalizability.

title: Knowledge-Driven Framework for Online Monitoring and Fault Diagnosis in Manufacturing Lines
authors: Chen Zheng, Xudong Li, Chengran Jiang, Qin Wang, Han Wang, Zhiqiang Cai
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Manufacturing lines pose substantial challenges for operational safety, system reliability, and real-time monitoring.
