To address these challenges, this paper proposes a comprehensive framework for online monitoring and fault diagnosis in manufacturing lines, which aim to eliminate human presence from hazardous production environments through automation and intelligence. Drawing inspiration from mature online monitoring and fault diagnosis techniques in single manufacturing system, the proposed method accounts for the high-dimensional, multi-source, and heterogeneous nature of industrial data in complex manufacturing lines that contains a number of manufacturing systems. The framework incorporates causal discovery and complex network analysis for key parameter identification, and further integrates a knowledge graph to support semantic reasoning for fault diagnosis and emergency strategy recommendation. This approach enables accurate real-time status assessment, interpretable fault analysis, and adaptive decision-making in high-risk, highly automated production scenarios.

title: A Unified Network Equilibrium for Integrated Shared Mobility Services
authors: Xu Chen, Bowen Fang, Xuan Di
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This paper aims to combine both economic and network user equilibrium for ride-hailing and ride-sharing services. This paper proposes a unified network equilibrium framework that incorporates three key components: travelers' mode choices, e-platforms' decisions on vehicle dispatch and driver-passenger matching, and network congestion effects. The framework evaluates the impact of e-platform operational strategies on system performance, including deadhead miles and total system travel time. Numerical experiments on the Sioux Falls network demonstrate how congestion, search friction, and private vehicle ownership influence equilibrium outcomes and provide insights into optimizing e-platform operations. Our key finding is, high e-pooling price and high matching friction could make roads more congested and in turn hurt e-pooling demands further. To promote pooling service, policy makers and planners should consider taking a series of countermeasures, including subsidy to pooling customers or to e-hailing platforms, congestion pricing to e-solo customers, and regulation. The proposed equilibrium framework can efficiently assist policymakers and urban planners to evaluate the impact of e-hailing and ridesharing services on traffic congestion.

title: Efficient Driving Corridor Extraction for Autonomous Vehicles Using Best-First Reachability Analysis
authors: Florian Lercher, Matthias Althoff
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Planning traffic rule-compliant trajectories for autonomous vehicles is computationally expensive. It has been shown that restricting the search space of the trajectory planner to a driving corridor facilitates planning. Existing methods based on reachability analysis have to compute the entire reachable set before they can provide a single corridor. Since planners often require only one corridor, the computation of reachable states irrelevant to that corridor is superfluous. To address this issue, we propose treating reachability analysis for driving corridor extraction as a best-first search problem. Thus, we quickly identify a first corridor to start trajectory planning as early as possible; additional corridors are computed on demand. We experimentally compare different best-first search strategies and demonstrate the advantages of our approach.

title: Accelerating Electromagnetic Simulation with an Adaptive Frequency Sweep Toolbox Developed for MATLAB
authors: Shilpa T N, Rakesh Sinha
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper introduces a freely accessible adaptive frequency sweep toolbox developed for MATLAB R2025a. The primary goal of this tool is to speed up electromagnetic simulations by generating accurate S-parameter data. Two types of approaches have been implemented in this toolbox, semiadaptive and fully adaptive. The fundamental theory behind these algorithms is based on the Loewner matrix method. This tool chooses the minimum number of frequency points adaptively based on the system response. This work also evaluates the efficiency of the developed toolbox in comparison to the MATLAB R2025a frequency sweep object. The results indicate that our toolbox produces accurate S-parameter data while significantly accelerating the electromagnetic simulation.

title: LVLM-MPC Collaboration for Autonomous Driving: A Safety-Aware and Task-Scalable Control Architecture
authors: Kazuki Atsuta, Kohei Honda, Hiroyuki Okuda, Tatsuya Suzuki
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel Large Vision-Language Model (LVLM) and Model Predictive Control (MPC) integration framework that delivers both task scalability and safety for Autonomous Driving (AD). LVLMs excel at high-level task planning across diverse driving scenarios. However, since these foundation models are not specifically designed for driving and their reasoning is not consistent with the feasibility of lowlevel motion planning, concerns remain regarding safety and smooth task switching. This paper integrates LVLMs with MPC Builder, which automatically generates MPCs on demand, based on symbolic task commands generated by the LVLM, while ensuring optimality and safety. The generated MPCs can strongly assist the execution or rejection of LVLM-driven task switching by providing feedback on the feasibility of the given tasks and generating task-switching-aware MPCs. Our approach provides a safe, flexible, and adaptable control framework, bridging the gap between cutting-edge foundation models and reliable vehicle operation. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach through a simulation experiment, showing that our system can safely and effectively handle highway driving while maintaining the flexibility and adaptability of LVLMs.

title: C Band 400W Continuous Wave Solid State Linear Power Amplifier for Troposcatter Communication
authors: Anirudh Kumar, Priyanka Pai, Yachamaneni Sandeep, Kavitha Vadipilla, Yogesh T. K.
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper deals with the design and development of C band 400W Continuous Wave Solid State Linear Power Amplifier. This SSPA consists of RF section with low power section module followed by a pre driver module and two 250W pallets combined to achieve 400W in C-band for 400MHz bandwidth. This is a compact, programmable, cost effective and indigenous SSPA developed using GaN based power amplifier with a better efficiency and a gain of >65dB. The low power section module is designed to drive the necessary power level to pre driver, Automatic Level Control (ALC) and an attenuator for output power adjustment integrated with microcontroller. The DC bias section handling 42V at 65A current provides the necessary bias voltages for Low Power section module, pre driver, two 250W pallets and microcontroller. For thermal management, forced air cooling provision is made with 3nos of 120mm square fans having each high CFM max flow capacity. It operates with AC power supply of 240V at 9.5Ampere. To ensure maximum utilization of fan air, converging duct is provided. The designed and fabricated prototype is tested for all the parameters and meets the target specifications.

title: LeafNet-BD: Preventing Mango Sapling Fraud Using Deep Learning and Leaf Image Classification
authors: Jafir Islam Siam, Sadia Afrin, Md. Rafiul Islam, Pulock Kumar Kundu, Md. Mortuza Ahmmed
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Mango (Mangifera indica L.) is one of the most economically significant fruit crops in Bangladesh, with different varieties being grown to cater to different consumer preferences. Nevertheless, farmers and buyers are regularly left confused about authenticity while purchasing saplings as mislabeling or fraudulent sales of mango varieties are common. This paper introduces LeafNet-BD, which is a deep learning model designed specifically to detect different varieties of mangoes via leaf images. The proposed model has exploited MobileNetV2, which is an efficient CNN designed specifically to work in resource-constrained settings, such as mobile devices and field settings. The model was trained to classify the different mango leaf varieties with high accuracy using the MangoLeafVarietyBD dataset, with 2,744 raw images of eight different varieties. The model attained an accuracy around 90% and was further converted to work on web-based interaction via the Gradio tool. LeafNet-BD has proven to be an affordable deep learning method usable for authenticity verification with the potential to minimize fraud in the mango nursery industry in Bangladesh.

title: Flow-Augmented Domain Adaptation for Fault Diagnosis under Varying Operating Conditions
authors: Zhiyan Zhan, Guang Zhao, Shiqiang Hu, Lingkun Luo
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Fault diagnosis in civil aircraft presents significant challenges due to variable operational conditions across flight phases, mission profiles, and aircraft platforms. Most existing deep learning-based methods are trained and validated on labeled data collected under fixed or known conditions, referred to as the source domain. However, these models often fail to generalize to rare or previously unseen scenarios, which form the target domain, where labeled fault data is typically unavailable. To compensate for this lack of supervision, pseudo-labeling is commonly adopted to estimate target-domain labels. Yet, under substantial domain shift, pseudo-labels are prone to noise and unreliability. This leads to a scarcity of high-confidence target samples, which are essential for aligning conditional distributions and learning robust fault representations. To overcome this limitation, we propose a Flow Matching-based Fault Diagnosis (FMFD) framework that explicitly synthesizes reliable target-domain features through class-conditional flow modeling. FMFD transforms low-confidence features into semantically consistent representations by learning their evolution paths within a structured latent space. This process is guided by cross-domain class prototypes that fuse source-domain knowledge with target-domain statistics. Extensive experiments on both benchmark datasets and a real-world civil aircraft platform demonstrate that FMFD consistently outperforms existing transfer learning approaches, providing a robust and generalizable solution for fault diagnosis under complex and label-scarce conditions.

title: Monitoring and Measurement Estimation Using LiDAR Sensors on Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Sungeun Cho, Seonghoon Ryoo, Chiwoo Roh, Soomok Lee, Jaehyun So
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Traffic monitoring is crucial for effective traffic management, planning, and safety assessment. Conventional monitoring methods depend heavily on fixed infrastructure such as loop detectors and stationary video cameras, which, despite their accuracy, have inherent limitations including restricted spatial coverage, susceptibility to adverse weather conditions. In contrast, the rise of autonomous vehicles (AVs) equipped with advanced sensors such as LiDAR presents a unique opportunity. Although primarily intended for vehicle navigation and safety, these sensors continuously collect rich datasets that inherently provide comprehensive traffic information. This study proposes a proof-of-concept framework to demonstrate the feasibility of extracting detailed traffic metrics—such as lane-level speed, density, traffic flow, and time-to-collision (TTC)—from vehicle-mounted LiDAR sensors. Using the publicly available Waymo Open Dataset and the MCTrack algorithm, the research highlights how individual AV sensor data can be leveraged to derive meaningful traffic indicators without requiring additional public sector infrastructure investment. Furthermore, this study considers a scenario in which aggregating multiple AVs' sensor data significantly enhances spatial coverage and improves the reliability and comprehensiveness of macro-level traffic metrics as AV market penetration increases.

title: Data Processing Framework for GNSS Reflectometry Payload in EOS-08
authors: Sunita Khokhar, Himanshu Sharma, Diksha Gupta, Mahendra PS Bhadoria, Prantik Chakraborty, Pankaj Kanti Nath, Ch V N Rao, Ananya Ray
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper introduces a comprehensive data processing framework which is used for on-board performance analysis of India's first space borne Global Navigation Satellite System Reflectometry (GNSS-R) payload, flown in EOS-08 satellite. GNSS-R is a powerful passive remote sensing technique that leverages Earth-reflected GNSS signals to monitor surface characteristics. We detail the complete processing chain which includes raw intermediate frequency (IF) data acquisition, Delay Doppler Map (DDM) generation and the subsequent estimation of geo-referenced geophysical parameters like surface reflectivity and Normalized Bi-static Radar Cross Section (NBRCS).

title: Resolution and Performance Characterization of THz Imaging Systems
authors: A. Mercy Latha, Rishi Ranjan, K. Kabilan
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: Terahertz (THz) imaging has gained prominence in nondestructive testing due to its ability to probe beneath optically opaque surfaces without ionizing radiation. However, achieving high spatial resolution in THz imaging systems remains a challenge owing to the inherent diffraction limitations and system design constraints. Systematic characterization of imaging systems is essential to enable quantitative image reconstruction, particularly through deconvolution techniques that rely on accurate estimation of the system's point spread function. In THz imaging, such characterization poses unique challenges due to the longer wavelengths, beam divergence, and material-specific interactions associated with THz radiation. This study presents a comparative characterization of two THz imaging systems: a real-time THz line scanner system and a raster-scan tunable THz imaging system. Key system parameters, including spatial resolution, PSF, divergence angle, and numerical aperture, have been experimentally evaluated using standardized experimental procedures and resolution targets. The methodology adopted for evaluation has been discussed, with emphasis on imaging performance at various frequencies. Results indicate that the raster-scan tunable THz system exhibits significantly superior spatial resolution compared to the line scanner. These insights are critical for guiding system selection and optimization in application areas such as nondestructive testing, material inspection, and biomedical imaging.

title: Adversarial Graph Transfer Network with Sinkhorn Distance for Cross-Domain Fault Diagnosis of Civil Aircraft Landing Gear System
authors: Tingqian Tu, Ningyun Lu, Mengjie Zeng, Bin Jiang
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: The cross-domain fault diagnosis of civil aircraft landing gear systems faces significant challenges due to domain shifts and complex mechanical interactions. To address these issues, this paper proposes an Adversarial Graph Transfer Network with Sinkhorn Distance (AGTNSD), a novel framework integrating adversarial domain adaptation and graph representation learning for robust cross-domain fault diagnosis. The model introduces a Dynamic Node Interaction Attention (DNIA) mechanism within a hierarchical graph neural network to adaptively capture fault-related interactions between critical components. DNIA dynamically adjusts attention weights based on node adjacency relationships, prioritizing fault propagation paths and enhancing interpretability. Furthermore, the framework employs Sinkhorn distance as a distribution alignment metric between source and target domains, enabling efficient domain-invariant feature learning through optimal transport theory. Adversarial training with gradient reversal layers and multi-level domain discriminators minimizes domain discrepancies while preserving diagnostic discriminability. Experimental results on real-world landing gear system datasets demonstrate that AGTNSD outperforms state-of-the-art methods in cross-domain scenarios, achieving superior accuracy and robustness under varying operational conditions. This work provides a promising solution for safety-critical fault diagnosis tasks with limited labeled target domain data.

title: A Multi-Agent Approach to the Problem of Cargo Delivery Planning Using Unmanned Aerial Vehicles
authors: Anastasiya Galitskaya, Vladimir Galuzin
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: This paper addresses the problem of unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) management in the context of cargo delivery organization. A planning approach for logistic operations is proposed, based on the use of knowledge bases and multi-agent technologies. The core idea is to represent orders and resources as intelligent agents that interact with each other through cooperation and competition. Task allocation is carried out through self-organizing agent interactions until consensus is reached, ensuring alignment between the system’s needs and capabilities. The configuration of the multi-agent system is adapted to specific business processes using ontologies, which formalize domain knowledge and provide model flexibility. The introduction of new parameters or changes in operating conditions can be implemented without reprogramming, through the modification of ontological models. The proposed approach ensures adaptability, scalability, and resilience of the logistics system to external changes. It is particularly relevant for applications involving dynamic order inflow and limited resources, such as the delivery of medical supplies, food, or commercial packages using UAVs.

title: Technique for Allocating Spectrum in Wireless Systems enabled by Semantic Communications
authors: Sridhar Iyer, Vijay Rayar, Prabhakar Manage
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: This article proposes a spectrum allocation (SA) technique which aims to achieve efficient transmission in a semantic communications enabled wireless system (SCWS). The proposed technique considers a dense small cell base station (SCBS) scenario and implements deep learning within the problem formulation. In comparison to existing studies, within the problem formulation, the proposed technique includes the interference due to users using the same spectrum on a different SCBS. For efficient SA, the proposed technique implements matching theory to find and evaluate dependencies trend existing between adjacent users SA and corresponding changes in interference. Simulation experiments demonstrate (i) better performance of proposed technique in comparison to both, bit level and alternate SC methods, and (ii) that mutual interference between users impacts the SCWS performance significantly.

title: An Orthogonal Polynomial Model-Based Digital Pre-Distortion System for Improved and Numerically Stable Linearisation of RF-PA
authors: Amrit Kumar Panigrahi, Tarun Kumar Suretia, Karun Rawat
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This brief introduces an Orthogonal Polynomial Model (OPM)-based Digital Pre-distortion (DPD) system. The Legendre Polynomial is used as the orthogonal polynomial basis for this DPD system, and its envelope is normalised using a percentile-based approach. The performance of the DPD system is evaluated using two ZX60-V63+ power amplifiers (PAs) connected in cascade at 2.2 GHz. The in-band performance is estimated using Normalised Mean Square Error (NMSE) and Error Vector Magnitude (EVM), and the Adjacent Channel Power Ratio (ACPR) is used to measure the out-of-band performance. A logarithmically scaled dispersion ratio parameter is introduced to quantify the numerical stability of the DPD system. A detailed comparison of the OPM and the Memory Polynomial Model (MPM) is presented here, which demonstrates that OPM consistently outperforms MPM across all metrics with superior numerical stability while requiring the same computational complexity. The measurement set-up uses the ZC706 evaluation board for DPD modelling and the AD9371 RF Transceiver for RF operation.

title: HEED-Q: A Cluster-Structured Q-Routing Protocol Based on HEED for FANET
authors: Yuhao Qu, Kang Zong, Zhipeng Meng, Jingpu Wang
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Information and Communication Networks (ICICN)
abstract: This paper proposes HEED-Q, a cluster-based reinforcement learning routing protocol designed to enhance scalability in mobile ad hoc networks. The protocol integrates Q-learning with a Hybrid Energy-Efficient Distributed (HEED) clustering framework, in which only cluster heads participate in the route learning process. This hierarchical design significantly reduces Q-table complexity and learning overhead as the network scales. Simulation results show that HEED-Q achieves faster convergence and maintains higher packet delivery ratios and lower end-to-end delays than traditional Q-Routing, particularly in dense and highly mobile scenarios. These results suggest that combining distributed reinforcement learning with structural partitioning offers an effective approach to supporting large-scale, dynamic networks.

title: A Theme-Based Citywalk Route Planning Algorithm: Considering Repeated Route Avoidance with Online Platform Data
authors: Jiaxiang Yang, Yuxin He, Hao Wang, Jingjing Chen, Qin Luo, Tian Lei
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Theme-based CityWalk has emerged as an immersive urban exploration paradigm demanding personalized routes with minimal repetition in real-world road networks. Traditional methods suffer from static Points of Interest (POI) evaluation and idealized network modeling, causing route redundancy and poor thematic alignment. As a variant of the Traveling Salesman Problem (TSP), we propose a three-stage iterative framework to solve it. By leveraging user interaction data from online platforms, we dynamically score POIs to reflect individual preferences. Our three-stage approach involves selecting key POIs through spatial clustering, generating an initial route using Ant Colony Optimization (ACO), and developing an algorithm named Two-phase Adaptive Repetition Avoidance (TPARA) to adaptively avoid repeated routes. Experiments conducted on the urban road network of Shenzhen demonstrate that the proposed optimization algorithm reduces the route repetition by at least 39 % and improves overall performance by at least 39.2 %, proving its effectiveness in enhancing personalized tourism experiences. This work bridges theoretical route planning problems and real-world urban network constraints, providing municipalities with a computationally tractable framework for enhancing cultural tourism experiences.

title: Microwave Testing of $\mathrm{n}-\text{Ga}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3} / \mathrm{p}$-Si Diodes via RF Sputtering for High-Frequency Applications
authors: Mandira Biswas, Amartya Paul, Hemant Kumari, Shubhankar Majumdar
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This work presents the RF characterization of an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n$</tex>-<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Ga}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3} / p$</tex>-Si heterostructure diode fabricated via RF magnetron sputtering. The wide bandgap <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Ga}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$</tex> thin film was deposited on a low-cost <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p$</tex>-type silicon substrate, forming a vertical heterojunction structure suitable for high-frequency applications. Structural analysis confirmed the formation of polycrystalline <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta-\text{Ga}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$</tex> with an optical bandgap of 4.62 eV. S-parameter measurements were carried out over the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5-11 \text{GHz}$</tex> range using a vector network analyzer with a coplanar waveguide test setup. Transmission line-based RF characterization was conducted to evaluate and eliminate the influence of the test fixture on the measured device response. To isolate the intrinsic device behavior, a MATLAB-based two-tier de-embedding technique was employed. After de-embedding, the device exhibited a return loss of 20.26 dB, minimal insertion loss of 0.32 dB, and reduced group delay, indicating efficient RF transmission characteristics. Strong contrast between ON and OFF states was observed, validating the diode's potential as an RF switch. These results underscore the viability of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Ga}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3} / \text{Si}$</tex> heterostructures in future microwave and high-frequency electronic systems.

title:
Evaluation of Mechanical Properties of C
                    <sub>60</sub>
                    Fullerene Reinforced Ti
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    Al under Uniaxial Tensile Deformation: A Molecular Dynamics Study
authors: Alexandr Narseev, Pavel Zakharov, Ustina Yankovskaya, Olga Komissarenko, Egor Shchepilov
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: This study presents a simulation of a Ti<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>Al metalmatrix composite reinforced with fullerenes. The simulation was performed using molecular dynamics methods, followed by calculations on the SPbPU supercomputer. Molecular dynamics techniques were employed to model the material’s behavior at the atomic level, accounting for interparticle interactions. A hybrid potential was used for a more accurate description of these interactions. A comparison was made between the mechanical properties of the “pure” material and the fullerenereinforced composite, with additional comparisons involving reinforced materials containing varying numbers of vacancies. Evaluated characteristics included yield strength, ultimate tensile strength, strain, and fracture load. Deformation was applied along the crystal direction corresponding to the [100] Miller index. For reinforcement, a $\mathbf{C}_{60}$ fullerene was placed at the center of the simulation cell. The results demonstrated a reduction in mechanical properties in both the reinforced material and the material with increased vacancy concentration. The decrease in ultimate tensile strength in both cases was attributed to several factors. Primarily, the fullerene exhibited favorable behavior under compressive strain but had a detrimental effect under tensile strain due to its dimensional constraints. Vacancies facilitated dislocation formation, leading to accelerated failure of the metallic matrix. The experimental conditions, particularly the periodic boundary constraints imposed on the model, also played a significant role. The findings indicate that both defect structures and reinforcing elements critically influence the mechanical properties of the material.

title: An EKF-based Algorithm for Rapid Extraction of the Fundamental Components of Fault Signals in Power Distribution Networks
authors: Yang Diao, Ronghai Zhang, Yao Chen, Yifei Chen
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Mechanical Engineering and Power Engineering (MEPE)
abstract: Extensive application of distributed generation (DG) systems and electronic power equipment has altered the characteristics of fault signals in power distribution networks (PDNs). As a corollary, interference from the decaying direct current (DC) and from the harmonic components of fault signals becomes increasingly difficult to ignore. The conventional algorithm based on the discrete Fourier transform requires at least one half of a fundamental period $(0.01 \mathrm{~s})$ to extract the fundamental components. In addition, due to the presence of a decaying DC component, this algorithm produces large errors and is unable to meet the needs of rapid extraction. Hence, this study presents a “prediction and correction"-based algorithm for rapid extraction of the fundamental components of PDN fault signals. This algorithm is established based on the basic principle of the extended Kalman filter algorithm and through construction of state and measurement equations for PDN fault signals that account for the decaying aperiodic and harmonic components. The simulation results show that the proposed algorithm can accurately extract fundamental signals within a quarter of a fundamental period $(0.005 \mathrm{~s})$, with a maximum relative error of less than 5%. On the one hand, this algorithm can be used to protect circuit breakers and improve fault removal and relay protection speeds; on the other hand, it can serve as a reference for dynamic stability analyses of isolated microgrids containing DG systems.

title: Federated Learning for Privacy Preserving AI: A Scalable Approach to Decentralized Model Training in Healthcare and Finance
authors: Ravi Teja Potla
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Information and Communication Networks (ICICN)
abstract: The increase in demand of data driven decision making in sensitive fields like healthcare and finance requires machine learning frameworks that maintain strict data privacy and follow regulations. Federated Learning (FL) provides a decentralized way to train models. It allows multiple organizations to learn together from distributed datasets without sharing raw data. But, traditional FL methods, such as Federated Averaging (FedAvg), face issues in real world situations. These issues arise from different data distributions among clients and the risk of information leaks from shared model updates. In this research study, we introduce a new federated learning framework with two main innovations: First the adaptive aggregation strategy that adjusts client contributions based on how stable they are and their quality, and second an optional differential privacy module at the server to make sure privacy guarantees. We tested the framework on two publicly available datasets: a heart disease dataset from the University of California, Irvine (UCI) repository and a large financial dataset from Kaggle. This simulates collaboration between hospitals and financial institutions. Experimental results show that our adaptive aggregation method boosts model accuracy by up to 4.2% compared to FedAvg, while still performing well even with differential privacy applied. The model achieves an AUC of 0.93 and an F1 score of 0.891, with minimal communication overhead. These results confirm the framework’s strength and its ability to support the ethical use of Artificial Intelligence in regulated and data sensitive areas. They also recommend it can scale effectively across larger federated networks.

title: BFISense: Using Beamforming Feedback Information for Wi-Fi Sensing
authors: Weixian Liao, Yudong Zhou, Zhongtao Lu, Ruiyang Zhou, Hewei Yu
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Information and Communication Networks (ICICN)
abstract: Wi-Fi sensing has garnered significant attention and investment due to its low cost, wide applications, non - invasiveness, and high precision. Recent years have seen progress in Wi-Fi-based wireless sensing, but current technologies heavily depend on Channel State Information (CSI)[4][7][8]. Acquiring CSI needs specialized devices, which have a real-world penetration rate below 10%[9], thereby limiting the expansion of its application. Recent research shows Beamforming Feedback Matrix (BFM) is similar to CSI and can facilitate Wi - Fi sensing[10]. Approximately 45% of commercial Wi - Fi devices can acquire BFM, solving CSI's application limitation[9]. This research innovatively employs BFM to train a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural network for Wi - Fi sensing. Through experiments designed to mimic real-world conditions, the model realizes functions like human action recognition[1][2][3][4], indoor localization[2][5], and trajectory tracking. Experimental results show that the BFM-based LSTM model performs well. It achieves up to 86.38% accuracy in human action recognition, maintains indoor localization error within 1 meter, and has good trajectory-tracking results. Compared with CSI-based methods, it has advantages in device range and complex-environment adaptability. In summary, BFM-based Wi-Fi sensing provides wide compatibility and has potential in fields like smart homes. It is anticipated to promote large-scale implementation of Wi-Fi sensing.

title: TGNet: Texture-Guided Network for Strip Steel Surface Defect Detection
authors: Sheng Gao, Honghao Wang, Lianlei Lin, Jiawei Wang
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Strip steel surface defect segmentation is a challenging task due to the high visual similarity between defect regions and the surrounding normal surface. Motivated by the sensitivity of texture features to subtle visual differences, as well as the success of camouflaged object detection (COD) in distinguishing visually similar patterns, this study explores the effectiveness of optimizing a deep model with texture features and proposes a texture-guided COD network (TGNet) for strip steel surface defect segmentation. In which a target texture learning module is used to guide the model to learn the texture features of the camouflaged object. A texture guiding module integrates target texture features and features extracted by the feature extractor. An adjacent fusion module integrates features from adjacent layers. The experimental results obtained on the four datasets demonstrate that the texture-guided method outperformed the other state-of-the-art methods for both strip steel surface defect segmentation and naturally camouflaged object detection.

title: A High Gain Wideband Dual-Polarized Stacked Antenna in Sub-6 GHz Band
authors: Savita Baraskar, Rinkee Chopra
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This study presents a design of high gain dual-polarized wideband antenna by stacking electromagnetically coupled symmetrical quarter sectoral annular patches on top of a differentially fed metallic circular patch. Hexagonal shaped metallic walls are used for mechanical support and to enhance the antenna gain. Multi-resonance of driven and electromagnetically coupled patches provide a wideband and high gain response. Systematic design steps are presented to demonstrate broadband and high gain behavior of the proposed antenna. The antenna is further fed by a wideband feed network to incorporate differential feeding. Radiation characteristics of the designed antenna reflect a stable dual-polarized boresight operation over 54 % bandwidth from 3.08 to 5.37 GHz, peak gain of 11.5 dBi 2 dB variation over bandwidth, back radiation of less than -15 dB in a small antenna profile of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.16 \lambda$</tex>. The designed antenna characteristics of polarization diversity, high gain and wideband operation make it suitable to be utilized for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 G}$</tex> services in sub-6 GHz band.

title: Propagation of Seismic Waves and the Research Results of Their Impact on the Rocks
authors: Alexander F. Pashchennko
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: The paper considers the problem of propagation of elastic seismic waves, caused by Plasma-Pulse treatment of oil and gas wells. The theoretical approach is observed. The results of experimental microseismic monitoring provided. It is shown that the low-frequency waves from PPT can propagate up to several kilometers away from the source of oscillations.

title: Experimental Characterization of Micro-Milled Sub-THz Folded Waveguide Structure for TWT
authors: Anurag Srivastava, M S Karthik, V Bhanu Naidu, Santanu Karmakar, K Niranjan Reddy, Subrata Kumar Datta
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: A broadband folded waveguide slow-wave structure was designed by MWS CST solver at sub-THz frequency spectrum. Therefore, high aspect ratio fully metallic copper structure was fabricated by high precision CNC micro-milling machine. The micron feature size structure dimensions were obtained within 5 micron tolerances. The insertion-loss measurement results were fairly matched with simulated results in the desired frequency band.

title: Zonal Feedback Control of the Heat Conduction Process by Means of Lumped Sources
authors: Kamil R. Aidazade, Samir Z. Guliyev
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: This study presents a numerical approach to solving feedback control problems for systems governed by distributed parameter equations, using the rod heating process as a case study. The powers of the heat sources serve as the control input. The approach implements feedback using the system’s phase state measured at selected spatial points, and introduces the concept of zonal control, wherein the system’s observable state space is partitioned into zones, each associated with a constant control value. Unlike conventional feedback laws - wherein control values continuously depend on the current state - the zonal strategy updates control inputs only when the system transitions between predefined regions of the state space. This reduces actuator wear and enhances robustness with respect to uncertainties present in the initial state and the surrounding medium’s temperature. The resulting feedback control problem is reformulated as a finite-dimensional parametric optimization problem. Gradient-based methods are employed for optimization, with explicit expressions derived for the gradient of the objective functional. Numerical experiments conducted on test problems demonstrate the efficacy of the approach in accurately tracking desired terminal temperature profiles while maintaining computational efficiency and robustness.

title: Adaptive Learning Systems: Enhancing Student Outcomes through AI Personalization
authors: Sadi Mohammad Meraj, Md. Mortuza Ahmmed
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is a disruptive innovation in contemporary education, particularly due to the development of adaptive learning systems (ALS), which personalize teaching/learning experiences by extracting data from individual learner performance. Using machine learning, natural language processing, and predictive analytics, adaptive learning systems continuously collect and analyze learners’ performance data and personalize learning materials in a real-time framework. This study considers the impact of AI-based adaptive systems on engagement, motivation, and student performance in various educational contexts. A mixed-methods design was employed using both quantitative and qualitative data from adaptive learning systems such as DreamBox and adaptive platforms such as ALEKS, Knewton, and Smart Sparrow. The findings demonstrated a 23learning outcomes from adaptive learning systems compared to traditional instructional methods. According to the survey data, over 80learning experiences, although both students and instructors raised some concerns about the data privacy of certain applications and translation algorithms. In conclusion, the study showed that AI-powered adaptive systems can significantly improve educational effectiveness, yet AI-enabled adaptive systems need to address existing problems to be implemented effectively such as ethics, infrastructure, and teacher readiness. Future research needs to include longitudinal impact data, consider the cultural contexts of applications, and create an ethical framework as guiding principles for equitable AI applications in education.

title: MIMO-OTSM: LMS Based Decision Feedback Equalization in Quasi-Static Channels
authors: Sapta Girish Neelam
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This work presents a robust and efficient detection scheme for MIMO systems utilizing Orthogonal Time Sequency Multiplexing (OTSM) modulation, designed to maintain high performance under practical hardware limitations, including carrier frequency offset (CFO). Employing decision feedback equalization (DFE) with a least mean squares (LMS) approach, the method effectively mitigates the combined effects of multipath fading, inter-antenna interference, and spatial correlation, enhancing signal quality. A carefully designed frame structure further minimizes interference and enables efficient parallel processing. Novel timing synchronization is achieved through a Gardner synchronization loop. This combined approach of low-complexity detection and robust timing recovery significantly improves MIMO-OTSM system performance in static channels, with simulations showing that a single-user (SU) system utilizing MRC outperforms conventional detectors, particularly in spa tially correlated environments.

title: 1176.45 MHz to 1602 MHz Wideband Low-Noise Amplifier for GNSS Receiver Systems
authors: Bargaje Ganesh Pandurang, Kamini Singh, Avinash Singh, Karun Rawat
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This article presents the design of a wideband low-noise amplifier (LNA) for Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) applications. When attempting to cover the entire GNSS frequency range, LNAs present challenges due to their high noise figure, limited gain, increased design complexity, and high cost. To address these issues with wideband operation, a series RC circuit was placed in shunt at the transistor's output, combined with multi-stub matching network. For performance validation, the LNA is designed and simulated with Keysight ADS software. The EM simulation results show a maximum gain of 34.7 dB, a noise figure consistently less than 0.83 dB, and an output P1dB greater than 15.2 dBm. The proposed LNA is a promising candidate and provides a cost-effective solution for multi-frequency GNSS receivers in satellite-based positioning systems.

title: Enhanced Spectral Efficiency in Input-Dependent IM-DD Terrestrial FSO Systems Using Receiver Spatial Array and Aperture Averaging Techniques
authors: Himani Verma, Kamal Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: In this paper, we analyze the use of receiver spatial array and aperture averaging techniques for spectral efficiency improvement in terrestrial free-space optical (FSO) intensity channels operating under signal-dependent noise and strong atmospheric turbulence. We consider a FSO communication with intensity modulation at the transmitter and direct detection at the receiver (IM-DD), subjected to strong turbulence-induced irradiance fluctuations modeled by the gamma-gamma distribution. A simple unified closed-form expression is derived to accurately characterize the asymptotic growth of the average spectral efficiency achievable with these fading mitigation techniques in the high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) regime. Numerical results demonstrate significant spectral efficiency improvements with increasing aperture size and number of receiving apertures.

title: AviationLMM: A Large Multimodal Foundation Model for Civil Aviation
authors: Wenbin Li, Jingling Wu, Xiaoyong Lin, Jing Chen, Cong Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Information Communication and Software Engineering (ICICSE)
abstract: Civil aviation is a cornerstone of global transportation and commerce, and ensuring its safety, efficiency and customer satisfaction is paramount. Yet conventional Artificial Intelligence (AI) solutions in aviation remain siloed and narrow, focusing on isolated tasks or single modalities. They struggle to integrate heterogeneous data such as voice communications, radar tracks, sensor streams and textual reports, which limits situational awareness, adaptability, and real- time decision support. This paper introduces the vision of AviationLMM, a Large Multimodal foundation Model for civil aviation, designed to unify the heterogeneous data streams of civil aviation and enable understanding, reasoning, generation and agentic applications. We firstly identify the gaps between existing AI solutions and requirements. Secondly, we describe the model architecture that ingests multimodal inputs such as air-ground voice, surveillance, on-board telemetry, video and structured texts, and performs cross-modal alignment and fusion, and produces flexible outputs ranging from situation summaries and risk alerts to predictive diagnostics and multimodal incident reconstructions. In order to fully realize this vision, we identify key research opportunities to address, including data acquisition, alignment and fusion, pretraining, reasoning, trustworthiness, privacy, robustness to missing modalities, and synthetic scenario generation. By articulating the design and challenges of AviationLMM, we aim to boost the civil aviation foundation model progress and catalyze coordinated research efforts toward an integrated, trustworthy and privacy - preserving aviation AI ecosystem.

title: CMOS-Compatible CPW Line Integrated with Electrostatically Actuated Lateral MEMS Switch for D-Band Communication
authors: Prince Gupta, Shakthi Priya M, Harshit Kumar, Mahesh Angira, Nalini Pareek, Dhairya Singh Arya
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents the design and analysis of a CMOS-compatible, electrostatically actuated lateral switch integrated on a coplanar waveguide (CPW) line optimised for D-Band communication applications. The structure is designed on a SOI wafer considering CMOS-compatible processes. The CPW-Line incorporates a laterally movable MEMS beam, enabling dynamic signal routing across the 100 GHz to 170 GHz frequency range. Electrostatic actuation achieves reliable lateral displacement with a stimulated pull-in differential biased voltage of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 20 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex> and a low insertion loss of approximately -2 dB when the switch is closed, indicating efficient signal transmission across the CPW line. In contrast, the isolation below -25 dB in the open state highlights effective signal suppression and minimal leakage. Additionally, a return loss below - 20 dB was observed, confirming excellent impedance matching at the input port.

title: Ensemble of Pre-Trained Models for Long-Tailed Trajectory Prediction
authors: Divya Thuremella, Yi Yang, Simon Wanna, Lars Kunze, Daniele De Martini
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This work explores the application of ensemble modeling to the multidimensional regression problem of trajectory prediction for vehicles in urban environments. As newer and bigger state-of-the-art prediction models for autonomous driving continue to emerge, an important open challenge is the problem of how to combine the strengths of these big models without the need for costly re-training. We show how, perhaps surprisingly, combining state-of-the-art deep learning models out-of-the-box (without retraining or fine-tuning) with a simple confidence-weighted average method can enhance the overall prediction. Indeed, while combining trajectory prediction models is not straightforward, this simple approach enhances performance by 10% over the best prediction model, especially in the long-tailed metrics. We show that this performance improvement holds on both the NuScenes and Argoverse datasets, and that these improvements are made across the dataset distribution. The code11https://github.com/dthuremella/Ensemble-of-Pretrained-Models for our work is open source.

title: Multi-source Information Attention Mechanism Feature Fusion Method for Motor Fault Diagnosis
authors: Xiaoyun Gong, Shikang Zhang, Lin Zhao, Yuemin Zhang, Ming Han
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: An electric motor is a critical piece of equipment in rotating machinery, and its operating condition directly affects the reliability of the engineering system. Efficient predictive maintenance and fault diagnosis technologies are crucial. Traditional intelligent diagnosis methods primarily rely on single-source sensor signals, making it difficult to fully characterize the composite fault characteristics of the motor. Therefore, to address the above issues, a multi-source information fusion deep convolutional neural network based on the attention mechanism is proposed in this paper. Firstly, this method extracts fault features of vibration and current signals separately through a multichannel architecture, which can effectively avoid the conflation of feature information. Then, the channel attention module is introduced to adaptively optimize the weight distribution during the multi-source feature fusion process, enhancing the collaborative representation of fault-sensitive features. Finally, dropout is adopted to reduce redundancy and enhance the extraction of high-quality features from different signal sources. Fault detection experiments conducted on the experimental platform show that the accuracy of this method reaches 96.37%, which is higher than that of single-source signal-based CNNs and traditional multi-source data fusion models, proving the feasibility and effectiveness of this method.

title: Priority-based Dynamic Power Allocation in Wi-Fi Concurrency Scenarios
authors: K Pavithra, Sonu Akhand, Satyabrata Sahu, Sivagurunathan Senthil Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 6th International Women in Technology Conference (WINTECHCON)
abstract: Modern smartphones are equipped to operate several wireless interfaces simultaneously, such as cellular networks, IEEE 802.11 (Wi-Fi) and Bluetooth. This concurrent operation presents significant challenges in managing throughput, power consumption, and safety constraints. Each of these interfaces plays a vital role in the overall performance and functionality of the device, and they collectively influence the Time-Averaged Specific Absorption Rate (TAS) requirements of the device, a metric that quantifies the amount of electromagnetic energy absorbed by the human body during interactions with the smartphone. Regulatory compliance requires that the cumulative RF exposure across these technologies remains within prescribed safety limits. Managing Wi-Fi transmit power is particularly challenging in concurrent scenarios due to dynamic variations in network factors such as traffic type, channel conditions, and Wi-Fi or non-Wi-Fi interference. This paper proposes a novel dynamic power allocation algorithm for Wi-Fi concurrency scenarios, like Station, Access Point, Wi-Fi Aware, Peer-to-Peer, etc., that operate within a unified TAS budget. The proposed algorithm dynamically adjusts transmit power in real-time based on Wi-Fi traffic, link quality, peer device information, and residual SAR budget. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach maintains TAS compliance while achieving significant throughput improvements across diverse network conditions.

title: MEPE 2025 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Mechanical Engineering and Power Engineering (MEPE)

title: Reed–Muller Sequence Enabled Low-complexity Index Redefinition based Sparse Vector Coding
authors: Linjie Yang
abstract: Sparse vector coding (SVC) has attracted considerable attention for ultra-reliable and low-latency communications due to its excellent block error rate performance. However, conventional SVC schemes suffer from high lookup-table and storage complexity when the number of information bits increases. In this letter, a low-complexity index redefinition–based SVC scheme aided by Reed–Muller (RM) sequences is proposed. By mapping the transmitted binary information bits to the generation parameters of RM sequences, the index tables storing the information about non-zero elements in the sparse vector are no longer required. Moreover, exploiting the algebraic structure of RM sequences eliminates the need for the entire measurement matrix in the multi-path matching pursuit algorithm, significantly reducing the storage overhead. Simulation results show that the proposed scheme achieves nearly identical block error rate performance to existing index redefinition–based SVC schemes, and even slightly outperforms when the information payload approaches the maximum supported by the RM-IRSVC structure.

title: RollShard: Atomic Multi-shard Transactions via Verifiable Stateless Off-chain Processing
authors: Dengcheng Hu, Jianrong Wang, Hao Xu, Xiulong Liu, Wenyu Qu
abstract: Ensuring atomic execution of cross-shard transactions is a fundamental challenge for sharded blockchains, particularly in scenarios demand coordination across multiple shards. However, existing solutions either rely on on-chain coordination, leading to high communication overhead, or leverage secure hardware for off-chain execution, imposing strong trust assumptions and reducing general applicability. To this end, we propose RollShard, a sharded blockchain that integrates stateless off-chain mechanism to efficiently process multi-shard transactions (MSTs). In RollShard, each MST is abstracted into a transaction DAG by the Sequencer Shard to ensure the authenticity of the transaction content and the correctness of its execution order. Batched MSTs are dispatched to off-chain executors, each of which simulates transaction logic using a virtual zero-state model integrate with a hierarchical state-delta tree (HSDT). The HSDT employs a Merkle Sum tree to precisely capture batched MSTs’ impact on per-shard account states. Based on the HSDT, the executor generates the zero-knowledge proof to attest the correctness of each shard’s state changes and global value conservation. The resulting net state deltas are then optimistically committed to the relevant shards without cross-shard coordination, reducing intra-shard coordination. We design a game-theoretic incentive mechanism to ensure rational behavior of off-chain executors, showing that honest execution forms a Nash equilibrium under collateral staking. Experimental results based on a prototype deployed in a local area network demonstrate that R<sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OLL</small>S<sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">HARD</small> significantly outperforms two baseline coordination models proposed in ByShard, namely the Linear and Distributed designs. Specifically, under high workload, RollShard improves throughput by 44.9% and 158%, and reduces cross-shard latency by 38.9% and 42.1%, compared to the Linear and Distributed models, respectively.

title: SAD: Sparse-Aware Diffusion Model for Single-Cell Gene Expression Completion
authors: Tianhao Li, Yixin Xiang, Zixuan Wang, Mengwen Liu, Quan Zou, Yongqing Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: Single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) is entering an era of foundation models that accept the complete gene atlas as input, yet most current datasets cover only 10-12k genes and contain numerous technical zeros, severely limiting the generalization of these models in downstream tasks. To address this, we pioneer the gene-completion task for scRNA-seq and present SAD, a diffusion-based framework tailored to extremely sparse data, capable of completing genes and correcting sparsity bias under high missing rates. Unlike imputation or reconstruction methods that rely on the i.i.d. assumption, SAD's completion paradigm can generate gene entries originally absent from the expression profile, be aware of and rectify sparsity-distribution bias, and supply foundation models with consistent, reliable inputs of more than 30k genes. Extensive benchmarks show that SAD significantly outperforms existing methods across multiple completion metrics, particularly in extreme scenarios with missing rates above 80%. This provides a data foundation for reusing missing scRNA-seq information and for precision-medicine applications. The code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/ZhangLab312/SAD</uri>.

title: Rethinking Transparent Object Grasping: Depth Completion with Monocular Depth Estimation and Instance Mask_supp1-3673898.mp4
authors: Yaofeng Cheng
abstract: Accurate depth maps are essential for robotic grasping. However, transparent objects often cause depth cameras to produce missing or distorted depth due to reflection and refraction, making grasping them particularly challenging. Precise depth estimation for transparent objects is therefore crucial. Existing end-to-end methods aim to predict depth directly from RGB-D inputs. However, depth distortion varies significantly with environment, viewpoint, and illumination, making it difficult to predict. Models trained on limited datasets often fail to generalize effectively in real-world applications. In this letter, we propose ReMake, a novel transparent object depth completion framework that injects structured priors to reformulate the depth estimation learning objective. First, we introduce a mask prior to explicitly separate transparent regions from reliable background depth. This separation ensures consistency between training and real-world application, where masked areas correspond to transparent regions and unmasked areas to opaque ones. This improves the model's generalizability in real-world applications. Second, we further incorporate relative depth as an additional prior obtained from monocular depth estimation, which encodes the depth relationship between transparent objects and their background to guide the reconstruction of transparent regions more effectively. Extensive experiments across datasets and real-world scenes demonstrate that ReMake significantly outperforms existing methods, especially in different-view prediction experiments, evaluating its generalizabilityCode and videos are available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://chengyaofeng.github.io/ReMake.github.io/</uri>.

title: Multi-level Multimodal Visual Neural Decoding for Generalized Visual Object Recognition
authors: Wenlong Hang, Junliang Wang, Shuang Liang, Qiong Wang, Guanglin Li, Jing Qin, Badong Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive and Developmental Systems
abstract: The decoding of human visual neural signals has garnered significant attention due to its potential to reveal the underlying cognitive mechanisms of the brain. However, existing visual neural decoding methods mainly focus on instance-level multimodal semantic correspondences, often neglecting local-level and concept-level supervisory information. These limitations substantially weaken their generalizability to downstream tasks. In this study, we propose a multi-level multimodal visual neural decoding (MMVND) framework that leverages multi-level semantic correspondences between paired image stimuli and brain activities to address these limitations. Specifically, we initially employ the instance-level alignment module to capture the correspondence between brain activity-image pairs. Subsequently, a cross-attention mechanism is utilized to learn the matching between tokens from different modalities. The local-level alignment module further exploits the local semantic information based on matched tokens to establish fine-grained local correspondences. To capture concept-level correspondences, we design a momentum update strategy to generate prototypes for different modalities. The concept-level alignment module then utilizes the prototype-based semantic information to capture the concept-level multimodal correspondences. Synergistic learning across multi-level multimodal semantic correspondences significantly enhances the performance of generalized visual object recognition. Extensive experiments conducted on publicly available visual neural datasets demonstrate the superiority of the proposed multi-level multimodal learning framework.

title: Space Vector Modulation with Common-Mode Voltage Reduction and Dead Zone Compensation for Cascaded H-Bridge Multilevel Converters
authors: Wenjun Zeng, Guohua Zhou, Nengmou Xu, Yongjie Chen, Wei Wang, Cui Wang
abstract: This article proposes two space vector modulation (SVM) methods to reduce common-mode voltage (CMV) and compensate for the effect of dead zones in cascaded H-bridge multilevel converters (CHBMC). Firstly, a zero CMV (ZCMV) SVM method is proposed to use reference phase signals to obtain optimal switching sequences, ZCMV switching states, and corresponding duty cycles by simple calculations and judgements. The proposed ZCMV SVM method can minimize switching frequency, reduce switching losses, and theoretically realize ZCMV output. Secondly, the dead zones in CHBMC and the effect of the dead zones on CMV and output waveforms are analyzed. A ZCMV with dead zone compensation (ZCMV-C) SVM method is proposed in this article. The ZCMV-C SVM method compensates for the asymmetrical output waveforms and CMV spikes caused by the dead zones, reduces the leakage current, and only requires simple calculations and judgements. Finally, compared with preceding methods, the proposed ZCMV SVM method requires the least computational time. Furthermore, the proposed two SVM methods can be extended to any level of converters without affecting the computational time. Simulations and experiments verify the effectiveness of the proposed two SVM methods.

title: RISC-V Vectorized Softmax Acceleration for IoT Edge Inference Systems
authors: Chin-Feng Lai, Po-Chih Liu, Shih-Hsin Huang, Hsiao-Hwa Chen
abstract: In Internet-of-Things (IoT) edge inference systems, Softmax is a core component in multi-class classification heads and attention mechanisms. In resource-constrained IoT edge devices, conventional numerically stable implementations require multiple passes over the data, which increases memory band-width pressure and leads to hardly predictable variable processing latency. This paper presents Conditional Online Softmax, a control-flow-predictable algorithm that fuses reduction and normalization into a single pass to cut memory traffic while preserving numerical stability. We pair it with a branch-free vectorized kernel implemented using RISC-V Vector Extension (RVV) intrinsics, which better exploits instruction-level parallelism on low-core-count edge processors. On a standard RISC-V edge platform, Conditional Online Softmax achieves a 7% speedup over the numerically stable safe Softmax, and a 43% speedup over the original online Softmax. Furthermore, manual RVV vectorization delivers a 2:9× speedup compared to GNU Compiler Collection (GCC) auto-vectorization. The resulting design reduces cache misses and improves latency predictability, making it a drop-in replacement for Softmax in streaming and micro-batch pipelines commonly found in IoT gateways and sensor nodes. The implementation is portable across vector length (VLEN) and standard element width (SEW) settings and requires no specialized accelerators, which simplifies its deployment in practical IoT edge inference systems.

title: EMC Personality Profile
authors: N/A
abstract: I would like to introduce you to Vasiliki (Vasso) Gkatsi, a young professional in our community, through this personality profile. Under the guidance of Professor Frank Leferink, she has been a participant in the “Pan-European Training, Research and Education Network on Electromagnetic Risk Management” (PETER), a Marie Curie doctoral training program funded by the EU. So, you might have met her recently at one of the EMC conferences.

title: Towards Intelligent Microfluidics: An LLM-Based Droplets Controlling Framework
authors: Fangdi Li, Zelin Wang, Dianhua Zhang, Yuke Pan, Jianguang Zhou
abstract: Digital microfluidics (DMF) is a versatile platform that enables parallel droplet operations and on-site programmable control, where droplet routing is a critical challenge for achieving automation. To enable automatic path generation from source to target electrodes and process-level feedback correction, this study proposes an adaptive intelligent control framework based on large language models (LLMs). The framework integrates language-guided topological map reconstruction, LLM-based path planning, and a dual closed-loop feedback mechanism to achieve high-precision droplet manipulation. To alleviate the spatial hallucination issues of LLMs in spatial reasoning such as discontinuous path planning and out-of-bound errors, a linguistic topological map reconstruction method is designed, improving path generation accuracy by 23.4% in complex obstacle environments compared to conventional spatial representations. In addition, a dual closed-loop feedback strategy is proposed, consisting of model self-correction feedback and camera-based image feedback, which enables real-time correction of routing deviations and enhances operational stability. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves 100% task success across various droplet distribution scenarios, significantly advancing the intelligence of microfluidic operations and providing a promising foundation for fully autonomous DMF systems.

title: Establishing Correlation between Material Properties and Space Charge Dynamics in Oil-Impregnated Paper Insulations
authors: Chinmay Y. Jani, Santosh C. Vora
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: The insulation systems in power equipment are continuously subjected to thermal, electrical, environmental, and mechanical stresses, which progressively alter their dielectric and structural properties over time. In addition to high-voltage operation, imperfections in the chemical and physical composition of insulation materials contribute to space charge accumulation. This study investigates the complex behavior of space charge in oil-impregnated paper (OIP) systems, using various types of insulating paper impregnated with oils at different stages of natural aging. The results demonstrate that the properties of insulating oil, in conjunction with those of the paper, play a critical role in space charge behavior. Key oil characteristics— such as interfacial tension (IFT), electrical conductivity, and dissipation factor (Tan δ)—are found to significantly influence charge transport and trapping phenomena. A strong correlation is observed between the degree of polymerization (DP) and IFT, consistent with the findings from Fourier Transform Infrared (FTIR) spectroscopy, indicating the concurrent degradation of both oil and paper. Furthermore, the dissipation factor plays a critical role in governing charge recombination and migration. These insights highlight the importance of concurrently monitoring multiple interrelated parameters to develop a comprehensive understanding of space charge dynamics and to assess the long-term reliability of insulation systems.

title: Effect of Electrode Charge Transfer on the Electric Field in Oil–Pressboard Insulation Systems: Measurements and Simulations
authors: Deniz Targitay, Markus Bier, Markus H. Zink, René Manger, Hans-Peter Öftering, Michael Geissler, Stefan Jaufer, Balz Schlittler, Andreas Küchler
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Mineral oil (MO)–pressboard (PB) composites are crucial to the insulation system of High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) converter transformers. Under direct current (DC) stress, the dielectric relaxation of these composite systems requires thorough investigation, particularly regarding local electric field distribution across different configurations. We address the lack of high-voltage data in literature by conducting simultaneous electric field and current measurements at voltages up to 85 kV, corresponding to 1 kV mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup> - 8 kV mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup> in MO, in two MO-PB arrangements: one with bare electrodes and another with PB-covered electrodes. The conductivities of both materials and their dependencies on the applied field strength are reported. The field measurement results are, supported by Poisson-Nernst-Planck (PNP) simulations, interpreted in terms of dominant negative homocharge transfer at the cathode in the bare-electrode arrangement. This is confirmed by a positive correlation of the asymmetry — quantified by the net surface charge density of the intermediary pressboard layer — with the magnitude of the applied voltage. Results from the covered-electrode arrangement reveal that this asymmetry can be significantly diminished by covering the electrodes. The relevant body of research is discussed and discrepancies are pointed out, offering a novel interpretation of the results. The present work provides valuable insights into the dielectric relaxation of practically relevant MO-PB insulation structures under DC stress.

title: Frequency Evolution and Halving Behavior of Gas Bubble Deformation in Oil under Power-Frequency AC Electric Fields
authors: Shanxin Niu, Tao Zhao, Jiaxue Xu, Hailin Liu, Yunpeng Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Bubbles generated in the oil-paper insulation reduce the dielectric strength and lead to insulation failure in power transformers. Previous studies have shown that under AC electric fields, bubbles typically deform at twice the power frequency, resulting in distortion of the electric field distribution in the oil gap. To explore bubble deformation characteristics during the AC pre-breakdown process, an observation platform with an 8-mm oil gap is developed to investigate a single air bubble with an initial diameter of approximately 2.5 mm. The periodic deformation of an air bubble under a stepwise-increasing 50 Hz AC voltage is systematically investigated via high-speed photography. A distinct frequency-halving phenomenon is observed, in which the dominant deformation frequency shifts from 100 Hz to 50 Hz with increasing voltage. At sufficiently high voltages, the deformation response becomes dominated by the 50 Hz component. Upon further increasing the voltage to 26.5 kV, the bubble enters a dynamically unstable state in the tested configuration, which can result in uncontrollable expansion and eventual gap breakdown. To elucidate the underlying physical mechanism, an asymmetric Duffing equation is formulated. The analysis reveals that this phenomenon arises from two distinct effects. First, the DC component of the electric force produces a static offset, which breaks the deformation symmetry and generates the 50 Hz component. Second, the nonlinear restoring force associated with surface tension induces a hardening effect, driving the shift of the deformation frequency from 100 Hz to 50 Hz. Compared with partial discharge monitoring, the observed change in bubble deformation frequency can also serve as an effective early-warning indicator of insulation failure.

title: Magnetic Soft Strain Sensor Based on Hall Device
authors: Jiayi Yang, Xu Chen, Hongyao Tang, Yebo Tao, Tingting Yu
abstract: Soft strain sensors are soft electronic devices capable of quantifying mechanical deformation, and they have been extensively explored in human health monitoring, and soft robotics. However, conventional resistive and capacitive soft strain sensors encounter a trade-off between sensing range and circuit complexity. To address this limitation, this work proposes a magnetic soft strain sensor based on a Hall device. The sensor consists of a silicone elastomer, a Hall sensor, a permanent magnet, and two rigid clamping fixtures. The fixtures are located at both ends of the sensor, with the Hall device embedded inside. Applying tensile strain increases the relative distance between the Hall device and the magnet embedded in the silicone, resulting in a reduced magnetic flux density at the Hall sensor and thereby enabling strain detection. This work investigates the influence of the magnet polarity and geometrical parameters on the sensing performance. In addition, the effect of the initial spacing between the Hall device and the magnet is also analyzed. Experimental characterization demonstrates that the proposed magnetic soft strain sensor achieves a gauge factor of 0.11 and exhibits excellent linearity (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.999). The sensor shows no signal drift under cyclic tensile loading, indicating stability. Finally, the feasibility of the magnetic soft strain sensor for human motion and health monitoring applications is demonstrated.

title: DualKanbaFormer: An Efficient Selective Sparse Framework for Multimodal Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis
authors: Adamu Lawan, Juhua Pu, Haruna Yunusa, Aliyu Umar, Muhammad Lawan, Adamu Sani Yahaya, Mahmoud Basi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: Multimodal Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis (MABSA) enhances sentiment detection by integrating text with complementary modalities, such as images, for a more comprehensive understanding of aspect-level sentiment. Despite notable progress, existing MABSA methods still lack strong aspect awareness, particularly in sentences with multiple aspects and conflicting sentiments or when visual cues are misaligned with the text. Together, these issues hinder accurate modeling of aspect-focused and cross-modal dependencies, limiting the effectiveness of MABSA. To address these challenges, we propose DualKanbaFormer, a novel framework with parallel Textual and Visual KanbaFormer modules tailored for MABSA. At its core, the model introduces Aspect-Driven Sparse Attention (ADSA) with an aspect-aware attention gating mechanism, which dynamically regulates the contributions of Scope, Focus, and Proximity attention branches conditioned on the target aspect. This ensures precise alignment between aspects and opinion signals. In addition, the selective state space model Mamba strengthens aspect–opinion alignment by modeling long-range dependencies across modalities. To enhance representational expressiveness and training stability, Kolmogorov–Arnold Networks (KANs) and Dynamic Tanh (DyT) replace conventional feed-forward and normalization layers. Finally, a CNN-based multimodal gated fusion module adaptively regulates cross-modal interactions, ensuring robust alignment between textual and visual features. Experiments on two benchmark datasets demonstrate that DualKanbaFormer outperforms several state-of-the-art models.

title: Covert Communication for Satellite Networks: Fundamentals, Applications, and Future Directions
authors: Hao Shi, Jiacheng Wang, Haichao Wei, Chengwen Xing, Na Deng, Nan Zhao, Dusit Niyato, George K. Karagiannidis
abstract: Due to its global coverage and seamless connectivity, satellite communication is considered a promising research area for the upcoming 6G networks. However, due to the inherent openness of wireless communications and unique characteristics of satellite communication, satellite links are vulnerable to eavesdropping and other attacks by malicious devices. Fortunately, covert communication is emerging as a highly effective technique for improving the security of information transmission in satellite networks by exploiting environmental uncertainties to hide transmission signals. Thus, in this article, we explore the potential of covert communication to address the security challenges in satellite communication. We first introduce the characteristics of satellite communication and secure transmission techniques. Then, we present the basics of covert communications and discuss critical aspects of covert satellite communication. Next, we study covert satellite-terrestrial communication networks focusing on their covertness and reliability, taking into account the spatial variation of a satellite transmitter and the influence of terrestrial jamming nodes. Finally, several open research challenges in covert satellite communication are outlined.

title: Feature-Preserving Offset Meshing
authors: Cao Hongyi, Xu Gang, Renshu Gu, Jinlan Xu, Rabczuk Timon, Xiaoyu Zhang, Yuzhe Luo, Xifeng Gao
abstract: None

title: Estimating Sequences with Memory for Minimizing Convex Non-smooth Composite Functions
authors: Endrit Dosti, Sergiy A. Vorobyov, Themistoklis Charalambous
abstract: First-order optimization methods are crucial for solving large-scale data processing problems, particularly those involving convex non-smooth composite objectives. For such problems with convex non-smooth composite objectives, we introduce a new class of generalized composite estimating sequences, devised by exploiting the information embedded in the iterates generated during the minimization process. Building on these sequences, we propose a novel accelerated first-order method tailored for such objective structures. This method also features a backtracking line-search strategy and achieves an accelerated convergence rate, regardless of whether the true Lipschitz constant is known. Additionally, it exhibits robustness to hyperparameter initialization that depends on the strong convexity parameter, a property of practical importance. The method’s efficiency and robustness are substantiated by comprehensive numerical evaluations on both synthetic and real-world datasets, demonstrating its effectiveness in data processing applications.

title: Compact Low-Profile Antenna with Integrated Lossy Layer for Radar Cross Section Reduction
authors: Ummer Rashid Dar, Mehran Manzoor Zargar, Archana Rajput, Kushmanda Saurav
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
