abstract: This paper presents a compact antenna design with low radar cross section (RCS) and low-profile features, while maintaining good radiation performance. In the proposed study, a lossy layer, typically used to absorb out-of-band frequencies, is directly integrated with the antenna. This lossy layer utilizes the antenna's ground as a reflective surface for absorbing out-ofband frequencies in the lower and upper bands of 4-8.2 GHz and 9.8-10.8 GHz, respectively, while allowing the antenna's in-band frequency (8.4 GHz) to pass through and radiate efficiently. To validate the simulated results, the integrated structure and conventional antenna are fabricated and measured. When compared with the conventional antenna, the proposed integrated antenna achieves an average monostatic RCS reduction of 15 dB and 13 dB in the lower band (3.6-8.2 GHz) and higher band (9.5-10 GHz), respectively. Further, measured bistatic RCS reductions of more than 80% and 70% are observed in the lower band (2-8.2 GHz) and upper band (9-11.5 GHz), respectively.

title: LEO Payload and Constellation Design for Monitoring GEO High Throughput Satellites
authors: Trivedi Balmukund, Pawan Barnwal, Saket D. Buch, Ujjwal Gangele, Subhash Chandra Bera
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: In-orbit monitoring of High Throughput Satellite (HTS) performance is demanded by satellite operators and regulators. One approach is to use LEO satellites to receive signals from different beams of any multibeam satellite for monitoring. In this paper, we examine the payload and constellation design aspects of the LEO-based system in terms of visibility duration, gap between beam measurements, and minimum antenna size for reliable measurements. By considering three generations of HTS covering Indian region as a case study, the critical design decisions are brought out. This study will be useful in planning and designing LEO-based monitoring systems and in techno-economic feasibility analysis.

title: Ansys-based study on the thermal-mechanical coupling life of the skin honeycomb bonding interface
authors: Zheng Wei, Qingyu Huang, Sa Wu
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: This paper presents a force-heat coupling simulation method based on Ansys Workbench, which is used to predict the fatigue life of the adhesive interface between the skin and honeycomb composite structure of a stealth aircraft under thermal cycling and random vibration loads. A finite element model including the absorbing coating, skin, adhesive layer, and honeycomb was established. The cohesive force model was used to simulate the failure of the adhesive layer. By introducing the temperature cycling (-50°C to 150°C) results into modal analysis, the thermal-vibration coupling (modal reduction of about 2%) was achieved, and random vibration (10 Grms) simulation was conducted. The results show that the shear stress of the adhesive layer caused by thermal expansion mismatch (>100 MPa) is much higher than the vibration stress, and it is the dominant failure factor. Based on the S-N curve generated by Fe-safe, the shear fatigue analysis was carried out, and the life distribution of the adhesive layer was predicted: the edge area deteriorates within 10-100 cycles, the delamination expands within 100-1000 cycles, and about 1/7 area undergoes large-scale damage in more than 10,000 cycles. The simulation results are consistent with engineering data, providing an efficient and low-cost life prediction approach for structural maintenance.

title: AutoF1: An RNN-Based Approach to Simulating Strategic Decision-Making in Formula 1
authors: Vivek Mistry, William A. P. Smith
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This paper addresses the challenges of providing real-time decision support for Formula One strategy engineers concerning car setup and tyre changes through the development of an AI-driven simulation tool. We investigate this by applying machine learning techniques, specifically recurrent neural networks, to historical race data obtained via the FastF1 Python library. This research demonstrates the process of structuring detailed race data for neural network training to build a simulation model capable of predicting race outcomes and aiding strategic decisions. The evaluation showed the simulation model's performance against historical races and illustrated its application through case studies simulating alternative strategies, which show efficient simulation capabilities. Finally, we explore future directions for enhancing the model's granularity and exploring advanced architectures were discussed.

title: Design of a Wideband Feedback-Stabilized C-Band MMIC Low-Noise Amplifier
authors: Saurabh Kumar, Shrija Bhattacharyya, Harshita Tolani, Anamiya Bhattacharya, Vinit Kumar, Chinmoy Saha, MukeshKumar K. Patel, Ch V. N. Rao
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents the design of a wideband Monolithic Microwave Integrated Circuit (MMIC) Low-Noise Amplifier (LNA) employing negative feedback. The two-stage amplifier is optimized for operation in the frequency range 4 to 8.5 GHz, achieving a noise figure below 1.76 dB across the frequency of operation. It provides a gain exceeding 26.5 dB with a gain flatness of 0.6 dB, along with a return loss better than 11.5 dB and an output P1dB greater than 15 dBm. The compact MMIC occupies an area of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.2 ~\text{mm} \times 2.0 ~\text{mm}$</tex> and is fabricated using the UMS PH25 GaAs pHEMT process.

title: A Comprehensive Benchmark Study of Transfer Learning-Based Deep Learning Models for Brain Tumor Classification
authors: Nur E Jannat Hema, Manosh Sur Choudhury, Sefatul Wasi, Siam Tahsin Bhuiyan, Fatin Israq, Noushin Anan, Saadia Binte Alam, Ashraful Islam, Rashedur Rahman
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Brain tumor classification is a crucial yet challenging task because of the subtle tumor region boundaries and limited availability of annotated medical datasets. Recent advancements in deep learning techniques enhance the automatic diagnostic process improving performance and reliability of results. This study aims to benchmark performances of 10 state-of-the-art deep learning models–spanning both Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Vision Transformers (ViTs)–for brain tumor classification specifically on the BraTS dataset. The CNN models include ResNet-50, ResNet-101, DenseNet-121, DenseNet-201, EfficientNet-b0, EfficientNet-b3, EfficientNet-b7 and MobileNet-v2 while the ViT architectures include ViT-16 and ViT-32. The models have been trained and evaluated under similar conditions ensuring fair comparisons. Quantitative as well as qualitative performances have been demonstrated and some valuable interpretations have been drawn from the findings. The most significant finding is that ViT-32 achieved the maximum performance among all (accuracy-83.05%, precision-78%, recall-70% and F1-score-79%) with computational expense, underscoring the need for balanced architectures in real-world clinical deployment.

title: Sensitivity and Confinement Loss Analysis of a Porous-core Decagonal Photonic Crystal Fiber (P-DPCF) Based Chemical Sensor
authors: Shahnaz Khatun, Fateha Samad, Mahfuzur Rahman
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: A porous-core decagonal photonic crystal fiber (P-DPCF) has been developed for use in chemical sensing in this work. The core region has a hexagonal structure with three layers of circular air holes, while the cladding is decagonal and features five concentric rings of circular air holes. A full-vectorial finite element method (FEM) is applied to perform the numerical analysis. This work observes that increasing the air-filling ratio and core radius results in higher sensitivity while simultaneously reducing the confinement loss. Additionally, reducing the core pitch leads to enhanced sensitivity and a mitigate in confinement loss. Furthermore, the effective mode area for the fundamental modes along the X and Y directions is evaluated by varying the core radius and pitch. This work is designed a PCF-based platform for chemical sensing applications, specifically for benzene detection, and the performance results are presented for benzene (n = 1.366). Here, the operating wavelength spans from 1.30 µm to 1.75 µm. At 1.55 µm operating wavelength, the fiber determines a larger relative sensitivity of around 81.55% and an extremely lower confinement loss of about 2.8994 × 10⁻<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</sup> dB/m.

title: Resilience Analysis of an Information Infrastructure Object Using a Queuing System Model with an Unreliable Device
authors: Elizaveta Barabanova, Mariia Filonova, Andrei Varuyeu
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: This paper develops the mathematical model of a queuing system with an unreliable device M/PH/1/1, constructs a state-transition graph, formulates a system of Kolmogorov equations, derives formulas for calculating the main performance indicators of the system, and presents numerical results.

title: Behavioral Segmentation and Clustering of Geospatial Trajectories
authors: Jessica L. Jones, Andrew T. Wilson, Renee Gooding, Daniel DeLayo, Keith Dalbey, Jon Whetzel, Nitin Sharan
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: The rapid growth of global positioning system (GPS) devices has led to a corresponding increase in the size of GPS datasets. While these large GPS datasets contain a wealth of information about the moving objects in them, manual classification and anomaly detection are prohibitively time consuming. We utilize unsupervised machine learning techniques to first identify the behaviors for individual moving objects and then cluster those objects by their behavioral sequences. In this way, trajectories behaving unusually as well as common patterns of behavior are both detectable in large datasets without requiring an a priori definition of “unusual” or “common.”

title: CQVPR: Landmark-aware Contextual Queries for Visual Place Recognition
authors: Dongyue Li, Jialei Chen, Ito Seigo, Hiroshi Murase, Daisuke Deguchi
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Visual place recognition remains challenging due to significant variations in appearance caused by lighting, viewpoint, and structural similarities across environments. To address this, we propose Contextual Query VPR (CQVPR), a novel method that bridges the gap between pixel-level and segment-level representations. Unlike conventional approaches that either rely on low-level appearance cues or high-level semantic partitions, CQVPR integrates fine-grained visual details with global contextual understanding through a set of learnable queries. These contextual queries capture high-level semantic structures within the scene, which are fused with dense pixel-wise features to form robust descriptors for retrieval. To encourage discriminative query learning, we introduce a query matching loss that promotes similarity among queries from the same location while pushing those from different locations apart. Extensive experiments on several datasets demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms other state-of-the-art methods, especially in challenging scenarios.

title: MIRAI: Multimodal Intelligence via Reflective agentic AI for enterprise knowledge assistants
authors: Priyadarshini Panemangalore Pai, Subrata Sarkar, Vemuri Krishna Sumanth, Vijaya Kirti Gupta, Garima Srivastava, Venkata Subba Rao Manne
publish: 2025 IEEE 6th International Women in Technology Conference (WINTECHCON)
abstract: Secure and efficient knowledge management based on enterprise chip design and verification documents or bug trackers, pose unique challenges, due to their complex, sensitive, heterogeneous, and multimodal nature. Rapid miniaturization of nodes, sophistication of Intellectual Property (IP)s, and evolution of system-on-chip (SoC) architectures have further added to the demanding scenario, where, within the available enterprise infrastructure, effective solutions have to be developed based on context and requirements of various operational settings. Available state-of-the-art solutions have limited scope of sensitivity to organizational dynamics and smart computational resource planning. In this paper, we present MIRAI- a Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrated Artificial Intelligence (AI) framework that combines language, vision, and structured data understanding through a reflective agentic architecture to provide real-time, contextual and explainable support in Design Verification and Verification (DV&V) tasks. The proposed system has been evaluated across use cases, demonstrating significant gains, i.e., up to 90-95% accuracy in information retrieval, in secure policy information access, AI-based query answering, anomaly detection, debugging, and knowledge mining and functional testing with improved coverage. We believe MIRAI offers a transformative approach to managing and leveraging design data, engineering workflows, and cross-functional team intelligence.

title: Evaluation of Positioning Accuracy under Different Railway Environments with Multipath Signal Mitigation Method
authors: Wenzheng Qiao, Jian Wang, Debiao Lu, Jiang Liu, Baigen Cai, Wei Jiang
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: The BDS (Beidou Satellite Navigation System) has been applied in the train control systems in several railway lines in China. The BDS has the advantage of providing maneuver positions either alone or in combination with other train-borne sensors. As train moves along the railway track, the train goes through different environments, as open area, viaduct, tunnels, urban canyon etc. In the BDS observation constrained environments, the multipath effect is the most significant issue affecting the positioning accuracy. How to effectively eliminate the multipath effect or even utilize it has become the current research focus. This paper proposes a signal classification method based on the comparison of satellite pseudorange and true range. The environmental data from the National Railway Test Center are used to perform ray identification tests on three scenarios: open area, urban canyon, and tunnel entrance. Taking the average positioning error and standard deviation as the standard, the positioning performance after eliminating multipath signals increased by 4.5 % in open area, 27.7 % in urban canyon areas, and decreased by 107.5 % in tunnel entrance areas.

title: FedMATD3: A Federated Reinforcement Learning Approach for Global Optimization in Multi-Agent Vehicular Task Offloading
authors: Lingqiu Zeng, Yong Huang, Fukun Xie, Qingwen Han, Lei Ye
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: With the rapid development of intelligent transportation systems, task offloading technologies have been widely adopted to shift computational workloads from resource-constrained vehicles to resource-rich edge and cloud servers, thereby improving the service quality of Internet of Vehicles (IoV) systems. However, the growing complexity of vehicular applications and the heterogeneity of service demands have led to critical challenges, such as dynamic resource imbalance, cross-domain scheduling difficulties, and inefficiencies in global optimization. These challenges require more intelligent and coordinated task offloading mechanisms capable of handling resource diversity and system-wide collaboration. To address these issues, a novel task-offloading model based on a federated reinforcement learning framework is proposed. Specifically, a Device-Edge-Cloud collaborative offloading model is constructed, and the Federated Learning Optimized Multi-Agent Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (FedMATD3) algorithm is developed to achieve global optimization and efficient cross-domain resource allocation. Extensive simulation experiments validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach. Compared with four classical baseline algorithms, FedMATD3 demonstrates superior performance in terms of task processing delay and task completion rate.

title: Diffusion-Based Generation and Imputation of Driving Scenarios from Limited Vehicle CAN Data
authors: Julian Ripper, Ousama Esbel, Rafael Fietzek, Max Muhlhauser, Thomas Kreutz
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Training deep learning methods on small time series datasets that also include corrupted samples is challenging. Diffusion models have shown to be effective to generate realistic and synthetic data, and correct corrupted samples through imputation. In this context, this paper focuses on generating synthetic yet realistic samples of automotive time series data. We show that denoising diffusion probabilistic models (DDPMs) can effectively solve this task by applying them to a challenging vehicle CAN-dataset with long-term data and a limited number of samples. Therefore, we propose a hybrid generative approach that combines autoregressive and non-autoregressive techniques. We evaluate our approach with two recently proposed DDPM architectures for time series generation, for which we propose several improvements. To evaluate the generated samples, we propose three metrics that quantify physical correctness and test track adherence. Our best model is able to outperform even the training data in terms of physical correctness, while showing plausible driving behavior. Finally, we use our best model to successfully impute physically implausible regions in the training data, thereby improving the data quality.

title: Surface Resistance Mapping of Graphite Battery Cathodes with a 10 GHz Inverted Single Post Dielectric Resonator Scanner
authors: Lukasz Nowicki, Corinne Nouvellon, Malgorzata Celuch, Fabien Monteverde, Bartłomiej Salski
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: We present an integrated microwave method for characterising graphene-based films that combines classical single-post and split-post dielectric resonators with a two-dimensional imaging single-post resonator. Free-standing graphene oxide and chemically reduced graphene oxide produced by a modified Hummers process were first benchmarked in stand-alone fixtures at five and ten gigahertz. The study is then extended to two-dimensional imaging, which uses numerical electromagnetic calibration and automated horizontal scanning to generate millimetre-resolution maps of sheet resistance across twenty-by-twenty-millimetre samples. The split-post resonator recorded a density-dependent real permittivity between six point eight and seven point four and a loss tangent up to zero point zero three five for graphene oxide, while the singlepost resonator showed that chemical reduction increases conductivity by about three orders of magnitude. Imaging revealed clear in-plane gradients; the reduced material displayed a sheet resistance that differed by only three percent between the two approaches yet showed noticeably lower spatial uniformity. The combined workflow links bulk and local microwave responses and offers a practical route for optimising graphene-based coatings for shielding applications.

title: ML-Based Prediction Framework for Varying Traffic Signal Control Strategies and Its GLOSA-Application
authors: Kevin Malena, Christopher Link, Sandra Gausemeier, Ansgar Trächtler
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This paper deals with the development and results of a prediction framework for traffic light control systems as well as the usage and benefits of such predictions in green light optimal speed advisory (GLOSA) scenarios. Various machine learning methods like support vector machines, neural networks or reinforcement learning were evaluated for their applicability in the prediction context and compared based on their efficiency and most importantly accuracy. The resulting prediction framework uses decision tree ensemble models combined with certain domain-specific model knowledge to forecast different control strategies. This method was chosen due to its best performance in various test scenarios. Very high accuracy and fidelity were achieved for standard control methods like fixed-time, time-of-day-based and ‘ordinary’ traffic-based programs. Only for the more sophisticated model predictive control which was tested lower accuracies were achieved. For the upcoming GLOSA application the penetration of equipped vehicles was varied for different traffic scenarios and control strategies. Results showcase high potentials for enhancing urban mobility and reducing environmental impact by lower emissions and waiting times. However, it is also clear from the studies presented in this contribution that the coordination of the control strategy with the GLOSA vehicles is of enormous importance.

title: Multi-Staged Framework for Safety Analysis of Offloaded Services in Distributed Intelligent Transportation Systems
authors: Robin Dehler, Oliver Schumann, Jona Ruof, Michael Buchholz
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: The integration of service-oriented architectures (SOA) with function offloading for distributed, intelligent transportation systems (ITS) offers the opportunity for connected autonomous vehicles (CAVs) to extend their locally available services. One major goal of offloading a subset of functions in the processing chain of a CAV to remote devices is to reduce the overall computational complexity on the CAV. The extension of using remote services, however, requires careful safety analysis, since the remotely created data are corrupted more easily, e.g., through an attacker on the remote device or by intercepting the wireless transmission. To tackle this problem, we first analyze the concept of SOA for distributed environments. From this, we derive a safety framework that validates the reliability of remote services and the data received locally. Since it is possible for the autonomous driving task to offload multiple different services, we propose a specific multi-staged framework for safety analysis dependent on the service composition of local and remote services. For efficiency reasons, we directly include the multistaged framework for safety analysis in our service-oriented function offloading framework (SOFOF) that we have proposed in earlier work. The evaluation compares the performance of the extended framework considering computational complexity, with energy savings being a major motivation for function offloading, and its capability to detect data from corrupted remote services.

title: Multi-Objective Calibration of Large-Scale Models Using Bayesian Optimization
authors: Huy Le, Hai L. Vu, Vu Nguyen
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Activity-Based Models (ABMs) are disaggregated models that simulate individual travel behaviors in detail, making them valuable tools for transportation planning and the analysis of multi-modal urban mobility. However, their calibration remains challenging due to high dimensionality, complex parameter interactions, and significant computational costs. We propose a Bayesian Optimization framework for multi-task calibration of large-scale ABMs, using a multi-task Gaussian Process to exploit inter-module correlations and improve sample efficiency. Calibration is structured as a two-phase process: initial per-module optimization followed by selective refinement, enhancing convergence and reducing redundant computation. We demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method through real-world ABM calibration, achieving improved accuracy over existing state-of-the-art methods, and validating its applicability to complex, multi-objective transportation models within advanced intelligent mobility systems.

title: EvMVX-Net: Uncertainty-Aware 3D Object Detection via Evidential Regression and Mixture of Normal-Inverse Gamma Distributions
authors: Zhiguo Zhao, Cong Zhao, Kun Chen, Xiaoxi Hu, Yuhuan Du, Yuxiong Ji
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Reliable 3D object detection is critical for safety in Autonomous Driving (AD) systems, especially in the presence of sensor noise. Traditional multi-modal 3D object detection methods based on LiDAR and camera inputs often ignore uncertainty modeling, limiting their trustworthiness in real-world deployment. To address this challenge, we propose EvMVXNet, a novel uncertainty-aware multi-modal 3D object detection framework that incorporates evidential deep learning and Normal-Inverse Gamma (NIG) distribution-based regression. Built upon the MVX-Net architecture, EvMVX-Net introduces an auxiliary LiDAR-only head alongside the standard fusion head, both modeled with NIG to estimate uncertainty. To effectively integrate the outputs and uncertainties from both branches, we incorporate a Mixture of NIGs (MoNIG) fusion strategy, enabling robust and trustworthy object detection. We validate EvMVX-Net on the KITTI benchmark and demonstrate its superiority over both sampling-based and samplingfree baselines. The proposed method achieves up to 5.56% improvement in average precision (AP) while providing calibrated uncertainty estimates, as evaluated by Expected Calibration Error (ECE). Extensive ablation studies further confirm the effectiveness of each proposed component. Our results highlight the importance of uncertainty modeling for reliable multi-modal perception in safety-critical applications.

title: Regression-Based S11 Prediction of Slot-Loaded Microstrip Patch Antennas using MATLAB Antenna Toolbox
authors: Abdul Lateef Haroon Phulara Shaik, Aaftab Zohra, K Tanuja, Rehamath Unnisa
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of time-intensive full-wave simulations in microstrip patch antenna design. Using over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1,200}$</tex> antenna configurations with varying patch and slot dimensions, we trained a lightweight regression model in MATLAB to predict the return loss (S11) within the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.5-3 \text{GHz}$</tex> range. A user-friendly GUI built with App Designer enables real-time prediction of S11 based on user-specified parameters without rerunning electromagnetic simulations. Our second-order regression model achieved an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> of 0.92 and a mean absolute error of 0.6 dB on unseen data, resulting in a reduction of over 95% in computation time. GUI outputs align closely with full-wave simulation results, particularly in cases where slot-loading achieves a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{20 ~ dB}$</tex> improvement in impedance matching. This tool serves as a practical aid for rapid prototyping and educational purposes, setting the stage for future inverse design frameworks.

title: Protocol-Aware Semantic Filtering for Reliable Adversarial Robustness Evaluation in DL-based Network Intrusion Detection Systems
authors: Mahaveer Prasad, Vinod Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: Deep learning-based Network Intrusion Detection Systems (DL-NIDS) have shown promising capabilities in identifying complex cyber threats. However, their susceptibility to adversarial attacks poses serious challenges to real-world deployment. This paper presents a comprehensive evaluation of adversarial robustness in DL-NIDS under three popular attack methods FGSM, PGD, and DeepFool. Beyond conventional metrics, we introduce a protocol-aware semantic validation framework that filters out adversarial samples violating domain-specific constraints. Our experiments reveal that many adversarial examples, though effective in pre-validated form, fail semantic checks and are unrealistic in operational networks. Post-validation analysis shows that FGSM and PGD attacks converge in effectiveness, highlighting the overestimation of threat severity in prior evaluations.We further perform a detailed feature-level perturbation analysis to understand how different attack methods exploit specific input dimensions. This analysis uncovers that PGD and FGSM perturb similar feature sets with diminishing impact after validation, whereas DeepFool introduces more dispersed and potent perturbations that persist beyond semantic filtering. Additionally, we extend our evaluation to traditional ML classifiers such as SVM, Random Forest, and k-NN, comparing their adversarial resilience under pre-validation and post-validation conditions. Interestingly, traditional models often experience more severe degradation under pre-validated attacks, while protocol-aware semantic validation mitigates the impact, resulting in higher post-validation accuracy in many cases. This underscores the universal need for protocol-aware evaluation. Our findings emphasize that ignoring semantic plausibility in adversarial studies may lead to misleading conclusions about attack severity and defense strength. This work advocates for semantically grounded adversarial evaluation to ensure trust and reliability in DL-NIDS, providing actionable insights for future research and deployment in security-critical environments.

title: Feature Parameter Prediction Based on A Hybrid CNN-Bi-LSTM with Attention Mechanism Fusion
authors: Xiaoxu Chu, Jinjun Cheng, Changjun Li, Haizhen Zhu
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Aiming at the issue of capacity degradation in lithiumion batteries during long-term use, this paper proposes a data-driven method combining a convolutional neural network (CNN) with a bidirectional long short-term memory network (Bi-LSTM) and an attention mechanism (CNN-Bi-LSTM-Attention) to predict the remaining service life of the battery. The CNN extracts features from the charging curve, while the Bi-LSTM extracts features from the discharging curve related to time dependency. After feature concatenation, an attention mechanism layer is introduced to enable the model to autonomously learn the importance weights of charging features and capacity temporal features. By comparing the error between the proposed hybrid model and the original model, the results show that the proposed hybrid model improves the mean squared error by 44.1%, outperforming the original model. This provides a more effective method for accurately predicting the remaining lifespan of batteries.

title: Design of Hippopotamus Optimization Algorithm Derived Microwave Absorber for Wideband Applications
authors: Ranjeet Pratap Singh Bhadoriya, Ravi Panwar, Garima Tiwari, Ankit Chandachoriya
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: Designing efficient microwave absorbers for X, Ku and K-band (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8.2-26.5 \text{GHz}$</tex>) applications demands a balanced approach to achieve wideband absorption, minimal reflection, reduced thickness, and lightweight characteristics. In this study, a novel multilayer microwave absorber (MMA) is proposed using the recently developed Hippopotamus Optimization Algorithm (HOA), inspired by the cooperative and social behavior of hippopotamuses. The optimization process compute frequency-dependent permittivity and permeability values of suitable dielectric material with an appropriate thickness. A multi-objective cost function is formulated to simultaneously minimize the reflection coefficient and overall thickness while maximizing absorption bandwidth within the targeted frequency range. The designed triple layer microwave absorber (TLMA) with the total thickness of 2.4 mm exhibits convincing results. This TLMA can absorb the incident microwave in a 13.2 GHz bandwidth. The result shows an excellent match with analytical and full-wave simulations. Optimized results confirm that the HOA-derived absorber achieves over 90 % absorption across more than 90 % of the Ku and K-bands with reduced thickness. This work demonstrates the potential of the HOA, which is fused with transmission line model in optimizing complex structures and provides a robust methodology for the tailored design of high-performance microwave absorbers.

title: Stability-Guided Safe Reinforcement Learning: A Lyapunov-Based Approach for Autonomous Vehicle Control
authors: Haonan Ding, Mingzhuo Zhao, Sunan Zhang, Weihua Wang, Weichao Zhuang, Guodong Yin
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: The stability of policy outputs is a key challenge faced by reinforcement learning (RL), as it directly impacts the safety of actions taken by autonomous systems. In this paper, we propose a Lyapunov-based RL method to address safety concerns in the decision-making and control processes of autonomous vehicles. Specifically, we introduce Lyapunov networks within the Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) architecture to assess the safety performance of the Actor network, with a particular focus on the stability of policy outputs. Building on a stability theorem, we demonstrate that stability conditions can serve as critical constraints in RL to guide the learning of a safe controller/strategy. The approach leverages the Critic's encouragement of positive rewards to promote exploration, while Lyapunov constraints are applied to limit unsafe behaviors. In a simulated highway autonomous driving environment, experimental results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm maintains robust stability under predefined safety constraints, achieving performance metrics that exceed existing industry benchmarks while ensuring compliance with operational safety requirements.

title: Physics Informed Neural Networks with Flow Loss for Axial Piston Pump Health Assessment
authors: Yue Wang, Yixiang Huang, Pengcheng Xia, Ying Pu, Chengliang Liu, Yanming Li
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Accurate health assessment of axial piston pumps is vital to overall system performance, yet the pumps’ intricate architecture, subtle fault signatures, and limited fault data challenge conventional diagnostics. This paper proposes a health-assessment method that couples a physics-informed neural network (PINN) with a Flow Loss Model. By fusing multi-sensor signals with flow measurements, the method inverts three physically interpretable parameters—κ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> (compression-loss coefficient capturing flow lost to fluid compressibility and chamber elasticity), κ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> (laminar-leakage coefficient quantifying clearance leakage in the low-Reynolds regime), and κ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> (turbulent-leakage coefficient)—to characterize pump health. The network is trained under a maximum a posteriori (MAP) objective that integrates measurement fit, physics-residual constraints, and priors to stabilize the inversion. Validation on a labeled experimental dataset shows that the proposed PINN surpasses an otherwise identical purely data-driven model in prediction accuracy and health-state recognition, while achieving superior physical consistency. Notably, κ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> increases monotonically with health degradation, providing a salient, physics-grounded indicator for distinguishing health states and evidencing strong generalization potential.

title: Optimal Deployment and Operation of Mobile Charging Stations for Multi-Family Dwelling EV Charging
authors: Xiaowei Chen, Satish V. Ukkusuri
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This study addresses the deployment and operational optimization of mobile charging stations (MCSs) to meet the growing demand for electric vehicle (EV) charging in underserved multi-family dwelling (MFD) areas. As EV adoption continues to rise, MFD residents face persistent challenges in accessing convenient and reliable charging infrastructure. To bridge this gap, we propose a comprehensive Mixed-Integer Linear Programming (MILP) framework that optimizes MCS fleet operations, including strategic hub selection, fleet sizing, routing, and scheduling. The model seeks to maximize net service benefits by minimizing depot activation and operational costs while ensuring that charging demand is fully satisfied. It incorporates key constraints such as MCS battery dynamics, routing feasibility, energy constraints, and service time windows. A case study in Indianapolis, Indiana, demonstrates the framework's effectiveness in serving high-demand MFD clusters via optimized multi-stop routes originating from a single activated depot, thereby significantly reducing system-wide costs. Compared to a baseline approach involving uncoordinated depot and vehicle assignments, the proposed method achieves as much as 45.1% cost savings while maintaining complete service coverage with fewer vehicles. These results underscore the potential of MCS-based solutions to enhance charging accessibility in infrastructure-constrained urban environments. The proposed framework provides a practical planning tool for municipalities seeking to accelerate EV adoption through flexible, cost-effective, and equitable charging strategies.

title: Design of Compact 1-Bit Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface for Enhancing Wireless Coverage
authors: Praween Kumar Nishad, Ekant Sharma, Prem Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: The Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (RIS) enables an alternative wireless propagation path by directing Electromagnetic (EM) waves from a base station to targeted directions. This technology is essential when the user is unable to receive a signal or when obstacles block the line of sight signal. In this work, a new RIS is designed and simulated to operate at a center frequency of 3.5 GHz. In addition, free-space path loss models for RIS-assisted wireless communications are presented. The RIS consists of 256 unit elements organized in a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16 \times 16$</tex> grid, each unit uses a 1-bit PIN diode to create a pattern for the reflective response of the EM wave. The proposed unit element is designed with two dumbbell-shaped conductor patches connected through the PIN diode. The RIS is simulated in the frequency range of 3.3 to 3.8 GHz, achieving reflected angles of 15,45, and 60 degrees, respectively, with different patterns and a maximum gain of 28dB when an EM wave is incident perpendicular to the RIS surface. The RIS is compactly designed with dimension of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$416 \times 416 ~\text{mm}^{2}$</tex>.

title: RAG-Enhanced Multi-Model Ensemble for Automated Vulnerability Detection Using SLMs
authors: Fateha Jannat Ayrin, Mizanur Rahman, Sujan Kumar Bhowmik, Syeda Sadia Alam, Vishwanath Akuthota, Masud Mohiuddin, Md Maruf Hasan Khondaker, Muntasim Ul Haque, Montaser Abdul Quader
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Automated vulnerability detection in source code is essential for modern software security, yet existing approaches suffer from high false positive rates, weak contextual understanding, and limited recall. This work presents the most comprehensive empirical evaluation to date of Small Language Model (SLM) based vulnerability detection, spanning more than twenty system configurations ranging from simple prompting baselines to advanced retrieval-augmented ensembles. Our methodology systematically examines how small LLMs (1–3B parameters) behave under different prompting strategies, static analysis integrations, ensembling techniques, and retrieval mechanisms. Through extensive ablation studies on the DiverseVul dataset, we find that many commonly assumed improvements such as complex Chain-of-Thought prompting, multi-model aggregation, or post-processing verification frequently degrade performance, largely due to overfitting and the inherent reasoning limitations of small models. In contrast, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) consistently enhances both recall and stability, providing an average F1 gain of 4–5% by grounding model predictions in relevant vulnerability examples. Our best-performing system, a RAG-enhanced stacking ensemble with structured reasoning, achieves a 71.2% F1-score, surpassing the state-of-the-art LLaMA 3.2 model by 5.2 percentage points. our findings demonstrate that effective vulnerability detection does not arise from increased architectural complexity but from combining lightweight models with robust retrieval and context-aware reasoning. This study establishes RAG-driven small LLMs as a practical, cost-efficient, and empirically validated direction for secure software analysis. our source code at https://github.com/rafi79/RAG-Enhanced-Multi-Model-Ensemble-for-Automated-Vulnerability-Detection-Using-SLMs.

title: ST-TransNet: An Adaptive Spatio-Temporal Transfer Network for City-Wise Traffic Speed Prediction
authors: Lei Chen, Xuetao Wei, Shiyao Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Traffic speed prediction is a core task of Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS), providing crucial support for urban traffic management. However, due to economic and infrastructure limitations, data scarcity in several cities adversely affects traffic prediction accuracy. To address this issue, we propose a transfer learning-based framework, Spatio-Temporal Transfer Network (ST-TransNet), for city-wise traffic speed prediction. ST-TransNet employs graph partitioning to obtain fine-grained traffic graph representations, leveraging Graph Attention Network (GAT) and Mamba to capture dynamic spatio-temporal features of traffic data. By adopting a parameter-sharing transfer learning strategy, ST-TransNet is pretrained on data-rich cities and fine-tuned on limited data from data-scarce cities, enabling knowledge transfer of traffic patterns. Experimental results on real-world traffic datasets show that ST-TransNet outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in data-scarce target domains. In addition, the accurate, robust, and generic traffic prediction results demonstrate the reliable application in data-limited cities.

title: 28 GHz Passive Reflectarray Employing Patch Size Modulation for Static Beam Steering
authors: Spandan Manna, Florian Reher, Amar Al-Bassam, Dirk Heberling
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This work presents the design, simulation, and experimental validation of a static reflectarray operating at 28 GHz, intended for passive beam redirection in millimeter-wave (mmWave) communication systems. The proposed design employs a phase gradient across a 20 by 20 unit-cell reflectarray, achieved by continuously varying the patch dimensions of the unit cells. The unit cell exhibits wideband reflection phase characteristics with low loss across 24 to 32 GHz, while the reflectarray is optimized for peak performance at 28 GHz. Full-wave simulations demonstrate the ability to redirect an obliquely incident beam toward broadside with high directivity and low sidelobe levels (SLL). The fabricated prototype is characterized in a compact antenna test range, and the measured radiation patterns closely match the simulations, confirming the main beam direction, beamwidth, and SLL. The proposed approach offers a compact, passive, low-cost, and practically realizable solution for static beam steering at mmWave frequencies, making it suitable for fixed backhaul or user-terminal applications in 5 G and beyond.

title: Narrowband, Dualband, and Broadband SRR Metamaterial for Mitigation of RF Blackout
authors: Nagavelly Tarun Teja, Amarjit Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: The Split Ring Resonator (SRR) plays a pivotal role in metamaterial engineering due to its ability to manipulate electromagnetic properties at subwavelength scales. This paper presents a comprehensive investigation of SRR-based metamaterial structures tailored for narrowband, dual-band, and wideband responses, aimed at mitigating radio frequency (RF) blackout experienced during atmospheric reentry of space vehicles. Initially, a single SRR unit is designed to operate at 6.6 GHz, exhibiting a strong resonance with a reflection coefficient of -48 dB. To achieve dual-band performance, a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \times 4 \text{SRR}$</tex> array is proposed, demonstrating resonances at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6. 2 ~ G H z}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6. 6 ~ G H z}$</tex> with reflection coefficients of -20 dB and -19 dB, respectively. For broadband operation, a modified square SRR structure is introduced, offering a broad resonance spanning from 7.5 GHz to 13.5 GHz, yielding a bandwidth of 6 GHz, which is suitable for wideband applications.

title: Impact of Commercial ACC Vehicles on Traffic Stability Considering Inter-Driver and Intra-Driver Heterogeneity in Human-Driven Vehicles
authors: Marouane Bouadi, Shi-Teng Zheng, Rui Jiang, Bin Jia
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This paper studies the impact of commercial Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) vehicles on traffic stability in a mixed traffic flow, accounting for inter-driver and intra-driver heterogeneity in human-driven vehicles. Inter-driver heterogeneity captures variability in driving styles and powertrain dynamics across vehicles, while intra-driver heterogeneity reflects temporal stochasticity in vehicle dynamics. These heterogeneities explain two key phenomena: the probabilistic nature of traffic instability and the concave growth pattern of traffic oscillations. Qualitatively, we have found that ACC vehicles cannot suppress traffic oscillations generated by human drivers. Quantitatively, their ability to reduce oscillation strength is insignificant even at high penetration rates for long vehicle platoons. These findings challenge assumptions about ACC's benefits and provide a foundation for designing next-generation automated vehicles capable of mitigating human-induced traffic oscillations.

title: Narrow-Beam Butler Matrix for 28 GHz Wireless Application
authors: Mantar Singh Mandloi, Leeladhar Malviya
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: For the coverage of the intended region, narrow beam radiation patterns are required in wireless communication. MIMO and 5G solves the problem of large spectrum, coverage, capacity, and data rate required for the intended user/direction. The presented 4-input 4-output Butler matrix design covers 27.90-29.05 GHz (2.18 GHz bandwidth) with 2:1 VSWR (-10 dB return loss) frequency band. The Butler matrix is designed on Rogers RO5880 substrate, having a dimension of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$48.82 \times 38.76 \times 0.254$</tex> millimeter cube. The research presents design and implementation of a narrowbeam <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \times 4$</tex> Butler matrix to cover minus 22 degree, plus 11 degree, minus 11 degree, and plus 22 degree directions for the 5G millimeter wave beamforming applications. The proposed design utilizes a network of hybrid couplers, delay lines, phase shifters, and crossovers to achieve multiple narrow-beam states. The presented design can be integrated with millimeter wave microwave circuits, arrays, and MIMO-arrays. The presented Butler matrix gain is 14.65 dBi at the radiating ports and at each ports efficiency is better than 89.85%.

title: A Ripple-Based Adaptive On-Time Control Buck Converter with Pseudo Switch Node Ripple Injection
authors: Bhavitha Chitimireddy Jaganmohan Reddy, Av Hareesh, Pradipta Patra
publish: 2025 IEEE 6th International Women in Technology Conference (WINTECHCON)
abstract: Adaptive On-Time (AOT) control has gained prominence in power management applications for its fast transient response, simplicity, and nearly constant switching frequency. However, its dependence on output ripple for switch triggering becomes unreliable with low-ESR ceramic capacitors, as they significantly attenuate the ripple signal. To overcome this, a pseudo-ripple injection strategy is proposed, enabling precise switching detection without disturbing the main power path. The architecture uses transconductance-based signal conditioning to effectively process ripple characteristics, enhancing detection robustness and suitability for low-noise, MLCC-dominated designs.

title: An Extrinsic Sensor Calibration Framework for Precise Probabilistic Joint Calibration of Camera, LiDAR, and Radar
authors: Bianca Forkel, Philipp Berthold, Mirko Maehlisch
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Accurate sensor data fusion requires accurate knowledge of the sensors' relative mounting poses. To calibrate the sensor setup of autonomous vehicles, we propose a tool for the joint 6D extrinsic calibration of cameras, LiDAR sensors, and radar sensors. Our optimization-based method achieves high accuracy by probabilistically accounting for measurement uncertainties and employing an advanced calibration target. In addition to optimized visual markers and geometric features detectable by cameras and LiDAR sensors, the calibration target includes a radar Doppler simulator. This enables radar sensors to detect the calibration target with high accuracy and unambiguity. The proposed calibration tool is quantitatively evaluated on realworld data and released as an open-source ROS package.

title: Constraint-Guided Multi-Task Reinforcement Learning for Autonomous Electric Taxi Dispatch and Energy Management
authors: Meng Zhao, Junyi Wang, Yanbin Zou, Donghe Li, Shitao Chen, Qingyu Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: With the rapid development of autonomous driving and electric vehicle technologies, autonomous electric taxis (AETs) are emerging as a critical pathway toward intelligent and low-carbon urban mobility. However, existing studies often treat order dispatching and energy management as separate tasks, neglecting the dynamic interactions and collaborative optimization between these two subsystems. Additionally, they typically lack explicit modeling of battery constraints and behavioral restrictions, resulting in compromised operational safety and poor policy convergence. To address these issues, this paper proposes a Constraint-guided Multi-task Value Decomposition Network (CM-VDN), a multi-agent reinforcement learning framework designed to achieve coordinated optimization of order dispatching and energy management in AETs. We introduce a constraint-aware multi-objective reward mechanism, explicitly embedding critical operational constraints such as battery boundaries, task mutual exclusions, and penalties for idle actions. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed CM-VDN significantly outperforms conventional reinforcement learning methods, achieving over 200% improvement in overall operational revenue while effectively eliminating constraint violations. The study provides solid theoretical and practical foundations for deploying autonomous electric taxi fleets in complex, dynamic urban environments.

title: Exploiting Adjacent Vehicle Interactions to Mislead Trajectory Predictions
authors: Zhanbo Sun, Yueyuan Du, Feilong Wang, Rong Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have proven effective in vehicle trajectory prediction by capturing multi-agent interactions. While recent studies have revealed that GNN models could be compromised by exploiting these interactions, their robustness under adversarial perturbations in the context of trajectory prediction remains under-explored. This study assesses the vulnerability of GNN-based trajectory prediction models via a spatiotemporal adversarial attack framework. We introduce a gradient-guided, time-dependent method to identify the most influential adjacent vehicle, followed by a spatiotemporal projected gradient descent approach to generate effective and practical perturbations that are injected into the identified vehicle to compromise the ego vehicle. Experiments conducted on a real-world dataset show that the proposed attack framework significantly degrades prediction accuracy, increasing the prediction deviation by 51.92 %. It is found that the framework produces larger disruptions than baseline attack strategies. Interestingly, while an advanced prediction model like Graph Attention Network (GAT) can improve the prediction accuracy over the baseline GNN, our findings reveal that it also exhibits increased vulnerability. These findings highlight the critical trade-off between model accuracy and robustness, with implications for improving the safety of intelligent driving systems enhanced by adjacent vehicle interactions.

title: Source of Radiation of a Dielectric Resonator Antenna: Technique to Verify the Theoretical Proposition
authors: Sanjit Mukherjee, Soumojeet Basak, Sk Rafidul, Debatosh Guha
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: The cause of radiation from a dielectric resonator antenna has been investigated over the last one decade in different phases in which the present group of authors has been deeply involved. The insight was developed based on rigorous theoretical modelling. The gap between theory and possibility of experiments has been explored in this work for the first time. This, to the best of our knowledge, is the first endeavor to validate the theoretical propositions which has been our only source of understanding so far.

title: Human-like Bangla Text-to-Speech Synthesis using the Orpheus TTS Model Fine-tuning
authors: Utshob Sutradhar, Naimul Hasan Nahid, Elahi Md Toufiq, Priyankar Biswas
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Bangla text-to-speech (TTS) synthesis faces persistent challenges due to limited speech resources and complex linguistic features. This paper presents a fine-tuned Orpheus neural TTS model aimed at generating human-like Bangla speech with higher naturalness and clarity. A multilingual Orpheus model was fine-tuned using an open-source Bangla speech dataset. The fine-tuned Orpheus model achieves a substantial improvement in speech quality, especially natural voice tone. The Orpheus fine-tuned Bangla TTS model shows exceptional performance with speaker similarity of 99.96%, Character Error Rate (CER) of 5.8%, Word Error Rate (WER) of 11.1%, along with high similarity in Mel-Cepstral Spectrum. The results show that the fine-tuned Orpheus TTS excels compared to other TTS systems. The framework put forth outlines a productive and scalable process for developing high-quality Bangla TTS systems and extends the effectiveness of Orpheus to more low-resource languages.

title: Joint Optimization of Traffic Signal and Vehicle Route via Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers
authors: Zhonggen Zhang, Jichen Zhu, Xiaoguang Yang, Kejun Long
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This study proposes an integrated optimization model for simultaneous traffic signal and vehicle routing. The model jointly optimizes decentralized signal timing plans and individual vehicle routes to minimize vehicle delays. It explicitly captures the relationship between individual routing decisions and the traffic flow dynamics at signalized intersections. To enhance computational efficiency, an Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM) is developed to decompose the integrated problem into several subproblems. Each vehicle can independently optimize its route while coordinating with the signal controller's decisions through information exchange. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method ensures better coordination between signal timing and vehicle routes, reducing the travel time and improving network efficiency. The ADMM algorithm solves each subproblem in approximately 0.1 s, indicating the ability of real-time implementation in real-world scenarios.

title: Safety Improvement of Car-Following for Connected Vehicle Flow Based on Individual Speed Guidance Upstream of a Lower Speed Limit Expressway Section
authors: Yunkuan Cui, Danya Yao, Xinrui Ni, Yi Zhang, Wei Zhou, Yuliang Qi, Ziyue Zheng, Hongjun Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: The deceleration process of traffic upstream of a fixed lower speed limit bottleneck is one of the important situations causing the major accidents, rear end collisions, on expressways. Vehicles' connectivity and cooperation can mitigate these risks by providing individual speed guidance based on their unique motion states. An environment model with a minimum duration value is built considering the acceptability of individual suggested speeds for approaching connected vehicles. A timely updated prediction control framework is developed to make the optimal control target of speed harmonization clearer and more intuitive. As this optimization problem is non-linear and high-dimensional, deep reinforcement learning is used to help get a feasible solution. The state space includes the suggestion speed at the last step and its duration, and the reward function includes the gap to the prediction control target in the framework. Training and validation simulation experiments show that the strategy can make a specific number of vehicles upstream driving through the deceleration section smoother and safer without much loss in efficiency by flexibly adjust their suggested speeds. A series of random validation scenarios show that the average and standard deviation of the related safety surrogate measurements over those scenarios are both improved a lot, particularly for those with high risks originally. At last, the analysis of the effects of different reward designs demonstrates it is necessary for our prediction framework to be included in the optimization process.

title: License Plate Detection and Recognition for Bangladeshi Vehicles
authors: Saifur Rahman, Md. Sabbir Al Ahsan, Sourav Adhikary, Md. Jamil Uddin
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: License Plate Detection and Recognition systems face significant challenges due to the complexity of Bangla characters, varying environmental conditions, diverse plate formats, and conditions prevalent in Bangladesh. This technology is crucial for Bangladesh's traffic management infrastructure, with applications in automated toll collection, parking management, and vehicle surveillance systems. This research presents a novel ensemble methodology that combines multiple preprocessing techniques with deep learning models to achieve robust recognition performance. The proposed system utilizes YOLOv8 for license plate detection, followed by an ensemble recognition pipeline that incorporates custom-trained EfficientNetB0 for Bengali character classification and EasyOCR as a hybrid method, with confidence-based voting for final predictions. The system was evaluated on 722 vehicle images which were under various lighting conditions and viewing angles. YOLOv8 achieved 99% license plate detection accuracy, and the ensemble approach achieved 94.90% full-plate recognition accuracy.

title: BSLM-CDAN: Batch-Norm-Stabilized and Locality-Weighted Conditional Adaptation for Cross-Condition Fault Diagnosis
authors: Yonghui Xu, Yusheng Zhang, Tianyu Gao
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Variable operating conditions induce covariate and conditional shifts that degrade bearing fault diagnosis. To stabilize conditional–marginal alignment within a CDAN backbone, an enhanced variant, BSLM-CDAN, which integrates a logit-level BN-stabilized classifier and a locality-weighted MK-MMD into CDAN, is introduced. The method integrates a logit-level Batch Normalization (BN)–regularized classifier to suppress gradient spikes, a locality-weighted multi-kernel maximum mean discrepancy (MK-MMD) to emphasize in-cluster target samples and mitigate outliers and pseudo-label noise, and adaptive loss scheduling that balances adversarial (conditional) and discrepancy (marginal) objectives. On CWRU cross-load transfers, BSLM-CDAN consistently outperforms DANN, ACDANN, and CDANN-JMMD; ablation results confirm the necessity of BN stabilization and locality weighting, and t-SNE visualizations display tighter, better-aligned class clusters. These findings indicate a practical, stability-oriented upgrade that makes CDAN-style adaptation more reliable for diagnosis under variable conditions.

title: On Using a Neural Network to Stabilize the Angular Position of a Satellite
authors: Margarita V. Maksimenko, Konstantin A. Orekhov, Vadim Yu. Sakharov, Alexey A. Tikhonov
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: The paper considers an artificial Earth satellite moving in a Keplerian circular orbit of arbitrary inclination. To stabilize the angular position of the satellite in the orbital coordinate system, an electrodynamic attitude control system is used based on the simultaneous use of two control torques - the Lorentz torque and the torque of magnetic interaction. The problem of stabilizing the satellite in the straight equilibrium position is solved, taking into account the influence of the disturbing effect of the gravitational torque. The decision is made in two stages. At the first stage, using the Monte Carlo method, the gain coefficients included in the expressions for the control torques are selected, and at the second stage, the neural network is trained based on previously found controls. The found controls are compared with similar controls previously constructed analytically. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is shown. The convergence of the control process to program motion under any randomly set initial conditions from a wide range is demonstrated.

title: Integrated Wheel Sensor Communication Using ESP32 - A Contribution Towards a Digital Twin of the Road System
authors: Ventseslav Yordanov, Simon Schäfer, Alexander Mann, Stefan Kowalewski, Bassam Alrifaee, Lutz Eckstein
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: While current onboard state estimation methods are adequate for most driving and safety-related applications, they do not provide insights into the interaction between tires and road surfaces. This paper explores a novel communication concept for efficiently transmitting integrated wheel sensor data from an ESP32 microcontroller. Our proposed approach utilizes a publish-subscribe system, surpassing comparable solutions in the literature regarding data transmission volume. We tested this approach on a drum tire test rig with our prototype sensors system utilizing a diverse selection of sample frequencies between 1 Hz and 32 000 Hz to demonstrate the efficacy of our communication concept. The implemented prototype sensor showcases minimal data loss, approximately 0.1% of the sampled data, validating the reliability of our developed communication system. This work contributes to advancing real-time data acquisition, providing insights into optimizing integrated wheel sensor communication.

title: Compact Encoding in Chipless RFID via RCS and Polarization Diversity
authors: Amit Kumar Baghel, Vinicius Uchoa Oliveira, Nuno Borges Carvalho, Pedro Pinho, Ajit Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: In this paper, a tag coding technique is presented based on polarization diversity and the magnitude variation of the radar cross section (RCS) of a single resonator/dipole operating at 5.8 GHz. Two parameters are controlled: the gap (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$g$</tex>) between the two parallel legs of the dipole and the polarization relative to the incident field. A total of four distinct states can be achieved using a single resonator. The tags were fabricated and experimentally measured at 5.8 GHz. A spatial density of 1.412 bits <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$/ \text{cm}^{2}$</tex> was achieved with this coding approach, which is quite promising.

title: Neurons-State-Attention-Driven Trustworthy Framework: An Interpretable Networks for Wind Turbine Gearbox Fault Diagnosis
authors: Chun Zhang, Yuhao Wu, Peng Chen, Meng Rao
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: In industrial wind turbine systems, gearbox fault diagnosis faces significant challenges due to complex operational conditions and the limitations of traditional diagnostic approaches, particularly in reliability assessment of deep learning models. Current methodologies often rely on black-box predictions, leading to uncertainty in fault detection accuracy. To address these challenges, this study proposes a novel Neurons-State-Attention-Driven (NSAD) framework for enhanced gearbox fault diagnosis in wind turbine systems. The framework introduces an innovative approach to quantifying model prediction confidence through systematic analysis of neuronal activation states and patterns, incorporating a sophisticated multi-component loss function that combines cross-entropy, multi-center, contrastive, and variance losses. This comprehensive diagnostic methodology demonstrates significant potential in advancing wind turbine gearbox diagnostics by improving both out-of-distribution detection rates and reducing in-distribution false positives, while providing interpretable confidence metrics for reliable fault detection.

title: Development of a Simplified Multi-Agent System for Fault Diagnosis
authors: Chunzhi Wu, Hua Zhong, Zhixin Jiao, Gaihong Yuan, Shuqi Hu, Jianyi Zhang, Rujun Sun, Chunyan Peng, Yougao Xiao
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: We present a lightweight, locally deployable agentic framework for fault diagnosis that treats large language models (LLMs) as controllers to orchestrate domain tools (feature extraction, visualization, and classifiers) under a unified workflow. Our goal is architectural and operational: to demonstrate how an LLM-driven pipeline enables traceable reasoning, plug-and-play tools, and maintainable workflows for PHM. We do not pursue head-to-head model comparisons. Instead, the framework is model-agnostic and uses best-of-breed, off-the-shelf models for each subtask, focusing on (i) natural-language tasking by engineers, (ii) on-demand tool orchestration, (iii) auditable, stepwise decision traces, and (iv) low-friction updates via prompt/tool changes rather than retraining. A case study illustrates end-to-end outputs that tie indicators, figures, and predictions to actionable maintenance suggestions, showing the framework’s value in real operations where data, tooling, and requirements evolve. The corresponding source code is publicly available on GitHub: https://github.com/chuznhiwu/dynamic_langgraph.

title:
Hydrothermally Synthesized Urea-Derived Co
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>4</sub>
                    Nanoparticles for Efficient Photocatalytic Degradation of Organic Dye
authors: Md Nafay Mahmud, Rifat Rafiu, Shouvik Kumar Paul, Samin Eshmum Enam, Md Mehidi Hasan, Fatema-Tuz-Zahra
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Cobalt oxide (Co<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</inf>) nanoparticles were synthesized through a urea-assisted hydrothermal route followed by mild annealing to obtain phase-pure spinel structures with controlled morphology. XRD analysis ensures crystalline Co<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</inf> with an average crystallite size of 25.7 nm, while FE-SEM discloses quite spherical nanoparticles (51.6 nm diameter). Optical measurements indicated a direct band gap of 2.388 eV, showing strong absorption in the visible region. Photocatalytic activity was observed using Rhodamine B as a model pollutant under 6 W UV irradiation, where about 50% degradation was gained within 15 minutes. Notably, while urea-mediated synthesis has been previously documented, this study provides the first report on the photocatalytic degradation of Rhodamine B using Co<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</inf> nanoparticles synthesized specifically via this urea-assisted hydrothermal precursor route. Despite the use of UV radiation, the band gap value confirms the material's inherent potential for visible-light-driven photocatalysis. The trend of rapid photocatalytic responses is attributed to efficient charge carrier separation and strong surface interactions between Co<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</inf> nanoparticles and dye molecules. These findings confirm that the urea-assisted hydrothermal synthesis enables controlled Co<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</inf> formation with effective and environmentally benign photocatalytic degradation performance of organic dyes.

title: BanglaPlateNet: A Real-Time Framework for License Plate Detection and Recognition on Bangladeshi Dataset Using YOLOv12 and EasyOCR
authors: Anika Bushra, Asif Talukder, Md. Abu Raihan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: License Plate recognition has already become a crucial component of the contemporary traffic, as it allows to enhance the road security, automate the toll collection, and assist the police officers. However, the Bangladeshi license plate is commonly hard to identify and spot, as they are often of various shape, font and color and are taken under low light or when crowded with people, etc. This paper proposes BanglaPlateNet, a novel real-time license plate recognition system for Bangladeshi plates, leveraging YOLOv12 for fast, efficient plate detection, and EasyOCR for accurate character recognition across diverse Bangla fonts and lighting conditions. In combination, they render BanglaPlateNet as fast and reliable with real world traffic conditions. The proposed model was trained using a Bangladeshi dataset and achieved 98.1% precision, 97.6% recall, 98.8% mAP@50 and 72.3% mAP@50-95. Those findings reveal that BanglaPlateNet is superior to its predecessors with the YOLO systems and can be successfully implemented in traffic monitoring and intelligent transportation systems in Bangladesh.

title: DeepScale: Forecast-Based Vertical Scaling in Cloud-Native MLOps
authors: Rohit Bankar, Deepak Nadig
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: Deep learning training workloads are inherently non-stationary and exhibit rapid variability, making static compute reservations in Cloud-native Machine Learning Operations (MLOps) pipelines either wasteful (due to over-provisioning) or risky (due to under-provisioning). We present DeepScale, a forecast-driven, high-frequency, in-place vertical pod autoscaling (VPA) framework that proactively right-sizes training pods without evictions or downtime. DeepScale continuously ingests per-pod CPU telemetry, forecasts near-term demand using time-series models (XGBoost, LSTM, N-BEATS), and applies policy-aware safety margins and buffers to produce robust resource recommendations. By leveraging in-place VPA in recent Kubernetes releases, resources are updated live with sub-minute convergence. Evaluated on three representative training pipelines, i.e., DNN (MNIST), CNN (CIFAR-10), and LSTM (IMDB), DeepScale reduces requested vCPU-hours by 38–91% over static and current in-place VPA baselines, hence lowering the cost proportionally. These results indicate that coupling predictive control with in-place vertical scaling yields a practical, self-optimizing, and cost-aware autoscaling solution for deep-learning training workloads.

title: Handover Failure Prediction in 5G and Beyond UEs using Deep Learning
authors: Nishtha Bansal, Yogesh Kumar Mishra, Rohit Polaswamy, Sathia Chandrane Sundararaju, Shrinath Ramamoorthy Madhurantakam
publish: 2025 IEEE 6th International Women in Technology Conference (WINTECHCON)
abstract: One of the unavoidable issues faced by the mobile UEs in any generation networks including 5G is mobility failure in RRC connected state or also called handover failure (HOF). Although there are multiple techniques available in literature for Radio Access Networks (RAN) to trigger handover (HO) command at the right time, User Equipment (UE) still face failures and these failures affect the service for a duration called HO interruption time (HIT). In this paper, we exploit the deep learning methods to predict the 5G HOF at the UE side and recover faster, thereby reducing interruption time during mobility failure scenarios. We developed Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) and Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) models and trained them with actual field conditions and achieved an accuracy of 97% with best recall value of 96%.

title: ParkFormer: A Transformer-Based Parking Policy with Goal Embedding and Pedestrian-Aware Control
authors: Jun Fu, Bin Tian, Haonan Chen, Shi Meng, Tingting Yao
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Autonomous parking plays a vital role in intelligent vehicle systems, particularly in constrained urban environments where high-precision control is required. While traditional rule-based parking systems struggle with environmental uncertainties and lack adaptability in crowded or dynamic scenes, human drivers demonstrate the ability to park intuitively without explicit modeling. Inspired by this observation, we propose a Transformer-based end-to-end framework for autonomous parking that learns from expert demonstrations. The network takes as input surround-view camera images, goal-point representations, ego vehicle motion, and pedestrian trajectories. It outputs discrete control sequences including throttle, braking, steering, and gear selection. A novel crossattention module integrates BEV features with target points, and a GRU-based pedestrian predictor enhances safety by modeling dynamic obstacles. We validate our method on the CARLA 0.9.14 simulator in both vertical and parallel parking scenarios. Experiments show our model achieves a high success rate of 96.57 %, with average positional and orientation errors of 0.21 meters and 0.41 degrees, respectively. The ablation studies further demonstrate the effectiveness of key modules such as pedestrian prediction and goal-point attention fusion. The code and dataset will be released at: https://github.com/little-snail-f/ParkFormer.

title: Can Magnetic Excitation in IBOB Body Resonance be Picked Up by Distant Wearable Capacitive Rx?
authors: Samyadip Sarkar, Lingke Ding, Shreyas Sen
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: High-throughput, energy-efficient links between implantable bioelectronics and wearables are essential for advanced medical monitoring and therapy. Existing Radio Frequency (RF) systems in the Med-Radio and ISM bands suffer deep tissue absorption, magnetic induction demands bulky coils and precise alignment, and quasistatic galvanic coupling experiences rapid signal attenuation away from the signal source. To overcome these limits, in light of the recently growing interest in capacitive-excitation-based Body Resonance Human Body Communication (BR HBC) for high-speed connectivity, this study introduces a novel communication modality between a magnetic implantable transmitter (Tx) and a wearable capacitive receiver (Rx). We conceptualize the human body as a distributed transmission line to enable guided body-aided transmission. Numerical simulations of a coil-shaped implantable Tx paired with a capacitive Rx demonstrate data rates exceeding 20 Mbps over an 80 cm coverage area. These findings pave the way for secure, lowpower, high-throughput implant-to-wearable networks, marking a significant step forward for next-generation bioelectronics.

title: Intention Enhanced Diffusion Model for Multimodal Pedestrian Trajectory Prediction
authors: Yu Liu, Zhijie Liu, Xiao Ren, You-Fu Li, He Kong
