The proposed study overcomes these shortcomings by proposing a dual-branch network, AgriChatNet, to combine ResNet50 and MobileNetV2 to simultaneously extract deep hierarchical and lightweight spatial data through tomato leaf images and provides voice and text response through a Bengali-enabled chatbot to provide easy access to the source of disease explanation as well as actionable suggestions. This architecture trained on 29,609 samples of PlantVillage, has an accuracy of 91.7% and a weighted F1-score of 93.65% exceeding all standalone baselines. The model-chatbot pipeline system, resulting in a higher level of diagnostic reliability and usability among Bengali farmers, will lead to wider usage of AI-based crop management.

title: SleevePose: Elastic Resistive Pressure Sensing Sleeve for Continuous Hand Pose Estimation
authors: Mengting Niu, Yufan Xiong, Ziyu Wu, Ying Qiu, Fangting Xie, Boyan Liu, Qijun Ying, Xiaohui Cai
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Interactive, Mobile, Wearable and Ubiquitous Technologies
abstract: None

title: 2.5D Object Detection for Intelligent Roadside Infrastructure
authors: Nikolai Polley, Yacin Boualili, Ferdinand Mütsch, Maximilian Zipft, Tobias Fleck, J. Marius Zöllner
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: On-board sensors of autonomous vehicles can be obstructed, occluded, or limited by restricted fields of view, complicating downstream driving decisions. Intelligent roadside infrastructure perception systems, installed at elevated vantage points, can provide wide, unobstructed intersection coverage, supplying a complementary information stream to autonomous vehicles via vehicle-to-everything (V2X) communication. However, conventional 3D object-detection algorithms struggle to generalize to the domain shift introduced by top-down perspectives and steep camera angles. We introduce a 2.5D object detection framework, tailored specifically for roadside infrastructure cameras. Unlike conventional 2D or 3D object detection, we employ a prediction approach to detect ground planes of vehicles as parallelograms in the image frame. The parallelogram preserves the planar position, size, and orientation of objects while omitting their height, which is unnecessary for most downstream applications. For training, a mix of real-world and synthetically generated scenes is leveraged. We evaluate generalizability on a held-out camera viewpoint and on adverse-weather scenarios absent from the training set. Our results show high detection accuracy, strong cross-viewpoint generalization, and robustness to diverse lighting and weather conditions. Model weights and inference code are provided at: https://gitlab.kit.edu/kit/aifb/ATKS/public/digit4taf/2.5d-object-detection

title: A Novel Stub-Loaded Spurline Structure for Miniaturized UWB Microstrip Filters with Harmonic Suppression
authors: Rupayan Halder, Arghyadeep Das, Md Abuzar, Dhruba Das, S. K. Parui
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: A novel stub loaded spur line with low pass filtering nature has been proposed for wide band application. The usability of the design has been proven by integrating it in the series arm of an ultra wide band filter with 102 % bandwidth over 4.9 to 15.1 GHz. A direct miniaturization of 24 % has been achieved. Further, it has been shown that the same structure provides more than 30 dB harmonic rejection in the UWB filter performance.

title: Enhancing Near-Field Communication Coil Performance Using Metasurfaces
authors: Zahra Hamzavi-Zarghani, Jasmin Grosinger
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: The paper presents the integration of a metasurface to near-field communication (NFC) coils to enhance the generated magnetic field. The coils are designed to operate at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 3. 5 6 ~ M H z}$</tex>, and the metasurface consists of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3} \times \mathbf{3}$</tex> split ring resonators (SRRs). Simulation results using CST Microwave Studio show improvement in the magnetic field of the coil when the proposed metasurface is added. To verify the effectiveness of metasurfaces in improving NFC systems, a different structure is considered for real applications. The transmission coefficient between two coils is measured with and without the designed metasurface. The measurement results confirm increasing the transmission coefficient between two coils when integrated with the metasurface as a result of increasing the magnetic resonance coupling.

title: Machine Learning-Assisted Performance Prediction for Surface Plasmon Resonance Sensors: From FEM Simulations to Real-Time Assessment
authors: Sheikh Shahriar Hossain, Md Jahirul Islam, Md Abdur Rakib, Md Rejvi Kaysir
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Surface Plasmon Resonance (SPR) enables real-time biochemical detection, yet standard Finite Element Method (FEM) simulations for sensor optimization are notoriously slow and computationally intensive. To overcome this bottleneck, we propose a machine learning framework to predict sensor performance rapidly across different plasmonic configurations. In this study, we consider the D-shaped optical fiber SPR designs due to their ease of fabrication, cost efficiency, and mechanical robustness. Here, we examine 9 ML algorithms, ranging from Linear Regression to Neural Networks (MLP, BNN, RBF-NN), on a dataset of 65 dual-layer configurations involving gold (Au), silver (Ag), copper (Cu), and graphene (C) in different topological combinations. XGBoost regression model proved most effective for Resonance Wavelength prediction (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.9895, 1.57% error), while Polynomial Regression optimally modeled the Full-Width Half-Maximum (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> ≈ 1). From correlation analyses, we get meaningful relationships between design parameters, such as the analyte refractive indices, material selections (1st and 2nd layers), and layer thicknesses, with targeted optical responses. Additionally, we deployed these models via a Streamlit web app, reducing analysis time from hours to seconds. Validation against unseen COMSOL data confirmed reliability, with average relative errors of 9.977%, 12.879%, and 1.995% respectively, for wavelength sensitivity, figure of merit, and Q-factor.

title: Enhanced Take-off and Landing Performance of Light Aircraft and Large UAVs with VariableIncidence Wings
authors: Nikolai N. Kokin, Sergey V. Karamov
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Mechanical Engineering and Power Engineering (MEPE)
abstract: One of the most important parameters of aircraft or UAVs with classic non-VTOL configurations is the take-off and landing path length, which depends on many other variables. One way to reduce the length of the take-off and landing path is to use a variable-angle wing on an aircraft, which is a complex multicriteria task. To find a quasi-optimal solution for constructing the design of an aircraft or UAV, it is necessary to take into account the following parameters as much as possible: airplane configuration type - high/mid/low-wing, wing airfoil, wing incidence, control-surface parameters, engine thrust, take-off mass, desired take-off speed, total aerodynamic drag, lift-to-drag ratio, propeller blade pitch and many others. This paper discusses requirements analysis, a modelling method, aerodynamic dependencies, multi-criteria parameterization, comparative evaluation of results, and design assessments of implementation for aircraft and UAVs in the 500 kg weight class, a popular category today. The conclusion demonstrates the technical feasibility, technical advantages, and results of the proposed approach.

title: Dynamic Optimization of Low-Temperature Heating Target Temperature for Lithium-Ion Battery Energy Storage Systems Based on DAUKF-LSTM
authors: Peijin Liu, Wenchi Sun
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Current lithium-ion battery energy storage systems face limitations in low-temperature operation due to empirically predetermined heating targets that remain constant during discharge cycles, lacking theoretical support for dynamic optimization and system-level energy efficiency. To address this, we propose a dynamic optimization method for energy storage systems, based on collaborative Dual Adaptive Unscented Kalman Filter (DAUKF) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks. First, to enhance energy system reliability, a DAUKF algorithm is developed for joint estimation of State of Charge (SOC) and State of Temperature (SOT), mitigating error accumulation issues inherent in conventional Dual Extended Kalman Filter (DEKF) methods under nonlinear time-varying conditions. Subsequently, an LSTM-driven dynamic efficiency–temperature mapping model is constructed to overcome static empirical rule constraints, enabling accurate prediction of system-optimal operating temperatures across SOC intervals. Furthermore, a "state estimation–temperature prediction–closed-loop optimization" framework is established, in which dynamic predictions provide feedback to optimize heating targets, thereby improving energy conversion efficiency and operational stability in low-temperature environments. Experimental results confirm significant enhancement in discharge efficiency at the system level, demonstrating the potential of the proposed method to support efficient and resilient energy storage operation in cold-region applications.

title: Early Pneumonia Risk Prediction using Internet of Medical Things and Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Himadri Neog, Nabajyoti Medhi, Revathi Radhakrishnan, Pinaki Chakravarty
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: The incorporation of Machine Learning (ML) with Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) has enabled continuous health monitoring and early disease prediction. This study presents an IoMT-ML integrated framework for early pneumonia risk prediction. Health vitals from 20 individuals were collected using IoMT sensors and used to generate synthetic data via Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) to mitigate data scarcity. A binary target label was introduced using the CRB-65 score, and SMOTE was applied for class balancing. Five ML algorithms including K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost and Support vector Machine (SVM) were trained on the synthetic data and validated on real hospital records. Among them, XGBoost has overall achieved superior results with 90.88% accuracy, 76.16% F1-score and 70.74% recall. Furthermore, stratified k-fold cross-validation confirmed the robustness of the framework for early pneumonia risk prediction.

title: Advanced Standardization in Digital Process Automation Systems
authors: Evgenij Yurkevich, Lidia Kryukova
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: In order to develop a methodology for finding effective solutions in managing the construction of digital production, the paper propose an approach to the multidimensional paradigm formation, which determines the trend of product intensification under the individual requirements of the customer. For the machine-readable regulatory documents formation, it shows rationality to introduce entity type identifiers into the SMART standard: “paragraph”, “graphic object” and “table cell”. The mechanism for setting standards using a SMART document allows you to collect requirements for heterogeneous technological processes into a single logical entity. In relation, digital production systems combining the characteristics of expert decision-making technologies and the production parameters of the artificial intelligence processes. In the construction of control mechanisms for process automation systems shows the rationality paradigm of using the norms established according to advanced standardization technologies of the unified architecture of an independent open distributed system platform (OPC UA).

title: Localized Gear Fault Diagnosis via Envelope Analysis of Heterodyne Downconverted Ultrasonic Signals
authors: Jingchao Zhang, Chunwei Wang, Yupeng Zhang, Shiyu Cen, Yingwei Li
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: To enhance the practicality of ultrasonic fault diagnosis techniques in gearbox fault detection, this study investigates the impact of heterodyne downconversion on feature preservation and diagnostic performance. First, based on modulation theory, it is theoretically derived and experimentally verified that the downconversion process does not alter the envelope spectrum characteristics of ultrasonic signals. Subsequently, a low-noise and low-distortion heterodyne-based ultrasonic signal acquisition system is designed and implemented. A circuit parameter optimization method based on correlation coefficient analysis is proposed to effectively suppress distortion and noise introduced during the downconversion process. Finally, under the same sampling rate conditions, comparative experiments are conducted using heterodyne-converted ultrasonic signals, original ultrasonic signals, and vibration signals. The results demonstrate that heterodyne ultrasonic signals exhibit a higher signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) in the diagnosis of localized gear faults. This research provides both theoretical and methodological support for the practical application of ultrasonic downconversion techniques in engineering scenarios.

title: Mission-Based Scenario Generation Method for Integrated Reliability and Safety Testing of Avionics Systems
authors: Jinyong Yao, Yuhui Nie, Jingtao Wu, Zhanpeng Ren
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: In the civil aviation sector, safety requirements for commercial aircraft are exceptionally stringent, with the allowable probability of catastrophic failures constrained below extremely low safety thresholds. Safety verification aims to ensure that the occurrence probabilities of failures across various hazard levels comply with these critical thresholds, whereas reliability verification focuses on evaluating the system’s ability to continuously perform its intended functions under specified conditions. Currently, significant overlaps exist between these two verification processes, particularly in environmental stress testing and functional performance assessments. Traditional independent verification strategies often lead to redundant test resource allocation and prolonged testing cycles, which are increasingly inadequate for addressing the complexity arising from the deep hardware-software integration characteristic of modern avionics systems. This study presents a graph-based methodology for constructing integrated test scenarios. Specifically, it analyzes the correlation between flight mission phases and environmental contexts and establishes a system-level fault propagation network. In this model, components of the flight control system are represented as nodes, while fault propagation paths serve as edges. System functions, mission profiles, and testing timelines are mapped into multidimensional scenario tuples. Guided by the directed graph, fault injection timing and content are systematically selected to design three-dimensional scenario tuples and corresponding fault injection configurations. By reusing overlapping test conditions, the proposed approach significantly enhances verification efficiency while maintaining test completeness and reducing overall costs. This methodology offers an innovative framework for the verification of aeronautical electromechanical systems and demonstrates broad applicability for the integrated verification of complex systems in domains with stringent safety requirements.

title: PointCompress3D: A Point Cloud Compression Framework for Roadside Lidars in Intelligent Transportation Systems
authors: Walter Zimmer, Ramandika Pranamulia, Xingcheng Zhou, Jiajie Zhang, Chuheng Wei, Ross Greer, Julie Stephany B. Perez, Mingyu Liu, Holger Caesar, Mohan Trivedi, Alois Knoll
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: In the context of Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS), efficient data compression is crucial for managing large-scale point cloud data from roadside LiDAR sensors. There is strong demand for compact storage, streaming, and real-time object detection due to the high data volume and need for rapid processing in time-critical applications. This work introduces PointCompress3D, a novel compression framework tailored for roadside LiDARs. It addresses the challenges of compressing high-resolution point clouds in real time while preserving accuracy and sensor compatibility. We adapt and integrate three state-of-the-art compression methods and evaluate them on real-world TUM Traffic datasets. Moreover, we deploy our framework on a live ITS test bed and test it under real traffic conditions. After fine-tuning, we achieve 10 FPS with compression sizes under 105 KB, a reduction of 50 times, while maintaining object detection performance comparable to the original data. We achieve a point-to-plane (d2) PSNR of 94.46 in extensive experiments and ablations on the TUM Traffic datasets. Code and video results are available on our website.

title: High-Resolution Forecasting of Road Surface Temperature Using Graph Neural Networks
authors: Shogo Takedomi, Keita Ishii, Teppei Mori, Akira Saida, Kota Okumura, Yuki Nakamura, Tetsurou Matsushima, Shunsuke Ono
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Effective winter road maintenance (WRM) is critical for the safety and mobility of road users in cold regions. High spatial resolution predictions of road surface conditions greatly improve WRM efficiency. Previous studies have explored spatial modeling to achieve this; however, traditional statistical models, such as Kriging, face limitations in capturing complex spatial dependency. In this study, we utilized graph neural networks (GNNs) for spatial modeling to predict road surface temperature (RST), which an essential road weather parameter, at high spatial resolution. Furthermore, by integrating this approach with time-series prediction model at road weather observatories and weather mesh forecast data, we propose a framework that enables high spatial-resolution RST predictions over future horizons. Experiments using real-world data from Sapporo, Japan demonstrated that the proposed method could predict RST at approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0}$</tex>-meter intervals along road networks with an root mean square error below 2° C for future horizons up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 4 h}$</tex>. In conclusion, the study findings represent a significant improvement in accuracy over current operational methods, offering valuable support for decision-making in WRM planning to enhance road safety and mobility.

title: Origin-Destination Pattern Effects on Large-Scale Mixed Traffic Control via Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
authors: Muyang Fan, Songyang Liu, Shuai Li, Weizi Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Traffic congestion remains a major challenge for modern urban transportation, diminishing both efficiency and quality of life. While autonomous driving technologies and reinforcement learning (RL) have shown promise for improving traffic control, most prior work has focused on small-scale networks or isolated intersections. Large-scale mixed traffic control, involving both human-driven and robotic vehicles, remains underexplored. In this study, we propose a decentralized multi-agent reinforcement learning framework for managing large-scale mixed traffic networks, where intersections are controlled either by traditional traffic signals or by robotic vehicles. We evaluate our approach on a real-world network of 14 intersections in Colorado Springs, Colorado, USA, using average vehicle waiting time as the primary measure of traffic efficiency. We are exploring a problem that has not been sufficiently addressed: Is large-scale Multi-Agent Traffic Control (MTC) still feasible when facing time-varying Origin-Destination (OD) patterns?

title: Is Intermediate Fusion All You Need for UAV-Based Collaborative Perception?
authors: Jiuwu Hao, Liguo Sun, Yuting Wan, Yueyang Wu, Ti Xiang, Haolin Song, Pin Lv
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Collaborative perception enhances environmental awareness through inter-agent communication and is regarded as a promising solution to intelligent transportation systems. However, existing collaborative methods for Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) overlook the unique characteristics of the UAV perspective, resulting in substantial communication overhead. To address this issue, we propose a novel communication-efficient collaborative perception framework based on late-intermediate fusion, dubbed LIF. The core concept is to exchange informative and compact detection results and shift the fusion stage to the feature representation level. In particular, we leverage vision-guided positional embedding (VPE) and box-based virtual augmented feature (BoBEV) to effectively integrate complementary information from various agents. Additionally, we innovatively introduce an uncertainty-driven communication mechanism that uses uncertainty evaluation to select high-quality and reliable shared areas. Experimental results demonstrate that our LIF achieves superior performance with minimal communication bandwidth, proving its effectiveness and practicality. Code and models are available at https://github.com/uestchjw/LIF.

title: Design of a 1-Bit Coding Metasurface with Prephasing to Mitigate the Quantization Lobe Under Plane Wave Incidence
authors: Deepak Kumar Sahoo, Debidas Kundu, Amalendu Patnaik
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents the design and analysis of a 1-bit coding reflective metasurface that employs prephasing to reduce the quantization lobe under plane wave excitation. The metasurface consists of two types of 1-bit elements, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{E}_{1}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{E}_{2}$</tex>, designed to generate binary phase responses offset by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 0}^{\circ}$</tex>. A compact <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$20 \times 3$</tex> array is configured by randomly distributing the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{E}_{1}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{E}_{2}$</tex> cells using a two-level <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(K=2)$</tex> prephasing scheme. Theoretical modeling of the prephased coding sequence is developed, and validated through array factor calculations and full-wave simulations in CST. The results show good agreement and confirm that the proposed method improves main beam gain while reducing quantized lobes. Moreover, its hardware complexity remains the same as 1-bit coding reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS). The approach offers a practical and efficient solution for low-cost beam steering at mm-wave frequencies.

title: Application of the TF-ResNet-MSA Model in the Complex Fault Diagnosis of EHA System Plunger Pumps
authors: Wenqi Zhang, Zhenbao Liu, Zhen Jia, Xingchen Ge, Luyao Wang
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: To address the challenge of accurately diagnosing complex faults in Electro-Hydrostatic Actuation (EHA) system plunger pumps, which are characterised by multimodal signals, weak fault features, and class imbalance under high-pressure and high-dynamic working conditions, this paper proposes a novel TF-ResNet-MSA model enhanced with transfer learning. The model utilises a ResNet-50 backbone, incorporates a Multi-Scale Attention (MSA) mechanism for adaptive weighting of local and global time-frequency features, and integrates a Transformer encoder to capture long-range dependencies among vibration, pressure, and acoustic modalities. Furthermore, batch-wise dynamic weighting is introduced during training to mitigate label imbalance, thereby enabling end-to-end fine-tuning with limited labelled data. The experimental findings, derived from a simulated dataset, demonstrate that the proposed model attains an accuracy of 0.982 and an F1-score of 0.977 across five distinct fault types. This outcome signifies a 6.9 percentage point enhancement over the ResNet-50 baseline. In real-world noise and varying working conditions, the model demonstrated an accuracy of 0.953 and an F1-score of 0.947, exhibiting only a 2.9-point degradation from the simulation. This performance significantly surpasses that of ablated models employing MSA (0.903) or Transformer alone (0.896). The confusion matrix analysis indicates a misclassification rate of less than 2.2%, and the model demonstrates an accuracy of over 0.918 under SNR = 10 dB. The findings demonstrate the model's superior diagnostic precision and robustness in practical EHA scenarios, offering an intelligent solution for fault detection and predictive maintenance in aerospace hydraulic actuators that are highly efficient and interpretable.

title: Offline Signature Verification System Using Siamese Networks with Four Distinct Backbone Architectures: MobileNetV2, VGG16, InceptionV3 and a Custom CNN
authors: Md. Kawsar Ali, Bristi Rani Roy, Falguni Yesmine Sapna, S.M. Koushik Shahriar
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: This document presents an in-depth examination of offline signature verification utilizing Siamese neural networks. Our study tackles the issue of advanced forgeries by assessing a Siamese network design with four different backbone architectures: MobileNetV2, VGG16, InceptionV3, and a custom CNN. For model evaluation, we employed the CEDAR dataset, a reputable benchmark known for offering a realistic testing framework. Our custom CNN surpassed all other models with an outstanding accuracy of 99.81%. This marks a notable advancement compared to conventional techniques and exceeded the performance of the pre-trained models, which also yielded strong results. Our system effectively accommodates natural variations in signatures while providing robust protection against forgeries. The results highlight the benefits of a specially designed CNN for this purpose, offering a useful guideline for choosing architectures in various biometric security applications. This research shows significant promise for practical implementation in areas like banking and legal documentation.

title: Dynamic Analysis of Cage in Double Row Angular Contact Ball Bearings
authors: Xin’Gang Yang, Kai Wang, Runtian Du, Hongqiang Yao, Hongyan Liu, Gangyi Hu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Mechanical Engineering and Power Engineering (MEPE)

title: Low-Latency LiDAR Data Quality Monitoring for ADAS Under Adverse Weather Conditions
authors: Juan D. González, Mirko Maehlisch
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)

title: Automated Route-based Conflation between Linear Referencing System Maps and OpenStreetMap Using Open-source Tools
authors: Gibran Ali, Neal Feierabend, Prarthana Doshi, Whoibin Chung, Simona Babiceanu, Michael Fontaine
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)

title: Optimizing Traffic Flow at Unsignalized Intersections via Centralized Reinforcement Learning for Connected and Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Fatima-Zahrae El-Qoraychy, Mahjoub Dridi, Jean-Charles Créput
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Intersection management is a key challenge in the development of intelligent transportation systems, especially with the rise of Connected and Autonomous Vehicles. Traditional control strategies, such as traffic lights or static priority rules, often fail to adapt to dynamic and dense traffic conditions, resulting in increased delays and suboptimal traffic flow. In this paper, we propose a centralized intersection management framework based on deep reinforcement learning (DRL), where a central agent directly controls the accelerations of vehicles approaching an unsignalized intersection. Leveraging spatial and temporal real-time information on vehicle states and intended trajectories in different traffic flows, the agent learns continuous control policies that ensure optimal navigation in real-world scenarios. We implement the Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3) algorithm to achieve stable and effective learning in a continuous action space. Simulation results using the SUMO traffic simulator demonstrate that our approach significantly outperforms traditional baselines such as fixed-time traffic lights, First-Come-First-Served and DRL-based approaches, such as Soft Actor-Critic (SAC). In high-density scenarios, TD3 reduces average waiting times by up to 87%, improves throughput by 14.5%, and shortens travel times by 28.2%. These results highlight the potential of our approach to enhance urban traffic flow and support future smart transportation systems.

title: A Novel Spiral Radiator-Based Quad-Port MIMO CP Antenna Using Characteristic Mode Analysis (CMA) for Wi-Fi 6E Applications
authors: Biku Raut, Bikash K. Santi, Deepak K. Naik, Dhruba C. Panda
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: In this work, a quad-port MIMO circularly polarized (CP) antenna featuring spiral-type radiators and an umbrella-inspired defected ground structure (DGS) is proposed for Wi-Fi 6E applications. The antenna offers an impedance bandwidth (IBW) of 5.36 to 7 GHz and an axial ratio bandwidth (ARBW) ranging from 5.82 to 6.64 GHz, ensuring robust circular polarization performance. The integration of the spiral-type radiators with the DGS produces a low axial ratio, while the unique Swastik-inspired configuration of the antenna elements significantly enhances port isolation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(>-25 \text{dB})$</tex> by reducing mutual coupling. The design demonstrates excellent MIMO characteristics, including a low envelope correlation coefficient (ECC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<0.001$</tex>), a high diversity gain (9.99 dB), minimal channel capacity loss <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(C C L<0.4)$</tex>, and balanced mean effective gain (MEG). These attributes make the antenna a convincing solution for advanced wireless communication systems operating below 7 GHz.

title: An Interpretable Ensemble Framework for Credit Card Fraud Detection on a Highly Imbalanced Dataset
authors: Md. Moniruzzaman, Md Al Hasnain Bin Arif, Md.Samaun Islam, Ashis Kumar Mandal, Md. Arshad Ali
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: The rapid growth of digital payment systems has made credit card fraud a serious global concern, causing substantial financial losses. Detecting such fraudulent activities is challenging due to the severe class imbalance in transaction datasets, where fraudulent cases are extremely rare. Many existing models suffer from overfitting or produce misleading outcomes under these conditions. To solve this problem, this study introduces a two-stage hybrid framework that focuses on both accuracy and clear interpretability. First, an unsupervised LSTM Autoencoder is trained only on normal transactions to learn regular spending behavior. Transactions with high reconstruction errors are marked as suspicious, and these errors are turned into a new feature called the Anomaly Score. Next, the SMOTE is used to balance the dataset, and a LightGBM classifier is trained on this improved data. The proposed system performs strongly on unseen test data, achieving an accuracy of 99.94% and an F1-score of 82.65%. Further analysis shows that the Anomaly Score plays a key role in the model’s decision process. Overall, this work offers a dependable, interpretable, and effective method for detecting credit card fraud in highly imbalanced datasets.

title: Developing Route Technology for Making Products in Distributed Instrument Manufacturing
authors: Mikhail Ponomarenko, Yuriy Andreev
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: The paper identifies key issues in creating a part manufacturing route during technological preparation of production. A method is proposed for forming a processing route that takes into account the current and planned capabilities of equipment and forming an optimal sequence of operations through the use of an intelligent enterprise resource management system.

title: Dictionary Learning-Based Methods for Semiconductor Wafer History Data Synthesis
authors: V. Kavya, Kumar Balakrishna, Sameera Bharadwaja Hayavadana, Akash Gaurav, Ankit Gupta, Siddhrath Jandial
publish: 2025 IEEE 6th International Women in Technology Conference (WINTECHCON)
abstract: We consider the problem of generating synthetic data for evaluating the performance of the root cause analysis (RCA) algorithms applied to yield excursion (YEx) systems in the semiconductor fabrication facility (Fab). We propose two computationally efficient synthetic dataset generation methods using dictionary learning for generating the wafer path history data along with a machine learning-based wafer quality prediction method for generating the target data for RCA pipeline. We compare and contrast the performance of our method with multiple synthetic dataset generation methods, namely, Markov transition matrix sampling and two generative adversarial network (GAN) architectures. Finally, we show the efficacy of the generated data using train synthetic test real (TSTR) technique on RCA algorithms available in the literature.

title: Cyclist Near-Miss Detection Using Lightweight Deep Temporal Neural Networks
authors: Khaled Saleh, Artur Grigorev, Adriana-Simona Mihaita
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Near-miss incidents, where cyclists narrowly avoid collisions, are critical for understanding and improving urban cycling safety but are often underreported in official statistics. This paper introduces XceptionCycle, a lightweight and efficient deep neural network designed to detect cyclist near-miss incidents using time-series data from Inertial Measurement Units (IMUs) and GPS sensors. Building on the XceptionTime architecture, our model incorporates inverted bottlenecks and multi-scale depth-wise separable convolutions to extract rich temporal features while maintaining a low computational footprint. We benchmark XceptionCycle on the large-scale SimRa dataset, demonstrating superior discriminative performance with an 81.99% Area Under the ROC Curve and strong robustness with a 48.33% Matthews correlation coefficient, outperforming state-of-the-art models, while requiring less than half the number of trainable parameters. These results highlight XceptionCycle's potential for real-time near-miss detection and its suitability for resource-constrained environments such as mobile safety applications.

title: A high-precision spacecraft bearing fault diagnosis method based on improved EMD-CNN-LSTM fusion
authors: Jiaqiang Li, Yi Du, Xiaoyang Pan
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: In view of the problems such as high nonlinearity, strong noise, and non - stationary signal interference faced by key components of on-orbit spacecraft (such as robotic arm bearings) during operation, this paper proposes an intelligent bearing fault diagnosis method based on the fusion of Empirical Mode Decomposition (EMD) and deep neural network. First, this method uses EMD to adaptively decompose the bearing vibration signal, extract multi-scale Intrinsic Mode Functions (IMFs), and effectively separate fault features from background noise. Subsequently, the selected IMF components are constructed into multi-channel inputs and fed into the Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) module to learn spatial local features automatically. Then, the Long Short- Term Memory (LSTM) network is used to model its temporal evolution law. Then, the fine-tuned AdamW algorithm designed is incorporated for optimization achieving end-to-end fault identification. Compared with traditional feature engineering and single model structures, this improved method shows significant advantages in weak fault detection, time-varying signal analysis, and anti-noise performance. Experimental results show that this method has excellent classification accuracy under simulated multi-fault conditions of spacecraft bearings, and has good robustness and generalizability, providing a feasible solution for the health monitoring and intelligent maintenance of key components of on-orbit spacecraft.

title: SIW Broadband Beam Scanning Antenna Utilizing Multiple Regression-Based Machine Learning Models
authors: Soumyadeep Ghosh, Sukomal Dey
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This work presents an SIW-based Leaky Wave Beam Scanning Antenna (SLWBSA) over the sub-10 GHz regime. The antenna is capable of radiating in both forward and backward directions using TE mode. The antenna is made with a semiperiodic transverse slot in a star-like configuration. The unit cell design is optimized using Machine learning (ML) based regression models like Linear Regression (LR), Polynomial Regression (PR), Support Vector Regression (SVR), Random Forest Regression (RFR), and eXtreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost). Such models are trained using a simulated dataset from the proposed antenna configurations. Finally, the RFR model predicts more cases with a gain above 10 dBi and supports a wider bandwidth. The proposed antenna works over 6.3 to 9.9 GHz with an average gain of 12 dBi over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 80$</tex> degree beam scanning for imaging or sensing applications.

title: A Compact and Efficient 2.47 GHz Rectenna for Biomedical Applications in the ISM Band
authors: Sreejita Samanta, Soumava Biswas, Nilanjan Dutta, Shrabani Mukherjee, Kaushik Mandal
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: A compact and efficient rectenna operating in the 2.4 - 2.5 GHz Industrial, Scientific and Medical (ISM) band is proposed for RF energy harvesting applications. The receiving antenna is a slotted rectangular microstrip patch with an inset feed and a defected ground structure (DGS), designed on a lowcost FR4 substrate of 1.6 mm thickness. The antenna exhibits a compact footprint of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$24 \text{mm} \times 21 \text{mm}\left(0.20 \lambda_{0} \times 0.17 \lambda_{0}\right)$</tex> and achieves resonance at 2.47 GHz with a measured gain of 1.49 dBi and return loss below - 10 dB. For rectification, a voltagedoubler topology is implemented using an SMS7621 Schottky diode with an LC matching network optimized in ADS. The simulated rectifier circuit achieves a peak power conversion efficiency (PCE) of 60.18 % with a load resistance of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${2.7 \mathrm{k} {\Omega}}$</tex> and an input signal power of 5 dBm at 2.47 GHz. Comparative performance analysis with existing works confirms the superiority of the proposed rectenna in terms of compactness and efficiency, making it a suitable candidate for low-power biomedical and IoT applications. The antenna design is experimentally validated, while rectifier testing is deferred due to component unavailability.

title: PreSimNet: A Unified Framework for Predicting and Simulating Car-Following Behaviors in Mixed Traffic Flow
authors: Hao Lyu, Wan Liu, Ting Wang, Quansheng Yue, Junyao Li, Yanyong Guo
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: The coexistence of autonomous vehicles (AVs) and human-driven vehicles (HVs) in mixed traffic flow introduces unprecedented complexity to vehicular interaction dynamics, particularly in multi-type car-following scenarios. However, existing research often focuses on limited interaction subsets, without reflecting a comprehensive understanding of mixed traffic behavior. More importantly, both trajectory prediction and car-following simulation rely on trajectory encoding information, and their potential for integration has not been fully explored. To address this, we propose PreSimNet, a physics-informed deep learning framework that seamlessly integrates trajectory prediction and car-following simulation. Firstly, we design a type-guided trajectory feature learning module in PreSimNet, utilizing a large-scale dataset of over 900 hours of real-world driving data, including four interaction types (AV-HV, AV-AV, HV-AV, HV-HV), to capture interaction subtleties. On this basis, we incorporate kinematic priors and cross- attention mechanisms to construct an accurate trajectory prediction module. In a synchronized and integrated manner, a domain-knowledge-driven mixture-of-experts approach is used to develop an adaptable and informed car following simulation module. Experimental results demonstrate that PreSimNet significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in both trajectory prediction and simulation tasks. Moreover, comparative experiments reveal two critical insights: while trajectory prediction excels in short-term position estimation, its open-loop nature inevitably accumulates errors in extended horizons. In contrast, simulation exhibits consistency with real-world driving behaviors with its closed-loop feedback. This result further confirms the complementary advantages demonstrated by the unified PreSimNet framework. This work provides a powerful toolkit for digital twin modeling in mixed traffic flow applications.

title: Smart Wearable Lenses on Kevlar Vests
authors: Vitor Freitas, Amit Baghel, Pedro Pinho
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper proposes an efficient Wireless Power Transfer (WPT) system for charging electronic devices and sensors, using Fresnel Zone Lenses (FZL) and smart textiles integrated into a Kevlar vest. The system was designed and simulated in CST Microwave Studio. At 5.8 GHz, a highdirectivity horn antenna serves as the transmitter. The receiver features a textile-based FZL, made from layers with varying permittivity, to focus the incoming beam onto an antenna embedded in the Kevlar vest. Simulations show that the textile FZL significantly enhances gain, providing a cost-effective and efficient solution for applications such as military equipment charging.

title: Mathematical Modeling on PM Pressure in Factory Testing for the Main Fuel Pump Regulator of an Aero-Engine
authors: Yan Zhou, Zhourong Fan, Zikang Yu, Shunong Zhang, Zhenyu Huang
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: This paper addresses the mathematical modeling on the pressure which called PM pressure in the uniform-speed static piston upper chamber, where a pressure oscillation fault can be often occurred during the open-loop factory testing for a certain type of aero-engine main fuel pump regulator (MFPR). The working principle of the speed regulator of the MFPR was analyzed for the PM pressure oscillation, and a transfer function block diagram was established based on the characteristics of open-loop detection in the factory. This paper establishes transfer functions and expressions for PM pressure with engine speed and inlet temperature T<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> as inputs and PM pressure as an output, mainly involving centrifugal flyweights, the pendulum valve, speed setting and temperature correction components, etc. The static and dynamic equation and transfer function for each part have been derived in detail. This study reveals key factors inducing PM pressure oscillation that under the open-loop factory testing situation, which lays a theoretical foundation for subsequent in-depth PM pressure fault injection simulation analysis. This work also provides a direct theoretical basis and quantitative support for design improvement to eliminate the PM pressure oscillation fault for the MFPR.

title: Modeling and Physical Insights Into Negative Bias Temperature Instability in CFETs
authors: Narasimhulu Thoti, Hannu-Pekka Komsa
abstract: This work presents a comprehensive simulation-based study of negative bias temperature instability (NBTI) in p-type complementary FETs (p-CFETs) employing a hybrid modeling framework that integrates multistate configuration (MSC), activated barrier double well thermionic (ABDWT), and transient trap occupancy models (TTOMs) within Sentaurus TCAD. Emphasis is placed on hydrogen-related degradation mechanisms, including species transport, trap depassivation, and charge trapping kinetics at both the channel/SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> and SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>/HfO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> interfaces. The model captures the evolution of interface charge and trap occupancy as functions of gate stress voltage (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V}_{\text {gs}\text {tr}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>), temperature (T), and stress time (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text {t}_{\text {st}\text {r}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>). Simulated results reveal that hydrogen diffusion and trapping dynamics lead to spatially extended degradation, not limited to the immediate channel interface. The framework provides spatially resolved insight into reliability-limiting mechanisms in advanced 3-D CMOS platforms.

                    Heterojunction Field-Effect Transistors With n-p-n Back-to-Back Diode Gate Structure
authors: Taeeun Lee, Seungwon Son, Yusup Jung, Sinsu Kyoung, Sola Woo
abstract: The ultra-wide bandgap (UWBG) semiconductor material, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\beta }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3,</sub> is a promising next-generation power semiconductor. However, several challenges inhibit the practical use of NiO/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\beta }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> heterojunction field-effect transistors (HJFETs) under forward gate bias. This study proposes an n-p-n gate structure by introducing an <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$n$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-type <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\beta }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> gate blocking layer (GBL) on NiO to suppress forward gate leakage and enhance thermal stability. An optimized n-type <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\beta }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>GBL with a thickness of 30 nm and Si doping concentration of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" m<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\Omega } \boldsymbol {\cdot }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathrm{L}_{\text {GD}} =8$</tex-math> </inline-formula> <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\mu }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>m) and a breakdown voltage (BV) up to 2161 V (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathrm{L}_{\text {GD}} =16$</tex-math> </inline-formula> <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\mu }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>m). TCAD simulations yielded a power figure of merit (PFOM) of 107MW/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathrm{L}_{\text {GD}} =14$</tex-math> </inline-formula> <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\mu }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>m, confirming an optimal trade-off between conduction loss and breakdown capability. The <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${I}_{\text {G}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V}_{\text {GS}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> characteristics of the HJFET and NPN-HJFET measured at various temperatures show that the HJFET exhibits approximately two orders of magnitude higher gate leakage current than the NPN-HJFET at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V}_{\text {GS}} =2$</tex-math> </inline-formula> V, along with a turn-on voltage shift of −2.8 mV/K. Short-circuit (SC) simulations at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V}_{\text {DS}} =1800$</tex-math> </inline-formula> V and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V}_{\text {GS}} =8$</tex-math> </inline-formula> V revealed a temperature increase rate of 308 K per 100 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\mu }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>s, reaching 1200 K at 280 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\mu }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>s, ensuring sufficient response time for circuit protection. Simulation results for the NPN gate structure revealed sustained high impedance at high temperature during SC test, demonstrating superior gate stability compared with conventional JFETs.

title: Dual-modal Soft Magnetic Skin with Anti-magnetic Interference Structure for Tactile Perception
authors: Pengwen Xiong, Huan Peng, Yu Zhang, Longkun Yu, Aiguo Song, Peter X. Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Traditional magnetic tactile sensors often suffer from external magnetic field interference. To address this issue, we first theoretically derived, through mathematical formulations, the fundamental cause of magnetic field interference, providing a solid basis for interference mitigation. Building on this understanding, this work proposes and designs a dual-modal soft magnetic skin with the capabilities to mitigate magnetic field interference. Inspired by the sensory mechanism of human skin, the designed magnetic skin can simultaneously capture dual-modal information, namely magnetic and force tactile information across spatiotemporal domains. A Bi-Convolutional Neural Network–Multilayer Perceptron (Bi-CNN-MLP) is constructed to fuse the dual-modal tactile information. In order to reduce the impact of external magnetic field interference, a novel Dynamic Weighting Coefficient Layer (DWCL) is proposed, which dynamically assigns optimal weights to each modality based on real-time input characteristics. Specifically, by analyzing temporal discrepancies between modalities during pre-contact sensing and quantifying the magnetic field strength of target objects, the DWCL autonomously adjusts fusion ratios to prioritize the modality with higher reliability under varying interference conditions. Furthermore, extensive experimental evaluations from multiple perspectives demonstrate that the proposed electronic skin exhibits robust multi-scale perception capabilities, thereby enabling accurate and stable tactile sensing, while the DWCL achieves substantial improvements in interference resistance compared with traditional fusion strategies.

title: A Highly Scalable and Fault-Tolerant Topology for Data Center Networks
authors: Mengjie Lv, Wenjie Wan, Fu Xiao, Weibei Fan, Lei Han, Sun-Yuan Hsieh
abstract: As the demand for cloud services and data-intensive applications continues to surge, the design of efficient and reliable data center network (DCN) topologies has become increasingly critical. However, traditional DCNs often face challenges of limited scalability, insufficient fault tolerance, and high communication latency. To address these issues, we introduce SFDC, a novel recursive and modular server-centric network topology. SFDC is built on a hierarchical element-layer structure that enables the construction of highly scalable and fault-tolerant networks. The modular design of SFDC supports flexible expansion, allowing for the integration of servers with varying network interface card (NIC) configurations without requiring significant redesigns. Furthermore, SFDC’s design effectively mitigates the growth of network diameter, ensuring low latency even at massive scales. We also propose a routing algorithm, SFRouting, which leverages SFDC’s hierarchical structure to efficiently compute unicast paths, while minimizing routing complexity and enhancing data transmission efficiency. Additionally, we present a multipath routing scheme based on disjoint path construction, which ensures robust communication by providing alternative paths in case of node or link failures, thus enhancing network fault tolerance. Experimental results demonstrate that SFDC outperforms existing DCN topologies such as BCube, DCell, and HS-DCell, exhibiting superior scalability, reduced network diameter, and enhanced fault tolerance while maintaining low latency and stable performance.

title: On the SDP Relaxation of Direct Torque Finite Control Set Model Predictive Control
authors: Luca M. Hartmann, Orcun Karaca, Tinus Dorfling, Tobias Geyer, Adam Kurpisz
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: This brief article formulates a semidefinite programming (SDP) relaxation for a long-horizon direct-torque finite-control-set model predictive control (FCS-MPC) problem. In parallel with this relaxation, a conventional branch-and-bound algorithm tailored for the original problem, but with an iteration limit to restrict its computational burden, is also solved. An input sequence candidate is extracted from the solution of the semidefinite program in the lifted space. This sequence is then compared with the so-called early-stopping branch-and-bound solution, and the best of the two is applied in a receding horizon fashion. In simulated case studies, the proposed approach exhibits significant improvements in torque transients, as the branch-and-bound alone struggles to find a meaningful solution due to the imposed limit.

title: A Dual-polarized Antenna With 5×5 Solar Cell Array for 5G Green Communication
authors: Wenxing An, Chenxi Liu, Xiaoqing Tian, Yu Luo, Min Li, Yi Wu, Kaixue Ma
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: A 5×5 solar cell array is integrated with the dual-polarized antenna for 5G green communications. Based on the different responses of the inductor at DC and RF bands, surface-mounted inductors are introduced into the solar cell array for both DC connection and dual-polarized performance. With this configuration, a dual-polarized broadband antenna is realized with measured -10-dB bands from 3.02 GHz to 5.0 GHz and 3.03 GHz to 5.0 GHz. It has a high port isolation of 32.5 dB and a low profile of 0.12λ (where λ is the wavelength of 4.0 GHz). The measured peak gains are 10.9 dBi and 10.8 dBi for two polarizations. The proposed dual-polarized solar cell antenna can offer flexibility for integrating dual-polarized antennas and solar cells in next-generation green communication.

title: Cross-Domain Collaborative Stacked Intelligent Metasurface for 6G Wireless Communications
authors: Yifu Sun, Jingyu Chen, Kang An, Haotong Cao, Zhi Lin, Yonggang Zhu, Qiang Cheng, Mohsen Guizani, Symeon Chatzinotas
abstract: The sixth-generation (6G) wireless communications aim to achieve wide coverage, ubiquitous connectivity, and ultra-reliable low-latency transmission by unleashing the potentials of cross-domain collaboration in space, semantics, and polarization. Recently, stacked intelligent metasurface (SIM) has emerged as a revolutionary paradigm for enabling multi-domain communication functionalities, i.e., offering high-degree-of-freedom (DoF) spatial beamforming, parallel light-speed semantic computing, and flexible polarization manipulation with low hardware cost and high scalability. However, the existing SIM-related works merely focus on single-domain manipulation, while the potentials of SIM for cross-domain collaboration remain unexploited in 6G wireless networks. To this end, we propose a cross-domain collaborative SIM (C-SIM), where the above capabilities in spatial, semantic, and polarized domains are seamlessly integrated to promotes a significant performance gain for 6G wireless networks. This paper provides a comprehensive overview of C-SIM, encompassing its preliminaries, architecture, and potential benefits. Moreover, we discuss the cutting-edge applications and identify open challenge associated with enabling C-SIM. Finally, a case study and numerical results are presented to quantify the benefits of C-SIM for 6G wireless networks.

title: A Fast and Adaptive LSTM-based Surrogate Model for Predicting Limitation Performance of SFCLs in Electric Aircraft Systems
authors: Yizhao Huang, Mohammad Yazdani-Asrami, Wenjuan Song
abstract: Superconducting fault current limiter (SFCL) is a promising device for improving the reliability, safety, and stability of electric aircraft by quickly and effectively limiting fault currents. There is a need to build a surrogate model that can fast and accurately predict the dynamic output of SFCL performance for aircraft system level studies as well as for developing digital twins. In this work, an electrothermal coupled finite element model of the SFCL was established to obtain the dataset during different fault scenarios. Then, a surrogate model is constructed using a long short-term memory (LSTM) network to predict the temperature, current, voltage, and resistance dynamic behaviors of SFCLs under faults. The results show that the proposed LSTM model can quickly predict the dynamic waveforms of SFCLs with different tape and fault parameters. The accuracy of prediction of the LSTM surrogate model is above 97%, and the relative error of the waveform predictions is lower than 5%.

title: STA-UNet: Lightweight Spectral Segmentation for Mowing Robots_supp1-3673901.mp4
authors: JensenFeng deng
abstract: Achieving robust semantic segmentation for autonomous mowing robots requires discriminating between materials with subtle visual differences under challenging outdoor conditions. To address this, we present a novel framework that co-designs feature-specific spectral imaging and a lightweight deep learning network. On the sensing front, we develop a low-cost multispectral camera using a custom multi-band-pass (MBP) filter, engineered to capture distinctive spectral signatures of key classes like grass and shrubs, enabling parallax-free and compact data acquisition. For efficient inference, we propose STA-UNet, a lightweight encoder-decoder network that innovatively incorporates a Spectral Attention (SA) module to adaptively fuse the acquired spectral bands, and a Cross-Scale Texture Perception (CSTP) module to enhance spatial feature representation. Trained on Grass8K—a new 8,191-image multispectral dataset for lawn environments—STA-UNet achieves a remarkable mIoU of 88.63%, outperforming a standard U-Net by 7.11% while utilizing only one-quarter of the parameters. The system achieves real-time segmentation on an edge device with 5 TOPS of computing power, demonstrating a practical, high-performance perception solution for resource-constrained robotic applications. The dataset and code are made available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/</uri> 1g1dzyc-Rn6iUWGGvIbnRr wRKUn2bic4?usp=drive link

title: Crowdsourced Delivery Problem With Couriers&#x2019; Preferences: A Data-Driven Method With Logic-Based Benders Decomposition_supp1-3640137.docx
authors: Jiage Huo
abstract: With the rapid development of food delivery platforms, crowdsourced delivery mode has become popular due to its cost-effectiveness and flexibility. The crowdsourced couriers can choose to accept or reject orders dispatched from the platform. Such behavior not only affects the income of the couriers and the customer service quality, but also brings about negative impacts on the platform’s operational efficiency and its reputation. It is essential to analyze the preferences of the couriers so that the platform can develop a “courier-friendly” and high efficient delivery dispatching strategy. Therefore, our study first uses a real-world food delivery dataset to identify the factors that influence crowdsourced couriers’ willingness to accept orders. In particular, we apply an extreme gradient boosting tree (XGBoost) to forecast the likelihood of the crowdsourced couriers accepting the delivery tasks. We further integrate the likelihood of order acceptance into a mixed-integer programming model to optimize the dispatching decisions for the platform. A multi-criteria objective function is proposed aimed at making a trade-off between the labor cost, service quality as well as couriers’ welfare. To solve the problem, a logic-based Benders decomposition algorithm is proposed, and an efficient analytical cut is developed to accelerate the algorithm. The numerical results verify that the proposed solution approach has significant advantages in terms of computational efficiency and solution quality compared to the off-the-shelf solver. Moreover, it is identified that, for on-demand delivery platforms, integrating courier preference information into the courier dispatch system is a vital strategy for achieving more efficient and cost-effective operations, as well as an important pathway to achieve social sustainable operations.

title: Monolithic integration of silicon microlenses in LWIR for narrow field - of- view TMOS temperature detectors
authors: Francesca Grossi, Silvia Nicoli, Mikel Azpeitia Urquia, Giuseppe Bruno, Lorenzo Tentori, Andrea Melloni, Francesco Morichetti
abstract: This paper proposes a novel architecture for a wireless temperature sensor based on TMOS technology operating in the long-wavelength infrared (LWIR) range, which is suitable for low-power applications. To reduce the field of view (FOV) of the sensor and increase its sensitivity, a full silicon microlens array has been integrated on the external side of the silicon top cap of the MEMS architecture as a wafer-level packaging solution. In addition, an anti-reflective coating (ARC) is deposited on the inner surface of the silicon cap to reduce reflections at the interface between silicon and air. The performance enhancement provided by the proposed approach is validated through sensitivity and FOV measurements carried out on some prototypical devices and supported by numerical simulations. A FOV reduction down to a 30% with respect to a reference device with 4x active subpixels is demonstrated, with no sensitivity degradation, thanks to an optical intensity gain provided by the combined effects of the microlens array and the ARC.

title: AoI Minimization in Heterogeneous MEC Networks: a Federated Learning-Assisted Hybrid DRL and Convex Approach
authors: Xiaoying Liu, Junhao Zheng, Kechen Zheng, Jia Liu, Tarik Taleb, Norio Shiratori
abstract: This paper investigates a dynamic heterogeneous mobile edge computing network (HMECN), where mobile devices (MDs) could offload their full tasks to a small base station (SBS) directly or the macro base station (MBS) in direct or relay mode. As age of information (AoI) is a comprehensive and accurate metric to capture the freshness of computation results, we formulate a long-term weighted sum AoI (LWSA) minimization problem in the HMECN by jointly optimizing the offloading decisions of MDs as well as the bandwidth and computation resource allocation of all base stations, subject to energy, delay and peak AoI constraints. To address the formulated non-convex mixed integer nonlinear programming problem, we decompose it into the offloading decision optimization (ODO) top-problem and the resource allocation optimization (RAO) sub-problem. Based on the decomposition, we propose a federated learning (FL)-assisted hybrid DRL and convex approach that is comprised of a safe multi-agent DRL algorithm, convex optimization and FL. The ODO top-problem is solved by the safe multi-agent DRL algorithm, which strictly ensures that the actions of each agent do not exceed its energy constraint and then paves the way for using convex optimization to solve the RAO sub-problem. FL is used to alleviate the training instability problem aggravated by multi agent settings via breaking the limitation of partial knowledge for each individual agent. Simulation results demonstrate the superiority of the proposed approach in terms of the LWSA, convergence, scalability and robustness in dynamic environments.

title: Spatial Short-Term Fourier Transform Based Single-Channel Single-Fiber 3D Shape Sensing_supp1-3674026.mp4
authors: Danqian Cao
abstract: Accurate three-dimensional (3D) shape sensing is vital for continuum robots in minimally invasive surgery. Conventional optical fiber methods depend on multi-fiber or multicore configurations, increasing integration complexity and associated costs. Single-fiber approaches support miniaturization, but struggle to decouple 3D bending and twist. We propose a single-channel single-fiber framework based on the spatial short-term Fourier transform (SSTFT) for real-time 3D reconstruction. A helically wrapped fiber encodes multiple deformation modes into periodic strain patterns, which localized Fourier domain analysis converts into curvature, direction, and twist parameters. These parameters feed a piecewise constant curvature and torsion model to efficiently reconstruct the backbone. Experiments on a 1.45 m sensor achieve average shape errors of 2.15 % (bending), 5.32 % (3D helix), and 7.90 % (twist). Compared to multi-fiber Frenet–Serret methods, our approach improves accuracy and robustness while reducing system complexity, demonstrating a promising low-cost, miniaturized shape sensing approach with potential applications in surgical navigation.

title: DeepMapping++: Balancing Efficiency and Storage for Lossless Lookup and Approximate Range Aggregation
authors: Hong Guan, Lixi Zhou, K. Selçuk Candan, Jia Zou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Efficient storage and query processing over tabular data, while balancing storage cost, query latency, and memory footprint, remains a fundamental challenge in the database community. In this work, we propose DeepMapping++, a neural-based data representation that leverages the memorization capability of deep neural networks to support efficient query processing in resource-constrained environments. DeepMapping++ has two flavors: DeepMapping-L for lossless look-up queries on categorical data and DeepMapping-R for approximate range aggregation queries on numerical data. To efficiently handle data modifications, DeepMapping-L integrates a lightweight auxiliary structure to correct prediction errors and support data modification operations, including insertions, deletions, and updates. DeepMapping-R further incorporates a buffer structure for caching partially aggregated values to reduce the need for model retraining. Experiments on real-world, synthetic, and benchmark datasets, demonstrated the effectiveness of DeepMapping-L and DeepMapping-R.

title: Adaptive Cell Orientation Control With Slip Compensation and Attentive Vision_supp1-3673946.mp4
authors: Changsheng Dai
abstract: Precise orientation of oocyte is a prerequisite for many cell manipulations, especially Intracytoplasmic Sperm Injection (ICSI), to prevent damage to the meiotic spindle. However, automating this process presents significant challenges due to the visual ambiguity of the polar body and the complex friction dynamics between the micropipette and the cell. In this letter, we propose a robotic system for high-precision oocyte rotation. First, we introduce a YOLO11-based detector enhanced with a high-resolution branch P2 and CBAM (Convolutional Block Attention Module) attention, achieving robust polar body detection (mAP@50 = 96.7% on 1028 images). Second, we develop a discrete-contour mechanics model that estimates minimum rotational torque for cell geometries, replacing traditional circle/ellipse assumptions. Finally, we design a dual-loop control framework that combines slip-compensated rotation with adaptive indentation regulation, enabling real-time compensation for biological variability of cells. Experiments on mouse oocytes show 0.51° orientation error with a maximum deformation (2.58 μm), outperforming PID-based baselines.

title: Phase-Locked Loop-based Strategy for Accurate Blanking Time Allocation around Zero Voltage Crossings in Bridgeless PFCs
authors: Alberto Pigazo, Paula Lamo, Francisco J. Azcondo, Christian Brañas
abstract: The totem-pole bridgeless PFC is becoming a de facto standard in high-end power supply designs due to the efficiency and power density it achieves. However, bridgeless PFCs distort the line current near the grid voltage zero crossings (ZC) because of gate delays, parasitic capacitances, and abrupt switch changes. Various strategies have been proposed to mitigate this issue, such as introducing blanking times for Si/SiC MOSFETs before the ZC and/or implementing soft-start procedures for GaN HEMTs after the ZC. These around-ZC operating states are typically triggered by detecting a predefined voltage threshold, which performs adequately under ideal conditions. However, distorted grid voltages and noisy measurements lead to imprecise and inconsistent triggering and duration of these around-ZC states. This work proposes leveraging the phase-locked loop (PLL), already used for grid synchronization, by utilizing its grid frequency and phase estimations to minimize the variability of the allocation and duration of the blanking times. Since the proposed strategy relies on grid frequency and phase estimations, it is independent of the employed current control approach and the specific power semiconductor technology. The theoretical analysis evaluates the sensitivity of blanking times to voltage and frequency variations for both the existing and proposed methods. The proposed method is analyzed and validated through simulation and experimentally on a 2.5kW GaN-based commercial prototype. The proposed strategy reduces the standard deviation of blanking time allocation under harmonic distortion, interharmonics, subharmonics, voltage sags, frequency dynamics, and additive voltage sensing noise, while preserving the overall power quality and efficiency performance of the converter.

title: Computational Modeling for Cardiac Current Localization using Sequence-Based Nelder-Mead Technique in Magnetocardiography
authors: K V Anu, B S Krisha, Vikas R Bhat, H Anitha
abstract: Background: Magnetocardiography (MCG) is a non-invasive technique that measures magnetic fields generated by cardiac electrical activity, offering superior spatial resolution compared to traditional electrocardiography (ECG) for localizing arrhythmic sources. Accurate dipole source localization remains a critical challenge in cardiac electrophysiology. Methods: This work developed a comprehensive computational framework integrating the Boundary Element Method (BEM) for forward electromagnetic modeling with the Nelder-Mead simplex algorithm for inverse source optimization. A realistic cardiac geometry was formed by extracting a standard dataset from ECGSIM software and strategically selected 16 nodes. An experimentally acquired 6 Hz pulse signal was used as the temporal pattern for current dipole sources; appropriate amplitude scaling was applied in the implementation to achieve physiologically realistic magnetic field strengths of 10–100 pT consistent with clinical MCG measurements. Magnetic field distributions were computed at 25 sensor grid locations configured in a planar array. Forward field calculations are performed using the Biot-Savart law, while the inverse problem minimized the squared error between measured and simulated magnetic fields by iterative optimization of the dipole position. Results: Proposed model achieves sub-millimeter localization precision across multiple validation scenarios. Comprehensive testing in 16 locations yields a mean localization error of 0.715 ± 0.192 mm (range: 0.318–0.968 mm), which with algorithmic convergence was achieved in 15-46 iterations. Under noise-free conditions, optimization framework reliably converged to solutions meeting the clinically relevant 1 mm accuracy criterion. Noise analysis using signal-to-noise ratios from -10 to 20 dB shown clinically acceptable performance (< 5 mm error) at SNR ≥ 5dB, with mean errors of 0.949 mm at 20 dB and 3.025 mm at 10 dB representing typical shielded and unshielded clinical environments. Comparative analysis against Levenberg-Marquardt, Genetic Algorithm, and Particle Swarm Optimization methods confirmed Nelder-Mead’s superior combination of localization accuracy (0.715 mm vs. 2.984-76.517 mm for other existing methods) and 100% convergence reliability across all test configurations. Conclusions: This BEM-Nelder-Mead framework shows reliable cardiac dipole localization with acceptable accuracy. MATLAB implementation offers reproducibility and adaptability for various cardiac source configurations, demonstrating potential for non-invasive arrhythmia diagnosis and therapeutic guidance.

title: KMS-Net: Kolmogorov–Arnold Based Multi-Scale Attention Network for Cardiac Segmentation
authors: Hassan Ali, Abid Mehmood, David Noule Tolno, S Sery Gahouidi Thierry, Muhammad Saeed, Naeem Ahmed
abstract: Accurate segmentation of cardiac structures in 2D echocardiography is crucial for identifying heart disease and measuring functional metrics like chamber sizes and ejection fraction. Conventional U-Net models are good in extracting local features but face difficulties when dealing with long-range dependencies in noisy ultrasound images, whereas pure Transformers capture global context at the cost of fine boundary detail.We propose KMT-Net, a novel hybrid architecture that effectively combines Kolmogorov–Arnold Networks (KANs) with modern multi-scale attention mechanisms. Spline-based KAN layers (grid size 7) provide highly expressive nonlinear modeling, residual convolutional blocks incorporate Efficient Multi-Scale Attention (EMA), an enhanced KAN-based Atrous Spatial Pyramid Pooling with Squeeze-and-Excitation (KASPPS) aggregates rich multi-scale context, Selective State-Space (SS2D) layers enable efficient long-range spatial modeling, and Multi-Scale Attention Gates (MSAG) refine skip connections for precise boundary delin- eation.On the CAMUS dataset (500 patients, 2CH/4CH views at ED/ES), KMT-Net achieves mean Dice scores of 0.9417 and 0.9241 (2CH) and 0.9518 and 0.9328 (4CH), clearly outperforming U-Net variants, TransUNet, Swin-UNet, U-Mamba, and recent KAN-based models.
