It attains state-of-the-art performance on the ACDC MRI dataset (mean Dice 92.65%, HD95 1.08 mm) and exhibits strong zero-shot generalization to the EchoNet-Dynamic echocardiography dataset, confirming its capability to robustly manage both local details and global context across various imaging modalities and clinical environments.The code of KMS-Net is publicly available at: https://github.com/Hassan48khan/ KMS-Net-Kolmogorov-Arnold-Based-Multi-Scale-Attention-Network-for-Cardiac-Image-Segmentation.

title: Design of Direct-Drive Arc Motors for Wheelchair Application with Modular Stators
authors: Feifan Ni, Zhenghao Li, Mingjin Hu, Sigao Wang, Shuangxia Niu, K. T. Chau
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics

title: An Adaptive Variable-Frequency ZVS Control Strategy for Four-Switch Buck-Boost Converter in Low-Voltage and High-Current Applications
authors: Xinyu Yin, Jianan Guan, Lei Gao, Fangang Meng
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: To address the loss of the ability to maintain zero-voltage switching (ZVS) in low-voltage, high-current four-switch buck-boost (FSBB) converters, this paper proposes an adaptive variable-frequency control strategy. From the perspective of extending the ZVS range, a frequency variation trajectory is proposed. Next, power inductor design principles are presented to address the filter design challenges arising from the wide operating frequency range. Finally, control variables are decoupled and DSP memory footprint is minimized through a 2D lookup table (LUT) combined with a PI feedback controller. Experimental validation on a 500 W FSBB converter prototype demonstrates full-load range ZVS maintenance at a 40 A output current with the peak efficiency reaching 98.528%.

title: Standards Activities
authors: N/A
abstract: Over the winter, our standards working groups have been, well, steadily working toward bringing more standards and updates near completion.

title: EMT Model Boundary Determination Using Floquet Theory-based Participation Factors
authors: Mahsa Sajjadi, Kai Sun, Deepak Ramasubramanian
abstract: To accelerate electromagnetic transient (EMT) simulations for power systems with inverter-based resources (IBRs), this paper proposes a new approach for determining the EMT model boundary using Floquet theory-based participation factors (PFs). The approach can significantly reduce the computational burden of EMT simulation to focus on a localized area whose components highly participate in the dynamics of interest such as sub-synchronous oscillations (SSOs), while the rest of the grid is represented using a simple Norton equivalent. Floquet theory enables the calculation of participation factors over a single cycle of the system in an EMT model, where the vector field exhibits periodic behavior around the synchronous frequency. A data-driven method is also proposed for estimating participation factors directly from the system's responses. The proposed EMT model boundary determination approach is first demonstrated on a two-area system and then validated on a 39-bus system with multiple IBRs.

title: Suppressing the Impact of Laser Frequency Drift on φ-OTDR Vibration Detection Based on Improved Variational Mode Decomposition and Its Influencing Factors Investigation
authors: Lijuan Zhao, Zimeng Huang, Zhiniu Xu, Xuzhe Zhang, Ziming Hua
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: To address the impact of laser frequency drift on measured vibration using phase-sensitive optical time-domain reflectometry (φ-OTDR) systems, an improved variational mode decomposition (VMD) method is proposed in this work to suppress the effects of frequency drift. Based on the Rayleigh backscattering (RBS) signal acquired from vibrating optical fibers, the influence of two key parameters of the VMD method on the demodulated waveform is investigated: the penalty factor α and the number of intrinsic mode functions k. The results demonstrate that the VMD method can effectively suppress the influence of frequency drift on demodulation. Optimal values of α and k are determined, resulting in a computation time that is only 0.06% to 0.08% of that of the WOA-VMD method, while preserving comparable accuracy. This approach substantially improves the real-time performance by eliminating the need for iterative optimization of α and k. The improved VMD method is realized by applying twice differential (TD) preprocessing to the phase signal after IQ demodulation and phase unwrapping, using fixed optimal parameters set of α and k, and identifying and removing drift-dominated intrinsic mode functions (IMFs) based on their frequency characteristics. Furthermore, the improved VMD method is systematically validated using experimentally acquired RBS signals under different vibration frequencies, vibration types, pulse repetition rates, optical pulse widths, and the sampling duration. Simultaneously, a high-fidelity numerical simulation model of coherent φ-OTDR is employed to investigate the impact of frequency drift rate and frequency drift type on the demodulated waveforms. The results reveal that the improved VMD method can effectively suppress frequency drift effects under different conditions, including different vibration frequencies, vibration types, optical pulse repetition rates, optical pulse widths, sampling duration, frequency drift rates, and frequency drift types. Compared with the original demodulated waveforms, the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) is improved by 22.56dB to 56.57 dB, demonstrating a wide range of applicability. Moreover, the accuracy of the improved VMD method increases with increasing sampling duration and optical pulse width. In addition, the SNR improvement relative to the original demodulated waveforms increases with increasing pulse repetition rate, optical pulse width, sampling duration, and frequency drift rate. Therefore, the proposed improved VMD method demonstrates high stability under various experimental conditions and can effectively suppress the impact of laser frequency drift on φ-OTDR demodulation.

title: Comparative Embedded Frequency Estimation Based on Digital PLL and Extended Kalman Filtering under Standardized Grid Conditions
authors: Simone Mari, Federico Centi, Andrea Fioravanti, Edoardo Fiorucci, Giovanni Bucci, Fabrizio Ciancetta, Andrea Credo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: This paper presents an experimental comparison between a digital phase-locked loop (PLL) and an extended Kalman filter (EKF) implemented as frequency estimators on identical embedded hardware platforms. Both algorithms were deployed on STM32-based microcontrollers and evaluated in real time using a calibrated electrical power standard as reference source. The experimental campaign followed test conditions inspired by IEC/IEEE 60255-118-1, including off-nominal steady-state frequencies, voltage amplitude variations, controlled harmonic distortion, and dynamic frequency steps. A systematic tuning procedure was adopted to ensure comparable steady-state noise levels for the two estimators, enabling a fair assessment of their metrological and implementation-related characteristics. The comparison jointly considers steady-state accuracy, statistical dispersion, dynamic response, and computational cost on the embedded target. The presented results provide quantitative insight into the practical trade-offs associated with PLL- and EKF-based frequency estimation under realistic grid conditions.

title: Channel Assignment for A Polar Code-Based Semantic Communication System
authors: Zhixiang Qiao, Yao Sun, Kairong Ma, Yixuan Fan, Runze Cheng, Chengsi Liang, Lei Zhang, Muhammad Ali Imran
abstract: Semantic communication (SemCom) has been deemed as a promising paradigm focusing on semantic meaning rather than raw data for efficient and robust information transmission. While machine learning (ML) has advanced semantic processing capabilities, practical SemCom system deployment requires to achieve a high utilization of wireless channels. Traditional channel assignment schemes operate independently of semantic content, i.e., all transmitted bits are treated equally, leading to inadequate protection of important semantic information and a high semantic error rate under adverse channel conditions. In this paper, we propose a channel assignment approach for polar code-based SemCom that aligns channel polarization with semantic importance. First, to accurately evaluate semantic importance, gaze-inspired attention modeling is employed to calculate the semantic contribution of image regions, together with the consideration of semantic correlation between entities. Then, based on the calculated semantic importance and reliability of the polarized channel, we formulate the polarized channel assignment as a 0-1 programming problem and propose a solution containing two assignment strategies for different transmission scenarios. The simulation results demonstrate that, compared to traditional polarized channel assignment approachs, our approach improves semantic transmission efficiency and robustness by effectively matching semantic entities with appropriate channel resources.

title: Wideband Circularly Polarized Roof-Glass Antenna for Vehicular GNSS Applications
authors: Zhipeng Zhang, Wenhai Zhang, Jiaqian Chen, Keying Huang, Xueguan Liu, Yilin Zheng, Zhixing Chen, Chi-Hou Chio, Zhuowei Zhang, Kam-Weng Tam, Quan Xue
abstract: In recent years, the panoramic sunroof becomes a popular choice for intelligent connected vehicles, thereby imposing the innovative vehicle antenna design with the large-area glass roof. This work proposes a novel circularly polarized coplanar waveguide (CPW)-fed antenna integrated into the vehicle's roof glass as a satellite navigation antenna, to meet the practical requirements of conformal vehicle designs. The antenna is initially implemented on a 100 mm × 100 mm single-layer glass substrate, achieving wideband characteristics through the synergistic integration of an asymmetric ground plane, a T-shaped feedline, an L-shaped strip, a U-shaped grounded strip, and symmetrically loaded stubs. The measured -10-dB impedance bandwidth of the proposed antenna is 52.90% (1.14-1.96 GHz) and 3-dB axial ratio (AR) bandwidth is 47.35% (1.08-1.75 GHz), with measured right-hand circular polarization (RHCP) gains exceeding 2.6 dBic across 1.16-1.61 GHz. Then, to enhance the environmental adaptability of the navigation antenna for diverse vehicular roof glass applications, the substrate is strategically reconfigured to 300 mm × 300 mm single/double-layer glass architectures. Through meticulous dimensional optimization, the operational bandwidth fully encompasses all navigation frequency bands. Next, the surface wave suppression is also demonstrated to improve the radiation pattern distortion by seamlessly integrating a coplanar frame structure on the antenna plane within the vehicle roof glass blackout border region. Finally, antenna prototypes are designed and fabricated to prove the concept. Measurements confirm an impedance bandwidth of 36.04% (1.16-1.67 GHz), AR bandwidth of 34.29% (1.16-1.64 GHz), and boresight RHCP gains exceeding 2 dBic in both xoz and yoz principal planes at B3/L1 navigation bands, meeting stringent automotive Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) antenna requirements. Furthermore, the developed antenna is connected to a GNSS receiver which tracks up to 59 satellites, with 50 of them exhibiting C/N₀ better than 35 dB-Hz, fulfilling the technical requirements for reliable positioning in intelligent connected vehicles.

title: Multimodal Detection of Alcohol-Impaired Driving Using Time-Series Classification in a High-Fidelity Simulator
authors: Jan-Philipp Göbel, Jan Cedric Mertens, Thomas Kundinger, Andreas Riener
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This study introduces a novel machine learning framework for detecting alcohol-induced driving impairment based on multimodal behavioral signals collected in a high-fidelity driving simulator. With 120 participants, the largest sample size in this field to date, the experiment captured both vehicle dynamics and eye-tracking data under sober and intoxicated conditions. To preserve temporal dependencies, a MiniRocket time-series classifier was employed, outperforming conventional feature-based models. A systematic signal-wise analysis identified steering behavior and drowsiness as the most informative indicators. Signal combinations further enhanced classification performance, achieving up to 85.2 % accuracy on unseen test data. While scenario-specific models (urban, rural, highway) provided only marginal improvements, a time window analysis revealed that just three minutes of driving data were sufficient for reliable detection. As the first study to integrate gaze and driving behavior in a scalable, signal-based approach, this work demonstrates the practical potential of multimodal time-series classification for real-time, in-vehicle driver monitoring.

title: RailSAM: Taming SAM with Adapter for Railway Segmentation
authors: Wenze Zhang, Chunmian Lin, Daxin Tian, Xuting Duan, Jianshan Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: The emergence of vision foundation model, such as the Segment Anything Model (SAM), has brought groundbreaking advancements to downstream tasks due to its zero-shot transfer capability. In this work, RailSAM is proposed by incorporating the SAM generalist architecture with domain-specific adapter for railway segmentation. By leveraging the pretrained knowledge from the frozen SAM encoder, we seamlessly inject domain-specific information through visual prompts into the mask decoder via carefully designed adapters. On the publicly available RailSem19 dataset, our experiments demonstrate that RailSAM significantly outperforms existing task-specific methods and exhibits remarkable robustness under challenging conditions. We hope this work would inspire in-depth exploration of vision foundation model for intelligent railway transportation. The code will be open-source soon.

title: Enhancing Autonomous Vehicle Test Scenario Reasoning in Language Models
authors: Denise Ataei, Ishaan Paranjape, Jim Whitehead
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Scenario-based testing is a promising approach to evaluate autonomous vehicles for safety due to its ability to evaluate several components at once. Automated generation of these scenarios in simulation is needed to address the scale and diversity requirements in scenarios. Large Language Models (LLMs) can address this need due to their ability for world modeling. However, these models are ineffective in their reasoning thereby limiting their abilities in generating complex, dynamic vehicle interaction scenarios. In this paper, we present Cruzway Scenario Reasoner, an LLM based system that enhances reasoning capabilities of language models for complex vehicle interaction questions from the Waymo Open Motion Dataset Reasoning dataset. This system consists of a suite of prompting approaches which include both Chain of Thought prompting as well as prompting based on model-based task planning in the Planning Domain Definition Language (PDDL). In addition, this system contains LLM as a judge modules for the effective evaluation of generated responses and PDDL models. With this system, we are able to elevate the reasoning capabilities of the OpenAI GPT 4o-mini model. We also provide detailed qualitative analysis of the language model responses to 15 scenarios categorized by complexity in the information provided.

title: An Explainable Hybrid Transformer-Based Lightweight Framework for Lung Cancer Classification
authors: Sazibul Islam Siam, Md. Saiful Islam, Krishna Das
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Lung cancer is one of the deadliest causes of cancer related diseases which actually demands more high accurate diagnosis. The way histopathological analysis is diagnosing around the world is time consuming, also not that much of accurate detecting the cancer, on the other hand Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) struggling so hard to cope with its long-range dependencies. For this instance, Vision Transformers (ViTs) are doing very well in terms of computational operations. MBv3-ViT, a lightweight hybrid architecture has been introduced in this paper for lung cancer classification from histopathological image. This model implements the computational efficiency of MobileNetV3 for especially robust local feature extraction with the help of global reasoning of a ViTs. The MobileNetV3 generates a feature with a 7x7x576 map, which is also projected to a 768-dimensional token sequence. Then it is processed through an 8-block ViT encoder. 15,000 images from LC25000 have been trained by us. We have trained encompassing benign, adenocarcinoma also the squamous cell carcinoma tissues. 99.80% accuracy and a 99.80% F1-Score has been achieved by the proposed MBv3-ViT model which also demonstrated the excellent performance. On the other hand, the benchmark models like LEViT, CoAtNet, and HorNet are significantly outperforming. Also, Grade-CAM has been integrated for Explainable AI(XAI), increasing the model trustworthiness. For assisting the pathologist in clinical practices, the high accuracy, lightweight design, and interpretability of MBv3-ViT creates its potential as a valuable and useful tool.

title: 3D Printed Bonds for Mmwave Interconnects: A Low-Complexity, Low-Loss Solution
authors: Sumin David Joseph, Edward A Ball, Jeff Kettle, Jonathon Harwell
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This work addresses the limitations of traditional bondwires in millimeter-wave (mmWave) chip-to-PCB interconnections, particularly at high frequencies such as those in the E-band. The inherent inductance of bondwires significantly impairs impedance matching and contributes to elevated insertion loss as frequency increases. For instance, a single bondwire with a length of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$480 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex> exhibits poor performance, with a return loss (S11) of -2 dB and an insertion loss (S21) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{- 4. 6 ~ d B}$</tex> at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 3 ~ G H z}$</tex>. While using multiple bondwires can offer marginal improvement, the performance remains suboptimal for high-frequency applications. To overcome these limitations, we propose the use of XTPL's Ultra-Precise Dispensing (UPD) technology for forming 3D-printed interconnects. These printed microstructures demonstrate significantly improved RF performance. At 73 GHz, a dual printed bond achieves an insertion loss better than 1 dB and a return loss better than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 ~ d B}$</tex>, highlighting its potential as a superior alternative to traditional wire bonding in mmWave system integration.

title: Mechanical Model and Failure Prediction of Aircraft Ground Towing Stability
authors: Yang Nanshan, Yu Jinhai, Li Wenwu
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Aircraft ground traction is a key link in airport operation, but under some unfavorable working conditions, accidents such as skidding or fuselage rollover may occur, resulting in serious safety risks and economic losses. Aiming at the problem that the current traction operation relies on experience and lacks quantitative risk assessment, a hybrid framework of theoretical mechanics and -PHM is proposed, and a traction instability prediction model covering six-dimensional parameter space is established, which can accurately quantify the influence of speed, acceleration, weight, center of gravity, friction coefficient and slope on traction stability and predict critical instability conditions. Then, through parameter sensitivity analysis, the influence law of speed, acceleration, friction and slope on failure risk is revealed. This study provides an innovative PHM solution for aircraft traction safety. By defining the physical criteria of critical failure modes, physical quantities are transformed into PHM health indicators to support risk prediction and decision optimization.

title: Strategic and Tactical Planning for Electric Connected and Automated Shuttle Buses for Public Transport Service
authors: A. Bozzi, C. Pasquale, S. Siri, A. Ferrara
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This work presents a two-level optimization framework for the execution of public transport service with electric connected and automated shuttle buses. This work considers two levels of planning: the strategic problem determines the minimum fleet size, considering an average transport demand and minimizing under-utilized travel; the tactical problem, based on the shuttle bus fleet defined at the strategic level, determines the assignment of shuttle buses to transport services by incorporating detailed routing constraints and energy dynamics across the network. Both problems are formulated as integer linear programming problems and solved using MATLAB Optimization Toolbox. A case study based on a realistic network scenario evaluates the performance of the framework. The results demonstrate how the two-layer approach enables effective fleet sizing, while the tactical solution provides task allocation by ensuring energy feasibility and operational efficiency.

title: 3-Watt Compact X-Band GaN HEMT Front End MMIC
authors: Pritam Kumar Sinha, Akhilendra Krishna, Rakhi Kumari, Poonam Singh, Umakant Goyal, Sangam Bhalke, Devendra Verma, Meena Mishra
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: The Aim of this work is to design & develop a compact X-Band Front End Chip in the frequency range of 8.5 GHz to 10.5 GHz and fabricated using 250 nm GaN HEMT on SiC process of UMS, France. The very compact MMIC die occupying only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.2\ \text{mm} \times 2.1$</tex> mm integrates a high efficiency power amplifier (PA), a low noise amplifier and SPDT switch. The fabricated TR chip has an output power of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>34.8\ \text{dB}$</tex>, a high gain of 29 dB with a flatness of 0.3 dB and efficiency in the range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$20 \%-24 \%$</tex> in the Tx chain. In the receive chain the gain of 22.8 with a flatness of 1.6 dB and maximum noise figure is 2.5 dB over the band.

title: TGDT: A Temporal Graph-based Digital Twin for Urban Traffic Corridors
authors: Nooshin Yousefzadeh, Rahul Sengupta, Jeremy Dilmore, Sanjay Ranka
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Traffic congestion along urban corridors formed by sequences of signalized intersections results in delays, economic losses, and increased emissions. Existing deep learning models for arterial traffic control lack spatial generalizability, rely on complex architectures, or struggle with real-time deployment. We propose the Temporal Graph-based Digital Twin (TGDT), a scalable framework that combines Temporal Convolutional Networks and Attentional Graph Neural Networks to dynamically model and assess corridor-wide traffic. TGDT estimates key Measures of Effectiveness (MOEs) at both the intersection level (e.g., queue length, waiting time) and the corridor level (e.g., volume, travel time). Its modular design supports any number of intersections and MOEs, delivering accurate, concurrent multioutput predictions. TGDT outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, remains robust under diverse traffic conditions, and requires only minimal input features. Fully parallelized, it can simulate thousands of scenarios in seconds, providing a cost-effective, interpretable, and real-time tool for optimizing urban traffic flow.

title: Generation of OAM Beam using a Uniform Circular Array of Aperture Coupled Patches
authors: Utsav Giri, Satyajit Chakrabarti, Madhurima Ghosh, Subhanita Saha, Vivek Kumar Singh, P H Rao
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This manuscript presents a uniform circular array (UCA) of aperture coupled rectangular microstrip patch antennas for generating the orbital angular momentum (OAM) beam at C-band. The UCA generates the OAM beam of mode <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{l} =+1$</tex> or -1. The UCA consists of 8 identical elements. The unit element is designed to work at the 7 GHz. Aperture coupled microstrip patch UCA design provides better mode purity and improved isolation between the feed and radiating layer. The measured impedance bandwidth of the antenna is wider than 56.8 %. The antenna exhibits gain of about 10 dBi and beam divergence of about 20 degree.

title: MFE-Driver: Multimodal Fusion Network for EEG-based Driver Emotion State Recognition
authors: Yongqiang Ma, Haodong Jing, Wenjie Gao, Haibo Hua, Xuetao Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: In recent years, the impact of drivers' negative emotions on driving performance and the safety of intelligent transportation systems has been of increasing concern, the emotional fluctuations, especially negative emotion can influence driving behavior, reaction time, and decision-making. EEG signals are widely used for emotion analysis due to their high temporal resolution, but there are limited studies on EEG-based driver emotion recognition and behavior prediction, especially in real driving scenarios. Most of the existing studies either focus on a single modality or lack efficient fusion of heterogeneous multimodal information. To address these challenges, we propose MFE-Driver, a novel multimodal fusion network that integrates EEG signals with behavioral and expression information for comprehensive driver emotion classification and behavior prediction. MFE-Driver is designed with a feature processing module for extracting task-specific representations, a feature enhancement module for aligning multimodal features, and a fine-grained feature fusion module for effective multimodal learning. Its multi-task learning strategy enables the model to capture complementary information across modalities, thus enhancing generalization capabilities. Experiments on a public driver EEG dataset show that MFEDriver achieves excellent classification performance, supporting studies of driver behavior and emotional states.

title: Digital Ecosystem and Mechanism for Controlling Production Pollution
authors: Vladimir Tsyganov
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: The article considers the problem of sustainable development of production in a corporation with regional divisions. A mathematical model of environmental pollution control in such a corporation under random external influences is constructed and studied. In this model, the Owner of the corporation controls production and the pollution associated with it. At the same time, he uses the advice of an environmental protection specialist - the Advisor. The regional division of the corporation is headed by the Head. Local production, managed by the Director, is subordinated to the Head. In addition, the Head can organize additional production by outsourcing. The Owner, the Advisor, and the Head do not know the minimum possible level of pollution associated with local production. Therefore, the Director can manipulate this pollution to obtain a higher class and a corresponding incentive from the Head. In addition, the Owner and Advisor do not know the minimum possible level of pollution associated with regional production. Therefore, the Head can manipulate regional pollution, taking into account outsourcing, to obtain a higher rank and a corresponding incentive from the Owner. A mathematical model of interaction between the Director, the Head, the Owner, and the Advisor under random external influences is constructed. A mechanism for managing corporate production has been developed, in which the Head and Director are interested in reducing pollution within their competence. This mechanism can be used as a basis for building a digital ecosystem for sustainable development of corporate production. As an example, environmental pollution control during servicing of passenger cars of the Russian Railways holding company is considered.

title: A Reconfigurable Metasurface for Wideband Absorption with a Notchband
authors: Neema K, Krushna Kanth Varikuntla, Deepti Das Krishna
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a reconfigurable metasurface designed for wideband absorption and notch band filtering. Reconfigurability is achieved by integrating RF switches into the structure. When the switches are in the ON state, the metasurface acts as a wideband absorber covering <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.77-8.2 \text{GHz}$</tex> for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$x$</tex>-polarized waves and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.99-7.54 \text{GHz}$</tex> for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$y$</tex>-polarized waves. In the OFF state, a notch band is introduced for both the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$x$</tex> - and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$y$</tex>-polarized incident waves, centered at approximately 5.26 GHz and 5.78 GHz, respectively. Absorptivity <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>90 \%$</tex> is obtained in all the absorption bands with an angular stability up to 40°.

title: Design and Analytical Framework for Upper-Body Humanoids Using Denavit-Hartenberg and Euler-Lagrange Methods
authors: Shifa Sulaiman, Niyaz Imamov, A P Sudheer, Tatyana Tsoy
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: Humanoid robots are increasingly utilized across fields such as education, personal assistance, healthcare, entertainment, emergency response, and space missions. A key foundation in their engineering is kinematic analysis, which helps defining robot’s range of motion and mechanical viability. This paper explores kinematic and dynamic modelling of a humanoid robot’s upper body. Using the Denavit-Hartenberg convention, a forward kinematic model is established, streamlining a representation of joint parameters and improving computational performance. Additionally, the EulerLagrange method is applied for dynamic modelling, enabling accurate control of movements and supporting thorough stability evaluation. The proposed design for a torso and arms replicates a human-like posture and gestures, thereby enhancing a robot’s ability to engage and adapt to a wide array of functional environments. Simulation studies are provided for showcasing torques acting at the joints of the humanoid robot.

title: Design and Implementation of a Wireless Audio Transmission System for Self Developed SDR Hardware Platform and GNU Radio Software Framework
authors: Yuxin Xia, Bin He, Jiaju He, Chen Yu
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Information and Communication Networks (ICICN)
abstract: In this paper, a wireless transmission system of quadrature phase shift keying (QPSK) audio data based on self-developed software-defined radio (SDR) hardware platform and GNU Radio software development framework is presented. The realization adopts a self-developed SDR hardware platform, which is equipped with XC7Z100 heterogeneous architecture chip of Xilinx and AD9361 RF transceiver. Firstly, this paper introduces the implementation framework of wireless audio transmission system based on QPSK, and then explains in detail the method of constructing the transmitter and receiver of QPSK wireless audio transmission system in GNU Radio. Finally, the system is tested and verified on the self-developed SDR hardware platform, and the results show that the system can transmit audio data efficiently and stably.

title: EjwT: Enhancing JWT Security through Dynamic Cryptographic & LSTM Networks
authors: Shishir Bhuiya, G. M. Shakil, Golam Md. Muradul Bashir, Md. Samsuzzaman
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: JSON Web Tokens (JWTs) are widely adopted as part of stateless authentication systems, but they are still vulnerable to theft of tokens, brute-force attacks, "alg none" attack risks, and unauthorized access due to the lack of encryption and server side revocation systems. In this paper, the Extended JSON Web Token (EjwT), a safe extension of JWTs based upon dynamic cryptographic randomization, is proposed. The main elements of the proposed scheme are nonce calculation, key identifier swapping, algorithm agility, and encryption using AES-GCM, together with real-time anomaly detection performed by Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks. This work trained the model with a dataset containing more than 33 million authentication events. Techniques like Interquartile Range (IQR) outlier filtering, SMOTE to handle class imbalance, and Pearson Correlation Coefficient driven feature selection are also employed. And with empirical validation, a 99.60% accuracy rate, 95.10% precision, 97.00% recall rate, and 96.04% F1-score are achieved, clearly offering a great step forward from traditional JWTs with regard to integrity, confidentiality, revocation, and resilience to brute force attacks with multi-layered risk-based authentication.

title: Graphene-Based Reconfigurable On-Chip Antenna for 6G/Satellite IoT
authors: P. Omsai, N. Bhavesh, M. Praveen, Guthi Srinivas, B.R. Sanjeeva Reddy, Ramesh Deshpande
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents the HFSS-based design and simulation of a graphene-based reconfigurable on-chip antenna for 6G and satellite IoT applications in the sub-terahertz frequency range. Multilayer graphene <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.0034 \mu ~\mathrm{m})$</tex> serves as the radiating patch, offering high conductivity, thermal stability, and tunable electromagnetic properties for compact, energy efficient integration on a silicon substrate. Utilizing graphene's voltage-dependent conductivity, the antenna dynamically reconfigures across three frequency bands: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 4 4}-\mathbf{2 5 0 ~ G H z}, \mathbf{2 5 6}$</tex> 266 GHz, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$276-280 \text{GHz}$</tex>, corresponding to conductivities of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2} \times 10^{4} ~\mathrm{S} / \mathrm{m}, 2 \times 10^{5} ~\mathrm{S} / \mathrm{m}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 10^{6} ~\mathrm{S} / \mathrm{m}$</tex>, respectively. The antenna exhibits strong performance in each band, achieving reflection coefficient minima of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{+} \mathbf{3 0. 7 ~ d B} \boldsymbol{,} \boldsymbol{+} \mathbf{3 8. 3 ~ d B}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{+} \mathbf{4 5. 1 ~ d B}$</tex>, with peak gains of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$14 \text{dBi}, 8.6 \text{dBi}$</tex>, and 8 dBi. Leveraging graphene's ultra-high carrier mobility (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 200,000 ~\text{cm}^{2} / \mathrm{V} \cdot \mathrm{s}$</tex>) and thermal conductivity <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\sim 5000 ~\mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m} \cdot \mathrm{K})$</tex>, the design ensures efficient signal transport and heat dissipation. Its atomic thickness enables ultra-compact, low-profile integration on-chip, making it highly suitable for MMIC and SoC platforms. These combined properties support scalable, reconfigurable, and thermally robust architectures, addressing the evolving needs of future 6G and satellite IoT deployments.

title: An Optimization Method of Marshalling Station Period Scheduling Plan Based on Ordinal Optimization
authors: Zhiqing Zhang, Chong Wang, Zhongcan Li, Wei Dong, Xiang Yan, Zhao Long, Xinya Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Marshalling stations are critical hubs in railway freight networks, where optimizing the period scheduling plan is paramount for overall operational efficiency. Traditional mathematical programming methods suffer from combinatorial explosion, rendering them impracticable in complex real-world scenarios. Although traditional heuristic algorithms can be used in complex real-world scenarios, it is difficult to evaluate the quality of solutions and simulation evaluation requires considerable computing resources and time. To address these issues, this paper applies the ordinal optimization(OO) method to optimize the period scheduling plan of marshalling stations(PSPMS) and proposes an optimization method of PSPMS based on OO(PSPMS-OO). This method uses the crude simulation model with faster running speed to evaluate the optimization objective of solutions so as to greatly improve the solving efficiency. Based on the OO theory, the quality of solutions is guaranteed and the quantitative evaluation for the quality of solutions is realized. Simulation experiments using the real-world data from a typical Chinese marshalling station demonstrate that PSPMSOO achieves solution quality the same as that of an optimization method of PSPMS based on genetic algorithm(PSPMS-GA), while reducing the solving time by 81.05 %. Furthermore, solutions obtained by PSPMS-OO are guaranteed to be within the top 5% feasible uniform sampling solutions at 95% confidence.

title: EAGLE-X: Cross-Language Code Clone Detection via Enhanced Graph Learning and Dynamic Feature Fusion
authors: Boshu Wang, Chengwan He
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Information Communication and Software Engineering (ICICSE)
abstract: Cross-language code clone detection (CLCD) is a critical task for ensuring the quality and consistency of multi-lingual software systems, yet state-of-the-art methods still face significant challenges. Sequence-based approaches often fail to capture explicit code structures, while graph-based methods are hampered by the structural heterogeneity and noise introduced by linguistic differences, and often lack effective mechanisms for multi-modal feature fusion.To address these limitations, we propose EAGLE-X, a novel multi-modal deep learning model. EAGLE-X employs a dual-path architecture to extract sequence and structure features in parallel. The sequence path utilizes the pre-trained model CodeBERT. The structure path leverages tree-sitter to generate a Concrete Syntax Tree (CST), which is then converted into a Semantic-Enhanced Graph via our designed normalization and enhancement strategies and encoded by a Graph Attention Network (GAT). We further introduce a dynamic feature fusion module that adaptively learns to combine multiple fusion strategies to optimally integrate the two modalities. The entire model is optimized end-to-end with a contrastive learning objective to learn a representation space that is robust to linguistic differences.Experiments on a public benchmark dataset indicate that EAGLE-X achieves competitive performance compared to existing state-of-the-art baselines. Furthermore, comprehensive ablation studies confirm that both our proposed semantic-enhanced graph and the dynamic fusion module make effective contributions to the model’s final performance.

title: MSSAP: Active Passive Subsurface Sounder for Martian Subsurface Exploration
authors: Prantik Chakraborty, Yogendra Sahu, Anish Kumar Mishra, Pankaj Kanti Nath, CVN Rao, Nilesh M Desai
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents details of MSSAP (Martian Subsurface Sounder Active Passive), a novel instrument concept for polarimetric subsurface imaging, dielectric profiling and exploration using rover based platform. Proposed instrument is an improvisation over conventional GPR systems which uses only active sounding for probing the subsurface. It is developed around a unique concept to carry out both active and passive mode microwave measurements of subsurface. This will provide independent information on both reflectivity and emissivity of the subsurface layers leading to more assertive resolution of the dielectric discontinuities and material properties. Gap areas and scientific objectives for Martian subsurface exploration along with instrument working principle is presented in this paper. Instrument design aspects along with simulation results for expected instrument performance are also presented.

title: BanglaClick: Cross-Platform Bengali Clickbait Teasers with Diagnostic Labels
authors: Ibnul Mansib, Mustakim Billah, Mohammad Shahidur Rahman
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Clickbait lowers the quality of online information, yet most detection work still focuses on English and on single platforms. We introduce BanglaClick, the first Bengali dataset that brings together news headlines, Facebook posts, YouTube titles, and Twitter texts, mirroring the code-mixed, cross-platform reality of today’s media. Almost five thousand teasers are stored in a 13-field table and tagged with a concise scheme: a Key-Fact flag, four FAST checks (Factuality, Attribution, Specificity, Tone), and counts of strong or weak triggers. Simple rules merge these tags into multi-level clickbait scores. Two annotators followed a clear guideline, and automated checks verified every label. We release platform-approved IDs, rich metadata, and scripts for rebuilding and analysing the corpus, giving researchers a solid benchmark for Bengali clickbait studies. The dataset and code are openly available at https://github.com/MBillahsust/BanglaClick.

title: Self Decoupled Arrow Head Shaped MIMO Antenna for Wi-Fi 6E Applications
authors: Harikrishnan P R, Abdulla P, Anitha George, Anju Iqubal
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: An arrow head shaped multiple input multiple output (MIMO) antenna is proposed for Wi-Fi 6E applications with enhanced diversity performance. The four element MIMO antenna is designed on FR4 substrate with a loss tangent of 0.025 and a relative permittivity of 4.3. The antenna element achieves a -10 dB impedance bandwidth of 2.4 GHz, centered at 6.2 GHz. The complete MIMO configuration, with a size of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$120 \text{m m} {\times} 1 2 0 \text{mm}$</tex>, ensures an isolation greater than 21 dB, channel capacity loss (CCL) of less than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.36 \text{bits} / \mathrm{s} / \text{Hz}$</tex> and envelope correlation coefficient (ECC) below 0.005 across the 5 - 7.4 GHz frequency range. Comparative analysis with recently reported MIMO antennas demonstrates that the proposed design offers superior isolation and ECC performance.

title: Intelligent Detection Method for Infrared Defects in Aircraft Honeycomb Structures Based on Lightweight Deep Learning
authors: Qirui Yang, Feng Jin, Tengfei Zhang, Guigang Zhang, Junling Fan, Wei Zhang, Dan Wang
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: To address the issues of weak defect features and high missed detection rates in infrared detection of aircraft honeycomb structures using traditional methods, this study proposes a detection scheme for infrared images of simulated honeycomb structures based on lightweight deep-learning models. Among existing deep-learning models, YOLO11-s, as a lightweight deep learning model specifically designed for industrial detection scenarios, stands out as an ideal choice for real-time detection of aviation structures due to its lightweight architecture and efficient inference performance. This research adopts the model as the core framework and integrates the advantages of mainstream deep learning models such as ResNet, VGG, and Transformer in feature extraction. It collects images of honeycomb structures with typical defects (water accumulation, debonding, and delamination) through infrared thermal imaging technology, and constructs an end-to-end defect detection model by combining adaptive histogram equalization preprocessing [1] and multiple sets of hyperparameter optimization experiments. The experimental results show that the optimized model has significantly improved detection accuracy compared with the original model, and exhibits good recognition ability for different types of defects, providing a new technical path for efficient detection of aviation structural defects.

title: Leveraging Motion Tracking for Sample Weighting in Motion-Based Calibration and Self-Assessment
authors: Thomas Wodtko, Marco Deuscher, Michael Buchholz
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Extrinsic sensor calibration is crucial for reliable and robust multi-sensor fusion in autonomous systems. While various approaches exist for obtaining such information for different sensor types, motion-based extrinsic calibration has proven to be best suited for online evaluation since it can be used in the environment in which an autonomous system is deployed. Nevertheless, once an extrinsic calibration is determined, it is subject to change due to external influences like temperature, vibrations, or even vandalism. Consequently, it must be assessed online to ensure the safe and robust operation of the autonomous system. Existing methods are only capable of recalculating an extrinsic calibration online; however, this may be computationally expensive and may potentially use corrupt data. In this work, we first present a weighting approach based on motion tracking. Then, leveraging the tracking results, we propose an online self-assessment approach for extrinsic calibration, including a certainty measure, which allows us to determine if recalibration is an option. Based on real-world motion patterns provided by the EuRoC MAV dataset, we evaluate our approach and demonstrate its superiority compared to state-of-the-art approaches. Our approach's applicability comprises various autonomous systems and, thus, contributes to the safe and robust operation of intelligent transportation systems.

title: Beyond Features: How Dataset Design Influences Multi-Agent Trajectory Prediction Performance
authors: Tobias Demmler, Jakob Häringer, Andreas Tamke, Thao Dang, Alexander Hegai, Lars Mikelsons
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Accurate trajectory prediction is critical for safe autonomous navigation, yet the impact of dataset design on model performance remains understudied. This work systematically examines how feature selection, cross-dataset transfer, and geographic diversity influence trajectory prediction accuracy in multi-agent settings. We evaluate a state-of-the-art model using our novel L4 Motion Forecasting dataset based on our own data recordings in Germany and the US. This includes enhanced map and agent features. We compare our dataset to the US-centric Argoverse 2 benchmark. First, we find that incorporating supplementary map and agent features unique to our dataset, yields no measurable improvement over baseline features, demonstrating that modern architectures do not need extensive feature sets for optimal performance. The limited features of public datasets are sufficient to capture convoluted interactions without added complexity. Second, we perform cross-dataset experiments to evaluate how effective domain knowledge can be transferred between datasets. Third, we group our dataset by country and check the knowledge transfer between different driving cultures.

title: Integration of Testing Scenarios for Automated Vehicles Using a Route Optimization Algorithm
authors: Joonho Park, Gyungtaek Oh, Haneul Park, Jaehyun Jason So
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This study aims to determine the most efficient execution route for a predefined set of autonomous vehicle test scenarios within K-City, a controlled testing environment in Republic of Korea. Each of the 47 scenarios is associated with a set of feasible zones, and the objective is to select one zone per scenario and derive an optimal route that minimizes total travel distance. To solve this problem, a hybrid method combining Genetic Algorithm and Integer Programming was applied. GA identifies valid combinations of scenario-to-zone assignments, while IP computes the shortest path that satisfies the required zone visit counts. The approach was validated through both a simplified <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5 \times 5$</tex> grid network and the actual K-City road network. The results demonstrated that the proposed method significantly reduces redundant movement and produces an execution route that is practical for real-world testing. The final path was segmented into five manageable subroutes to address operational constraints such as setup time and personnel fatigue.

title: Numerical Analysis and Optimization of a Quasi-D-Shaped PCF-Based SPR Biosensor for Malaria Detection
authors: Md Jarjis Mondal, Mohammed Imamul Hassan Bhuiyan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: To survive malaria, early and precise detection of the disease is essential. PCF-SPR biosensors are a highly sensitive, fast and reliable technology for detecting particular bio-molecules in the analyte. This paper introduces a novel quasi-D-shaped PCF-based SPR biosensor for malaria detection. The design is simulated using Comsol Multiphysics v6.2. To implement the design of PCF, a number of circular air holes of three different sizes are used. One side of the PCF is polished to form a smooth plane to facilitate metal deposition. Gold (Au) is selected as the plasmonic layer along with titanium dioxide (TiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) as the adhesive layer. Design parameters, materials and configurations are optimized for the best sensing performance. The motivation of the research is to design the PCF-SPR biosensor for malaria detection, while maintaining high sensitivity and simpler fabrication approaches. The sensor requires NIR radiation of wavelength 1330-1400 nm for detection purpose. The proposed design results in high sensing performance with a peak wavelength sensitivity of 19,528.57 nm/RIU and a resolution of 6.67 × +10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−5</sup> RIU.

title: Choke Ring Loaded Crossed Dipole Antenna with Low Multipath Effects
authors: Rachita Tripathy, Sagi Sravan Kumar, Sajith P, Situ Rani Patre
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a compact single-fed circularly polarized (CP) wideband crossed dipole antenna (CDA) integrated with dual corrugated conical shaped choke rings on a circular flat ground plane for Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS) applications. These choke rings help to mitigate multipath propagation effects thereby reducing the sidelobes and backlobes. The proposed antenna can achieve an impedance bandwidth of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$77.3 \%(1.00-2.26) \text{GHz}$</tex> for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{S}_{11} \leq-10 ~\text{dB}$</tex> and axial ratio bandwidth of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{6 0. 7 \%} (\text{1. 1 0 - 2. 0 6}) \text{GHz}$</tex> for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{AR} \leq 3$</tex> dB ensuring better polarization purity. The single-fed CDA is placed on a circular substrate having a radius of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.2 \lambda$</tex> at a height of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.27 \lambda$</tex> (where <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\lambda$</tex> is the wavelength at lowest CP frequency). The conical shaped concentric rings suppress multipath components by enhancing the cross polar difference of more than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 5 ~ d B}$</tex> to avoid positioning error in GNSS antennas. The antenna achieves a 3 dB AR beamwidth higher than 750 with a stable gain of more than 7.8 dBic across all operating bands.

title: Characterization of a Highly Flexible UWB Antenna for Body-Centric Wireless Communications
authors: G Dhayanithi, A Praveena
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents the design, prototyping, and characterization of an ultra-wideband (UWB) antenna optimized for wearable body-centric wireless communication systems. The antenna is implemented on a lightweight, flexible, highly transparent and biocompatible polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) dielectric material. A modified circular monopole configuration, resembling an inverted umbrella, is adopted to enhance bandwidth and radiation characteristics. The compact antenna, measuring <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$50 \times 41 \times 1.5 ~\text{mm}^{3}$</tex>, achieves a bandwidth of 3.1 to 12 GHz with a VSWR less than 2. The design is validated through simulations and measurements in flat, bent, and on-body configurations. Specific Absorption Rate (SAR) analysis is performed on a four-layer human tissue phantom at multiple distances <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(5 ~\text{mm}, 10 ~\text{mm}$</tex>, and 15 mm), with results well within FCC safety limits. The proposed antenna exhibits robust performance, making it well-suited for wearable UWB applications.

title: Neural Network-Based Diagnosis of the Technical Condition of Thermal Power Equipment Using Operational Parameters
authors: Oleg Protalinsky, Alexander Andryushin, Yulia Yagupova, Leonid Antonenko, Timur Fetkullin
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: Under the intensified operational demands placed on thermal power units at contemporary heat and power plants, there is an urgent need for the development of intelligent methods for assessing and forecasting equipment health. This paper introduces a neural network-based diagnostic framework grounded in the parametric analysis of operational data, which enables the detection of latent and subtly manifested defects in power generation components prior to their critical escalation. The proposed methodology employs artificial neural networks architecturally tailored to process multivariate time series that represent the technical parameter profiles of various subsystems within the power plant complex. Particular emphasis is placed on the application of this approach to the condition monitoring of steam turbines-identified as among the most vulnerable and mission-critical elements of the thermal power system. As an illustrative case study, a Type-T turbine is modeled functionally to implement the neural processing of data acquired during in-service operation. Model training is conducted using composite technical condition indices calculated for key turbine subsystems, incorporating weighted coefficients and adherence to regulatory standards. The results demonstrate that the proposed approach not only facilitates the early identification of equipment degradation but also supports the construction of reliable prognostic assessments of remaining operational life, thereby laying the groundwork for the adoption of adaptive maintenance strategies based on predictive diagnostic principles.

title: Fault Root Cause Diagnosis Based on Time Series Prediction and Permutation Algorithm
authors: Zihao Li, Shujie Liu, Yang Xiong, Youkang Sun
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: In the era of Industry 4.0, root cause diagnosis of industrial faults has been increasingly important in Prognostics and Health Management (PHM). Accurate root cause diagnosis of faults can effectively reduce industrial production losses and provide valuable guidance for subsequent repairs and maintenance. Traditional faults root cause diagnosis methods rely on extensive professional knowledge and complex mechanism modeling, making it challenging to meet the demands of modern industrial tasks. This paper proposes a fault root cause diagnosis method that combines time series prediction and permutation algorithms. Firstly, the method employs a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network integrated with a Wavelet Transform Convolution (WTC) and a Channel Attention Mechanism (CAM) to predict fault-related variables. Subsequently, a permutation algorithm based on the prediction model is applied to extract the causal relationship matrix between variables, thereby diagnosing the root cause of the fault. Experiments on the Tennessee Eastman process dataset validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: LeMaxViT: Lightweight Vision Transformer for Explainable Multi-Class Ocular Disease Detection Using Simple Eye Images
authors: Mehedi Hasan Pritom, Rahnuma Shaolin Oyshi, Mohammad Shahin Uddin, Md. Iqbal Hossain, Md. Maniruzzaman, Md. Shakhauat Hossan Sumon
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Timely diagnosis of eye conditions including cataract, glaucoma, diabetic retinopathy (DR), and age related macular degeneration (AMD) is important to avoid blindness. The majority of currently developed deep learning methods use either OCT or fundus imaging, which are expensive and cannot be readily implemented in mass screening. This paper presents a lightweight vision transformer model, LeMaxViT, to classify using simple external eye images and classify multi-class ocular diseases. The model utilizes a soft voting ensemble to improve predictive performance, which got 97.44% accuracy, 97.46% F1, and good generalization as measured by five-fold cross-validation with the average accuracy of 98.87%. The time analysis of inference illustrates real-time applicability, as well as, effective processing, despite ensemble computation. Grad-CAM visualization makes predictions more interpretable by emphasizing the parts of labels that make the most significant contribution to each prediction. These findings indicate that ocular disease detection through simple eye images can be done accurately, explainably and computationally efficiently to provide a scalable and accessible solution to early screening in real-life medical care conditions.

title: IDM Based Computation of Reactive Energy Density Around Near-Field Focused Antenna Arrays
authors: Debdeep Sarkar, Yahia M. M. Antar
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: In this paper, we propose the use of IDM (infinitesimal dipole modelling) based approach to quantify the reactive energy density around near-field focusing antenna arrays. We apply the concept of vector potential and FSGF (Free Space Green's Functions) and we compute the reactive energy density, by utilizing the classical definition (made by Collin and Rothschild in 1964) as well as the newly introduced electromagnetic (EM) Lagrangian density based approach. Using examples of URAs (uniform rectangular arrays) composed of IDs (infinitesimal dipoles) we demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed methodology for evaluating the angular distribution of reactive energy density along an observation sphere. The results of this paper would be useful for antenna engineers designing near-field focused arrays for communication as well as wireless power transfer applications.

title: Temporal Spatial Multi-Scale Compressor Trajectory Prediction Network
authors: Xiucong Zhao, Shuai Liu, Yechen Qin, Chenhao Lin, Chao Shen
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Precise prediction of surrounding-agent trajectories is a cornerstone for ensuring safety and efficiency in autonomous driving. Yet, as traffic density and scene complexity grow, Transformer-based models—with their quadratic attention cost—struggle to operate in real time without sacrificing accuracy. To bridge this gap, we introduce Temporal-Spatial Multiscale Compression (TSMC), a novel framework that hierarchically compresses input features across both temporal and spatial dimensions, effectively “zooming out” on less critical interactions while retaining full resolution for salient ones. By reducing the number of tokens processed at each stage, TSMC lowers the computational load when handling large numbers of traffic participants and complex contexts. We integrate TSMC into several state-of-the-art trajectory predictors and evaluate on two popular benchmarks (Argoverse I and Argoverse II). Results show consistent gains in prediction accuracy and a speed-up in inference, with negligible model-size increase. TSMC thus offers a flexible, plug-and-play solution for long-horizon, high-density trajectory forecasting in real-world autonomous systems.

title: Wideband Transmission Type Linear-to-Circular Polarization Converter with High Angular Stability for Millimeter-Wave Applications
authors: Javid Ahmad Ganie, Debdeep Sarkar, Kushmanda Saurav
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper introduces a transmission-type linear-to-circular polarization converter designed for wideband millimeter-wave applications, operating effectively across the frequency range of 27-37 GHz. The proposed converter features a compact unit cell with dimensions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.33 \lambda \times 0.33 \lambda$</tex>, fabricated on a single-layer substrate, which simplifies manufacturing while maintaining high performance. The design achieves a 3-dB axial ratio bandwidth of 31.5%, demonstrating robust polarization conversion capabilities. A significant advantage of this converter is its angular stability, sustaining consistent performance up to an incident angle of 40°, making it suitable for diverse deployment scenarios.

title: Compact Optically Transparent UWB Antenna with Dual Polarization for Diverse Applications
authors: Rabia Yahya, Saleh Alfawaz, Amal El-Ghazaly
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper introduces an innovative, compact, optically transparent antenna featuring ultra-wideband (UWB) and polarization diversity, designed for a range of applications such as wireless communication systems, including LTE, WiMAX, 5G networks, IoT devices, and biomedical applications. Despite its compact size (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$25 \text{mm} \times 25 \text{mm}$</tex>) and transparency, the antenna maintained excellent performance, offering good matching across the entire UWB band and high isolation between its dual ports. The measured performance of the antenna demonstrated its superiority over previously proposed designs, making it ideal for integration with biomedical devices where visual transparency may be needed. It is not only suitable for wireless communication, but also for RF energy harvesting and inter-vehicular applications, particularly because of its dual-polarized operation and potential circular polarization features, supported by symmetrical structures with similar and centered radiation patterns.

title: Compact Multi-Wideband Wearable Patch Antenna for ISM Band, WLAN, WBAN, and 5G IoT Applications
authors: Pooja Sharma, Ajay Kumar Poddar
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: A compact, flexible star-shaped antenna is designed in this paper to cover multiple bands for WLAN, WiFi, IoT, health monitoring applications, and the 6 GHz spectrum for 5 G applications. SRR with a meander line, L, and inverted L shapes surrounded the designed antenna. This antenna is partially grounded. The antenna provides a 2.54 GHz bandwidth and a 540 MHz bandwidth from 5.64 GHz to 8.18 GHz and 9.18 GHz to 9.72 GHz, with measured S11 of - 39.26 dB and 30.44 dB at a resonance frequency of 6.2 GHz, which also covers the ISM band and 9.35 GHz, respectively. The substrate is made of a 1 mm-thick, flexible FELT material with a low dielectric constant. Measurements of several antenna parameters show that the antenna is appropriate for health monitoring, 5 G IoT, and Wi-Fi applications over the frequency ranges from 5.60 GHz-8.18 GHz, 9.18-9.72 GHz.

title: A Monte Carlo Tree Search Approach for Operating Profile Planning of Freight Trains on the Long Steep Downward Slope Sections
authors: Xiang Wang, Zhengwei Luo, Qianying Ma, Zixu Zhao, Jidong Lv, Ming Chai, Kaicheng Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Train speed profile planning is important to ensure safe and efficient operation of freight train premise and foundation. Due to long station spacing of freight railway lines, existence of large gradient long steep downward slope sections, and feature of electric-pneumatic joint cyclic braking, traditional speed profile planning method for small gradient long steep downward slope sections is hard to meet the requirements of safety and other multi-objective constraints in high gradient downhill sections. In this paper, a Monte Carlo Tree Search(MCTS) based freight train cyclic braking profile planning method is proposed to solve the problem of safe and efficient operation on long steep downhill sections. Firstly, the cyclic braking state transfer model of freight train is built up based on Markov decision-making process with optimization objectives of coupler safety, pneumatic braking distance and electric braking time in combination with constraints of speed limit and braking for freight train running on long steep downhill sections. Secondly, train state equations such as braking distance and coupler force are established by Zhai method with multi-mass point model of freight train, and the cyclic braking profile solution algorithm based on MCTS is designed. The paper takes HXD1 locomotive and C70 gondola car as control objects, binds data of typical long steep downhill sections with maximum gradient of 30% as basis for simulation analysis. Compared with DQN method, the maximum coupler force of MCTS algorithm is 6.36% lower and the average speed is increased by 0.34 km/h, which is able to reduce longitudinal impulsive force of freight train and improve running efficiency.

title:
High-Performance Lead-Free Bifacial RbGeBr
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    Perovskite Solar Cell: A SCAPS-1D Numerical Study
authors: Tapos Chandra Saha, Md. Abul Bashar, Utshob Sutradhar, Md. Mustafizur Rahman, Arifuzzaman Rajib
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Perovskite solar cells (PSCs) were identified as the future of photovoltaics owing to their adjustable bandgap, superior optoelectronic characteristics, and low manufacturing expenses. Lead-free perovskites were considered less hazardous, more stable, and more flexible alternatives to conventional lead-based perovskites. In this paper, RbGeBr<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> was modeled in the SCAPS-1D simulator to optimize the inorganic PSC as the photoactive layer. To find the optimized solar cell, different types of Electron Transport Layer (ETL) and Hole Transport Layer (HTL) were used. The initial optimized configuration without an HTL achieved a power conversion efficiency (PCE) of 19.51%. Upon incorporating graphene oxide (GO) as the HTL, an efficiency of 25.61% was attained by the final optimized device configuration, with a V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OC</inf> of 1.18 V and FF of 82.87%. The J-V and Q-E curves showed the performance analysis with and without the HTL layer to find out the optimum result. The analysis of the temperature dependence of the device performance was conducted between temperatures of 278 K and 348 K. The Temperature coefficient was found in parts per thousand per Kelvin, which was comparable to a base solar cell. Additionally, Simulation data revealed that modification of the device structure from monofacial to bifacial significantly enhanced the PSC performance. The proposed device achieved an overall performance of PCE of 30.68% for high-efficiency bifacial photovoltaic applications.

title: Integrated Predictive Modeling for Cardiovascular Disease and Stroke Risk
authors: Md. Nurul Alam, Dhiman Sarma
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: According to the World Health Organization, stroke affects fifteen million individuals annually—causing a death every 4–5 minutes, with Bangladesh ranking 84th globally (0.3%). As reported with clinical guidelines and epidemiological studies, stroke is recognized as one of the cardiovascular disease indicators contributing to the 17.9 million annual cardiovascular deaths worldwide. Early detection of high-risk individuals can significantly reduce the incidence of cardiovascular disease and stroke. This study aims to develop and evaluate an integrated machine learning model that predicts future cardiovascular and stroke risk. It can support clinical decision-making by interpreting the risk factors aligns broadly with WHO and AHA cardiovascular risk assessment principles, adapted to local clinical practice in Bangladesh. A dataset of 5,050 patients was collected from government and non-government hospitals in Chattogram, the Chittagong Hill Tracts (CHT), and Cox’s Bazar, Bangladesh, using demographic, clinical, and laboratory features with correlation-based feature selection to retain the most influential predictors. Six machine learning algorithms were evaluated for the prediction of cardiovascular disease and stroke: Support Vector Machine (SVM), Decision Tree, Random Forest, Logistic Regression, K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) and Multinomial Naive Bayes. The entire dataset was divided into a 70:30 ratio, using 70% for training and 30% for test purposes. Hyperparameter tuning, Confusion matrices, Correlation matrix, ROC-AUC curve, and cross-validation techniques were used to optimize the performance of each algorithm. The Support Vector Machine algorithm was found to be the best-performing model, with a cross-validation score of 95%, test score of 95%, precision of 95%, recall of 96%, and f1-score of 95%. This study aims to create a clinical support system that will help to diagnose cardiovascular disease and stroke at the primary stage.

title: Field Test of Cooperative Driving Algorithms for Lane Merging and Emergency Evasion Using V2V
authors: Kana Kim, Jaejun Lee, Junmyeong Park, Heesang Yoon, Hakil Kim
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This paper presents a V2V-based cooperative driving framework that implements cooperative lane merging (CLM) and emergency trajectory alignment (ETrA) to improve the safety and efficiency of autonomous vehicles. The proposed method exchanges cooperative awareness messages (CAM) and collective perception messages (CPM) to coordinate planned trajectories. The CLM algorithm evaluates the feasibility of merging using shared trajectory information and enables safe integration through structured intent negotiation. The ETrA algorithm adjusts the follower vehicle trajectory to align with the lead vehicle's emergency path, allowing synchronized avoidance maneuvers and maintaining a safe distance. A field evaluation conducted on public roads in Incheon, Korea, demonstrated that the proposed approach improves safety by increasing time-to-collision margins, enhances traffic efficiency through higher target speed retention and merge success rates, and mitigates secondary collision risks. The results confirm the effectiveness of the CLM and ETrA in enabling robust cooperative behavior in real-world autonomous driving scenarios.

title: Research of Server Resilience to Cyberattacks Using Queuing System with Correlated Arrival Flows
authors: Elizaveta A. Barabanova, Stanislav E. Kazakov
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: In this paper, we propose the approach to analyzing the resilience of critical information infrastructure facilities using a queuing system with correlated arrival flows in the case of an abruptly change service rate. To provide the resilience of the server, it is proposed to investigate its performance metrics before and after possible cyberattacks. The analytical model of queuing system with correlated arrival flows obtained using the probability translation matrix method is proposed.

title: Machine Learning Assisted Design and Optimization of Wide-Angle Steering Reflector Antenna with Isoflux Profile
authors: Ramesh Chandra Gupta, Vijay Kumar Singh, Milind B. Mahajan
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: A multi-objective optimization approach using machine learning has been successfully applied for design of wide-angle steering reflector antenna in order to meet stringent requirements. In this antenna, feed system remains fixed and only reflector rotates during steering. Using regression-based surrogate model, a Matlab code has been developed for multivariable and multi-objective optimization for above purpose. We have used Ticra Tool for database generation for MLassisted optimization code. Database is also appended by data obtained from design equations. We have controlled near field at feed aperture due to reflected rays from reflector, thereby insuring good cross-polar performance of antenna in all steering cases. The antenna provides 2-axis steering by rotation of reflector in elevation direction and rotation around nadir axis. When the beam is steered from boresight to edge of steering coverage polygon, it provides isoflux beam steering. That is, gain is increasing with increasing elevation angle to compensate free space path loss. The steering antenna is analyzed using physical optics/physical theory of diffraction (PO/PTD) methods. The reflector surface is shaped to tailor near field at feed aperture to minimize feed scattering. This antenna provides isoflux gain and good axial ratio over wide-angle (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 40^{\circ}$</tex> in Az and EL) steering.

title: Human Mobility Modeling with Household Coordination Activities Under Limited Information via Retrieval-Augmented LLMs
authors: Yifan Liu, Xishun Liao, Haoxuan Ma, Brian Yueshuai He, Chris Stanford, Jiaqi Ma
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Understanding human mobility patterns has long been a challenging task in transportation modeling. Due to the difficulties in obtaining high-quality training datasets across diverse locations, conventional activity-based models and learning-based human mobility modeling algorithms are particularly limited by the availability and quality of datasets. Current approaches primarily focus on spatial-temporal patterns while neglecting semantic relationships such as logical connections or dependencies between activities and household coordination activities like joint shopping trips or family meal times, both crucial for realistic mobility modeling. We propose a retrieval-augmented large language model (LLM) framework that generates activity chains with household coordination using only public accessible statistical and socio-demographic information, reducing the need for sophisticated mobility data. The retrieval-augmentation mechanism enables household coordination and maintains statistical consistency across generated patterns, addressing a key gap in existing methods. Our validation with NHTS and SCAG-ABM datasets demonstrates effective mobility synthesis and strong adaptability for regions with limited mobility data availability.

title: MP-RBFN: Learning-Based Vehicle Motion Primitives Using Radial Basis Function Networks
authors: Marc Kaufeld, Mattia Piccinini, Johannes Betz
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This research introduces MP-RBFN, a novel formulation leveraging Radial Basis Function Networks for efficiently learning Motion Primitives derived from optimal control problems for autonomous driving.
