While traditional motion planning approaches based on optimization are highly accurate, they are often computationally prohibitive. In contrast, sampling-based methods demonstrate high performance but impose constraints on the geometric shape of trajectories. MP-RBFN combines the strengths of both by coupling the high-fidelity trajectory generation of sampling-based methods with an accurate description of vehicle dynamics. Empirical results show compelling performance compared to previous methods, achieving a precise description of motion primitives at low inference times. MP-RBFN yields a seven times higher accuracy in generating optimized motion primitives compared to existing semi-analytic approaches. We demonstrate the practical applicability of MP-RBFN for motion planning by integrating the method into a sampling-based trajectory planner. MP-RBFN is available as open-source software: https://github.com/TUM-AVS/RBFN-Motion-Primitives

title: A Self-Supervised Transformer for Unusable Shared Bike Detection
authors: Yin Huang, Yongqi Dong, Youhua Tang, Alvaro García Hernandez
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: The rapid expansion of bike-sharing systems (BSS) has greatly improved urban “last-mile” connectivity, yet largescale deployments face escalating operational challenges, particularly in detecting faulty bikes. Existing detection approaches either rely on static model-based thresholds that overlook dynamic spatiotemporal (ST) usage patterns or employ supervised learning methods that struggle with label scarcity and class imbalance. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a novel Self-Supervised Transformer (SSTransformer) framework for automatically detecting unusable shared bikes, leveraging ST features extracted from GPS trajectories and trip records. The model incorporates a self-supervised pretraining strategy to enhance its feature extraction capabilities, followed by fine-tuning for efficient status recognition. In the pretraining phase, the Transformer encoder learns generalized representations of bike movement via a self-supervised objective; in the fine-tuning phase, the encoder is adapted to a downstream binary classification task. Comprehensive experiments on a real-world dataset of 10,730 bikes (1,870 unusable, 8,860 normal) from Chengdu, China, demonstrate that SSTransformer significantly outperforms traditional machine learning, ensemble learning, and deep learning baselines, achieving the best accuracy (97.81%), precision (0.8889), and F1-score (0.9358). This work highlights the effectiveness of self-supervised Transformer on ST data for capturing complex anomalies in BSS, paving the way toward more reliable and scalable maintenance solutions for shared mobility.

title: Machine Learning Classification of Crash Risk During Takeover Based on Driver Readiness and Scenario Features
authors: Tse-Yi Kuo, Yong-Ci Chang, Fu-You Huang, Hsueh-Yi Lai
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Despite the promise of conditionally automated driving, drivers remain responsible for taking control when the system reaches its operational limits. Takeover is a complex task influenced by driver readiness—often affected by non-driving-related tasks (NDRTs)—and by the specific characteristics of the driving scenario. Furthermore, takeover involves not only reaction time but also post-takeover control, both essential for assessing takeover quality. Thus, this study focuses on crash occurrence as an integrated outcome of these factors. A driving simulator experiment was conducted with 64 participants, featuring varied time budgets, road curvatures, and obstacle positions. Driver readiness was manipulated through NDRTs to induce different levels of cognitive and physical readiness, measured primarily via eye-tracking. Using XGBoost, crash occurrence was successfully classified with an F1-score of 0.9389. Feature importance analysis revealed that scenarios involving curved roads and limited maneuvering space (e.g., fewer lanes) are more prone to crash occurrence. Physical readiness emerged as a particularly critical factor in mitigating these risks. Therefore, future automated vehicles should integrate real-time assessments of both driver state and driving environment to enable adaptive and context-aware driver assistance systems.

title: Zeroth-Order Mode Operated Via-Free Metasurface Inspired Miniaturized Circularly Polarized Wearable Antenna
authors: Nibash Kumar Sahu, Ravi Kumar Gangwar
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel miniaturized wearable antenna that resonated in the 2.45 GHz ISM band with right hand circular polarization (RHCP) for on-body communication. Miniaturization is achieved by modifying a via-free single-layer unit cell (SLUC) resonating in 3.65 GHz to a double-layer unit cell (DLUC) of the same thickness for resonating in 2.45 GHz. Following that, the existence of the zeroth mode by the DLUC is investigated using the dispersion characteristic. Then the DLUC is assembled into a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1} \times \mathbf{2}$</tex> array configuration to develop the required miniature metasurface and incorporated beneath an unbalanced coplanar waveguide ground (UCPWG) based straight radiator to excite the zeroth mode. The UCPWG is designed in such a way that there is no requirement of additional feed, as well as it helps in leading the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$y$</tex>-component of the electric field by 90 degree from the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$x$</tex>-component of the electric field, resulting in RHCP. The antenna facilitates an impedance bandwidth of 120 MHz, a gain of 4 dB, an efficiency of 0.67, an axial ratio bandwidth of 400 MHz, and a SAR of 1.04 W/kg. To validate the simulation results, an antenna prototype is fabricated and measured, which shows excellent matching with each other.

title: CDA-SimBoost: A Unified Framework Bridging Real Data and Simulation for Infrastructure-Based CDA Systems
authors: Zhaoliang Zheng, Xu Han, Yuxin Bao, Yun Zhang, Johnson Liu, Zonglin Meng, Xin Xia, Jiaqi Ma
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Cooperative Driving Automation (CDA) has garnered increasing research attention, yet the role of intelligent infrastructure remains insufficiently explored. Existing solutions offer limited support for addressing long-tail challenges, real-synthetic data fusion, and heterogeneous sensor management. This paper introduces CDA-SimBoost, a unified framework that constructs infrastructure-centric simulation environments from real-world data. CDA-SimBoost consists of three main components: a Digital Twin Builder for generating high-fidelity simulator assets based on sensor and HD map data, OFDataPip for processing both online and offline data streams, and OpenCDA-InfraX, a high-fidelity platform for infrastructure-focused simulation. The system supports realistic scenario construction, rare event synthesis, and scalable evaluation for CDA research. With its modular architecture and standardized benchmarking capabilities, CDA-SimBoost bridges real-world dynamics and virtual environments, facilitating reproducible and extensible infrastructure-driven CDA studies. All resources are publicly available at https://github.com/zhz03/CDA-SimBoost.

title: Design of a Dual Passband Metasurface in L Band
authors: Vishnu Kumar Mishra, Ravi Kumar, Biswajeet Mukherjee, Vinit Kumar, Ambati Praveen Kumar, Jolly Dhar, M. Thottappan, Somak Bhattacharyya
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: In this paper, a dual-band metasurface-based filter is proposed, featuring a top metallic patch with two modified circular slots on a 1 mm thick FR4 substrate. The proposed device has a periodicity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.17 \lambda$</tex>, where <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\lambda$</tex> corresponds to the center frequency of the lowest passband. It exhibits two passbands centered at 1157.8 MHz and 1588.4 MHz, and a stopband at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 3 3 9. 1 ~ M H z}$</tex>, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 ~ d B}$</tex> passband bandwidths of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 7 0}$</tex> and 230 MHz, respectively. The device demonstrates very low insertion loss, remaining below 0.5 dB. The structure also exhibits polarization-independent characteristics due to its fourfold symmetric design. A stable response is observed for oblique incidence angles up to 60°. The proposed filter is suitable for applications in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{L}_{\mathbf{1}}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{L}_{\mathbf{5}}$</tex> bands, including navigation and space communication.

title: A Technical Overview of Continuous and Discrete Diffusion-based Language Models
authors: Haiyang Zhao, Jian Huang, Shangsong Liang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Information Communication and Software Engineering (ICICSE)
abstract: Diffusion-based language models (DLMs) have recently emerged as a non-autoregressive paradigm that departs from the strictly sequential generation of traditional language models. By iteratively refining noisy text representations in parallel, DLMs achieve faster inference and improved global coherence. The adaptation of this framework to discrete language data has resulted in two primary technical branches: Continuous Diffusion Models, which operate in a continuous embedding space, and Discrete Diffusion Models, which define the process directly on the token vocabulary. This survey provides a focused technical review of the core implementation aspects that underpin these two paradigms. We systematically deconstruct and compare foundational topics, including the methods for handling discrete variables ; the formulation of diffusion steps ; the design of noise schedules ; the evolution of objective functions for stable training ; and various sampling techniques for generation. By presenting a detailed analysis of these components, this work serves as a technical guide for researchers and practitioners, clarifying the principles and trade-offs essential for advancing this promising field.

title: Meta-Learning Enhanced Hybrid Neural Architecture for Millimeter Wave Channel Estimation in High-Mobility Scenarios
authors: Chaoran Du
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Information and Communication Networks (ICICN)
abstract: Millimeter wave (mmWave) channel estimation in high-mobility scenarios faces critical challenges due to rapidly time-varying multipath components and severe Doppler spread. Existing compressed sensing methods suffer from cubic computational complexity scaling with massive antenna arrays, while conventional deep learning approaches fail to maintain estimation accuracy under dynamic channel conditions with Doppler shifts exceeding 500 Hz. This paper proposes a hybrid 3D convolutional neural network-gated recurrent unit (CNN-GRU) architecture synergistically integrated with model-agnostic meta-learning (MAML) to address these limitations.

title: Unsupervised Anomaly Detection for Six-Axis Robots Considering Multivariate Coupling Network
authors: Fengqi Zhang, Zhiye He, Guojin Si, Tangbin Xia, Lifeng Xi
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Six-axis collaborative robots are widely used in industries such as consumer electronics and automotive manufacturing. Detecting anomalies in their operation is critical, as faults can cause downtime and disrupt entire production lines. However, the complexity of working conditions and the high-dimensional, heterogeneous sensor data present major challenges for accurate detection. This study proposes an unsupervised anomaly detection framework that integrates multivariate coupling analysis with graph-based deep learning. A condition-adaptive segmentation method based on Matrix Profile Distance (MPDist) is used to extract stable operating cycles. Multifactual Detrended Cross-Correlation Analysis (MF-DCCA) then constructs a coupling network that captures nonlinear dependencies among variables. The network and time-series sequences are input to a Variational Graph Autoencoder (VGAE), which reconstructs the structure and computes anomaly scores. Validated on real-world industrial data, the method improves detection accuracy by up to 30.94% and achieves a 100% anomaly detection rate. The results show that mining inter-sensor relationships and leveraging deep learning enhances anomaly identification. This framework supports reliable, real-time robot health monitoring in industrial environments.

title: MTS-Dialog-Bangla: A Dataset and Benchmark for Bangla Clinical Dialogue-to-Note Summarization
authors: Alifa Shanzidah Manarat, Md. Sozib Hossain, Kazi Ishrak, Ashrafun Zannat
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: In recent years, automatic clinical note generation has made significant progress for widely used languages such as English and Chinese. However, this field remains almost untouched for Bangla, even though it is the world’s seventh most spoken language. A key obstacle is the absence of open datasets for clinical dialogue summarization, which limits research and practical adoption in countries like Bangladesh, where most medical documentation is still written by hand. To address this gap, this research presents MTS-Dialog-Bangla, the first open-source Bangla clinical dialogue-to-note dataset, containing 1,701 dialogue– note pairs translated and refined from the English MTS-Dialog dataset. The dataset is benchmarked using several state-of-the-art multilingual transformers (mBART-50, IndicBART, mT5-base, BanglaT5) and large language models (BanglaLLM, GPT-4o). Among these, mT5-base performed the best, achieving a BERTScore F1 of 0.7619 and a ROUGE-1 F1 of 0.2421. A rigorous performance analysis demonstrated that fine-tuning improves factual completeness and reduces hallucinations in generated notes, and it also reveals the limitations of reference-based metrics for clinical summarization. MTS-Dialog-Bangla establishes the first public benchmark for Bangla clinical dialogue summarization, supporting both zero-shot and fine-tuned evaluation, and lays the groundwork for advancing Bangla clinical NLP.

title: The Hearing Impairment Phenomenon in Audio-Visual Sound Source Localization
authors: Tianyu Liu, Peng Zhang
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Information and Communication Networks (ICICN)
abstract: Audio-visual sound source localization (AV-SSL) leverages audio to identify the sounding object within the visual space. By mapping visual and audio modality representations into a shared space, cosine similarity-based methods have demonstrated strong localization performance. In this work, we discover a phenomenon in existing methods, termed Hearing Impairment (HI), which refers to the scenario where the network localizes a specific object in the image regardless of the input audio. To measure the extent of HI, three additional audio-visual mismatched datasets (Un-VGGSS, Un-S4 and Un-AVSS) are constructed and a novel metric is introduced, which is combined with mIoU to evaluate sound source localization performance comprehensively. We trained using the latest six AV-SSL methods on VGGSound, S4, and AVSS datasets, then evaluated them on VGGSS, S4 Test, and AVSS Test. Results indicate that some specific methods perform well in localization, but fail to distinguish whether the visual object is producing the sound. Future work should incorporate the evaluation of the model’s ability to differentiate sounding objects, rather than only focus on localization accuracy.

title: Hybrid Convolutional Network for Bearing Remaining Useful Life Prediction
authors: Huaiwang Jin, Sheng Wang, Yang Yang, Hang Wang, Zhongding Fan, Yongbin Liu
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Since graph convolutional networks can handle non-Euclidean data and can capture the interrelationships among different signal samples, they have been widely applied in the field of remaining useful life (RUL) prediction. However, when using graph convolutional networks for bearing RUL prediction, the over-smoothing problem limits the increase in the number of network layers, thereby weakening the model's feature extraction ability. To enhance the feature extraction ability of graph convolutional networks and improve the accuracy of bearing RUL prediction, in this paper, a bearing RUL prediction method based on graph convolution network and time convolution network is proposed. In this method, a PathGraph is constructed based on the temporal sequence of the degradation signal to extract the temporal dependency between samples; and a network model integrating graph convolution and time convolution is constructed, which effectively extracts the temporal and spatial features contained in the graph samples. Through experimental verification, the results show that this method can effectively improve the prediction accuracy of the RUL of bearings.

title: Explainable AI-Based Deep Learning Approach for Asphalt Pavement Crack Detection
authors: Md Zubair Azam, Sabab Asfaq, Nymur Rahaman Antor, Nafisa Tasnim, Shahriar Siddique Arjon, Tamanna Yasmin
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Fractures and damage in road infrastructure, if not detected in a timely manner, increase safety risks, disrupt vehicular traffic and significantly increase maintenance costs. Conventional human-based inspection processes are slow, expensive, and challenging to implement on a large scale. The study presented an Explainable Artificial Intelligence supported lightweight Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) based framework, which is capable of detecting cracks in asphalt pavement with high accuracy. One of the highlights of the study is that images of the road surface have been collected from real-world environments, including both urban and rural contexts, different weather and lighting conditions, and multiple types of cracks. The collected images have been hand annotated to produce high-quality ground-truth labels. Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-score, ROC-AUC and Confusion matrix have been used to evaluate the performance of the proposed model. To ensure interpretability, the dominant feature regions in the forecast using Grad-CAM are presented in the form of heatmap, which are verified by comparison with the engineering interpretation. The test results indicate that the proposed framework is able to provide and support transparent and understandable decisions while maintaining high classification efficiency with 98.40% accuracy, which is effectively applicable to real-field road inspections even with limited computational resources.

title: Robust Model Predictive Control of High-Frequency Vibration in an Advanced Mobility Unit
authors: Jun Wang, Ning Zhang, Zihong Li, Hangyu Lu, Cheng Wang, Guodong Yin
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Aiming at the high-frequency vibration caused by the structural characteristics of the steering system in the advanced mobility unit when driving at high speed, which is exacerbated by the high degrees of freedom enabled by mechanical decoupling, an active control strategy based on robust model predictive control (RMPC) is proposed in this study. Firstly, a nonlinear model of the advanced mobility unit integrating steering motor and the magic formula is established, and the influence of the unsprung mass on the critical stabilization speed of the system is revealed by the Hopf bifurcation. To address the challenges of model uncertainty and external disturbances, a two-layer RMPC architecture based on the nominal model and error compensation is constructed. The simulation results demonstrate the strong robustness of the control strategy to parameter uncertainties and time-varying disturbances. The research overcomes the limitations of the traditional mechanical steering system and provides theoretical basis and methodological support for the active control of shimmy for advanced mobility unit.

title: Collision-Avoidable Platooning Control of Automated Vehicles with Dynamic Event-Triggered Strategies
authors: Pengyuan Liu, Derui Ding, Xiaohua Ge
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This paper focuses on platoon control under the framework of model predictive control (MPC), where a dynamic event-triggered strategy (DETS) is employed to govern the information exchange and a collision avoidance constraint is considered to guarantee the safety of automated vehicles. First, the tracking, platoon, smoothness and control input costs are systematically taken into account to reflect the comprehensive performance and a relaxed constraint about collision avoidance is proposed to handle the challenge of distributed calculation. In light of the constructed cost and the relaxed constraint, a DETSbased distributed MPC algorithm is provided to acquire the desired control sequence. Through theoretical analysis, a sufficient condition is received to guarantee the collision-avoidable platoon performance under the proposed MPC algorithm. Finally, the effectiveness of the designed control scheme is verified by simulation examples.

title: Broadband Cylindrical Vector Beams Generation via Integrated Electro-Polarization and Acousto-Optic Modulation
authors: Jiayu Liu, Zhengwei Zhang, Jiafeng Lu, Xianglong Zeng
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Information and Communication Networks (ICICN)
abstract: Acousto-optic interaction in diverse optical fibers plays an essential role in structured light modulation due to its inherent capability of dynamic manipulation and fast response, yet conventional acoustically induced fiber gratings (AIFGs) are typically limited to a narrow 3- dB bandwidth of only about 10 nm, restricting their applications in ultrafast systems. Here, we demonstrate an AIFG with a significantly enhanced 3-dB bandwidth up to 281 nm, which is several tens of times wider than conventional AIFGs. By optimizing the fiber etching technique, the dispersion turning point (DTP) was precisely tuned to 1550 nm, achieving a high mode conversion efficiency of 97%. With the integration of a home-made electrically driven polarization controller (EPC), flexible and stable polarization control was realized, which further contributes to the generation of various broadband cylindrical vector beams (CVBs). This approach offers a viable path for versatile manipulation of ultrafast light beams for the applications ranging in adaptive mode-locked lasers, stimulated emission depletion microscopy and material processing.

title: Oblique Incidence Analysis of FSS using a Dual-Section Parallel Plate Waveguide
authors: Patinavalasa Megh Sainadh, Saptarshi Ghosh
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents the design and characterization of a parallel plate waveguide (PPW) system for analyzing the angular response of frequency selective surfaces (FSSs) under TE-polarized oblique incidence. The compact PPW structure, formed by two parallel metallic plates supporting the transverse electromagnetic (TEM) mode, enables accurate transmission and reflection measurements. A wideband absorber is used as a representative FSS, and its unit cell response is evaluated under both normal and oblique incidences. To introduce angular excitation, an initial approach rotates the FSS layer within the PPW. While this provides accurate transmission (S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">21</inf>) results, reflection (S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf>) is limited, as only part of the reflected wave returns to the input port. To overcome this, an improved setup is proposed by joining two identical PPW sections at an angle <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \theta$</tex>, with the FSS inclined at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\theta$</tex>. This configuration establishes a controlled excitation and reception path for both S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf> and S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">21</inf>. The simulation results confirm that the proposed method enhances angular accuracy while maintaining a compact and low-complexity structure, offering an efficient alternative to conventional free-space setups for FSS evaluation.

title: Joint Estimation of Frame Synchronization and LDPC Encoder Using Deep Learning
authors: Nayim Ahamed, Jayishnu Uttane, Swaminathan R.
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: In contemporary wireless communication systems, frame synchronization and channel encoder identification are critical for reliable signal processing, particularly in scenarios with limited or no prior knowledge of the transmitted signal structure. Traditionally, these tasks are performed separately, often resulting in increased system complexity and reduced efficiency. This paper proposes a novel deep learning-based approach using capsule networks (CapsNet) to jointly estimate frame synchronization points and low-density parity-check (LDPC) encoders over Rayleigh fading channels. The model is evaluated using five different LDPC codes based on the fifth generation (5G) new radio (NR) standard and eleven distinct frame synchronization positions. The proposed model achieves over 95% classification accuracy at a 5 dB signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) under Rayleigh fading conditions for input length of 1680. Furthermore, it reaches 100% accuracy for all input lengths when the SNR exceeds 10 dB under the same channel conditions. Moreover, comparisons were made between our proposed CapsNet model and existing models from the literature, clearly demonstrating that our model significantly outperforms them in classification accuracy, especially at low SNR level. These results highlight the robustness and effectiveness of our model, demonstrating its superior performance in practical wireless communication scenarios.

title: Handover Optimization in Heterogeneous Wireless Networks: A Scheduler Approach
authors: Md. Shohidul Islam, Mst. Fateha Samad, Shah Ariful Hoque Chowdhury
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Mobility in wireless communication becomes a prime demand of users having wireless connectivity for cellular communication irrespective to user environment. Recent years wireless user type and its used device faced a drastic evolution in the industry. To support the user growth, Ultra Dense Network (UDN) deployed. However, this deployment is not uniform over the horizon. Practically wireless cells deployed according traffic pattern. This creates heterogeneous network (HetNet) environment in wireless domain with a blessing of having capability to connect more user to the network. Small cell in HetNet creates more cell boundary and obviously more handover opportunity in the network. Some of the handover specially while user have movement towards the cell boundary or as an effect of cell breathing actually ping pong in nature. To elaborate, user connectivity flips between serving and neighboring cell frequently. This creates performance issues such as handover failure, high resource consumption which leads to Quality of Service (QoS) degradation. Therefore, optimization in handover control parameter (HCP) is a good choice to improve ping pong handover or unnecessary handover. Handover Margin (HOM), Time to Trigger (TTT), and handover evaluation strategy can optimize unnecessary handover. This paper explains a way out that dynamically adjust handover control parameter and re- evaluation of Reference Signal Received Power (RSRP) which gives an approximately 70.63, 67.61, and 67.55 improvement in rural, urban, and suburban area respectively for ping pong handover optimization (PPHO).

title: A Compact Implantable Antenna for Biotelemetry Applications in Arteriovenous Grafts
authors: Manogna Naidu Neerukattu, Anil Kumar Nayak, Kambiz Moez, Amalendu Patnaik
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: The increasing demand for real-time health monitoring has driven significant advancements in wireless implantable medical devices, particularly for organ-specific applications such as kidney diagnostics. These systems use implantable antennas to establish biotelemetry communication between the internal device and external monitoring units. However, designing antennas suitable for implantation has several challenges, especially in complex anatomical regions like the abdomen. High signal attenuation, size limitations, restricted gain, and adherence to safety regulations are among the constraints. This work mainly presents the design and characterization of a compact, low-profile, high-gain implantable antenna optimized for kidney applications operating in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.4-2.5 \text{GHz}$</tex> ISM band. Initially, the antenna was designed with a coaxial feed placed at its center. Four lshaped slots are etched to obtain the resonant frequency. Two shorting pins are employed to improve the impedance matching. Four circular slots are used on the ground to further enhance the gain of the antenna. The antenna is simulated in a human tissue phantom model representing realistic abdominal conditions. The measured fractional impedance bandwidth of 31.42 %, simulated gain of -24 dBi, and acceptable SAR have been obtained. The antenna has compact, low profile and is suitable for wireless biotelemetry in arteriovenous grafts.

title: Structural and Electromagnetic Characterization of Al, Co, and Y-Substituted Gadolinum Iron Garnet for Microwave Frequency Applications
authors: Brijesh Bharti, Somak Bhattacharyya, Pradip Kumar Roy
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: The synthesis, structural analysis, and electromagnetic characterization of aluminum, cobalt, and yttrium-substituted gadolinium iron garnet for use in microwave frequency applications are examined in this work. A solid-state synthesis method was used to create ferrite nanoparticles with concentrations of Co and Y ranging from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$X=0$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$X=1$</tex>, with the concentration of Al fixed at 0.2. The resultant ceramics were calcined for two hours at 1300° C. Using Fourier transform infrared spectroscopy, scanning electron microscopy, and X-ray diffraction, structural analysis has been examined, and it shows clearly that a phase of the desired structure has formed. After that, the sample was pressed to make it highly dense and then sintered at 1300° C for 2 hrs. After that, its electromagnetic properties were examined using MPMS3 and VNA, showing good EMI shielding properties in the Ku band. It shows strong absorption peaks for different compositions around −23} dB. Al-Co concentrations were associated with increased coercivity, according to magnetic measurements made using an MPMS3 system.

title: High Gain Low RCS Broad-Band Antipodal Vivaldi Antenna
authors: Krithika Penagaluru, Soumya KK, Preeti Kumari, Vineet Gupta, Vijay Kumar Sutrakar
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: In this work, a high gain low radar cross section (RCS) Antipodal Vivaldi antenna (AVA) is designed operating in the frequency band of 2.2 GHz to 50 GHz. The baseline design considered from available literature is first validated using commercial electromagnetic solver. Subsequently, two different AVA designs are proposed with improved gain and reduced RCS. The Proposed AVA is having dimensions of length 35 mm and width 32 mm, designed on FR4 substrate with 1.6 mm thickness. Reflection coefficient, voltage standing wave ratio (VSWR), radiation pattern, gain, and RCS characteristics are studied in details. The full wave simulation results of proposed design show that the reflection coefficient is below -10 dB in the frequency range of 2.2 GHz to 50 GHz. Further, RCS analysis of proposed AVAs shows lower RCS compared to the reference antenna from 0 to 60 degree in azimuth angles.

title: Characterization of $4 \times 4$ Scalar Metasurface Loaded Printed Dipole Antenna for Resonance Frequency Tuning and Cross-Pol Modulation
authors: Anusuya Nandi, Swarnadipto Ghosh, Chinmoy Saha, M. Jaleel Akhtar
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This study presents a dual-layer <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \times 4$</tex> scalar metasurface-loaded printed dipole antenna (MLPDA) designed for wideband frequency tuning and cross-polarization modulation. The antenna employs a rotation-based control mechanism, where the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \times 4$</tex> scalar metasurface and printed dipole antenna is individually rotated. The <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \times 4$</tex> planar array consists of scalar meta unit-cells, each measuring <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.22 \lambda_{0} \times 0.22 \lambda_{0}$</tex> (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\lambda_{0}$</tex> at 7.3 GHz), strategically placed to modulate the radiation characteristics of a printed dipole (PD) located on the bottom layer. By rotating both the metasurface (MS) and the PD from 0° to 90°, the structure achieves effective impedance variation, enabling broad resonance frequency (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$f_{r}$</tex>) tuning and dynamic cross-polarization (XP) control. By exploring all possible rotational combinations, a curve-fitting model is developed, along with corresponding residual distribution to numerically characterize the radiation behavior of the MLPDA model.

title: A Novel Quadruple Boost Reduced Component Nine-Level Inverter with Self-Balancing Capability
authors: Md Mehedi Hasan, Md Ahsanul Alam, Meherun Nesa
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: In medium and high-power applications, multilevel inverters have gained popularity because of their capacity to synthesize output voltages with reduced harmonic distortion and device stress. However, conventional multilevel inverters suffer from drawbacks such as large component counts, complex control, and capacitor voltage imbalance. This paper presents a novel nine-level inverter topology with quadruple boost and inherent self-balancing capability while minimizing the number of component count. Utilizing only 8 switches, 3 diodes, and 2 capacitors, it produces nine-level output voltage. The capacitor voltage is systemically added to the input DC voltage source to boost the output voltage. The self-balancing property maintains equal capacitor voltages automatically through symmetrical charging-discharging cycles. A low switching-frequency based optimum nearest level modulation (ONLM) technique is applied to enhance the power quality. The topology is modeled and simulated in MATLAB/Simulink under resistive and inductive loads. Results demonstrate a boosted output voltage four times higher than the input DC source, and a total harmonic distortion of 8.90% without any filter. With a properly designed LCL filter, the THD drops to 1.24% conforming IEEE standard. The superiority of the proposed circuit was compared with that of other recently published studies. The proposed inverter is well suited for renewable energy integration, electric vehicles, and stand-alone power conversion applications.

title: Maximizing Learning Outcomes Through Tutorials with AI Engagement using SmartBooks
authors: Charliemaign Stanley S. Cruz, Jhoanne Mae C. Toriaga, Jomar Adams Ganding, Shealtiel William S. Chan, Lance Austin G. Elizaga, Marlou Del N. Tarranco
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Education and Multimedia Technology
abstract: None

title: Inference-Time Enhancement of Generative Robot Policies via Predictive World Modeling_supp1-3673995.mp4
authors: Han Qi
abstract: We present generative predictive control (GPC), a framework for inference-time enhancement of pretrained behavior-cloning policies. Rather than retraining or fine-tuning, GPC augments a frozen diffusion policy at deployment by coupling it with a predictive world model. Concretely, we train an action-conditioned world model on expert demonstrations and random exploration rollouts to forecast the consequences of action proposals produced by the diffusion policy, then perform lightweight online planning that ranks and refines these proposals via model-based look-ahead. This combination of a generative prior with predictive foresight enables test-time adaptation. Across diverse robotic manipulation tasks—state- and vision-based, in simulation and on real hardware—GPC consistently outperforms standard behavior cloning and compares favorably to other inference-time adaptation baselines.

title: AerialExtreMatch: A Benchmark for Extreme-View Image Matching and Localization_supp1-3673915.pdf
authors: Shen Yan
abstract: Image matching serves as a core component for UAV localization guided by satellite imagery. However, this task remains highly challenging due to the extreme viewpoint discrepancies between low-altitude UAV images and nadir-view satellite maps. Existing datasets primarily focus on ground-level or high-altitude UAV imagery, lacking sufficient coverage of the geometric variations typical of real aerial scenarios. To address these limitations, we introduce AerialExtreMatch, a large-scale, high-fidelity dataset tailored for extreme-view image matching and UAV localization. It consists of approximately 1.5 million synthetic image pairs rendered from high-quality 3D scenes, simulating diverse UAV and satellite viewpoints to enable robust training of image matching models. To support fine-grained evaluation, we construct a hierarchical benchmark with 32 difficulty levels. These are defined using three geometric criteria: overlap ratio, scale variation, and pitch difference. In addition, we collect a real-world UAV localization dataset with geo-aligned reference maps of varying visual quality. Extensive experiments involving 16 representative detector-based and detector-free methods demonstrate that models trained on AerialExtreMatch achieve substantial performance gains in both image matching and real-world localization under extreme-view conditions. The dataset and code are available on https://github.com/Xecades/AerialExtreMatch https://github.com/Xecades/AerialExtreMatch.

title: Vision-Based Real-Time 3D Position Sensor for Spherical Actuators Using ICP Pose Estimation
authors: Minoh Jeong, Junkyu Kim, Kyu-Sung Lee, Minki Kim
abstract: This paper presents a vision-based real-time 3D position sensor for spherical actuators using Iterative Closest Point (ICP) pose estimation. Unlike conventional rotary motors with fixed axes, spherical actuators enable omnidirectional rotation, which makes traditional one-dimensional sensing methods, such as encoders, Hall sensors, and resolvers, unsuitable for accurate orientation measurement. To address this challenge, we propose a non-contact sensing approach that reconstructs three-dimensional surface point clouds from monocular camera images and estimates the full 3D orientation of the spherical rotor. Arbitrary and non-uniform visual patterns are applied to the rotor surface, and both intentional patterns and natural surface imperfections (e.g., scratches, dust, and wear) are exploited as geometric features for pose estimation. Edge features are extracted using Canny edge detection, reconstructed into 3D point clouds through a geometrically consistent mapping model, and aligned via ICP to estimate the spherical angles θ and ϕ. Experimental validation is conducted using a spherical rotor with a diameter of 15 cm. Under single-axis rotations, a minimum mean absolute error of 0.45° in θ is achieved after pixel-per-revolution calibration. Under dynamic multi-axis rotational motions, the estimated tangential azimuth direction ϕ exhibits compact zero-centered error distributions within approximately 0.2°– 0.9°, confirming consistent 3D directional estimation. Long-term stability analysis further shows that cumulative angular error accumulation remains approximately 0.15° per degree under appropriate sensing conditions. A computational architecture comparison demonstrates the feasibility of real-time operation. The complete processing pipeline, including image preprocessing, 3D reconstruction, and ICP-based pose estimation, achieves a total processing time below 32 ms per frame, satisfying real-time requirements at 30 fps. These results confirm that the proposed vision-based sensor provides a robust, non-contact, and practical solution for real-time 3D orientation sensing in spherical actuator systems.

title: GAIN: Global-Atomic INteraction Graph for Few-Shot Class-Incremental Learning
authors: Fan Lyu, Linglan Zhao, Chengyan Liu, Yinying Mei, Zhang Zhang, Baoqing Yu, Tao Zhang, Fuyuan Hu, Liang Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Few-shot class-incremental learning (FSCIL) aims to develop models that can continually learn novel classes from only a few training samples while retaining knowledge of previously learned classes. The primary challenges of FSCIL lie in the limited available data in novel sessions, which can cause severe overfitting and exacerbate the catastrophic forgetting of novel classes. Prior studies have demonstrated the effectiveness of modeling graph relationships in few-shot learning, which can alleviate overfitting through feature aggregation. Building on these insights, we extend the concept to the incremental learning scenario and propose a Global-Atomic INteraction (GAIN) graph learning framework for FSCIL. Specifically, we introduce an Atomic-session Graph Network (AGN) to capture relationships among samples within each session, enabling the aggregation of similar samples and the extraction of more representative global-session features. Additionally, we design a Global-session Graph Network (GGN) that connects global-session features across base and novel classes, facilitating knowledge transfer between sessions and enhancing overall learning performance. Finally, we utilize a multi-stage strategy for training the GAIN model, which suppresses the training challenges posed by limited data in incremental learning sessions. The multi-stage training strategy is designed to build a GAIN graph from base to novel sessions and improve the capacity via an extra pseudo-incremental stage. Extensive experiments on three widely used FSCIL benchmarks demonstrate that our approach consistently outperforms existing methods and establishes new state-of-the-art performance. Our code will be released upon acceptance.

title: Full-Field Numerical On-Orbit Autonomous Calibration Method and Accuracy Assessment of Star Tracker
authors: Haijun Wang, Xuedi Chen, Haiyang Zhan, Xinyuan Liu, Fei Xing, Qun Hao
abstract: The star tracker is the most accurate attitude determination device that guarantees the success of a spacecraft mission. On-orbit calibration of star trackers is essential to correct the deviation of geometric parameters caused by fluctuations of space-environment conditions. However, traditional calibration methods are mostly based on parametric models and involve iterative optimization calculations, making it difficult to effectively correct full-field distortion and achieve autonomous on-orbit calibration. Therefore, we developed a full-field numerical on-orbit autonomous calibration method. During the on-orbit stable operation, the satellite drives star trackers to scan and image the star map, while the star trackers continuously accumulate residuals between the measured and theoretical star positions. When stars cover the entire field of view, the refined grid-based numerical calibration model of the distortion residuals is established. Subsequently, the precise correction of the full-field distortion is performed via bilinear interpolation. The proposed method has been validated in SuperView Neo1-03. The mean distortion residuals after calibration are reduced to approximately 0.07 pixels. The attitude determination accuracy can achieve 0.321″(1σ). The method enabled the satellite to become China's commercial optical remote sensing satellite with the highest geolocation accuracy.

title: ARACHNID: A Neuromorphic Camera Array System for Space Situational Awareness_supp1-3669869.pdf
authors: Esteban Vera

title: Vision-Based Autonomous Drone Landing on Moving Platforms With Uncertain Motion via Deep Reinforcement Learning_supp1-3674011.mp4
authors: Hyondong Oh
abstract: This paper addresses vision-based autonomous landing of quadrotor drones on moving platforms with uncertain motion. Vision is attractive due to its low weight, low cost, and ability to provide direct relative observations without global reference frames. However, traditional visual landing relies on requiring accurate estimation and tuning, which limits robustness. Deep reinforcement learning (DRL) offers a data-driven alternative but often degrades under motion uncertainty or intermittent visual loss from a limited field of view (FOV). The key challenge is active perception, maintaining visual observability of the landing pad under FOV constraints during aggressive maneuvers. To address this challenge, we propose a vision-based DRL framework that jointly learns perception, estimation, and control, guided by an active-perception reward that couples visibility maintenance with control performance for stable touchdown. Simulation results demonstrate robustness over visual servoing and existing DRL baseline, including landings on a platform moving at speeds up to 8 m/s under limited visibility. Real-world experiments further confirm the feasibility and stability of the proposed approach.

title: Absolute Tropospheric Refractivity Tomography using Multi-angle Spaceborne D-InSAR
authors: Yuanhao Li, Tonghua Ran, Cheng Liu, Zhiyang Chen, Junpeng Ren, Xin Xie, Yuxiao Wang, Cheng Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: High spatial resolution three-dimensional (3-D) tropospheric refractivity is important for the inversion of tropospheric parameters and accurate analysis of meteorological activities. Currently, spaceborne differential interferometric synthetic aperture radar (D-InSAR) is a novel tool for high spatial resolution tropospheric refractivity measurement in the absence of obvious surface deformation. However, due to the two-dimensional (2-D) imaging constraint of synthetic aperture radar (SAR), only line-of-sight (LOS) integrated tropospheric refractivity can be captured, resulting in the loss of three-dimensional (3-D) structure information of troposphere. Moreover, traditional D-InSAR measurement results can only reflect the relative change of tropospheric refractivity between the two observation moments of interferometric SAR pair. The two drawbacks limit the effectiveness of D-InSAR-inversed tropospheric products in meteorological applications. To address the issues, this paper proposes a tropospheric refractivity tomography method by time-series joint processing of multi-angle spaceborne D-InSAR derived tropospheric refractivity and numerical weather prediction (NWP) data. The approach leverages simultaneous or nearly simultaneous multi-angle measurements from a multi-satellite SAR system or a multi-beam SAR system to retrieve 3-D tropospheric refractivity, and incorporates NWP data for absolute recovery. The simulation results show the method can achieve high-accuracy 3-D absolute tropospheric refractivity, with sub-kilometer resolution and root mean square error (RMSE) lower than 10 under different weather conditions. Using the TanDEM-X and SUPERVIEW NEO-2 constellations to verify the proposed method, the cross-validation accuracy of the retrieved tropospheric refractivity with ECMWF, GFS and local radiosonde is less than 13.

title: Blockchain-Based Reputation-Driven Computing Scheme for Space-Air-Ground Integrated Edge Computing Networks
authors: Sicheng Liu, Wenxiao Shi, Xue Wang, Zhu Han
abstract: Space-air-ground integrated edge computing (SAGI-EC) networks enhance the quality of service (QoS) through seamless access, flexible deployment, and scalable resources. While most studies focus on optimizing computation offloading algorithms, challenges such as incentive mechanism design, data trustworthiness, and device selfishness remain understudied, hindering SAGI-EC network adoption. To address the above challenges, in this article, we first propose a SAGI-EC framework based on a reputation incentive mechanism and introduce reputation as a metric to measure the reliability and trustworthiness of devices. Then, we employ a multi-weight subjective logic model to calculate device reputation values, propose an interaction weight update method based on task importance degree, and design a reputation blockchain for storing and managing device reputation data. Moreover, we propose a reputation-based space-air-ground integrated edge computing network (RSIEC) algorithm that dynamically updates interaction data and task priorities. Numerical results indicate that the proposed scheme can effectively reduce the average total time delay and improve the tasks' success rate by suppressing and filtering unreliable network devices.

title: Efficient Resource Allocation for RSMA-Based Semantic Image Transmission With Shared Knowledge Base
authors: Lei Chen, Wei Wu, Feng Tian
abstract: This paper studies resource allocation for multi-user semantic image transmission using rate-splitting multiple access (RSMA) with a shared knowledge base. The knowledge base stores common semantic features to reduce cross-user redundancy, while RSMA transmits shared semantics via common streams and user-specific content via private streams. The core challenge lies in the mutual dependence between semantic partitioning and resource allocation, coupled with time-varying channels and evolving user demands requiring long-term optimization. We develop a hybrid reinforcement learning framework addressing this coupled optimization by integrating proximal policy optimization (PPO) for power management and deep deterministic policy gradient (DDPG) for knowledge base adaptation. The framework designs a quality-of-experience (QoE) reward function balancing semantic fidelity with transmission delay. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves superior QoE performance, accelerated convergence, and reduced latency compared to traditional methods.

title: Spatiotemporal Decoupled Learning for Spiking Neural Networks_supp1-3671461.pdf
authors: Jibin Wu
abstract: Spiking neural networks (SNNs) have gained significant attention for their potential to enable energy-efficient artificial intelligence (AI). However, effective and efficient training of SNNs remains an unresolved challenge. While backpropagation through time (BPTT) achieves high accuracy, it incurs substantial memory overhead. In contrast, biologically plausible local learning methods are more memory-efficient but struggle to match the accuracy of BPTT. To bridge this gap, we propose spatiotemporal decoupled learning (STDL), a novel training framework that decouples the spatial and temporal dependencies to achieve both high accuracy and training efficiency for SNNs. Specifically, to achieve spatial decoupling, STDL partitions the network into smaller subnetworks, each of which is trained independently using an auxiliary network. To address the decreased synergy among subnetworks resulting from spatial decoupling, STDL constructs each subnetwork’s auxiliary network by selecting the largest subset of layers from its subsequent network layers under a memory constraint. Furthermore, STDL decouples dependencies across time steps to enable efficient online learning. Extensive evaluations on seven static and event-based vision datasets demonstrate that STDL consistently outperforms local learning methods and achieves comparable accuracy to the BPTT method with considerably reduced GPU memory cost. Notably, STDL achieves <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$4\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> reduced GPU memory than BPTT on the ImageNet dataset. Therefore, this work opens up a promising avenue for memory-efficient SNN training. Code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/ChenxiangMA/STDL</uri>

title: Intelligent Reinforcement Learning and Control for Humanoid Locomotion Using Self-Triggered Adaptive Dynamic Programming
authors: Xiaoyu Wu, Chao Cun, Lijun Liu, Haisheng Xia, Zhijun Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Humanoid robots are increasingly deployed in complex environments, where achieving stable and robust locomotion remains a major challenge due to model uncertainties, payload variations, and external disturbances. Model-based control approaches, such as whole-body control (WBC) combined with model predictive control (MPC), can generate dynamically feasible motion trajectories, but their performance strongly depends on the accuracy of the underlying robot model and may degrade under unmodeled dynamics. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a dual-layer locomotion control framework based on self-triggered adaptive dynamic programming (ADP). The upper layer employs an MPC–WBC pipeline to generate high-frequency nominal motion references using a nominal model, while the lower layer introduces a residual reinforcement learning (RL) controller that provides corrective torque inputs only when significant state deviations are detected. This residual structure enables adaptive compensation for modeling errors and external disturbances without interfering with the nominal controller. The ADP-based residual policy is learned online using a critic neural network, and a self-triggered mechanism is designed to reduce unnecessary learning updates and computational overhead. Rigorous theoretical analysis is provided to establish closed-loop stability of the proposed framework. Experimental results on a humanoid robot demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves locomotion robustness under disturbances while maintaining stable walking and turning behaviors.

title: ICECTE 2026 Title Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)

title: Frequency-Aware Discrepancy and Polynomial Loss Weighting for Multi-Source Domain Adaptation in Rolling Bearing Fault Diagnosis
authors: Bo Wang, Liangjiang Liu, Aimin Shi, Mengdi Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Multi-source fault diagnosis methods for rolling bearings under variable operating conditions suffer from limited generalization due to severe domain shifts and spectral inconsistencies. To address this issue, this paper proposes a novel Frequency-Aware and Weighted Multi-source Domain Adaptation Network (FWMDA-Net), which effectively leverages knowledge from multiple source domains to enhance diagnostic performance in an unlabeled target domain. A novel Frequency-Aware Multi-Scale Maximum Mean Square Discrepancy (FAMS-MMSD) is introduced to capture both class-wise and spectral discrepancies, enabling fine-grained alignment of class-conditional distributions while dynamically adjusting kernel bandwidths based on domain-specific frequency structures. Additionally, a polynomial loss weighting strategy is adopted to progressively adjust the trade-off between classification and alignment losses during training, thereby improving training stability and model generalization. Extensive experiments conducted on both public datasets and a laboratory bearing test platform demonstrate that FWMDA-Net achieves superior diagnostic accuracy and robustness compared with state-of-the-art single-source and multi-source domain adaptation approaches.

title: ICICSE 2025 Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Information Communication and Software Engineering (ICICSE)

title: Leaf-Tissue Interaction and Absorption Behavior Under 2.45 GHz Electromagnetic Radiation Exposure in Tectona Grandis
authors: Anurag Suresh, Harikrishnan V S, Aiswarya S, Meenu L, K A Unnikrishna Menon
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: Recent advances in wireless technology have led to the overexposure of living beings to electromagnetic radiation (EMR). The continuous exposure to these EMR creates health hazards for living communities based on the the amount of absorption and its interaction with different parts. It is dependent on the material characteristics of the tissue and the field distribution based on the frequency of exposure. This research work investigates the absorption characteristics of leaves of Tectona grandis (teak). The dielectric properties of the leaves were experimentally measured using the Keysight N1500A-001 solid dielectric probe kit. A microstrip patch antenna (MSA) operating at 2.45 GHz is designed as a source of EMR, and SAR analysis is performed using ANSYS Electronic Desktop 2025. The relationship of the SAR value with respect to the position, thickness, and area of the leaves is observed, and the SAR value of the leaf shows a linear relationship with the surface area. The SAR value ranges from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$13.058 \mathrm{W} / \text{kg}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$17.982 \mathrm{W} / \text{kg}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.567 \mathrm{W} / \text{kg}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.824 \mathrm{W} / \text{kg}$</tex> when the leaf is placed in the direction of main lobe and back lobe of the antenna respectively.

title: Integration Test CodeCoverage Report generator
authors: Abhirup Chatterjee
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Information Communication and Software Engineering (ICICSE)
abstract: Code coverage is widely used to evaluate the effectiveness of unit tests, but the coverage achieved by integration tests—validating real component interactions, data stores, and network boundaries—remains largely invisible in most pipelines. This paper presents the Integration Test Code Coverage (ITCC) framework, which makes integration coverage measurable, actionable, and easy to integrate into continuous workflows. ITCC instruments the application under test with JaCoCo, performs a local deployment on Jenkins, executes the integration suite, collects coverage artifacts, and generates reports at statement, method, and class levels. These reports help quality engineers identify untested execution paths and design scenarios to close coverage gaps.ITCC is non-intrusive, requiring no code changes, integrating with a Jenkins pipeline job, and reusing standard JaCoCo formats. It enables (i) targeted expansion of integration scenarios, (ii) a quality gate to enforce minimum thresholds, (iii) trend tracking across builds, and (iv) pull-request feedback highlighting uncovered changes. While unit coverage remains essential for fast feedback, ITCC reveals whether end-to-end flows exercise critical paths—APIs, database calls, messaging, and security checks—helping teams detect high-impact defects earlier. The result is a balanced, data-driven test portfolio and greater confidence in production readiness.

title: Multi-Robot Task Allocation: Impact of Limited Information Sharing
authors: Jayakisan Nayak, Satwik Mondal, Sudipta Saha, Chayan Sarkar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: Multi-Robot Systems (MRS) are becoming increasingly vital across various domains. In many practical applications, inadequate infrastructure necessitates decentralized operations, making inter-robot data sharing a critical challenge. As the scale of the system grows, both in terms of the number of robots and the operational area, achieving effective system-wide (global) data sharing becomes more difficult, which in turn affects the efficiency of task allocation in large-scale MRS. Emerging 5G network technologies provide facilities for localized data sharing, which occurs much faster compared to global ones. In this work, we study multi-robot task allocation under 5G-assisted localized inter-robot communication. Specifically, we provide a comprehensive comparison of three distinct well-known approaches. Our findings reveal that greedy selection-based strategies perform poorly when information availability is limited. Interestingly, task allocation strategies based on regret-maximization heuristics perform better under restricted information conditions than with full system visibility. Furthermore, our results show that a multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) strategy outperforms all other approaches, exhibiting minimal performance degradation even with significantly constrained information sharing.

title: MSD-LLM: Predicting Ship Detention in Port State Control Inspections with Large Language Model
authors: Jiongchao Jin, Xiuju Fu, Xiaowei Gao, Tao Cheng, Ran Yan
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Maritime transportation is the backbone of global trade, making ship inspection essential for ensuring maritime safety and environmental protection. Port State Control (PSC), conducted by national ports, enforces compliance with safety regulations, with ship detention being the most severe consequence, impacting both ship schedules and company reputations. Traditional machine learning methods for ship detention prediction are limited by the capacity of representation learning and thus suffer from low accuracy. Meanwhile, autoencoder-based deep learning approaches face challenges due to the severe data imbalance in learning historical PSC detention records. To address these limitations, we propose Maritime Ship Detention with Large Language Models (MSD-LLM), integrating a dual robust subspace recovery (DSR) layer-based autoencoder with a progressive learning pipeline to handle imbalanced data and extract meaningful PSC representations. Then, a large language model groups and ranks features to identify likely detention cases, enabling dynamic thresholding for flexible detention predictions. Extensive evaluations on 31,707 PSC inspection records from the Asia-Pacific region show that MSD-LLM outperforms state-of-the-art methods more than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$12 \%$</tex> on Area Under the Curve (AUC) for Singapore ports. Additionally, it demonstrates robustness to real-world challenges, making it adaptable to diverse maritime risk assessment scenarios.

title: BatchEnsemble-Based Perception Uncertainty Quantification for Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Zhengyi Li, Hongyu Hu, Yang Xing, Chen Lv
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Quantifying uncertainty significantly enhances the reliability of perception in autonomous vehicles and provides more comprehensive environmental information for downstream modules. However, most existing perception methods lack the capacity to effectively estimate the associated uncertainty. To address this gap, we propose a BatchEnsemble-based network for uncertainty quantification in 3D object detection using point cloud data. Specifically, a BatchEnsemble-based convolutional layer is designed to reduce the memory overhead associated with ensemble-based paradigms. Building upon this, a series of probabilistic object detection networks are constructed by directly modeling object attributes using multivariate Gaussian distributions, thereby enabling the parallel extraction of both object features and their associated variances. Subsequently, an uncertainty-aware fusion strategy is introduced to integrate and filter multiple detection results based on an uncertainty quantification metric—namely, the Uncertainty Index—thereby yielding more reliable and comprehensive outputs. The proposed method is validated on the KITTI dataset. Experimental results demonstrate its competitive accuracy performance and effectiveness across various scenarios, including objects of differing detection difficulty, identification of false-positive results, and under adverse conditions such as snowy weather and sensor degradation.

title: Optimizing Federated Learning Performance in GEO Satellite Networks using Successive Convex Approximation
authors: Phuc Hao Do, Tran Duc Le, Aleksandr Berezkin, Ruslan Kirichek
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) in GEO satellite networks faces challenges due to high communication latency and limited bandwidth. This paper proposes a Successive Convex Approximation (SCA) approach for client selection to optimize FL performance, balancing latency reduction with model accuracy. By formulating client selection as a multi-objective optimization problem, SCA iteratively solves convex subproblems to select clients based on accuracy, bandwidth, and delay metrics. This mitigates GEO network delays, ensuring efficient model aggregation. Experiments on standard datasets show that SCA outperforms random and greedy selection, significantly reducing latency while maintaining high accuracy and faster convergence. The method’s robustness across diverse data distributions suits applications like remote sensing and disaster response. Future work includes quantization techniques, real satellite testbeds, and adaptive parameter weighting for improved client diversity.

title: Deep Learning-Based Calibration Pattern Detection for Enhanced RGB-LiDAR Sensor Fusion
authors: Sushant Hanumantrao Shivankar
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Information and Communication Networks (ICICN)
abstract: Accurate sensor calibration is vital for improving the performance of multi-modal systems in robotics and autonomous navigation. This study introduces a novel approach that leverages deep learning-based segmentation models to automate the detection of calibration pattern borders in RGB and LiDAR data. By employing advanced neural network architectures and classical computer vision techniques, the system eliminates the need for manual labeling. Experimental results demonstrate enhanced precision, with the U-Net model trained from scratch achieving an average pixel accuracy of 93.4% and an Intersection-over-Union (IoU) score of 0.79. Furthermore, integration with the ATOM framework led to an 18% reduction in reprojection error compared to manual annotations, validating the method's effectiveness in improving extrinsic calibration outcomes. These results confirm the system’s capability to enhance both accuracy and efficiency, offering a robust and scalable calibration solution for real-world robotic applications.

title: Social-Interaction-Aware Trajectory Prediction with Attention of Heterogeneous Traffic Participants
authors: Dongming Han, Sizhe Cheng, Jinxiang Wang, Zhenwu Fang, Duanfeng Chu, Guodong Yin
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Trajectory prediction is a critical component for efficient decision-making and safe planning in autonomous driving systems. However, the behavioral uncertainty of heterogeneous participants in mixed-traffic environments poses significant challenges to driving safety. This paper proposes a social-interaction-aware trajectory prediction (SITP) method that incorporates the interaction features of heterogeneous traffic participants (HTPs). First, the temporal and spatial features are extracted in parallel using a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network and a Graph Convolutional Network (GCN), respectively. By integrating social interaction information, including participants' relative motion states and physical size, the extraction of spatial features can be enhanced. Second, an interaction attention module with interaction mask and absence mask is designed to extract the attention distribution toward the social interactions of different participants. Finally, a temporal decoder based on LSTM is employed to generate the predicted trajectory. The proposed method is trained and evaluated on the InD dataset. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed SITP method achieves superior long-term prediction accuracy compared to baseline algorithms and exhibits strong adaptability across scenarios with varying levels of complexity.

title: MEC-based Smart Surveillance in 5G/6G Networks: Intrusion Detection and Loitering Monitoring
authors: Deveshwar Singh, Samaresh Bera, Ankur Bansal
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: Mobile Edge Computing (MEC), empowered by deep learning techniques, plays a pivotal role in enabling real-time service delivery for applications with stringent latency requirements in 5G and beyond-5G (6G) networks. Although numerous studies have proposed resource allocation and model placement schemes for MEC environments, most of these efforts remain theoretical and are validated primarily through simulations or emulations.In this paper, we present a practical implementation of an MEC-based smart surveillance system deployed and tested on a 5G testbed. The system aims to detect human presence, identify intrusions, and monitor loitering behavior in real time. To achieve this, we employ two state-of-the-art deep learning models – InsightFace for intrusion detection and YOLOv8-Pose for loitering analysis. The models are deployed at the MEC of the 5G testbed to minimize latency with optimize inference efficiency. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system can accurately detect and monitor individuals within a few hundred milliseconds, validating the effectiveness of MEC-based deep learning deployment for real-time surveillance. Furthermore, we discuss key challenges, limitations, and lessons learned from our testbed-based implementation.

title: DRL-Based Optimization of LQR for Virtual Coupling Train Interval Control
authors: Feijie Gong, Wei Shangguan, Hongyu Song, Yichen Dun, Baigen Cai
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Virtual Coupling (VC) allows trains to operate in formation, remarkably promoting operational efficiency. The inter-train interval control is crucial for the safe operation of VC. To address this, this study proposes a Linear Quadratic Regulator (LQR) interval controller based on DRL optimization. A quadratic optimization model is built using the LQR state feedback control law, and the control output is derived by solving the Riccati equation. To overcome the low accuracy caused by suboptimal LQR parameters, the Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (DDPG) algorithm optimizes the parameters. An Actor-Critic network tunes control parameters, with LQR parameters as DDPG actions, interval control errors as states, and reward maximization linked to error reduction. Optimized parameters are achieved through iterative learning. This approach is stable while improving the accuracy of the interval control by 68.5%.

title:
Design and Simulation of a Novel All-Inorganic CZTSSe/ZnSnN
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Dual-Absorber Solar Cell Achieving 31.52% Efficiency
authors: Imtiaz Ahmed, Badhan Halder, Imrul Siddiki, Surovi Akhter Eity, Afshana Begum
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: The development of environmentally friendly perovskite-based solar cells requires researchers to solve several difficult challenges which need to be addressed for efficient and stable device creation. This study presents a novel all-inorganic dual-absorber solar cell structure with power conversion efficiency (PCE) exceeding 31%. The proposed configuration is optimized through the SCAPS-1D simulation platform, emphasizing the optical synergy of two absorber layers. It integrates a narrow-bandgap perovskite material- CZTSSe, as top absorber with a wide-bandgap ZnSnN<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> bottom absorber. The studied device architecture - FTO/ZnO/CZTSSe/ZnSnN<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>/NiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</inf>/Au, theoretically achieved an open circuit voltage V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">oc</inf> = 0.8915 V, short circuit current density J<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">sc</inf> = 42.18 mA/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, fill factor (FF) = 83.75%. The materials required to build this solar cell are composed of non-toxic and earth-abundant substances. Moreover, being all inorganic, the prospective device has better longevity. Therefore, this work confirms the viability of sustainable materials for next-generation high-efficiency photovoltaic devices with enhanced photo-conversion performance.

title: Quantifying Fault Causality via Adaptive Mode Decomposition: A Novel Framework for Multi-Fault Diagnosis in Noisy Aero-Engine Rotors
authors: Haoyang Wu, Zhiwen Zhao, Xiongfei Zhai, Yankai Wang
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: To address the diagnostic challenges of strong coupling and spectral aliasing in multi-fault scenarios of aero-engine rotors, an adaptive feature mode decomposition method integrated with Granger causality analysis is proposed. By quantifying the physical modality-fault relationships through Granger causality, interpretable separation of fault components is achieved. This methodology successfully resolves the decoupling of typical rotor faults—rubbing, misalignment, and imbalance—under strong 150Hz high-frequency noise interference, enabling precise fault causal tracing.

title: Structure Segmentation for Performance Improvement of Planar Antenna and Its MIMO Implementation
authors: Huma Khalid, Rajeev Jyoti, Amalendu Patnaik
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper introduces the segmentation concept, implemented through a machine learning approach, to enhance the isolation of a MIMO antenna system. Segmentation of the radiating part of a planar antenna not only affects the coupling due to radiated waves but also disturbs the surface wave in a MIMO scenario. Taking a candidate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 2$</tex> MIMO in the DSRC band for the V2X application, this article demonstrates a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 4. 1 5} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex> increase in isolation compared to its classical counterpart, without using any isolation enhancement technique, which is usually implemented with the insertion of a third structure between two consecutive antennas. Although the primary goal is to enhance the isolation, through segmentation, better impedance matching is introduced without compromising the resonant frequency. The abovementioned characteristics are verified by simulation in this paper.

title: Design and Analysis of a Dual-Band Wearable Antenna for WBAN Applications
authors: Bhavani Vantakala, Thanmai Gadagamma, Sulakshana Chilukuri, Lakshman Kukkadapu
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: A dual-band wearable antenna is proposed for Wireless Body Area Network (WBAN) applications using a flexible denim substrate with dielectric constant 1.7. The overall dimensions of the antenna are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.846 \lambda 0 \times 0.769 \lambda 0 \times 0.019 \lambda 0$</tex> calculated at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.54 \text{GHz}, 5.76 \text{GHz}$</tex>. The proposed antenna is designed with a hollow arc-shaped slot on the left and a rectangular coil-structured slot on the right of the substrate. A Coplanar Waveguide (CPW) feed is utilized to improve impedance matching and broaden the bandwidth, making it well-suited for integration with wearable devices. The antenna exhibits resonances at 3.52 GHz and 5.76 GHz, aligning with the operational frequencies of WiMAX systems and WBAN frequency bands.
