When placed on a human phantom model to simulate on-body deployment, it achieves bandwidths of 1750 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{MHz}(49.4 \%)$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1780 \text{MHz}(31 \%)$</tex> at the respective frequencies. The use of a denim substrate ensures mechanical flexibility and wearability, making the antenna ideal for body worn communication systems. The proposed design offers balance between compact size, wide bandwidth and conformability, addressing the demands of modern wearable applications.

title: Surveillance-Based Spatial Temporal Traffic Accident Detection: A Novel Dataset and Tailored Algorithm
authors: Sixuan Xu, Tianran Zhang, Lei Zhao, Wei Zhou, Stefan Nastic, Chen Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Traffic Accident Detection (TAD) in surveillance videos is a critical task in Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS). However, current TAD does not analyze the fine-grained information of the specific accident, only identifies the existence or occurrence time of traffic accidents in a video. This study presents a novel Dataset named STTAD that covers fine-grained information such as multiple categories and their Spatial Temporal Occurrence Regions in surveillance videos. Moreover, a tailored deep learning algorithm named STFN is proposed for the implementation of Event-Level TAD. Experimental results demonstrate that STFN could effectively extract the video features and detect the Spatial Temporal Occurrence Regions of multiple accident categories, but further efforts are indeed needed in Event-Level TAD. The STTAD dataset and the tailored algorithm will be open-sourced for research use available through https://github.com/ZTR02/STTAD.git.

title: Multi-Stage Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for on-Demand Food-Delivery Services with a Mixed Fleet of Human Couriers and Drones
authors: Yulong Hu, Yali Du, Sen Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: This work studies a novel collaborative multimodal delivery system for urban food logistics, combining the complementary strengths of human couriers and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs). The system features a two-stage delivery process: human couriers execute the initial collection and transportation of orders to strategically located launchpads, while drones subsequently complete the final delivery segments to kiosks. To unleash the potentials of this promising hybrid system, we develop a Multi-Stage Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MSMARL) framework that synergistically combines decentralized decision-making through a Multi-Agent Markov Decision Process (MAMDP) with centralized coordination via dynamic bipartite matching. Courier and drone agents learn anticipatory drop-off and relocation policies in the MAMDP, while the matching mechanism periodically optimizes agent-task assignments using the learned value functions from MAMDP. To enable effective multiagent coordination learning, we develop a progressive multi-stage curriculum training strategy consisting of two key phases: an initial pretraining stage where agents learn independently under simplified collaborative assumptions, followed by a joint finetuning phase that optimizes the complete system through realistic interaction dynamics. Comprehensive experiment results based on Hong Kong food delivery data validate the effectiveness of our MS-MARL framework.

title: Opportunistic Communication using Intelligent Reflecting Surface for 6G Systems
authors: Kanchan Verma, Ankit Dhabriya, K S Arjun, K Vamshidhar, A G Sarvesha
publish: 2025 IEEE 6th International Women in Technology Conference (WINTECHCON)
abstract: Intelligent reflecting surfaces (IRSs) have emerged as a promising solution to enhance coverage and spectral efficiency in 6 G networks due to their flexible deployment and low cost. In this paper, we investigate the use of a low complexity opportunistic beamforming (OBF) scheme employing IRS with N elements for terahertz (THz) single-input single-output (SISO) communication systems, where the IRS has the same structural characteristics as the THz propagation channel. We derive the upper bound on asymptotic scaling laws of average throughput by considering random molecular absorption coefficient, which follows a gamma distribution. The upper bound is the function of signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), spread loss, array gains, IRS elements, distance between the IRS and each user, and the scale parameter of the beta distribution. Through Monte Carlo simulations, we validate our theoretical and simulation results. Moreover, we compare a THz channel-aware IRS-assisted OBF scheme with its non-channel-aware counterpart. The channel-aware scheme performs better due to its structural compatibility with the THz channel model. Finally, we evaluate the performance of both IRS-assisted OBF schemes with SISO systems against multiple-input single-output (MISO) systems. For MISO systems, we consider perfect beamforming with ideal channel state information (CSI), which entails significant training overhead, feedback requirements, and hardware complexity for THz systems. In contrast, IRS-assisted OBF schemes under SISO systems provide a compelling trade-off by achieving an $O\left(N^{2}\right)$ array gain with N reflecting elements, while simultaneously offering high energy efficiency and significantly reducing implementation complexity, training overhead, and hardware cost. These advantages make IRS-assisted OBF highly suitable for practical deployment in THz communication systems.

title: AEDHunter: Investigating AED Retrieval in the Real World via Gamified Mobile Interaction and Sensing
authors: Helinyi Peng, Akihito Taya, Yuuki Nishiyama, Kaoru Sezaki
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Interactive, Mobile, Wearable and Ubiquitous Technologies
abstract: None

title: Highly Directive V-Band Fabry-Perot Cavity Antenna for Train-to-Train Communication
authors: Z. A. Pandit Jibran, Kumud R. Jha, Satish K. Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: Proposed for V-band applications, a Fabry-Perot cavity antenna (FPCA) featuring an ultra-thin partially reflective surface (PRS) demonstrates exceptional performance is reported here. Full-wave simulations of the antenna indicate a maximum gain of 19.3 dBi and a bandwidth of 3.4 GHz at a center frequency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 0 ~ G H z}$</tex>, with a compact overall thickness of just 5.5 mm which may find its applications in train-to-train (T2T) millimeter wave communications.

title: Application of Video-Computer Psychodiagnostics to Assessing Individual Employee Psychological Characteristics
authors: Ekaterina Novikova, Mais Farkhadov, Vitaly Skopin
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: This paper examines the application of video-computer psychodiagnostics to assessing individual employee psychological characteristics when forming teams to perform complex and critical tasks in extreme conditions. The use of video-computer psychodiagnostics to form teams in extreme situations is a promising approach that combines computer vision, artificial intelligence, and data analysis to optimize actions in emergency situations. This paper also analyzes the pressing issue of improving the efficiency and safety of professional teams performing complex and critical tasks in extreme conditions (emergencies, military operations, space exploration, work in hazardous industries, etc.). The critical importance of considering the individual psychological characteristics of employees when forming such teams is substantiated. An innovative approach to assessing individual psychological characteristics using video-computer psychodiagnostics is presented. The need to address ethical issues and ensure the reliability of algorithms is emphasized. A conclusion is drawn regarding the significant potential of video-computer psychodiagnostics for optimizing personnel selection and team formation in extreme activities.

title: Analysis of Road Traffic Congestion Considering Individual Differences in Forward Movement Time
authors: Masaki Kimura, Shigeo Shioda
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: The time it takes for a driver to start moving their vehicle after the preceding vehicle has begun to move (forward movement time) depends on various factors including the driver's driving skills. It is known that differences in the forward movement time among drivers have a significant impact on the occurrence of congestion. This study aims to quantitatively evaluate the effect of variability in forward movement time on traffic congestion using a mathematical model, with the goal of developing congestion mitigation methods utilizing autonomous driving technology. Theoretical analysis and simulation results indicate that the conditions under which congestion occurs are strongly influenced by individual differences in forward movement time. Furthermore, the results obtained in this study show good agreement with congestion data from expressways in Japan.

title: Learning Traffic States and Fundamental Diagram via Physics-Informed Neural Operators
authors: Qi Gao, Xuan Di
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Traffic state estimation (TSE) reconstructs the spatio-temporal evolution of traffic states, such as density and velocity, from limited sensor observations, playing a pivotal role in traffic monitoring and control. Traditional TSE approaches mainly bifurcate into model-driven and data-driven methods, each facing distinct limitations in accuracy, generalizability, and data efficiency. Motivated by the recent success of hybrid learning paradigms, this paper introduces a novel framework that leverages Physics-Informed Neural Operators (PINO) to jointly learn the traffic state and underlying fundamental diagram parameters directly from partial observations. By embedding physical constraints into Neural Operators, the proposed method achieves superior generalization across varying traffic conditions without the need for retraining. Experiments on simulated traffic dynamics governed by Lighthill-Whitham-Richards (LWR) and Aw-Rascle-Zhang (ARZ) models, and real world Next Generation SIMulation (NGSIM) dataset demonstrate the performance of our approach compared against baseline methods in both traffic state reconstruction and model parameter estimation, highlighting its potential for real-time and scalable deployment in transportation systems.

title: LMI conditions for parameters of the one robust control algorithm
authors: Aliya Imangazieva
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: A robust algorithm for controlling a linear dynamic plant subject to external uncontrolled disturbances under conditions of parametric uncertainty of the plant model is considered. The control action is formed on the basis of the auxiliary loop method using observers of the system variables. The structure of the regulator is such that it allows first to form a special signal carrying information about external perturbations and parameter perturbations, and then to compensate its negative effect on the whole system. The input and output of the control system are scalar quantities. In this paper, we propose a new procedure for finding the parameters of the considered algorithm, based on the technique of linear matrix inequalities LMI. The search procedure is based on the requirement to the closed-loop system of stability property, formulated in the form of existence of Lyapunov function. The conditions for the closed-loop system in the form of linear matrix inequalities are obtained, which are solved with the help of program packages, for example, the YALMIP package of MATLAB environment.

title: Scalable Low Latency Cascaded EKF-based ROV Platform for Underwater Surveillance
authors: Ad-Deen Mahbub, Nainaiu Rakhaine, Mirja Nihal Baig, Mohammad Shahidur Rahman
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: This paper presents a low-cost, scalable underwater ROV that is capable of overcoming limitations like maneuver-ability, sensor modularity, and latency, which are commonly recognized in the case of existing platforms. The system is equipped with eight thrusters that allow full control of all six movement axes, a hierarchical controller, and Ethernet-based low-latency communications. The cascaded Extended Kalman-filter is modeled for AUV odometry estimation fusing low-cost Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU), pressure data & Sonar ranging sensors, lowering the mean positional error under ±5 cm, and the mean orientational error is within ±2.6°. Initial hydrodynamic simulations confirm the efficiency of the chosen thruster positions and control parameters. Indoor tests show that the device can perform stable movements in all three axes around 0.9m/s, maintain 50Hz communication, and run for more than two hours continuously under surveillance and maneuver. The findings suggest that the platform has better and more versatile application domains than current low-cost ROVs; thus, the platform is suitable for underwater surveillance and research activities like sonar SLAM.

title: Design of a Compact Sub-6 GHz Power Amplifier with Radial Stub-Based Bias Network for Harmonic Control
authors: Mahima Patel, Shrawan Kumar Patel, Debarati Sen, Mrinal Kanti Mandal
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a compact wideband RF power amplifier (PA) designed for sub- 6 GHz 5G applications. The present design employs a radial bias tee along with shunt capacitors to achieve enhanced harmonic suppression and improved RF-DC isolation, which in turn greatly simplifies the output matching network. The radial stub-based bias network facilitates wave shaping by effective harmonic termination and impedance transformation, contributing to increased poweradded efficiency (PAE). The PA is implemented using a 6-W GaN HEMT transistor and fabricated on a RO4003C substrate. Measured results demonstrate a gain of over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 ~ d B}$</tex> and saturated output power of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$33-36 \text{dBm}$</tex> across <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.5-5 \text{GHz}$</tex>. Peak drain efficiency and PAE reach 85% and 81%, respectively. The proposed solution occupying only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$26.3 \times 32.4 \text{mm}^{2}$</tex> offers a compact and efficient design suitable for high-performance RF front-end modules.

title: Label-Free Glucose Sensing Using a High-Q Terahertz Metamaterial Absorber
authors: Afzaal Ahmad, Muhammad Zubair, Vimal Kumar Chaudhary, Jalil Ur-Rehman Kazim, Muhammad Imran, Qammer Abbasi
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This work presents a highly sensitive terahertz (THz) metamaterial absorber tailored for glucose detection in aqueous environments. The absorber features a multi-resonant structure with four distinct absorption peaks at 2.02, 4.41, 5.06, and 5.39 THz, achieving absorption efficiencies of 99.8%, 99.2%, 96.4%, and 95.2%, respectively. Among these, the 5.06 THz resonance exhibits the highest quality factor of 265, making it ideal for high-resolution sensing applications. To evaluate its biosensing performance, a 1 micrometer thick analyte layer was modeled with refractive index values ranging from 1.3 3 (pure water) to 1.45 (30% glucose solution). The resulting resonance shift yielded a sensitivity of 262.5 GHz/RIU and a figure of merit (FOM) of 7.17, demonstrating the absorber's capability to detect subtle changes in glucose concentration. These results highlight the potential of the proposed design for non-invasive glucose monitoring and other biomedical sensing applications in the THz regime.

title: Development of Multichannel Spectral Complexes
authors: Anton Morozov, Vitold Pozhar
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: Multichannel technical complexes, which comprise several working together devices are widely used in science and applications. To develop such a system, one need to resolve a lot of engineering problems regarding consistency, flexibility and reliability of heterogeneous units interaction. This paper discusses general problems and principles of such instrument integration using as an example a multispectral imaging system, which contains several video cameras recording in different spectral ranges. Particular attention is paid to typical problems: lack of hardware synchronization, limited list of available control parameters and undocumented behavior of individual units. An approache ensuring modularity, scalability and fault tolerance is presented for the example. It presumes following techniques: design via interfaces, hierarchical organization of the control system, simulation models. The described approach provides integration process simplification, increased flexibility, and debugging time reduction. The presented approach seems to be general enough and can be applied to the development of other multichannel complexes for various purposes.

title: Toward Intelligent Secure Wireless Networks: Coupled-Phase STAR-RIS with DRL Optimization
authors: Gaurav K. Pandey, Devendra S. Gurjar, Suneel Yadav, Eugen Slapak, Juraj Gazda, Yuming Jiang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: This work investigates a simultaneously transmitting and reflecting reconfigurable intelligent surface (STAR-RIS)-assisted secure communication system with coupled phase-shifts. We consider a multiple-antenna base station (BS) transmitting confidential information to indoor and outdoor users via a STAR-RIS, utilizing the energy-splitting protocol. In this scenario, an indoor and an outdoor eavesdropper attempt to intercept signals from the BS. For this setup, we formulate a sum secrecy rate maximization problem by jointly designing beamforming vectors at the BS and the transmission and reflection coefficients of the STAR-RIS. To tackle the non-complex problem, we introduce a hybrid deep reinforcement learning approach that integrates dueling double deep Q-networks with soft actor-critic algorithms. Simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm in improving secrecy performance over baseline schemes. The findings further reveal the impact of coupled phase shifts, providing insights into the trade-offs between performance gains and the limitations introduced by coupled phase configurations.

title: Sum-rate Maximization of Active RIS-aided SWIPT Cooperative Underlay D2D-NOMA Networks
authors: Pranjul S. Chauhan, Raviteja Allu, Keshav Singh, Vimal Bhatia, Sudeep Biswas
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: In recent years, active reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (ARIS) have gained significant research attention due to their ability to change the phase shift and amplify the signal amplitude. In this paper, we incorporate an ARIS to enhance the performance of a simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) cooperative non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) assisted device-to-device (D2D) communication system. Specifically, we address the sum-rate maximization problem by optimizing joint beamforming, transmit power, and the power allocation at RBS, as well as phase shift matrices at ARIS. The original non-convex sum-rate optimization problem is decomposed into three sub-problems by using the Lagrangian dual transform, the quadratic transform, and alternative optimization. Then, these sub-problems are addressed using the successive convex approximation methodology. Simulations demonstrate the performance improvement of the proposed ARIS-aided underlay D2D-NOMA system over the benchmark schemes.

title: Design and Analysis of Platinum Diselenide ($\text{PtSe}_{2}$) Integrated Slotted Bowtie Antenna for 6G Communication (W-Band)
authors: Shakthi Priya M, Prince Gupta, Nalini Pareek, Dhairya Singh Arya
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents the design and development of a high-performance, antenna-integrated detector precisely tailored for W Band communication, with potential application in 6 G communication systems, by leveraging the distinct properties of two-dimensional (2D) thin film nanostructures. The technique incorporates a planar, radially slotted, rounded bow-tie antenna patterned on a dielectric substrate for efficient coupling. To enhance field localization and detector sensitivity, platinum diselenide (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{PtSe}_{2}$</tex>), a transition metal dichalcogenide (TMDC) known for its distinct optoelectronic properties, is placed at the feed location. The proposed antenna-integrated detector is designed to operate at the W Band, with its center frequency resonating at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 4 ~ G H z}$</tex>. The simulational results of the antenna demonstrate satisfactory performance characteristics, with a maximum gain of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2. 0 6 ~ d B}$</tex>, including a stable radiation pattern across the targeted frequency range (77 GHz to 107.5 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{G H z}$</tex>, as well as scattering parameters remaining below <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{- 1 0 ~ d B}$</tex>. The achievable bandwidth of the proposed antenna design is 46 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{G H z}$</tex>, and the effective channel conductance of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P t S e}_{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> films incorporated into the antenna structure is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 5 4 1 ~ m J}$</tex>.

title: Generalized Route Choice Modeling via Fusion of Structural Knowledge and LLM-Inferred Context
authors: Shuhan Qiu, Xiannan Huang, Guoyang Qin, Xuejian Chen, Jian Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Route choice modeling (RCM) underpins travel behavior analysis, traffic policy evaluation, and network performance forecasting. Despite advances from discrete choice models to deep learning, existing methods struggle to integrate implicit contextual factors—such as spatial semantics—beyond explicit link attributes. Here, we propose a generalized link-based RCM framework that fuses structural knowledge from classical models with Large Language Model (LLM)-inferred context to both streamline the modeling process and boost predictive performance. Our approach tokenizes link transitions, enabling the seamless integration of structured features with LLM-derived semantics to enhance expressiveness and behavioral fidelity. We identify three key mechanisms: (1) granularity alignment between links and tokens for structured-context fusion; (2) implicit information compensation through LLMs' encoded environmental information; and (3) controlled generalization anchored by domain constraints and trajectory data. Experiments on diverse OD transfer tasks show that our LLM-enhanced models reduce path flow Jensen-Shannon divergence by up to 45 % compared to traditional Recursive Logit (RL), and outperform deep RL (DRL) models by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 0 - 2 0 \%}$</tex> on average. These results advance a unified paradigm for integrating data, structural knowledge, and contextual information to improve traveler behavior modeling.

title: RADE: Learning Risk-Adjustable Driving Environment via Multi-Agent Conditional Diffusion
authors: Jiawei Wang, Xintao Yan, Yao Mu, Haowei Sun, Zhong Cao, Henry X. Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Generating safety-critical scenarios in high-fidelity simulations offers a promising and cost-effective approach for efficient testing of autonomous vehicles. Existing methods typically rely on manipulating a single vehicle's trajectory through sophisticated designed objectives to induce adversarial interactions, often at the cost of realism and scalability. In this work, we propose the Risk-Adjustable Driving Environment (RADE), a simulation framework that generates statistically realistic and risk-adjustable traffic scenes. Built upon a multiagent diffusion architecture, RADE jointly models the behavior of all agents in the environment and conditions their trajectories on a surrogate risk measure. Unlike traditional adversarial methods, RADE learns risk-conditioned behaviors directly from data, preserving naturalistic multi-agent interactions with controllable risk levels. To ensure physical plausibility, we incorporate a tokenized dynamics check module that efficiently filters generated trajectories using a motion vocabulary. We validate RADE on the real-world rounD dataset, demonstrating that it preserves statistical realism across varying risk levels and naturally increases the likelihood of safety-critical events as the desired risk level grows up. Our results highlight RADE's potential as a scalable and realistic tool for AV safety evaluation.

title: Uncertainty-Aware Remaining Useful Life Prediction with Bayesian Deep Learning: A Function-Space Variational Inference Approach
authors: Wei Wang, Zhiqiang Cai, Han Wang, Mingli Liu, Shubin Si
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Remaining Useful Life (RUL) prediction is a core component of Prognostics and Health Management (PHM) for industrial equipment, providing a solid foundation for risk-aware maintenance and operational decision-making. However, traditional point estimation methods struggle to cope with the complexity of operational environments and the diversity of degradation mechanisms, leading to limitations in the reliability and practical value of prediction results. Bayesian Neural Networks (BNNs) offer a theoretical basis for uncertainty modeling in RUL prediction, but most existing approaches rely on parameter-space variational approximations, which are constrained by high-dimensional and nonlinear structures and therefore cannot accurately characterize complex posterior distributions. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a Bayesian deep learning method for RUL prediction based on Function-Space Variational Inference (FSVI). By modeling and optimizing the predictive distribution directly in function space, this approach overcomes the limitations of traditional parameter-space inference and significantly enhances the expressiveness and reliability of uncertainty quantification. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms mainstream parameter-space approaches in both predictive accuracy and uncertainty modeling, providing a novel theoretical foundation and technical pathway for trustworthy uncertainty quantification and prediction in equipment health management.

title: Simulating Autonomous Quadcopter for Aerosol Treatment of a Farmland
authors: Sekou Diane
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: Aerosol treatment of agricultural crops is often performed in order to protect them from insects, weeds and diseases. The paper presents the modeling results of a technological process for aerosol treatment of a farmland site with a spraying quadcopter. A methodology for modeling an autonomous drone is given. Simplified kinematic and technological models of the quadcopter are described. A graph algorithm for planning the flight path is proposed, which accounts for variable aerodynamic properties of the sprayed aerosol mixture and uncertainty of wind force and direction. Two criteria were formalized (precision and uniformity of spraying) to assess the effectiveness of treating the farmland zone with an aerosol mixture. Several ways to further enhance the presented approach are outlined.

title: Robust Steering Control of an Advanced Mobility Unit Considering the Communication Time Delay
authors: Zihong Li, Ning Zhang, Hangyu Lu, Jun Wang, Cheng Wang, Guodong Yin
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: A vehicle chassis equipped with advanced mobility units exhibits high mechanical decoupling, rendering its steering control particularly vulnerable to communication delays and external disturbances. To address this issue, a disturbance-observer-based robust steering control method is proposed considering a communication time delay. First, a steering dynamics model of the advanced mobility unit is established about the kingpin axis, with the communication time delay modeled as a constant. Subsequently, a composite controller comprising model-based feedforward compensation, proportional-derivative (PD) feedback, and an outer-loop disturbance observer is designed. By employing a Lyapunov-Krasovskii functional, linear matrix inequalities incorporating both delay and disturbance terms are derived to synthesize the PD controller gains. Simulation results demonstrate that, under a 30 ms communication time delay and triangular torque disturbances of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{\pm 1 0 0 ~ N m}$</tex>, the steady-state tracking error for constant steering commands is limited within 1 deg, and the tracking error for sinusoidal steering commands is nearly negligible, while the system remains stable throughout. These results validate the effectiveness of the proposed method in suppressing the effects of communication time delay and disturbances while achieving high-precision steering angle tracking performance.

title: FiReFly: Fair Distributed Receding Horizon Planning for Multiple UAVs
authors: Nicole Fronda, Bardh Hoxha, Houssam Abbas
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: We propose injecting notions of fairness into multi-robot motion planning. When robots have competing interests, it is important to optimize for some kind of fairness in their usage of resources. In this work, we explore how the robots' energy expenditures might be fairly distributed among them, while maintaining mission success. We formulate a distributed fair motion planner and integrate it with safe controllers in a algorithm called FiReFly. For simulated reach-avoid missions, FiReFly produces fairer trajectories and improves mission success rates over a non-fair planner. We find that real-time performance is achievable up to 15 UAVs, and that scaling up to 50 UAVs is possible with trade-offs between runtime and fairness improvements.

title: Machine Learning-Assisted Signal Integrity Prediction in High-Speed Interconnects
authors: Shilpa Pavithran, Alex James
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: Ensuring signal integrity has become critical in highspeed PCB interconnects as frequencies scale into gigahertz ranges. This work presents a machine learning-assisted approach to predict the S parameters, such as return loss <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(S_{11}\right)$</tex>, insertion loss <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(S_{21})$</tex> and crosstalk <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(S_{31}, S_{41})$</tex>, which are key indicators of SI degradation in a coupled microstrip structure. A dataset of 75 geometrical configurations was generated by varying geometric parameters of the interconnect and used to train regression models. The introduction of derived features in terms of effective length was used for ML model generalization. Among the ML models tested, Random Forest achieved the best performance with an accuracy of 94% and a root mean square value less than 1.2 dB. The model was validated against the unseen test cases, and prediction results were compared against HFSS results, demonstrating its capability to accelerate early-stage SI analysis.

title: Load- and Interference-Aware CoATI-Based ADR Schemes for LoRaWAN IoT Networks
authors: J Uma Mahesh, Judhistir Mahapatro
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Networks and Telecommunications Systems (ANTS)
abstract: LoRaWAN technology has become foundational for massive IoT deployments requiring long-range, low-energy communication. Adaptive Data Rate (ADR) algorithms play a critical role in optimizing network capacity, energy efficiency, and reliability. However, existing ADR studies lack comprehensive evaluations across dense-to-large scale scenarios and do not fully address gateway load and interference. This paper presents a robust LoRaWAN ADR schemes, supporting Industrial, Suburban, and Rural–Agricultural scenarios with configurable network densities and interference levels. We benchmark three algorithms: (1) Standard-ADR, the LoRaWAN baseline; (2) CoATI-ADR, a metaheuristic-based, load-aware ADR algorithm that considers link quality and SINR metrics; and (3) Binary-CoATI-ADR, a binary population-based variant of CoATI-ADR that is also load-and interference-aware, but treats interference as an explicit, separately weighted fitness objective while jointly optimizing spreading factor, transmit power, and gateway assignment. Our results show that the proposed CoATI-based algorithms outperform Standard-ADR in single-channel LoRaWAN networks, achieving reductions in average transmission power of up to 79.5% and energy savings of up to 31.7%. They maintain network reliability, with Effective Packet Delivery Ratio remaining within ±0.1% of Standard-ADR, and improve fairness by up to 0.34% in dense and large-scale scenarios.

title: An Efficient Method to Search Valuable Cubes for Trivium
authors: Qing Xu, JiaXiang Zhang, Xiaodong Zhang, Xiaodong Yuwen, Jianxiong Wan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Information Communication and Software Engineering (ICICSE)
abstract: The cube attack is currently the most effective attack against Trivium. The major bottleneck of cube attacks on Trivium is the time-consuming construction of mother cubes that yield many valuable subcubes.In this paper, we presented a novel framework to search for valuable cubes. We propose two algorithms that dramatically accelerate this process: one rapidly constructs mother cubes, and the other efficiently detects linear terms in superpolies. Using the resulting framework, we construct mother cubes at least 10× faster than the previous best method and find new valuable cubes for 820-, 825-, and 830-round Trivium.

title: A Noval Encrypted Traffic Detection Framework Based on Multi-Granular Feature Fusion
authors: Delong Liang, Zijie Chen, Long Wen, Bo Yao, Jianqing Li, Yuanyuan Pan
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Information and Communication Networks (ICICN)
abstract: The proliferation of encrypted network traffic has introduced significant challenges to traditional cybersecurity approaches, particularly in the realm of traffic analysis. Encryption protocols enhance user privacy but also conceal critical information in network traffic, making malicious activities difficult to detect. To address this issue, we propose PGNet, a novel deep learning-based framework that integrates both packet-level and flow-level features for encrypted traffic classification. PGNet represents network traffic using two complementary modalities: Traffic Interaction Graphs (TIGs) to model temporal and spatial dependencies at the flow level through a Graph Neural Network (GNN), and grayscale pixel images to capture fine-grained spatial patterns at the packet level via a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). A multi-granular feature fusion mechanism is then employed to combine these representations, enabling a comprehensive and robust characterization of encrypted traffic. Evaluated on benchmark datasets, PGNet demonstrates superior performance in terms of classification accuracy, precision, and recall, while maintaining resilience against variations in traffic patterns.

title: Enabling Scalable, Privacy-Preserving Smart Pedestrian Crossings with CSI-Based Sensing on COTS Hardware
authors: Nayan Gokull Dharmaraj, Dileeka Dias, Kasun T. Hemachandra
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Real-time pedestrian sensing at crossings is essential for Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS). Yet traditional vision-based solutions and wearable devices face privacy, cost, and deployment challenges. While Wi-Fi channel state information (CSI) has shown promise in indoor environments, its potential for dynamic outdoor environments remains largely unexplored. This paper presents a device-free, CSI-based system for detecting human movement and walking direction at crossings and alert oncoming vehicles to enhance pedestrian safety. A twostage classification framework is adopted with noise suppression and Doppler-sensitive features. Classical and deep learning models are evaluated across four outdoor locations and seven subjects, achieving 96.9% accuracy for movement detection and up to 62% accuracy for classification of movement direction. These findings demonstrate the feasibility of deploying low-cost commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) hardware such as ESP32 and Raspberry Pi for scalable, privacy-preserving ITS applications.

title: Robotic Aspects and Intelligent Navigation of Medical Instruments for Minimally Invasive Procedures
authors: Olga Gerget, Tatuana Naumchik, Danil Gerget
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: The paper describes a robotic platform developed for the precise delivery of medical instruments during minimally invasive procedures (TAVR). A method for controlling a medical instrument from the standpoint of a flexible continuous robot is proposed. This method is based on the principle of the assumption of piecewise constant curvature. Modification of the FABRIKx algorithm is demonstrated. There is presented the visual feedback, which is formed on the basis of neural network image processing (segmentation of anatomical structures and detection of key points). Experiments have shown high accuracy in segmentation (Dice 0.881) and tracking (accuracy 0.97 at 50 fps).

title: Route Search Algorithm for Predicting Traffic Congestion Based on Other Vehicles' Routes
authors: Yafei Wang, Junpei Tokunaga, Hiroyuki Ebara, Naonori Ueda
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: In the near future, as autonomous vehicles become more prevalent, the demand for car navigation systems is expected to increase significantly. Therefore, developing navigational solutions that allow vehicles to reach their destinations smoothly, even under congested conditions, is critical. Moreover, with the proliferation of vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V) communication, real-time status information of other vehicles can be leveraged to further optimize routing. This paper proposes a shortestpath algorithm that reflects real-time traffic conditions by incorporating other vehicles' routes using a cost-variable timeexpanded network. The effectiveness of the proposed algorithm was evaluated through multi-agent simulation experiments.

title: Filled-state-space Experience Replay
authors: Xiangbei Liu, Wei Huang, Xian Guo
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Information and Communication Networks (ICICN)
abstract: Reinforcement Learning (RL) has made significant strides in diverse applications such as industrial production, robotics, and gaming competitions. However, RL algorithms face challenges in efficient data utilization, exploration, and agent robustness due to limitations in experience replay (ER). To address these challenges, this paper proposes the Filled-state-space Experience Replay (FER) algorithm, which enhances the experience replay method by sampling transitions based on their distribution in the state space, effectively filling the state space. By automatically generating mini-buffers and employing uniform distribution-based data filtering, the FER algorithm ensures that rare data are fully retained while redundant common data are removed. Consequently, the capacity of the replay buffer is fully utilized, accelerating training efficiency. Experimental results demonstrate that the training efficiency and robustness of the FER algorithm surpass those of SAC and PER algorithms, while its time cost remains acceptable. These findings highlight the potential of FER in advancing RL methods, providing a promising avenue for future research and application.

title: SMAE-DIM: Vehicle-Centric Semantic Masked AutoEncoders Pre-Training by Distilling Multimodal Foundational Models
authors: Alexandre Marques, Pedro Ferreira, Bruno Silva, Jorge Batista
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: In this work, we present SMAE-DIM, a vehiclecentric semantic Masked Autoencoder (MAE) pre-training by distilling multimodal foundational models. Our approach integrates MAE-based Masked Image Modeling (MIM) with CLIPstyle distillation, leveraging a curated, large-scale vehicle dataset (Automobile1M) and a visually grounded, unpaired text corpus. Unlike traditional vision-language models that require carefully curated image-caption pairs or large-scale aligned datasets, our method distills semantic knowledge from a pre-trained CLIP model without the need for direct image-text alignment during training. We also introduce Automobile1M-a largescale, in-domain dataset specifically curated for vehicle-centric pre-training, alongside with a dedicated distillation text corpus focused on visually grounded language. Specialized distillation losses were employed to enhance open-vocabulary logits during vision-language distillation by leveraging image embeddings as auxiliary pseudo-text embeddings, thereby strengthening semantic alignment between visual and textual modalities. Experiments demonstrate that SMAE-DIM effectively transfers to vehiclespecific downstream tasks via both linear probing and fine-tuning, unifying structural and semantic understanding within a single pretraining framework.

title: Factors influencing students’ continued intention to use Chat GPT for Academic Purposes
authors: Lamia Akther Urmi, Md Ahasan Habib, Md Rakib Hasan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Students’ intention to continue using ChatGPT for academic purposes is investigated in this study. A structured questionnaire was created to gather first-hand information from software engineering students in order to accomplish this goal. Here, finds the relevant financing agency. Delete this if there are none. 285 valid responses were analyzed using the Expectation Confirmation Model (ECM) and Structural Equation Modeling with Partial Least Squares (SEM-PLS). The results suggest that users’ enjoyment (EN) is strongly influenced by task skills (TS) and task difficulty (TC), and that pleasure has a good correlation with student satisfaction. The results further indicate that student satisfaction (SA) increases through the confirmation of perceived usefulness (PU). In addition, satisfaction with ChatGPT is found to be a key determinant of students’ continuance intention (CI) to use the tool for academic activities. Overall, the study provides empirical evidence on the factors shaping sustained academic use of ChatGPT among university students.

title: An Efficient and Explainable Hybrid CNN–Transformer Model for Retinal Image Classification
authors: Md. Mahid Arfan Rahat, Shahriar Mahmud Kabir, Safayetuzzaman Turag, MD. Abdul Bakki, Rakib Hossain Robin
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is a progressive microvascular complication of diabetes and one of the leading causes of blindness worldwide, underscoring the need for early and automated screening systems. Existing deep learning methods for binary DR classification (DR vs. non-DR) often rely on complex architectures that require substantial computational resources, limiting their use in resource-constrained clinical environments. To address this limitation, we propose an efficient parallel feature fusion framework designed for retinal image analysis, combining a lightweight MobileNetV2 branch for robust local feature extraction with a MobileViTv2 branch for capturing essential global contextual information. Evaluated on a publicly available fundus image dataset, the proposed hybrid model achieved an accuracy of 97.84% for binary DR classification, outperforming established baseline architectures, including CNN-based models (ResNet18, EfficientNet-B4, VGG16, and MobileNetV3) and transformer-based models (ViT-Base, DeiT-Base, Swin-Base, and Swin-Tiny). The model also maintains computational efficiency with only 4.93 million parameters, and its interpretability is supported by Grad-CAM visualization, which confirms attention to clinically relevant regions. The combination of high accuracy, interpretability, and low computational cost positions the proposed MobileNetV2–MobileViT architecture as a practical solution for scalable and real-time DR screening in healthcare applications.

title: AI Agent Debug: Transforming Silicon Hardware debug with Machine Learning and GenAI
authors: Trupti Joshi, Nakul Choudhari, Ashwani Kumar, Vishal B Bhogade
publish: 2025 IEEE 6th International Women in Technology Conference (WINTECHCON)
abstract: Managing hardware and software failures in large-scale systems is a critical challenge that demands efficient and accurate automated solutions. This paper presents a comprehensive automated failure management workflow leveraging Generative AI (GenAI) and cloud-based clustering models. The proposed system integrates data extraction, clustering, similarity analysis, and failure resolution into a unified framework using advanced natural language processing (NLP) techniques. The workflow comprises two main components: (1) comprehensive failure database creation via APIs and clustering models, and (2) daily failure analysis generating actionable insights and automated recommendations. Experimental results demonstrate significant improvements in processing efficiency, handling 800+ records daily with 92% precision and 88% recall, showcasing GenAI’s transformative potential in intelligent error analysis systems for modern computing infrastructure.

title: Comparison of Traditional and Large Model Methods for Bearing Fault Classification Tasks
authors: Kangli Xu, Chao Li, Wei Dong, Chunxiao Xing, Yunya Sun
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: With the development of transportation industry, the requirements for equipment safety are increasing. As a core component, the fault diagnosis and health management of bearings have become a research focus. The data used for bearing fault diagnosis are mostly time series data, which increases the difficulty of analysis. In recent years, Transformer-based large models have shown strong capabilities in fields such as natural language processing, promoting researchers explore the application of large models in the field of time series data analysis. However, it is uncertain whether the method based on large models suitable for the time series data analysis task of bearing fault diagnosis. Therefore, this study focuses on bearing fault classification tasks, by designing different task scenarios and comparing the performance of traditional methods (such as CNN and RNN) and large model-based methods. For the method of large model, a time series prediction model with MLP classifier architecture is constructed, using the prediction model as a feature extractor and the MLP as a classifier. The experiment results show that in simple scenarios, the traditional methods are more stable and easier to converge, while in complex scenarios, large model shows better feature extraction abilities. And due to the lack of long-term strong temporal dependencies in bearing vibration data, the advantages of large models cannot be reflected in various tasks of bearing fault diagnosis. Finally, this paper proposes future research in model architecture optimization, task scenario design and expansion of applicable fields.

title: Development and Implementation of A WEB-Based System for Monitoring Service Orders and Security in a Laundry
authors: Joseph Christoffer Condori Murga, Kin Meng Ordaya Loo, Alex Ricardo Contreras Anton, Sario Angel Chamorro Quijano
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Information Communication and Software Engineering (ICICSE)
abstract: Currently, process digitization has become a fundamental tool for improving operational efficiency in various sectors. In this context, a project was developed focused on implementing a web-based management system to optimize the services offered by the laundry. The system was designed to control and monitor the receipt, processing, and delivery of garments, as well as to guarantee the security of customer data and internal operations. Through a digital interface, the web system allows employees to register orders, check the status of services in real time, and manage resources efficiently. The platform includes functionalities based on user management, inventory control, and order traceability. A quantitative, applied, and descriptive- explanatory methodology was used with a sample of 50 employees, collecting data through surveys, direct observation, and document review. The results showed a significant improvement in response times and a reduction in errors in order registration. Unlike other existing systems in laundry, this project addresses monitoring, achieving comprehensive service management. In conclusion, the developed web system represents an effective and scalable solution to improve the quality of service in small and medium-sized enterprises in the sector. confirm the system's positive impact on operational performance and user experience. Furthermore, the satisfaction index improved considerably, rising from around 60% at the start of implementation to between 80% and 90% in the most recent period. In addition, usage behavior indicators, such as an average of 60 interactions per user, demonstrate greater familiarity with the platform and a more efficient workflow. These quantifiable improvements mean that the system has effectively supported staff in their daily operations and continues to solidify its position as a reliable tool for managing and controlling service orders.

title: Human Tracking Algorithm Evaluation with Digital Human Models in Gazebo
authors: Timur Gamberov, Ramil Safin, Elvira Chebotareva, Tatyana Tsoy, Mikhail Svinin, Evgeni Magid
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: Digital human models (DHM) for virtual environments allow a cost-effective and reproducible evaluation of computer vision algorithms that are employed in robotics, e.g., human detection, tracking and following. Yet, many popular robotics simulators do not achieve a reasonable level of realism and diversity required to rigorously test such algorithms under varying conditions. This paper introduces a set of customizable DHM for the Gazebo simulator, which features a range of clothing styles and dynamic walking animations. The developed DHM are evaluated using a differential-drive robot with a LiDAR and a monocular camera, that executes human tracking and following algorithm in a virtual office of the Gazebo environment. Through virtual experiments, we measure an average tracked distance and a frequency of tracking failures. The results underscore a potential of DHM for scalable and reproducible evaluation of vision-based algorithms, contributing to the development of more reliable systems in dynamic, human-populated settings.

title: Channel Measurements for Ultra-Low-Power Tissue-Coupled Communication in Personalized Cardiac Pacing and Continuous Monitoring
authors: Anyu Jiang, Dianelys Suarez, Ayan Biswas, Baibhab Chatterjee, Shreyas Sen
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: Leadless pacemakers (LPs) offer a minimally invasive solution for cardiac rhythm management but their battery life is limited by energy-inefficient, intermittent RF-based communication. This work explores Electro-Quasistatic In-Body-to-Out-of-Body Communication (EQS-IBOBC) as a low-power alternative for continuous monitoring. EQS-IBOBC achieves 100 X lower energy/bit than Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE), enabling more frequent real-time raw data transmission and patient-specific cloud-based analytics. Finite Element Method (FEM) simulations using a detailed anatomical model show an EQS-IBOBC channel transfer function between -75 dB and -90 dB, depending on transmitter size. Experimental validation using a PBS-filled mannequin confirms similar channel transfer function (-83 dB for heart-to-wrist), demonstrating practical feasibility. These results indicate that EQS-IBOBC can extend battery life while supporting continuous, secure data transmission in leadless pacemakers, potentially transforming remote cardiac care by enabling proactive, personalized, at-home treatments.

title: GRaft: Credit-Based Byzantine-Tolerant Consensus for Geo-Redundant Rail Transit Cloud Platforms
authors: Xinghe Zhang, Lianchuan Ma, Kaicheng Li, Tao Tang
publish: 2025 IEEE 28th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
abstract: Cloud computing, with its centralized management and elastic resource scalability, has become a key enabler for modern rail transit system. However, rail transit signal system demands high reliability and availability, typically requiring distributed architectures and geographic redundancy, conditions that clash with traditional centralized cloud models. To overcome this, we introduce an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{N}-2 \times 2$</tex>-out-of2 geo-redundant architecture built on a safe cloud computing platform, paired with GRaft, a consensus algorithm tailored for rail transit geo-redundancy. GRaft employs a credit-based node scoring mechanism that evaluates static configuration metrics, historical consensus participation, and communication latency to optimize leader election and prevent vote splitting. To further secure operation under Byzantine conditions, GRaft integrates an RSA-PSS digital signature scheme for realtime detection of malicious leader behavior and to guarantee consensus correctness. Experimental results show that our method maintains stable election and consensus latencies while significantly enhancing geo-redundant switchover performance, providing support for fault tolerance and continuous operation in cross-region distributed cloud environments.

title: Crack Defect Detection and Evaluation Method Based on Spiking Neural Network
authors: Yanxiao Wang, Zihao Lei, Zhizhen Ren, Guangrui Wen, Yu Su, Zhifen Zhang
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: Intelligent crack assessment in aero-engine turbine blades using eddy current detection is a key research focus. Eddy current detection, based on electromagnetic induction, enables rapid, non-destructive testing of conductive materials like turbine blades. However, traditional evaluation models often underperform due to complex signal variations in real-world environments. To address this, we propose a spiking neural network (SNN) method combining a residual network architecture with a frequency space-time attention module. The model first extracts signal features via an encoder, then enhances feature capture using the attention module, and finally performs end-to-end crack assessment. Compared to artificial neural networks (ANNs), our method improves bio-interpretability, time-series modeling, and dynamic signal processing. Experiments demonstrate superior performance in detecting inner-wall blade cracks, with feature visualization confirming domain-invariant extraction. The fusion of residual architecture and attention module enhances signal characterization, offering a promising approach for aero-engine quality inspection with significant engineering value.

title: Design of Dual-Band Orthogonal MIMO Antenna with Integrated Spiral Stub for C/X-Band Circular Polarization
authors: Lakshman Kukkadapu, Jani Pasha Shaik, Thanmai Gadagamma, Bhavani Vantakala
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a compact <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 2$</tex> circularly polarized MIMO antenna designed for modern high-frequency wireless applications. The antenna utilizes a CPW-fed monopole with an integrated spiral stub, enabling tri-band operation without the need for additional decoupling structures. Orthogonal placement of elements ensures high isolation and polarization diversity. The final design achieves impedance bandwidths covering 5.65-7.38 GHz and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7.65-11.21 \text{GHz}$</tex>, with strong return loss (up to 39.95 dB) and circular polarization maintained at multiple resonances. Measured results confirm excellent isolation exceeding 15 dB and an envelope correlation coefficient below 0.001, validating the antenna's suitability for WiFi-6E, radar, and sub-12 GHz communication systems.

title: Characteristic Mode Analysis of UWB Cavity Resonator Antenna
authors: Sumithra Paneerselvam, Kannadassan Dhanaraj, Krushna Kanth Varikuntla
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: The growing demand for high data rates, wide spectral coverage, and precise system performance, particularly in 5G and IoT, has accelerated the need for broadband antennas with stable gain and consistent broadside radiation patterns. To meet these requirements, we propose the fabry perot cavity resonator (FPR) antenna, where electromagnetic field oscillations resulting from multiple reflections between two parallel reflective surfaces enable ultra wideband operation through constructive interference. The design employs a dipole antenna as the primary radiating element, enclosed by a reflecting surface in ' <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$c^{\prime}$</tex> shape that combines the features of an FPR and a yagi-uda antenna. The proposed antenna operates over 1.5 GHz to 3.5 GHz frequency range, exhibiting a stable broadside radiation pattern and a consistent gain of approximately 3 dB, with a reflection coefficient (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{11}$</tex>) performance better than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<-10 \mathbf{~ d B}$</tex> across the band.

title: Addressing Class Imbalance in Medical Data: A Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) Approach for Chronic Kidney Disease Prediction
authors: Md Arnob Hasan Joy, Md Tamzid Omor Bhuiyan, Md. Rimon Miah, Jayed Bin Alamgir
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) is a significant health concern in contemporary society. Due to its early asymptomatic nature, it is frequently referred to as the silent sickness. Consequently, the early prediction of this condition is imperative. Nonetheless, a challenge persists regarding class imbalance in CKD datasets, which are huge. Therefore, properly predicting this condition is unfeasible. This research employed SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique) to balance the dataset and enhance the accuracy of CKD predictions. A range of supervised machine learning methods was employed to predict CKD. We evaluated the performance of machine learning models prior to and after the use of SMOTE. The application of SMOTE improved the performance of the machine learning algorithms. The assessment criteria employed in the models included accuracy, precision, recall, F1 score, and AUC-ROC. Initially, the models exhibited subpar performance in precision, recall, and F1 scoring. Following the use of SMOTE, the precision and recall metrics showed enhancement. The most effective model was XGBoost. It achieved an accuracy of over 96.1%, a precision of 92.6%, and an F1 score of 93.4%. This research will be advantageous for researchers and physicians.

title: Stub-Augmented Low Profile Magneto-Electric Dipole Antenna for Unified 2G to 4G and Sub-6 GHz n78 Band Coverage
authors: Vadlamudi Roja, Balakrishna Islavath, Indhumathi K, D Sindhanaiselvi, T. Shanmuganantham
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a novel, low-profile magnetoelectric (ME) dipole antenna optimized for seamless integration across legacy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(2 \mathrm{G} / 3 \mathrm{G} / 4 \mathrm{G})$</tex> and emerging 5 G sub- 6 GHz frequency bands, including n78 and portions of the n77 band. The innovative incorporation of vertical stub augmentations into the ME dipole structure enables substantial bandwidth enhancement without increasing the antenna's height or complexity. The proposed design achieves an impressive impedance bandwidth of over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$103.32 \%(1.3-4.0$</tex> GHz) with VSWR<2, ensuring broad coverage of key wireless communication standards. Simulation results demonstrate a peak gain exceeding 9.0 dBi and excellent polarization purity, with cross-polarization levels below 20 dB across the operating band. With its compact footprint, wideband performance, and high radiation efficiency, this antenna is ideally suited for nextgeneration base stations and space-constrained wireless access point deployments.

title: Few-Shot Fault Diagnosis for Electro-Hydrostatic Actuators Based on CNN Feature Extraction and Meta-Learning
authors: Zhan Zhang, Shuanglu Li, Chun Zhao, Chuanjie Lu, Yibo Song, Xiaoli Zhao, Jianyong Yao
publish: 2025 Global Reliability and Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Xian)
abstract: This paper proposes a fault diagnosis method for Electro-Hydrostatic Actuators (EHA) based on the integration of Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) feature extraction and meta-learning strategies. As a high-precision linear actuator, EHA is widely used in aerospace and aviation, with a complex operating principle and high-performance standards. To address the challenge of small sample data I n EHA fault diagnosis, this study combines the Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning (MAML) algorithm with CNN for feature extraction and employs meta-learning to improve the model's generalization ability. Experimental results show that the model, incorporating meta-learning, achieves a classification accuracy of 97.13% in small sample environments, significantly outperforming traditional CNN models. This method provides an effective solution for EHA fault diagnosis and effectively addresses the small sample problem.

title: Design of Compact Dual Band Quarter Mode SIW Filter for Ku and Ka Band Applications
authors: Ganaraj P S, Koushik Guha, Kavicharan Mummaneni, J. Iannacci, Massimo Donelli, Mahesh Angira, Deepak Bansal, Girolamo Tagliapietra
publish: 2025 IEEE Microwaves, Antennas, and Propagation Conference (MAPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a compact dual band Quarter Mode Substrate Integrated Waveguide (QMSIW) filter for Ku band and Ka Band applications. A triangular QMSIW cavity is coupled using Coplanar Waveguide (CPW) to achieve an isolation between the two pass bands. The two pass bands achieved are, one formed by the fundamental TE101 mode and the other formed due to higher order mode by TE102 mode. The QMSIW miniaturized structure is further miniaturized by loading the structure with the Complementary Split Ring Resonators (CSRR). The structure is designed and simulated over the RT/Duroid 6002 substrate having a thickness of 0.508 mm. The Insertion achieved in simulation is well below the required -15 dB for both the passbands. The resonant bands for Ku frequency band were obtained between 14.69 GHz and 15.52 GHz and for resonant band for Ka frequency band were achieved between 28.4 GHz and 29.52 GHz. Tunability of 100 MHz in lower passband and 200 MHz in upper passband is achieved using RF MEMS SPDT switch. The tunable filter is designed, analyzed and simulated using ANSYS HFSS.

title: A Phase Noise Suppression Scheme Utilizing Gaussian Basis Expansion for CO-FBMC
authors: Ran Bi, Jian Ge, Huifeng Li, Yuetong Ge, Xiaoyan Niu, Yongtao Huang
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Information and Communication Networks (ICICN)
abstract: This paper presents a phase noise suppression method based on Gaussian basis expansion and applies it to the coherent optical filter bank multicarrier (CO-FBMC) transmission system. Compared to the traditional CO-OFDM system, CO-FBMC offers superior spectral efficiency and enhanced resistance to multipath interference, making it a strong contender for next-generation optical communication systems. However, CO-FBMC also faces phase noise interference, which degrades system performance. To address this issue, the proposed Gaussian basis expansion phase noise suppression algorithm effectively improves the system's bit error rate (BER) performance. Simulation results show that, when the laser linewidth is 1 MHz, the BER of the CO-FBMC system is optimized from 9.48×10-4 to 6.48×10-4. Compared to the orthogonal basis expansion method, the BER is improved by half an order of magnitude, demonstrating the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed algorithm in phase noise suppression.

title: Sustainable IoT-Based Deep Water Culture Hydroponic for Leaf & Plant-Based Cultivation System with Real-Time Monitoring and Alerts
authors: Taseen Rahman, Nadman Ahmed Chowdhury, Kazi Shahed Mamun, Md. Mahim Rahman, Zannatul Musta, Md Sajid Hossain
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer &amp; Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE)
abstract: A deep water culture (DWC) hydroponic system was developed and tested with the purpose of measuring the quality of nutrient solution and plants. The system consists of the combination of a pH sensor, TDS sensor, the temperature sensor, and an ESP32 microcontroller to constantly obtain the nutrient reservoir data. The idea supports the Sustainable Development Goals, specifically Goal 15: Life on Land by reducing pressure on natural soil ecosystems and allowing a sustainable, land-independent way of food/crop production that coexists with nature. The sensor readings are sent to Blynk cloud dashboard through Wi-Fi, where the real-time data can be observed. Once a parameter goes out of the threshold values defined, the ESP32 sends a notification through the Telegram bot. The outcomes indicate that the pH was kept in 5.6-6.9, the TDS was in 853-1075ppm and temperature of solutions was kept between 26.4°C and 32.7°C. On the same time period the plant height increased from 5 inch (12.7 cm) to more than 20 inches (50.8 cm). Through the integration of IoT, the proposed system demonstrates the presence of reliable real-time control, active data recording, and efficient created alerts, which depict the ability to improve the precision and sustainability of modern hydroponic farming.

title: A DenseNet-OpenPose Framework for Static Biometric Recognition via Human Body Keypoints
authors: Baixiang Xu, Wenzhang Zhang, Bintao Hu, Yuanrui Huang, Guangyu Ren, Hengyan Liu
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Information and Communication Networks (ICICN)
abstract: The conventional biometric modalities, including fingerprints, iris patterns, and facial features, have been considered for recognition during the past few years. To increase the recognition accuracy, we propose a novel biometric recognition framework to consider more static anthropometric parameters, which include body shape, height, arm span, and limb proportions. In addition, we propose a DenseNet-enhanced OpenPose architecture that is developed to extract 2D skeletal keypoints from RGB images, incorporating a Coordinate Attention (CA) mechanism to enhance keypoint localisation accuracy. Based on the extracted keypoints, robust static measurements and morphological descriptors are derived to construct discriminative biometric features. The proposed model is evaluated on the Southampton Gait Database using both Object Keypoint Similarity (OKS)-based precision and classification accuracy metrics. Experimental results demonstrate that even under conditions involving varied viewpoints and partial occlusions. The proposed approach offers a privacy-preserving alternative for biometric recognition, with promising applications in scenarios such as healthcare monitoring, non-cooperative surveillance, and contactless identity verification.

title: Investigation of the Reflection Coefficient of Radio Waves from a Solution of Water with Salt Impurities Contained in a Vessel, the Transverse Dimension of which is Comparable to the Wavelength of the Radio Signal
authors: Ovchinnikov Dmitry Aleksandrovich, Baranov Sergey Anatolevich, Barbin Nikolai Mihailovich, Kuanishev Valery Taukenovich
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Information, Control, and Communication Technologies (ICCT)
abstract: Water is the most common solvent and its properties vary depending on the impurities it contains. This allows it to be used as a sensor to indirectly monitor other processes. During the study, we measured the reflection coefficients of ultrahigh-frequency radio waves from a container filled with an aqueous solution. The transverse size of the container was comparable to the wavelength of the radio waves. Reflection from this surface is complex and can be seen as reflection from a multilayer structure. By measuring changes in the reflection coefficient, we can judge changes in the complex permittivity of water and evaluate the course of other processes. This contactless method of tracking water changes is useful because it can be used without the need for optical visibility of the object or proximity to it. This makes it different from other technologies. As part of the study, a solution of water with sodium chloride impurities was used to evaluate the sensitivity of the method being considered. One of the unique aspects of this research is that it was conducted not in a laboratory setting, but under conditions that more closely resemble real-world usage, with many potential sources of industrial radio noise. Based on the findings of the study, plots of the dependence of the reflection coefficient on a solution of water containing salt impurities were created, the measurement error was calculated, and conclusions were drawn regarding the sensitivity of the method, considering the specific equipment used for the measurements. This study was conducted as part of a statefunded project at SibGUTI (Grant No. 071-00003-25-00).
